- **`recordGateInRunningSet` now records EVERY gate-role node's open, not only `main-session-gate` — closing a gap where a code-reviewer/security-reviewer/adversarial-verifier gate stayed invisible to `open-ready`'s speculative admission — #439.** The guard was scoped strictly to `node.role !== 'main-session-gate'`, so opening any OTHER gate role serially (`open-next` / the fused advance) never landed it in the running set as a `kind:'gate'` member — leaving both the write-lane fence and the `serialLive` derivation blind to it, so `open-ready` refused an otherwise-eligible speculative read with `serial_node_live` as if the gate did not exist. The guard now checks membership in `GATE_ROLES` (`main-session-gate` plus the review gates `code-reviewer`, `security-reviewer`, `adversarial-verifier`), matching the already role-symmetric close-side removal and `speculativeReviewOnGateClose`, so a gate opened through any of these roles now correctly flips `serialLive` false and lets `speculative_open_policy: auto` admit an eligible speculative descendant. A non-gate role remains an unconditional no-op — byte-identical for every ordinary serial write/read open.
- **Exact-file-disjoint docs write siblings now co-open in isolated legs instead of being forced serial — #702.** `docs/` is a top-level path area not in `SHARED_INFRA`, so two nodes each declaring a different exact `docs/*.md` file got a coarse-RED disjointness verdict; combined with the surface-granular `parallel_allowband_collision` refusal (#587), the natural "N independent docs writers in parallel" shape was out of grammar by construction — a docs-heavy epic paid ~60–100 min of avoidable serialization per bundle. The fix is file-granular across three seams. **Freeze (1):** the declared-`fanout()`-group disjointness loop now LIFTS the runtime `coarse` relaxation (#593) — via `groupWriteOverlapRelaxable`, a pair-loop reuse of `disjointWriteSets` + `writeOverlapRelaxable`, no second comparator — scoped to PURE-docs-allowband groups under the SAME retained net (NET-1 post-dominating `code-reviewer` over the group's own legs + NET-2 no-PROTECTED + `hasUnresolvableEntry` resolvability + exact-never-relaxes); a NON-docs coarse fan-out (`api/`, `crates/`) keeps today's freeze refusal (freeze stays stricter than runtime for code, mirroring the `shared-infra` "must serialize" wall). **Freeze (2):** the allowband arm splits HARD (`CHANGELOG.md`/`README.md` or a glob/dir `docs/` token — still refuses on ≥2 legs) from SOFT (exact-file `docs/**` — admitted when exact-file-disjoint + gate-covered, per-pair for inferred antichain siblings). **Runtime (3):** the per-leg barrier (`--leg-barrier`) threads a new `opts.legScoped` so a leg's out-of-declared-set docs write now refuses `write_set_overflow` (no silent both-apply at the synthesis merge) while the group/union and whole-plan barriers stay byte-identical (docs still exempt there). **(4)** `README.md` joins `CHANGELOG.md`/`ROADMAP.md` in `PROTECTED_BASENAMES`, making "stays single-leg" a structural NET-2 guarantee. First test coverage for `parallel_allowband_collision` beyond the walkthrough pins: freeze regression + relaxed-behavior (`simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`), leg-visible docs-overflow + barrier mode-parity anti-leak (`test-adaptive-node.js`), and the `--parallel-safe` docs floor + `README.md`-PROTECTED pin (`test-commit-node.js`). `plan-validator.js` + `classifier.js` mirrored across all four editions via `edition-sync.js`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). See [D-702-01](docs/decisions/D-702-01.md).
- **Codex role children now inherit the main session's model and reasoning effort — #687.** All 48 generated role profiles omit both runtime keys; installer/preflight safely migrate exact legacy Sol/medium and Sol/xhigh pairs while rejecting partial or foreign pins. Standard/reasoning tiers remain declarative metadata with 20/40-minute wait budgets. A live Codex 0.144.3 parent/child probe proved the shared omission path, and reasoning-floor roles still require fresh classified parent-session proof rather than trusting inheritance or tier metadata alone. See [D-687-01](docs/decisions/D-687-01.md).
- **Fail-closed hardening of the review/repair proof, the advance-path durability, and the barrier-ref sweep — #688, #689, #691.** A batch of strictly-tightening, defense-in-depth fixes for already-unreachable inputs. #688: `proveRebindAdmissible` now fails closed on an absent ledger (instead of degrading to admit-all) and restricts owner-gate liveness attribution to `{pending, in_progress}` (a selector-pruned `n/a` gate is excluded); the plan grammar refuses a node id that is an `Object.prototype` key (`__proto__`, `constructor`, …) at freeze; and `isCanonicalBlobMap`'s enumeration-order check is now order-insensitive so a correctly-built `candidate_declared` map with a canonical-integer path key (`10`, `2024`) no longer fails its own validation. #689: `writeFileAtomic` in the opt-in `fast`/`full`/`phase4` advance scripts now fsyncs the parent directory after `renameSync` (the same platform-fail-soft fix #685 landed on the adaptive path). #691: the `barrier-ref-sweep` keep-pass now keeps a project whose `workflow-state.md` is unreadable through a `chmod 000` directory (any non-`ENOENT` stat fault ⇒ keep), reapable only on a clean `ENOENT`.
- **Archived projects no longer strand their barrier gc-anchor refs — #686.** `--record-base` anchors each node's baseline under `refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/<project>/<node>` so `git gc` cannot prune it mid-window, but `--drop-base` is window-locked to `pending` (D-424-01) and archive never reaped the refs — leaving ~239 stranded across ~42 archived projects. `archiveProjectDir` now reaps this project's own `barrier/<tag>/*` refs after the archive is verified (fail-soft — never blocks or rolls back the archive), and a new one-shot `kaola-workflow-claim.js barrier-ref-sweep` collects the pre-existing stranded refs under a keep-everything-live discipline: it keeps every tag belonging to a live project across all `git worktree list` roots (active-folder tag, live `running-set.json`, or an unreadable-but-present `workflow-state.md`), is ADD-only on collisions (fail-safe under-reap), scoped to `barrier/` only (group `barrier-base/*` and `leg-base/*` keep their own lifecycles), network-free, and fails closed if the worktree set cannot be enumerated. Adversarially hardened across four over-reap classes (keep-universe, worktree-path parse, keep-signal). See [D-686-01](docs/decisions/D-686-01.md).
- **Simultaneous-failed-gates repair no longer dead-ends — #683.** When two review gates fail on one candidate at the same time, `repair-node` used to wedge (`would_orphan_in_progress`, then `candidate_digest_changed` / `original_barrier_failed`) and only a whole-plan discard recovered. Repairs now resolve in place via an explicit, append-only journal re-bind transaction: a failed attempt survives an unrelated sibling repair when — and only when — every changed path is byte-exact with the reviewed candidate (its own producer slice and the undeclared residue) or is positively attributed to another writer whose repair is currently live and whose declared set is disjoint, with the writer's barrier baseline re-anchored to a synthetic tree that keeps its own reviewed diff bit-for-bit. Every partition is fail-closed (`candidate_residue_changed` / `candidate_slice_changed` / `candidate_delta_unattributed` / `rebind_base_rewrite_unsafe`), the five-consumed-repairs breaker cannot be laundered through the new binding, and the crash-retry variant (crash between settle and consume, then the reopened writer edits) is consumable on retry rather than wedging. See [D-683-01](docs/decisions/D-683-01.md).
- **Restored mutation-killing coverage for the #682 fail-closed paths — #684.** Six load-bearing review/repair guards (the `open-next` and `open-ready` journal fences, `candidate_digest_changed`, the unique-maximal-producer `repair_requires_replan`, `writer_identity_changed`, and the missing-journal compliance/failed-attempt witness detector) plus the #664 collective fold now each have a regression that fails when its single guard is individually disabled, driven through the real subcommand/CLI; the #664 fold is exercised on a real frozen plan with a real journal attempt, and both fold implementations have independent single-site killers.
- **`writeFileAtomicReplace` now fsyncs the parent directory after rename — #685.** A settled durable-state write (the review journal, `workflow-plan.md`, `workflow-state.md`, the roadmap mirror) could revert to its pre-rename directory entry after power loss because the atomic-replace helper fsynced only the temporary file, not the containing directory. The canonical helper (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`, byte-identical across all four editions) and its genuine sibling (`kaola-workflow-roadmap.js` + edition ports) now fsync the parent directory after `renameSync`, platform fail-soft (a directory that cannot be opened or fsynced degrades silently and never turns a previously-accepted write into a refusal). This discharges the R17 durability follow-up deferred by [D-682-01](docs/decisions/D-682-01.md).
- **Adaptive review failures now form crash-safe, fail-closed transactions with agent-selected bounded repair — #682.** A plan-bound `.cache/review-attempts.json` journal records the effective verdict, exact sequence or fan-out gate identity, receipt generation, candidate digest, producer proof, lifecycle settlement, and repair consumption without becoming a scheduler or second workflow state machine. Failed gates return to `pending` without exposing successors; exact-member fan-out settles by strict majority; retries resume every durable close and repair seam idempotently; canonical findings preserve zero/one/multiple-owner routing without harness selection; repair requires the agent's attempt-bound unique-maximal writer plus unchanged-candidate and original-barrier proofs; sibling cleanup uses the greatest authoritative gate-local ordinal regardless of physical journal order; and each logical gate permits five consumed repairs before the sixth remains unresolved. Planner and operator guidance now favors independently verifiable semantic boundaries for high-risk persistence/concurrency work while preserving coherent large nodes and avoiding numeric size thresholds. The pre-existing lack of parent-directory fsync in the shared atomic-replace helper remains separately deferred.

### Documentation

- **The Codex installation guide now configures long-poll subagent joins.** The recommended user-owned `~/.codex/config.toml` posture sets both the default and per-call maximum Multi-Agent V2 wait to Codex's one-hour ceiling, explicitly distinguishes a mailbox timeout from a subagent runtime deadline, requires repeated long waits without a Kaola-imposed total deadline while work is progressing, forbids status-only polling or interruption, explains how to merge existing TOML keys safely, and requires a fresh Codex session after the edit.

## [6.22.2] - 2026-07-13

### Fixed

- **`open-ready` lane-group baselines no longer strand on a Phase-2 abort or a pre-journal crash — #680.** The crash-window sequels to #674/#678, both crash/race-window-only and pre-existing. (a) `runOpenReady`'s two Phase-2 ledger-seed aborts (`baseline_failed`, `node_not_in_ledger`) now drop the shared group baseline AND the per-member baselines exactly like the Phase-1 aborts (the drop helpers were hoisted to `runOpenReady` scope); the earlier "--drop-base is illegal in Phase-2" reasoning was corrected — the ledger flip is in-memory until `writeFile(planPath)` runs after the loop, so the on-disk row stays `pending` and the drop is legal. (b) A hard-crash (SIGKILL-class) between recording the Phase-1 baselines and writing the `opening` running-set journal previously stranded `barrier-base-*` files/refs with no marker for `reconcile-running-set` to find; `runReconcileRunningSet` now runs an additive orphan-baseline sweep (hoisted above the `!running` early-return, mirroring the orphan-leg sweep) that drops only a `barrier-base-*` with NO live owner — a keep-set of in_progress ledger rows + running-set node ids + live lane_group member/group ids, with sanitizer collisions only ever ADDing to the keep set (fail-safe under-reap) and fail-soft on any error. A group baseline is KEPT whenever the running-set is torn/absent and any member is in_progress (its `lg-<...>` id is unrecoverable from the ledger, so its deadness is unprovable), and reaped only when authoritatively dead (a readable running-set, or zero in_progress rows). RED-first regressions cover the Phase-2 drop, the pre-journal orphan reap, and the live-baseline keep (false-positive guard); an in-run adversarial change-gate caught and drove the repair of a live-group-baseline drop on the exact torn-Phase-3 path before ship. `adaptive-node.js` GENERATED via `sync:editions`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`gap-sweep`'s `foreign_run_gaps_output` guard no longer skips a non-existent foreign target — #681.** The #679 belt-and-suspenders residual: the guard was gated on `&& fs.existsSync(outputPath)`, so an explicit `--output` aimed at a non-existent `run-gaps.json` outside the scanned project's own `.cache/` still wrote a stray fresh file into a foreign/archive tree. The existence precondition is dropped — the guard now refuses ANY `--output` whose basename is `run-gaps.json` and whose resolved path differs from the project's own `.cache/run-gaps.json`, whether or not it already exists (a scan never writes a `run-gaps.json` outside its own `.cache/`); the legitimate in-project `--output` (resolved-equal to the project's own default artifact path) is exempt, so a first-run in-project write is unaffected. `gap-sweep.js` COMMON canonical↔codex + rename-normalized GitLab/Gitea forge ports; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`worktreeDirtyState` now fail-closes an `existsSync` stat failure instead of misrouting a present worktree to `'missing'` — #677.** The #672 sibling: `worktreeDirtyState` (`kaola-workflow-claim.js`) hoisted a bare `fs.existsSync(wtPath)` gate out of the porcelain-probe try to distinguish `'missing'` (path absent) from `'unprobeable'` (path present, probe threw — kept by both sweep consumers), but `fs.existsSync` returns **false for a genuinely-present path** whose parent dir is unreadable (`chmod 000` / EACCES on an ancestor) — misrouting a real worktree to `'missing'`, which the destructive sweep consumers (`cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup`, `cmdLegacyWorktreeCleanup`) prune (registry entry removed + merged-branch ref deleted). The gate is now a fail-closed `fs.lstatSync(wtPath)` inside try/catch: only `ENOENT` returns `'missing'`; any other stat error (EACCES/ENOTDIR on an unreadable parent) returns the existing `'unprobeable'` keep state — no new state, both consumers already keep it unconditionally. Also closes the #672 A1 coverage gap: a mirrored `cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup` unprobeable-keep regression (only `cmdLegacyWorktreeCleanup` was covered) plus a root-guarded parent-unreadable RED. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea claim editions synchronized; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`open-ready`'s Phase-1 group-form aborts now also drop the shared GROUP baseline — the #674 symmetric gap — #678.** #674 dropped each per-MEMBER barrier baseline before every post-baseline Phase-1 leg-provisioning abort, but the shared group baseline (`barrier-base-lg-<group_id>` file + `refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/<project>/lg-<...>` ref), recorded once before the member loop, was NOT dropped on those same 5 aborts (`stub_commit_failed`, mid-loop `baseline_failed`, the three `leg_provision_failed` variants) — stranding it, so a later reuse could misattribute a sibling's landed diff. A guarded `dropGroupBaseline()` helper (guarded on `groupBaselineSha`, reusing the idempotent `--drop-base --node-id <group_id>` the close/merge path already uses) now fires alongside the member drop at all 5 aborts; a RED-first regression forces a group-form abort and asserts the group `barrier-base-lg-*` file + ref are both gone. Two out-of-scope crash/race-window residuals — the Phase-2 aborts drop neither baseline class, and a pre-journal SIGKILL strands baselines with no `opening` marker — were filed as #680. `adaptive-node.js` GENERATED via `sync:editions`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`gap-sweep` refuses `foreign_run_gaps_output` instead of clobbering an archived `run-gaps.json` a live scan does not own — #679.** The #675 residual: `runScan`'s `project_archived` refusal fires only when the ACTIVE project dir is GONE, so with a LIVE project dir AND a same-named leftover archive both present, an explicit `--output` aimed at the archive's `run-gaps.json` bypassed the refusal (`projectDir` still exists) and the live scan clobbered a prior cycle's archived gap evidence. `runScan` now refuses `foreign_run_gaps_output` (before any scan/`mkdir`/write) when the resolved `--output` basename is `run-gaps.json`, is not this project's own `.cache/run-gaps.json`, and already exists — never overwriting a foreign/archived cycle's evidence; a legitimate in-project explicit `--output` (equal to the project's own default artifact path) is unaffected. A belt-and-suspenders residual (a **non-existent** foreign target still gets a fresh write, harmless — no machine consumer) was filed as #681; a flagged lexical-`path.resolve`-vs-`realpathSync` symlink edge was proven non-exploitable (safe over-refusal only). `gap-sweep.js` COMMON + rename-normalized forge ports; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`schema.locateSection` now anchors its heading match to the classifier's `^##\s+<heading>\s*$`, closing the heading channel of the `plan_hash` wedge — #673.** The post-#670 sibling: #670 aligned `locateSection`'s FENCE detection with the hash-covering classifier, but its HEADING match still used a loose `startsWith('## ' + heading)` prefix while the classifier uses an anchored `^##\s+<heading>\s*$`. They disagreed — `locateSection` would prefix-match a decoy `## Node Ledger Extra` (forward), and miss `##  Node Ledger` / `##\tNode Ledger` the classifier accepts (reverse) — so a runtime section reader could select a heading the classifier does not, risking a `plan_hash` mismatch wedge on resume/barrier. `locateSection`'s opener is now the classifier's anchored `^##\s+<heading>\s*$` regex (identical heading-escaping) and its next-heading terminator is the classifier's `^##\s`; the `i > 0` line-0 guard is retained (line-0 parity is structurally unreachable in a frozen plan — line 0 is always the `#` title or the `<!-- plan_hash -->` comment — so it is inert). A heading-decoy regression asserts `locateSection` ↔ `classifier.sectionBody` selection parity across the suffix / two-space / tab / line-0 cases end-to-end (no `plan_hash_mismatch` on resume); an adversarial change-gate found zero divergence across 80,000+ fuzzed heading constructions. Applied byte-identically across the four `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` editions (the byte-anchor group); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **The archive-completeness gate is now source-relative — an incomplete archive can no longer silently delete a run's gate evidence — #676.** `verifyArchiveComplete` gates archive completeness before either live copy is deleted, but it previously checked only `workflow-state.md`, so an adaptive archive missing the plan, `finalization-summary.md`, and all per-node `.cache/` gate evidence still passed and both live copies were deleted (a consumer lost every gate verdict / RED→GREEN record while the receipt claimed `archive_commit: done`). It now verifies the archive DEST preserves **every evidence file that actually exists in the live SOURCE** — the top-level `workflow-plan.md`/`workflow-state.md`/`finalization-summary.md`/`fast-summary.md` plus every `.cache/*.md` node-evidence file (any node-id shape, minus the fixed-name machinery sidecars `final-validation.md`/`run-gaps-manual.md`/`selection-evidence.md`/`doc-docking.md`/`doc-updater.md`) — refusing with a typed `archive_incomplete` before any deletion or roadmap/issue/label side effect if the copy dropped a source-held file; `workflow-state.md` remains an unconditional identity anchor and the atomic in-place `renameSync` path stays ungated (it cannot drop files). Source-relative was chosen over the issue's literal absolute per-`workflow_path` floor (a value call routed to the operator) because it catches the exact evidence-loss with zero test-fixture breakage and preserves the minimal-finalize contract — decision record `docs/decisions/D-676-01.md`. An adversarial change-gate refuted a first attempt whose node-evidence glob (`^n\d*-.+\.md$`) missed free-form and role-named evidence names; the broadened enumeration was re-verified across all four editions. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea claim editions synchronized; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`gap-sweep`'s scanner refuses `project_archived` instead of corrupting archived gap evidence — #675.** `runScan` hardcoded its input `.cache` dir to the pre-archive `kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache`, so a `--json` scan run AFTER `cmdFinalize` archived the project folder (a) recreated a stray active `kaola-workflow/{project}/` dir with an empty sweep, and (b) with an explicit `--output` at the archive, overwrote the archived `run-gaps.json` with an empty `sweptClasses: []`. The scanner now refuses with a typed `project_archived` (when the active folder is gone but an archive exists) before any `mkdir`/read/`--output` write, so no stray active dir is recreated and no archived `run-gaps.json` is clobbered; the live and never-claimed scan paths are unchanged. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized.

- **`open-ready` lane-group provisioning no longer serial-degrades on a consumer `.cache/` gitignore, and no longer strands member baselines that a later serial open reuses — #674.** Two coupled defects in the parallel-write engine's group-open transaction, both live-observed in a consumer repo. (a) The seeded member evidence stubs were staged with `git add` (no `-f`), so a consumer that gitignores `.cache/` (a common local-evidence convention) refused the add → the co-open aborted with `stub_commit_failed` and the authored parallel-write antichain silently serial-degraded. The stub paths are explicitly enumerated by the script, so they are now `git add -f`'d (they MUST be tracked so legs inherit them). (b) The per-member barrier baselines are recorded (tree snapshot + gc-anchor ref + freshness token) BEFORE the stub commit, and a group-form abort did not drop them — so when a member later opened SERIALLY, `--record-base`'s crash-resume idempotency REUSED the stale baseline and the member's close barrier misattributed a sibling's landed diff as `write_set_overflow`. Every post-baseline group-form abort (`stub_commit_failed`, mid-loop `baseline_failed`, the three `leg_provision_failed` variants) now transactionally drops the recorded member baselines (file + ref) so a later serial open re-records fresh. RED-first regressions reproduce both defects (the exact consumer incident); an adversarial change-gate audited every abort path for baseline-drop completeness. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized via `sync:editions`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **Two porcelain-probe fail-opens are now fail-CLOSED: a silent synthesizer leg-drop and a destructive worktree sweep — #672.** Both were pre-existing, out-of-mandate residuals deferred from #669 (verified byte-identical pre-#669). (1) `synthesizeLevel`'s leg-dirty `git status --porcelain` probe (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) collapsed any exec/overflow error to `dirty=''`, reporting the leg **clean** so real committed/working leg content was **silently omitted from the octopus merge** (the ancestry-only `leg_omitted_from_merge` guard does not cover this working-tree probe). It now carries the 64 MB `GIT_MAX_BUFFER` cap and, on any probe failure, **loudly refuses** (`leg_dirty_probe_failed`) rather than merging without the leg. (2) `worktreeDirtyState` (`kaola-workflow-claim.js`) caught a probe failure as `'missing'`, and the destructive sweep consumers (`cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup`, `cmdLegacyWorktreeCleanup`) treat non-`'dirty'` as removable — so a probe fault on a real, dirty legacy worktree could feed a `git worktree remove --force`. A new distinct `'unprobeable'` state is now unconditionally KEPT by both consumers (a `skipped_unprobeable` bucket checked before every removal branch, with no `--force`/`--archive`/`--export` override); `'missing'` still prunes a genuinely-absent path. RED-first regressions drive both probe-error paths (leg-not-dropped; worktree-kept); an adversarial change-gate live-verified both consumers across every flag and a >64 MB ENOBUFS shape. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized (adaptive-node GENERATED via `sync:editions`; the divergent claim forge ports hand-edited); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **A task-mirror write fault now emits a one-line `mirror_write_failed` warning instead of a raw multi-line stack trace — #671.** The durable `workflow-tasks.json` mirror is regenerated by shelling the task-mirror CLI, and its write (`kaola-workflow-task-mirror.js`, the `main()` `fs.writeFileSync`) had no local guard — so an `EISDIR` (e.g. `workflow-tasks.json` colliding with a directory) or any other write fault crash-printed a raw multi-line stack trace to stderr. The invoker (`refreshTaskMirror`) already fail-opens on the child's non-zero exit (a mirror write must never block a run), but the child's stack trace still leaked, adding noise that can mask a real failure in a long log. The write is now wrapped to emit a concise one-line `mirror_write_failed` envelope (`{ status, path, message }`) via the script's existing shared emit/refuse helper and exit non-zero — no stack trace — with the fail-open contract in the invoker unchanged. A regression drives the directory-collision path and asserts no multi-line stack-trace output. Applied across the canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea task-mirror editions; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`schema.locateSection` fence detection now shares the classifier's `^\s{0,3}` indent anchor — an indented-fence decoy can no longer wedge `plan_hash` — #670.** The #665 residual: the hash-covering classifier `markdownFenceTransition` anchors CommonMark fences at `^\s{0,3}` on the raw line (a 4+-space-indented backtick run is an indented code block, NOT a fence), but `locateSection` still detected fences with `ln.trim().match(...)` — ANY indentation. A 0-indent opener followed by a 4-space-indented pseudo-closer made `locateSection` end a fence early and select a fenced DECOY heading the classifier keeps inside the fence, risking a `plan_hash` mismatch wedge on resume/barrier. `locateSection`'s fence regex is now `/^\s{0,3}(`{3,}|~{3,})(.*)$/` matched against the RAW line (both scan sites), byte-identical to the classifier and to the now-shipped `release.unreleasedSection` (#665) fix; the run-length/family/empty-suffix closer semantics are unchanged. A 4-space-indented decoy regression asserts `locateSection` ↔ `classifier.sectionBody` selection parity end-to-end (no `plan_hash_mismatch` on resume). Applied byte-identically across the four `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` editions (the byte-anchor group — not `sync:editions`-generated); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **`git status --porcelain` family ENOBUFS-hardened; the plan-validator dirty-fence now fails CLOSED on a probe it cannot trust — #669.** #666's sibling tier: the `git status --porcelain` family is unbounded in dirty/untracked path *count* (not repo size), and #666 deliberately scoped it out. Every `status --porcelain` content probe now carries the explicit 64 MB `GIT_MAX_BUFFER` cap — the `--parent-clean-check` dirty-fence in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`, plus the probes in `adaptive-node` (leg-dirty + the two path-scoped barrier checks), `claim`, and `sink-merge` (bounded `git worktree list --porcelain` sites are left uncapped). The crux: the dirty-fence's `catch` previously collapsed any probe error to `porcelain = ''` — zero dirty paths — so on an `ENOBUFS` overflow a protective dirty-worktree fence silently **passed** on a huge tree (fail-OPEN, the opposite of the validator's posture). It now emits a typed `cannot_prove_clean` refuse with a non-zero exit on **any** exec failure (ENOBUFS past the 64 MB cap, a corrupt/locked `.git/index`, or any other git fault), never falling through to `pass`; its consumers already treat any non-`pass` fence result as fail-closed, so no consumer changed. Two regressions in `test-adaptive-node.js` pin it: a `>1 MB` porcelain of out-of-allowband production paths (fail-open against the unpatched fence) and a corrupted-`.git/index` fault (the discriminator that pins the unconditional fail-closed catch). Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized (the plan-validator + adaptive-node GENERATED aggregators via `sync:editions`; the divergent `claim`/`sink-merge` forge ports hand-edited to match); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **Post-audit hygiene: a vacuous repair-harness assertion, an undocumented release-receipt disposal step, and an unwired GitLab stderr-leak assertion — #668.** The `#434-b` `runRepairNode` RED-control assertion now observes real repair-node output instead of a placeholder; the release working-sequence and CLI docs now name the typed `stale_release_receipt` refusal and the required `.cache/release-receipt.jsonl`/`chain-receipt.json` disposal step before the next `--prepare`; and the GitLab clean-nonzero stderr-leak regression case now carries a wired output assertion instead of running unchecked.

- **Unbounded `execFileSync`/`spawnSync` git calls no longer crash `ENOBUFS` past 1 MB of output — #666.** Every unbounded-in-repo-size git call now carries an explicit 64 MB `maxBuffer` cap via a per-script local `GIT_MAX_BUFFER` constant: 10 sites in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (the `computeCodeTreeHash` `ls-tree -r` crash site plus the `diff --name-only` / `ls-files --others` / `diff-tree -r --name-only` freshness and finalize-check probes) and one each in the `adaptive-node`, `claim`, `sink-merge`, and `run-chains` sibling scripts. This upstreams the live worktree-hash hot-patch — previously carried only as a reinstall-wiped hand-patch in the installed copy, so every fresh install regressed — and extends it to every sibling of the same git-plumbing pattern; fixed-size probes (`rev-parse`, `merge-base`, `--quiet` diffs, `for-each-ref`, single-pathspec diffs, bounded `log -n <k>`) are deliberately left uncapped. A `>1 MB` synthetic-tree regression in `test-adaptive-node.js` drives `computeCodeTreeHash` past Node's default 1 MB buffer and asserts a stable hash where the unpatched code silently returned `null` (ENOBUFS swallowed → fail-closed stale). Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized (the plan-validator + adaptive-node GENERATED aggregators via `sync:editions`; the divergent `claim`/`sink-merge` and rename-normalized `run-chains` forge ports hand-edited to match); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **Adaptive `repair-node` now collectively folds a completed adversarial fan-out group's stale votes — #664.** After a downstream writer is repaired, the gate-reset resolves each completed `adversarial-verifier` fan-out group as a single post-dominating unit (mirroring `reopen-node`), resets every member row to `pending`, and purges the group's `.cache/adversarial-verifier-*.md` receipts — closing the repair-path twin of #658, where parallel skeptics never individually post-dominated, so their round-1 votes survived a repair and still satisfied `--verdict-check` on code that changed after they voted. The mid-vote `would_orphan_in_progress` refusal is preserved; the legacy role-prefix format is purged through the CLI dispatch (the `readdir` seam is wired, matching `reopen-node`) and the purge dedupes per receipt name so two same-label groups at different origins are both cleared. Canonical + Codex + GitLab + Gitea editions synchronized via `sync:editions`.

- **`#660` fence semantics extended to the two residual consumers — #665.** `schema.locateSection` (the ×4 byte-identical adaptive-schema anchor) and `release.unreleasedSection` now apply the classifier's fence-transition rules — same-family + run-length ≥ opener + empty/whitespace-suffix closer — implemented locally (no classifier import, preserving byte-identity), so a 5-backtick `## Node Briefs` fence enclosing a fenced `## Node Ledger` decoy no longer wedges the runtime ledger path into a `plan_hash` mismatch on resume, and a fenced `## ` line inside `[Unreleased]` no longer truncates the changelog section in either accounting direction. The release parser's fence match is anchored at CommonMark's `^\s{0,3}` indent so an indented (4-space) backtick run — an indented code block, not a fence — cannot swallow a subsequent release heading and fail-open the `changelog_incomplete` gate. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea editions synchronized (adaptive-schema byte-identical ×4; release canonical↔codex byte + rename-normalized forge ports).

- **The claim-overlap guard now fails closed on a structurally-ambiguous or unparseable fast-summary `## Scope` — #667.** An unclosed fence or a duplicate `## Scope` heading (which the section scanner reports as ambiguous) previously collapsed to an empty write set and let the overlap guard silently classify an overlapping fast-path candidate green; the overlap-guard consumer now reads the claimed Scope's parse state and returns a conservative `red` (write set indeterminate) instead. The scanner primitives are unchanged and still refuse to manufacture a missing section; a genuinely absent `## Scope` is unchanged (not treated as an overlap). Applied across the canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea classifier editions. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-667-01.md`.

## [6.22.1] - 2026-07-12

### Added

- **Frozen per-node wait-budget extensions for evidence-backed long-running work — #655.** An optional hash-covered `wait_budget_minutes` Nodes column can extend, but never shorten, the effective role/model no-interrupt floor up to 720 minutes; nondelegable nodes and metric-optimizer dual budgets refuse explicitly. The validated value and `planner_override` source now survive every opener, durable running-set/top-up, crash reconciliation, and direct dispatch defense, while old frozen invalid cells fail closed at consumption and true no-override plans remain byte-compatible.

### Fixed

- **Injected release issue sets are now authoritative in both changelog-accounting directions — #663.** Online verification refuses `changelog_incomplete` when a closed issue intended for the release is absent from `[Unreleased]`, while unknown Unreleased references retain a distinct fail-closed mismatch. Both sides deduplicate deterministically, public reference order remains stable, offline verification stays explicit and git-log-only, and canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea release scripts remain synchronized.

- **Codex control-plane agents now use an exact isolated spawn contract — #656.** `issue-scout` and `workflow-planner` receive bounded, self-contained briefs without the parent transcript; Codex pins direct `agents.spawn_agent` calls to `fork_turns: "none"`, stable sanitized v2 task names, named-role profiles without transient model/effort overrides, and one corrected same-role retry for malformed argument shape. Structural regression guards parse the literal spawn objects fail-closed, including duplicate/conflicting keys, while Claude retains its native isolated `Agent(...)` prompts.

- **Adaptive downstream review gates now rotate stale evidence automatically after writer repair — #654.** Reopening the same gate under a fresh nonce reseeds its full evidence stub, preventing an earlier blocking verdict from poisoning the repaired close, while same-open crash/resume remains byte-for-byte stable and malformed or cross-node bindings remain fail-closed. The writer-repair path keeps the blocking reviewer report available as the repair brief until the downstream gate genuinely reopens.

- **Release verification now reads the full `[Unreleased]` section instead of stopping at its heading — #662.** A single structural helper now serves issue accounting and the public verification envelope, preserving first-seen reference order with deduplication through the next real level-2 heading or EOF. Value-level regressions cover exact references, EOF and next-heading boundaries, non-heading lookalikes, duplicates, and a genuine `changelog_incomplete` refusal; all four release-script editions remain synchronized.

- **Release tags now bind the committed release surface instead of the pre-bump `HEAD` — #661.** The unsafe one-step `--cut` is refusal-only and replaced by a crash-resumable `--prepare` → release-only commit → full offline chain receipt → `--release-check` → `--tag` protocol. Version-scoped prepare and publication receipts bind the independent root/Codex versions, exact eight-file allowlist, candidate SHA, full unwaived chain receipt, tag name, and verified tag-tree bytes; strict idempotency rejects contradictory terminal state. Every authorization-relevant Git fact probe now distinguishes an empty result from execution failure, local tag creation uses an atomic candidate-bound ref update, and post-create failures compare-delete only the newly created matching ref or report `tag_rollback_failed`. Canonical, Codex, GitLab, and Gitea release scripts remain synchronized. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-661-01.md`.

- **Adaptive adversarial fan-out evidence now follows frozen node identity end to end — #658.** New fan-out groups seed, dispatch, verify, reset, and reopen only canonical `.cache/<node-id>.md` receipts derived from the exact shared fan-out label and dependency origin. Missing, foreign, duplicate, or stale bindings fail closed; independent groups cannot dilute one another; a one-pass/one-refutation tie remains blocking. Archived cardinality-greater-than-one role-prefix receipts remain readable only when exactly one legacy group is attributable, eliminating the manual bridge files required by earlier Finalization runs.

- **GitLab claim-classification fixtures are hermetic by construction — #659.** Every unit fixture explicitly owns issue view, project discovery, and remote-note inputs, while a local unexpected-call sentinel names omitted dependencies before ambient CLI, HOME, authentication, network, or remote state can be reached. Controlled CLI shims remain available to separately named degradation/integration tests.

- **Workflow section and Node Briefs identity is fence-aware across every edition — #660.** One family-, run-length-, and closer-suffix-aware Markdown scanner now governs Meta, Nodes, Node Ledger, Node Briefs, brief parsing, presence, validation, and hashing. Fenced decoys cannot become headings or briefs, shorter delimiters cannot close longer fences, duplicate/unclosed ambiguity refuses structurally, and a fenced Briefs decoy remains hash-equivalent to a genuinely briefless plan.

## [6.22.0] - 2026-07-10

### Added

- **A self-contained adaptive-cycle evidence archive — planner attestation on every Codex startup surface, terminal sink-journal disposal, consumer candidate binding, and durable selection/run-gap evidence — #653.** A live Codex consumer run completed correctly but its archive could not prove the whole cycle without raw-session archaeology; four coordinated mechanisms close that gap. **(A) Attestation:** `--attest-planner-spawn` is now mandated on every planner startup surface (the 3 `workflow-planner.toml` byte-identical profiles, the 3 adapt SKILLs at both the single-issue and bundle startup calls, and `agents/workflow-planner.md`), machine-locked by needles in all four contract validators; `cmdFinalize` appends a script-owned `## Attestation` section to the archived `finalization-summary.md` — both column-0 status fields (`claim_planner_attested` / `finalize_contractor_attested`, a clean result stated positively) plus every `ATTESTATION WARNING` verbatim — and `## Closure` carries the same fields, so a missing delegation can never be summarized as unqualified-clean. **(B) Journal lifecycle:** a terminally successful direct sink now disposes its own transaction journals — `disposeSinkJournals` (root+codex `sink-merge.js` byte-pair, hand-mirrored into both divergent forge ports) unlinks `sink-receipt.json`/`sink-fallback.json` from live and archive `.cache` after the final receipt is captured, emitting `journal_disposed`; crash-resume before terminal success keeps the journal untouched, and the finalize/plan-run completion prose now instructs deleting (never committing) any residue — the "clean and synced" trap that had steered agents into journal commits. **(C) Candidate binding:** consumer `final-validation.md` gains a column-0 `validated_candidate_hash` (64-hex over the landable snapshot minus the validation-invisible band; producer: new `plan-validator --candidate-hash [--json]`, refusing `candidate_hash_unavailable` fail-closed); the consumer `--finalize-check` arm refuses `final_validation_unbound` (missing/malformed hash — an omitted field cannot bypass the binding) or `final_validation_stale` (structural `recorded_candidate_hash` + `current_candidate_hash` payload) with precedence `final_validation_unverified > final_validation_failed > final_validation_unbound > final_validation_stale`, and `cmdFinalize` refuses BEFORE any archive/commit side effect — proven by a walkthrough negative leg (wrong hash → live folder survives, zero archive). No test re-execution anywhere on the path (the gate compares two hashes); self-host chain-receipt behavior and the citation fields stay byte-unchanged in decision terms. **(D) Selection + run-gap durability:** the router docks the issue-scout's full reply at `kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/selection-evidence.md` (closure receipt gains advisory `selection_evidence: present|absent`), and the gap-sweep gate gains reverse containment — a summary-observed `## Run gaps` entry absent from the sweep refuses `observed_gap_unseeded` with the exact unseeded tuples (vacuous pass now requires BOTH sides empty; seed observed gaps via `run-gaps-manual.md` before the sweep). In-run discoveries hardened the run itself: the plan-run/next command surfaces are byte-generated (skeleton + slots — hand-edits are silently wiped by regeneration), so the write sets were plan-repaired to carry the generation set. Change-gate chain: code review (2 low fenced notes) + adversarial verification NOT-REFUTED (8-leg surface-drop battery all chain-locked; pre-seeded-summary fence proven; 10-input parser attack all fail-closed; the issue's own vacuous-gap shape now refuses). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-653-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): claim.js root+codex pair with both forge claim ports mirrored from the full accumulated diff, sink-merge ×4, gap-sweep ×4, closure-contract ×4, adaptive-schema ×4, plan-validator (GENERATED ×4), the six plan-run + six next routing surfaces via the generation seam, and all four contract validators; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **A mechanical pre-tag release gate — check-only `--release-check` beside `--finalize-check`, refusing typed on a missing, red, stale, waived, incomplete, or unresolvable-chain-set receipt at the release-candidate sha — #651.** v6.21.3 was tagged with a red Claude chain and no validation receipt; the release procedure was prose-only, and the existing receipt machinery guarded only the adaptive finalize path. `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js --release-check [--json] [--candidate <sha>] [--receipt <path>]` reads the same `.cache/chain-receipt.json` that `run-chains` stamps and refuses with a structurally typed reason, precedence `chains_unverified > chains_stale > chains_empty > repo_kind_undetermined > chains_incomplete > chains_red > chains_waived`: missing/unparseable receipt; strict headSha EQUALITY vs the candidate (default HEAD) — a `headSha` of `unknown`/missing refuses, the deliberate inversion of `chainReceiptGreenness`'s leniency, and no codeTreeHash relaxation is substituted since a release tag names an exact commit — with the hint-only `stale_paths`/`stale_kind` culprit diagnostics attached on the sha-mismatch arm and graceful degradation on uncertainty; a dirty-stamped receipt (tighten-only addition); an unresolvable expected chain set fails CLOSED as `repo_kind_undetermined` (a release is self-host-by-definition — deliberately stricter than finalize's consumer downgrade); a receipt not COVERING every `package.json`-declared `test:kaola-workflow:*` chain refuses `chains_incomplete` with structural `missingChains`/`expectedChains` (coverage-before-greenness — an official `run-chains --chains <subset>` receipt can no longer green-light a tag, the v6.21.3 incident class); any red chain; any waived (`accepted_red`) chain refuses `chains_waived` — a waiver is legal at adaptive finalize but never for a release tag. Self-owned only: receipt + `package.json` + local git, zero mutation, no CI/CD coupling. Wired into the documented release flow (docs/conventions.md § Release + README release checklist): bump commit → `KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1` run-chains receipt at the untagged candidate → release check → tag → online `npm test` → push; the shipped evidence includes an end-to-end walk of that exact sequence, which surfaced and fixed the blocker that previously made it dead: the gitlab/gitea forge-helpers suites captured ambient `KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE` at require time and went red under a blanket offline run — both suites are now hermetic (suite-top unset; the deliberate offline sub-tests unchanged; zero production-script changes). The release-commit hygiene rule is recorded: a release/tag commit bundles version bump + release docs only; anything more re-runs the gate. `kaola-workflow-release.js --cut` is deliberately untouched (explicit non-reversal of D-632-01). 13 walkthrough cases lock the contract (green-at-candidate pass; missing/unparseable; older-sha stale WITH exact culprit hints; unknown-headSha degrade; dirty stamp; advanced-HEAD; waived; empty; red-over-waived precedence; explicit `--candidate`; subset refusal + structural payload; incomplete-over-red precedence; fail-closed no-package.json probe). In-run adversarial loop: the change-gate adversary REFUTED the first cut with two executed counterexamples (the documented sequence dead-ending under blanket OFFLINE; a subset receipt passing) — both repaired in-run via plan-repair + reopen, re-reviewed, and confirmed resolved by independent re-attack. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-651-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): plan-validator (GENERATED aggregator, canonical + regenerated ×3 forge ports via `npm run sync:editions`) + the walkthrough + the two forge-helpers suites; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

### Fixed

- **Two strictness-lock gaps in the evidence-shape / merge-diff test suites — #652.** Test-only. Gap 1: beside the existing hollow-seed refusal controls, three new negative controls (T6b-bare / T6b-bare2 / T7e-bare) pin that a BARE colon-less token line (`RED`/`GREEN` for tdd-guide, `build-green` for implementer) refuses with the correct `missingTokenClass` — closing the partial-weakening hole where a future matcher accepting bare token-name lines while still refusing empty values would have slipped through green. Gap 2: the four merge-diff equality filters in `test-adaptive-node.js` were broader than the production band (`!s.startsWith('kaola-workflow/')` vs `isBarrierInvisible`'s `kaola-workflow/{project}/**`); narrowed via a test-local `isProjectWorkflowChurn(s, project)` helper mirroring the production band shape at all 5 call sites, with a red-first SYNTH-CROSS-PROJECT-LEAK case proving the narrowing binds (a planted `kaola-workflow/other-project/**` leak was observed slipping through the broad filter pre-narrowing and is caught after, while the active project's own workflow churn stays legitimately filtered). Production `checkEvidenceShape` and `isBarrierInvisible` byte-unchanged. Shipped in the #651 bundle; all four chains green at the bundle head.

## [6.21.5] - 2026-07-10

### Fixed

- **Repaired the Codex 0.144.1 MultiAgentV2 reserved-tool schema regression introduced by v6.21.4 — #650.** Role-aware dispatch now uses the proven direct `agents` namespace with visible `agent_type` metadata and encrypted task messages; the server-reserved `collaboration.spawn_agent` name is no longer used with Kaola's extended role/model schema. Preflight and the profile installer parse inline and dotted `tool_namespace`/`hide_spawn_agent_metadata` settings, refuse nested transport as `codex_v2_encrypted_transport_unsafe`, refuse reserved or hidden role schemas as `codex_v2_role_transport_unsafe`, and report namespace/metadata/readiness in doctor output. Generated issue-scout and plan-run routing, workflow-init guidance, and all three Codex editions carry the same contract. Live Codex A/B validation reproduced the HTTP 400 under the old configuration and proved an encrypted `issue-scout` child completes under `tool_namespace = "agents"`.

## [6.21.4] - 2026-07-10

### Fixed

- Adapted Codex MultiAgentV2 dispatch for Codex CLI 0.144.1 by requiring its direct-only collaboration transport, refusing unsafe nested Code Mode exposure with `codex_v2_encrypted_transport_unsafe`, and routing issue-scout and adaptive role dispatch through direct collaboration tools so plaintext task prompts are not misclassified as encrypted payloads.
- **Restored the v6.21.3 release validation gates after the strict evidence-shape migration left test fixtures stale — #649.** Real-git scheduler fixtures now use one registry-driven, leg-aware evidence writer that populates the same authoritative parent or isolated-leg cache the runtime resolves; role-specific speculative, race, fused-gate, and mixed-frontier fixtures carry non-empty column-0 values; raw merge-diff assertions exclude workflow cache artifacts just as the production allowband does; and the walkthrough interrupted-outcome fixture uses `build-green: pass`. The production matcher remains strict, with hollow tdd-guide and implementer seeds still refused. This repairs a test/release-gate regression, not an unproven runtime defect.

## [6.21.3] - 2026-07-10

### Changed

- **Codex subagent tiers now adapt to the current named-profile load order with static Sol profiles.** Codex 0.144 reloads a named role profile after applying transient spawn overrides, so Kaola no longer passes per-spawn `model`/`reasoning_effort`. Eight carry-out roles (`code-explorer`, `knowledge-lookup`, `tdd-guide`, `implementer`, `doc-updater`, `issue-scout`, `contractor`, `metric-optimizer`) pin `gpt-5.6-sol` at `medium`; planning, architecture, repair, review/security/adversarial, workflow-planning, and synthesis roles use standalone profiles pinned to `gpt-5.6-sol` at `xhigh`. Dispatch cards expose the expected pair plus `codex_profile_mode`, `codex_profile_tier`, and `codex_profile_compatible`; a plan/profile conflict refuses as `codex_profile_tier_mismatch`, a null pair as `codex_tier_unresolved`, and child JSONL that disproves the standalone-profile pair as `codex_profile_runtime_mismatch`. Installer and doctor validation enforce the exact two-tier pin policy across all three Codex plugin trees. Every DAG node role now writes its full nonce-bound deliverable directly to `dispatch.evidence_file`, returns only a compact orchestrator summary, and must pass `record-evidence --verify` before the cache artifact can feed a downstream node; a disconnected encrypted summary can continue only when the child is terminal and that durable artifact is valid. The out-of-ledger `workflow-planner` and `contractor` roles keep their complete workflow/plan/phase/finalization artifacts as the authoritative durable result and mirror into a seeded cache when supplied.

## [6.21.2] - 2026-07-09

### Fixed

- **Finalize-tail validation now avoids blind repeat four-chain runs where the workflow can reason about freshness — #648.** The plan-run/finalize runtime prose now makes the self-host chain receipt stamp the last pre-Finalization action after all code and test-consumed prose land, and clarifies that workflow state plus inert, non-test-consumed docs do not stale the receipt. `plan-validator --finalize-check --json` keeps the fail-closed `reason: "chains_stale"` contract but may add best-effort `stale_paths`, `stale_paths_truncated`, and `stale_kind` diagnostics for clean, resolvable stamped receipts; unresolvable or dirty stamped receipts degrade to the prior generic stale refusal. Consumer final-validation prose now allows a truthful citation of an unchanged terminal change-gate validation run (`source: cited:<node-id>`, `validated_command`, `validated_at_head`, `reuse_boundary`) while leaving the machine gate unchanged: column-0 `verdict: pass` remains the parsed requirement. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-648-01.md`. Cross-edition: generated validator ports and routing surfaces stayed in sync; walkthrough coverage includes code/prose/mixed stale diagnostics, truncation, inert-doc freshness, and degradation cases.

## [6.21.1] - 2026-07-09

### Changed

- README refreshed to match the shipped v6.21.0 surface: the Philosophy section now states the five first-principles axioms (`templates/axioms.md`, #645) instead of the pre-axiom three-priority list; the workflow-roles table gains the missing `synthesizer` and `metric-optimizer` rows with role paragraphs (#463, #634); `issue-scout` is documented as profile-governed (Opus under the default `higher` profile, #646) and the "three reviewer agents" profile prose updated to four; the adaptive sections document the `## Node Briefs` node-to-node channel + close-time consumed-proof (#642), the completed per-role evidence contract (#643), and the read∥write co-open relaxation with typed `serialDegradeReason` (#622/#641); `stale-worktree-cleanup` documents the `skipped_unmerged` never-delete-unproven-work guard (#620). Documentation-only.

### Fixed

- **Codex preflight now parses quoted TOML table headers without leaking `features.multi_agent_v2` state -- #647.** `parseTomlTableName` now accepts quoted/basic/literal and array-of-table headers while preserving path-segment and array-table metadata, so real Codex config tables such as `[projects."..."]` and `[plugins."..."]` reset scanner state instead of making later `enabled` or `max_concurrent_threads_per_session` keys look like `features.multi_agent_v2`. The fix keeps duplicate-key ambiguity fail-closed, rejects quoted single-segment dotted keys and exact array-of-table v2 headers as supported v2 config, and adds cross-edition regressions across the preflight/installer mirror surfaces. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

## [6.21.0] - 2026-07-09

### Added

- **A canonical first-principles axiom layer — one root file, reached by embedding rather than per-edition copies, with a machine-enforced byte-identity drift guard — #645.** Several topic-specific precedence rules already existed (decomposition ordering, CI-not-a-gate, adaptive-as-default) but nothing named a single, cross-cutting tie-breaking order for a situation none of those rules already resolves. `templates/axioms.md` is the new canonical, provenance-free Layer-0 source: five axioms in priority order (correct first; then save human time; then spend as little as possible; machines decide facts, humans decide values — routing irreversible/value-laden calls to the consent valve; own your own verdicts — never let a system the workflow does not own be the judge of done), a tie-breaker protocol (walk the axioms in order and record an OPTIONAL one-line derivation in the node's `.cache` evidence — its absence never blocks a gate, deliberately not wired into any close-time evidence-shape check), and a tighten-only hard boundary (an axiom may only make an agent stricter, never license skipping a typed gate, refusal, or barrier). Design choice: reach consumers by EMBEDDING the identical block into the six workflow-init CLAUDE.md-template surfaces (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs) rather than per-edition file copies — `templates/` has no runtime `require()` consumer, so the `BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS` mechanism built for required scripts doesn't fit; the drift guard is instead a new `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` scenario (`testAxiomBlockByteIdentity`) asserting `templates/axioms.md` opens with `## First Principles` and is embedded byte-identically in all six surfaces. A provenance-free reference pointer (naming the axioms plus the tighten-only clause, not the full block) is additionally required on the six generated `next` routing surfaces via a new `nx-first-principles` entry in `templates/routing/required-blocks.js`'s single-source manifest — obligating all six surfaces by derived-universe construction, never a hand-typed file list. No scheduler, validator, or gate logic changed — this is a tie-breaking layer for cases no existing rule already resolves, not a new gate. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-645-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): the six workflow-init surfaces + the six `next` routing surfaces + the required-blocks manifest + the walkthrough drift guard; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **Issue-scout now carries a governed higher-profile model tier, reversing an earlier ungoverned-by-design decision now that the full two-node render contract exists — #646.** `issue-scout` had no `agents/profiles/higher/` file, so it installed `sonnet` under every `--profile` with no lever to raise it — an intentional prior decision (issue-scout follows the read-only, pre-claim `adversarial-verifier` dispatch pattern; no `{X_MODEL}` placeholder needed) that a later regression reinforced when a stray `model="{ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL}"` placeholder shipped in command prose with no matching `model_for_placeholder` case, breaking install-render and getting reverted rather than completed. This bundle lands the full atomic wiring that earlier attempt lacked: a new `agents/profiles/higher/issue-scout.md` (byte-identical to the base agent except `model: opus`, mirroring `code-reviewer`'s higher-profile shape) plus `ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL` wired into BOTH of `install.sh`'s placeholder lists (`model_for_placeholder`'s case and `render_command_file`'s placeholders array, landed together in the same edit — a partial land of either alone reproduces the old regression) plus an install-rendered `model="{ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL}"` dispatch line on the three `workflow-next` COMMAND surfaces only (Codex SKILLs keep prose-only scout dispatch, matching the model-less `issue-scout.toml` twins across all three plugin trees — a placeholder in the shared routing body would have leaked into the Codex skills). `DEFAULT_AGENT_MODELS['issue-scout']` stays `sonnet` and `REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES` is untouched — this is a quality-tier lever under `--profile=higher`, not a reasoning-floor change. New render-completeness pins close the old regression shape for good: `test-install-model-rendering.js` asserts `manifest['issue-scout'] === 'opus'` under `--profile=higher` and `'sonnet'` under common, and `validate-workflow-contracts.js` explicitly pins the `model="{ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL}"` dispatch line on `commands/workflow-next.md` (a surface outside the generic `phaseCommands` dispatch scanner, which would not otherwise cover it). **In-run repair:** the generic `{X_MODEL}` placeholder strip in `sync-opencode-edition.js` was mangling the scout-dispatch paragraph on the generated opencode surface (an empty inline-code span plus two install.sh/placeholder sentences false for opencode) — caught by this run's own `code-reviewer` and `adversarial-verifier` gates and fixed in the same run with a scoped paragraph-level rewrite (placed before the generic strip) plus a tightened `test-opencode-edition.js` assertion, proven load-bearing by an independent reproduction of the mangled residue. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-646-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `install.sh` + the three `workflow-next` command surfaces + the four contract validators + `test-install-model-rendering.js` + `sync-opencode-edition.js`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **A durable node-to-node information channel — planner-authored `## Node Briefs`, `dispatch.goal_line` + `dispatch.upstream_evidence` on every opener card, and a close-time consumed-proof — #642.** Node-to-node information transfer was not mechanically guaranteed: a producer node's full output (a `code-architect` blueprint, a `planner` recommendation, a `code-explorer` map) reached its downstream consumer only via the orchestrator's transient context, never the machinery — `buildDispatch` carried no upstream pointer at all, and the pre-built `ctx.goal_line` socket sat unwired. `## Node Briefs` is a new hash-covered plan section (one column-0 `### <node-id>` heading per brief, parsed fence-aware exactly like `sectionBody`'s h2/h3 boundary handling); its presence conditionally appends to `computePlanHash`, so a briefless plan hashes byte-identically to before (every existing frozen/in-flight plan resume-checks unchanged). Freeze gains two typed refusals: `brief_unknown_node` (a brief names an id absent from `## Nodes`) and `brief_duplicate_node` (two `### <node-id>` blocks reused the same id — the second silently shadowed the first with no refusal until this repair), both freeze-only with operator hints. The unwired `ctx.goal_line` socket is now populated from the node's brief, and a new conditionally-attached `dispatch.upstream_evidence: [{node_id, role, path}]` array derives from `depends_on` (project-qualified `.cache` paths, the barrier-exempt band) — both wired at all three openers (`open-next`, `open-ready`, and `close-and-open-next`'s fused advance), byte-identical to today when absent (a briefless plan or a root/depless node). At close, a new `checkUpstreamConsumed` recomputes the frozen `depends_on` producer set, reads each upstream's line-1 `evidence-binding: <id> <nonce>`, and requires the consumer to echo a column-0 `upstream_read: <id> <nonce>` — HARD-refusing `upstream_not_consumed` (zero ledger mutation) for an IMPLEMENT_ROLES consumer of a producer node with a missing or stale echo, advisory for every other pairing. A universal n/a-skip carve-out mirrors `checkEvidenceShape`'s own n/a carve-out — a consumer that legitimately skipped its work (e.g. a `build-error-resolver` skipped because the build was already green) proves nothing either way, so the two close gates now render the same verdict on the same n/a evidence instead of disagreeing. The card, all three openers, and the cached open envelope NEVER emit the upstream nonce — only line 1 of the upstream's own evidence file carries it, so a correct echo is a genuine read-proof, not a fabricable field. On resume or after a context compaction, the in-progress node's re-dispatch context is re-derived from the cached `.cache/<op>-envelope.json` (disk is authoritative, never memory). `agents/workflow-planner.md`'s compact-plan posture now spells out the exact planner-facing brief syntax (one column-0 `### <node-id>` heading per brief; any other layout — bullets, a table, bold names — parses as zero briefs, so a malformed section fails loudly instead of silently no-opping the whole channel), mirrored into its three `.toml` twins. All six `#400` routing surfaces relay `goal_line` verbatim into the role dispatch and instruct reading each `upstream_evidence` file before starting plus recording the echo. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-642-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` + `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (GENERATED aggregators, canonical + regenerated ×3 forge ports via `npm run sync:editions`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **Completed the per-role evidence-recording contract — registry rows, agent-file evidence-contract sections, and a future-agent wall for every node role — #643.** A roster audit found roughly half of the fourteen node roles carried no (or presence-only) evidence contract — `build-error-resolver` (a WRITE/IMPLEMENT role with no registry row), the read-producers `code-architect` / `code-explorer` / `knowledge-lookup` / `planner` / `issue-scout` (no row, vendored profiles never adapted), and others — so a lossy one-line persistence of a rich deliverable passed the close gate outright (`checkEvidenceShape` asserted `evidence-binding` presence only, degenerating the token list to a single entry). Eight new `ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY` rows name the content-bearing token(s) each role's evidence must carry (`code-architect`: `files_to_create|files_to_modify` + `build_sequence`; `code-explorer`: `findings`; `knowledge-lookup`: `findings` + `sources`; `planner` / `issue-scout`: `recommendation`; `build-error-resolver`: `build-green`; `synthesizer`: `merge_outcome`; `doc-updater`: `docs_updated`) — every role now reaches at least two tokens, the future-agent wall's floor. `checkEvidenceShape`'s close gate generalizes its hardcoded-branch else-arm into a registry-driven check (a present key requires a non-empty value; an alternation is satisfied by any one alternative), so a truncated producer evidence now genuinely refuses instead of passing on bare token presence. The evidence-contract prose — read-producer roles RETURN their deliverable for orchestrator persistence via `record-evidence --stdin` (never touching the seeded `evidence-binding:` header themselves); write roles SELF-WRITE directly into the seeded `.cache/{node-id}.md`, preserving that header verbatim — is rendered into the eight newly-covered role profiles across all four agent-profile surfaces (root `.md` + three plugin `.toml` editions, byte-identical ×3, forge-neutral); `synthesizer` was already compliant and needed no edit. A new future-agent wall in `validate-vendored-agents.js` (`checkFutureAgentWall`) machine-enforces both halves of the contract on every node-role agent going forward: a `ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY` row with at least two tokens (or an explicit `PRESENCE_ONLY_RATIONALE` entry — the allowlist ships empty), plus a role-kind evidence needle whose KIND is derived from the agent's own `tools:` front-matter manifest (Write/Edit present → write-kind SELF-WRITE needle, else read-kind RETURN needle — never a hand-maintained list), now fail-closed: an agent `.md` with no parsable `tools:` line at all refuses `agent_contract_manifest_missing` instead of silently defaulting to the weaker read-kind needle. Mirrored `.toml` needle checks land in the codex/gitlab/gitea contract validators. The six `#400` routing surfaces' stale hardcoded READ-ONLY/WRITE role enumerations are replaced by the same manifest-derived sentence, with the current roster listed only as examples, so the next agent addition cannot silently fall outside either list. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-643-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (GENERATED aggregator) + the agent-profile ×4-surface roll-out + the six routing surfaces + the enforcement scripts; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **A leg-contained writer now co-opens behind live reads, and an `observes: scratch` consent tier lets a scratch-only gate co-open a legless docs writer — relaxing the two over-conservative read∥write scheduler guards while keeping the other 17 — #641.** A `{read ∥ write}` antichain the planner authored as parallel was serialized by the scheduler (the read-first-exclusive partition G1 + the `write_awaits_drain` hold G2). An audit of the adaptive scheduler's 19 concurrency-gating guards found only G1/G2 over-conservative; the other 17 are load-bearing (each protects a restated invariant). The invariant G1/G2 protect — **closed-work observation** (a read/gate renders its output over a tree of only closed-node work + its own scratch) — is now enforced *structurally* (worktree isolation) and *contractually* instead of purely *temporally*, never dropped. **R1 (default-on, structural):** in the `write_awaits_drain` else-branch, a leg-contained writer (a lone writer forms a size-1 lane group, reusing the #596 speculative-group machinery) co-opens behind live reads under four fail-closed preconditions — `legCoupled` (`KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0` forces the hold), no live `lane_group`, clean parent (`parentCarriesProductionDirt`), and validator `--parallel-safe` ok — each miss returning the byte-identical hold plus a typed additive `serialDegradeReason` (`parallel_writes_off`/`lane_group_live`/`parent_dirty`/`overlaps_live_writer`/`parallel_safe_indeterminate`, mirroring the #616 pattern). The **G4 `merge_awaits_read_drain` fence is retained** — the leg's last-member merge is held while any read is live, so the reads observe an untouched parent tree; this is the mirror of the #622 read-direction relaxation. **R2 (consent-tier):** a hash-covered `observes: scratch` `## Nodes` annotation, freeze-legal ONLY on an `adversarial-verifier` read node (typed-refused on `code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer`/`main-session-gate` via `observes_scope_role_invalid`, on a node with a write set via `observes_scope_not_read`, and on an unknown scope via `observes_scope_unsupported`), permits a *legless* writer over a dirty parent to co-open behind that scratch-only gate iff its declared set is validation-invisible (`isScratchObservableSurface`: allowband docs **minus** #547 test-consumed prose, plus the writer's own `.cache` evidence). A key correction baked in: **barrier-invisible ≠ observation-invisible** — the allowband contains chain-verdict-affecting prose (`docs/api.md`/`CHANGELOG.md`/`README.md`), so the safe predicate is `isValidationInvisible` (allowband minus test-consumed), never `isBarrierInvisible`, and a full-diff `code-reviewer` is never co-open-safe against a concurrent write. Serial-fallback byte-identity (AC5) is preserved: `KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0` + no running set is byte-identical to before, no `serialDegradeReason` field. **This run's own gates earned their keep:** the `code-reviewer` and the `adversarial-verifier` (the latter reproducing each shape end-to-end against the real CLIs) both surfaced two residuals — (A1 medium) a live `kind:'gate'` main-session-gate is invisible to the co-open preconditions and the G4 merge fence, and (A2 low) `testConsumedExtra` is not threaded into the R2b band call — and the adversary confirmed BOTH are real but **fenced** (no verdict-time invariant violation is constructible; single-orchestrator discipline + write-set-aware G3 + atomic close fence A1; the scratch contract + no-post-domination bound A2), so neither blocked the ship; both are filed as **#644**. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-641-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` + `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (GENERATED aggregators, canonical + regenerated ×3 forge ports via `npm run sync:editions`; the `adaptive-schema` byte-group was untouched — no forge-neutral constant needed); RED-first pins in `test-adaptive-node.js` (co-run, merge-fence-under-co-run, each fail-closed row byte-identical, mixed-set crash-reconcile, R2b band cap), `test-validation-allowband.js` (the band predicate), and `test-adaptive-handoff.js` (the freeze refusals); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially, unwaived — serial mode).
- **A new `metric-optimizer` role serves optimize-shaped work — direction, not destination, with no fixed acceptance threshold at freeze — #634.** Every node the planner could author was task-shaped (an AC known at freeze, one attempt plus bounded repair); nothing served "make it faster / smaller / less flaky," where no honest AC exists until the ratchet has actually run — the write-side complement of #486's still-unshipped question-shaped design. `metric-optimizer` is the one closed-library extension this needed: an ordinary `IMPLEMENT_ROLES`/`WRITE_ROLES` member (`CANONICAL_ROLES`, model tier `sonnet`) so **G1 `code-reviewer` and G3 `main-session-gate` post-dominance are inherited for free** — no new node shape, no scheduler change. Its contract lives in a new `## Meta` block, `optimize(<node-id>)`, parsed once by the validator and `plan_hash`-covered automatically: `metric_command`, `metric_paths` (must stay disjoint from the node's `declared_write_set` — evaluation isolation), `direction` (`min`/`max`), `budget_iterations` (1..50, `OPTIMIZE_ITER_CAP`), optional `budget_wallclock_minutes` (≤120, `OPTIMIZE_WALLCLOCK_CAP`, overrides the dispatch card's tier-derived wait budget), optional `regression_gate` (falls back to the Meta `validation_command`), `metric_repeats` (default 1, median-of-K), `min_delta` (default 0), and `patience`. The `metric_command` output contract is a column-0 `metric: <number>` line, last-match-wins (`parseMetricValue`, mirroring `parseNodeVerdict`). **Six new fail-closed freeze rules (OPT-1..6):** exactly one `optimize()` block per `metric-optimizer` node and vice versa; `metric_paths` non-empty and disjoint from the write set; the budget caps above; `direction`/`metric_repeats`/`min_delta` well-formed; **a change-gate `adversarial-verifier` must post-dominate every optimize node** (OPT-5, reusing `gateUncovered` exactly as G1/G2 do — the measured metric claim is the node's entire deliverable, so it never finalizes unverified); and a `regression_gate` must resolve from somewhere, or the ratchet is refused as Goodhart bait (OPT-6). The role profile (`agents/metric-optimizer.md` + the 3 forge `.toml` twins) carries the bounded ratchet protocol: per iteration, propose one change inside the declared write set, apply it, run the regression gate (a red gate is an automatic reject), measure the metric (median of `metric_repeats`), then accept (commit `kw-opt(<node-id>) iter <k>: <old> -> <new>`, new baseline) or reject (a **scoped** `git restore --source=HEAD -- <write-set>` — **`git reset --hard` is FORBIDDEN**, since it is not scoped to the write set and could destroy work outside the iteration); stop at `budget_iterations`, `patience` consecutive rejects, or the wallclock budget, whichever fires first; log every iteration (accepted and rejected); never ask a judgment question inside the loop — anything that needs one stops the loop and routes through the existing `write-halt` valve. The evidence contract (`ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY['metric-optimizer']`: `evidence-binding`, `metric_baseline`, `metric_final`, `iterations_used`, `regression-green`) is enforced by a new `checkEvidenceShape` branch requiring each of the four non-binding tokens carry a **non-empty** column-0 value, not just token-key presence. **In-run adversarial catch:** this run's own change-gate adversary found that branch missing — a `metric-optimizer` node could close COMPLETE on a fully hollow seeded stub (every D6 token key present, every value empty, zero ratchet log); fixed and re-reviewed in the same run (RED-proven: the pre-fix function independently reproduced `{"ok":true}` on the hollow stub). Three further OPT freeze-rule hardening opportunities this run's `n5-review` gate surfaced, plus two more this run's own adversary found (a directory-shaped `metric_paths` escape and a fenced-decoy `optimize()` block), are all non-blocking — each has a downstream backstop (the change-gate verifier, the evidence-shape gate, or `plan_hash` coverage) — and are filed as **#639**. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-634-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains run; a recurring `test-{run,gitlab-run,gitea-run}-chains.js` timing-sensitive sub-suite flaked intermittently, confirmed (by a control run against pristine main with this diff absent) to be a pre-existing environmental class, not a #634 regression.
- **A two-layer routing-surface generation seam that makes silent block-drop impossible by construction — #630.** The six `#400` routing surfaces per topic (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILLs) were kept in sync by hand-copy propagation — a documented drift class (#369/#380 shipped 4-of-6; #624 lost a whole finalize gate block on the forge-codex SKILLs while all four chains stayed green). #630 lands the two-layer design settled by the earlier shaping run (`docs/investigations/2026-07-08-630-636-routing-generation-seam.md`), on the fenced base #636 delivered. **Layer 1 (presence, all 18 surfaces incl. finalize):** a single-source required-block **manifest** (`templates/routing/required-blocks.js`, 18 blocks) + a derived-universe presence checker in `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` — each block's obligated surface set is **computed** from its topic + runtime/surface-type tags × the emitted-targets registries, so a block *structurally cannot* obligate a 4-of-6 subset; a T3-style red-proof battery + a superset proof + a bidirectional orphan-sentinel guard the checker itself. This closes the #624 whole-block-drop class. **Layer 2 (byte, the 12 template-shaped plan-run/next surfaces):** a new prose generator (`scripts/generate-routing-surfaces.js` + `templates/routing/{plan-run,next}.skeleton.md` + `slots.js` + `rename-table.js`) reproduces those 12 surfaces byte-for-byte from one canonical skeleton per topic (surface-type/runtime/forge slots + a per-script forge-noun rename table + sub-sentence splices), with a `--check` byte-guard wired into **all four** `npm run test:kaola-workflow:*` chains — so a hand-edit that drifts from the generated output reds its own chain (catching the present-but-wrong-prose class the token-pin regime missed). Finalize stays **Layer-1-only** (manifest-guarded, hand-authored — its ~2:1 command↔SKILL rewrite makes byte-generation a precedence-#1 accuracy risk). **Shipped as a behavior-preserving NO-OP CAPTURE:** the skeletons are reverse-engineered so `--write` reproduces the current committed surfaces byte-for-byte — the 12 surfaces are byte-unchanged by #630. Both the code-review and the change-gate adversary **live-proved** the guarantee (a planted gate-token drop reds the manifest; an unpinned-prose mutation on a generated surface reds `--check` though the reachability test + validators stayed green).
