The `T1..T15` route-reachability pins, the #624-fix gate flags, and the deliberate gitea/gitlab `mr|pr)` finalize-sink contract pins are all untouched (the manifest is additive-superset over them). Accepted residual: a marker-preserving *interior* gut of the `fn-closure-audit` finalize block stays green (equal to the pre-#630 T6 strength) — tracked as a hardening follow-up (**#637**). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-630-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): the four `validate-*-contracts.js` + `package.json` chains + edition trees; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

### Documentation

- **Settled design for the routing-surface generation seam (#630) + cross-runtime dispatch-pin single-sourcing (#636) — read-only shaping run.** A probe → assume → adversarially-critique → converge investigation produced `docs/investigations/2026-07-08-630-636-routing-generation-seam.md`: a **two-layer design** — a single-source required-block **manifest** giving a *presence* guarantee across all 18 routing surfaces (finalize included; closes the #624 whole-block-drop class *by construction*, because the per-block surface set is derived from runtime/surface-type tags and therefore cannot silently obligate 4-of-6) **plus** byte-generation confined to the template-shaped `plan-run`/`next` topics (finalize stays hand-authored but manifest-guarded — its 2:1 command↔SKILL rewrite makes generation a precedence-#1 accuracy risk for no added presence guarantee). The adversarial critique ran the chains and caught three fixes now folded into the design: (R1) the #636 write-set must also relocate the **#611 unconditional-mandate pin family** (a SKILL-only split of the gitlab/gitea shared validator loops) or fencing the Codex-dispatch block reds all four chains; (R2) the presence check must be an **additive-superset, content-token, derived-universe checker with a structural red-proof**, not a bare-marker sentinel that re-admits a hollowed-block-yet-green state; (R3) the gitea `mr|pr)` case is a **deliberately machine-pinned contract**, not drift to "correct." Coupling: **ship #636 first, standalone** (its validator relocation is a prerequisite for #630's fenced generation), then **#630** as a fresh re-plan on the fenced base. #630 and #636 remain OPEN pending the two build runs.

### Fixed

- **Closed the two fenced residuals of the #641 read∥write co-open relaxation, plus an in-run adversarial catch at a third seam — #644.** #641 shipped with two known-fenced, non-blocking residuals, both surfaced by its own `code-reviewer` and independently confirmed real-but-fenced by its `adversarial-verifier`: (A1, medium) a live `kind:'gate'` (main-session-gate) was invisible to the R1/R2b co-open preconditions and to the G4 merge fence (`liveReadsAtMerge` counted `kind==='read'` only), so a `{main-session-gate ∥ writer}` antichain could co-open or merge behind a live gate; (A2, low) the plan's `validation_test_consumes` widening (`testConsumedExtra`) was not threaded into `tryR2bLeglessCoopen`'s `scratchObservableWriteSet` call, admitting a self-hosting fork's extra test-consumed prose to the runtime R2b band. Both are now fixed: `liveReadsAtMerge` counts `kind:'gate'` alongside `kind:'read'` (holding the last-member merge while a gate is live); the relaxed `write_awaits_drain` else-branch and `tryR2bLeglessCoopen`'s co-open precondition both hold with a typed `serialDegradeReason: 'gate_live'` while any `kind:'gate'` member is live; `parseValidationTestConsumes(planContent)` now threads through as `opts.testConsumedExtra` into the `scratchObservableWriteSet` call, closing the fork-widening gap. This bundle's own `adversarial-verifier` gate independently reproduced a THIRD, previously-undocumented instance of the same class at an adjacent seam: the fused advance in `close-and-open-next` had no live-gate check at all, so whether a pending writer co-opened behind a live gate depended on `## Nodes` table order (a writer row preceding the gate's row opened the writer in_progress; a gate row preceding the writer's row correctly stayed closed-only) — a table-order-dependent bypass, not a genuine invariant violation (fenced by single-orchestrator discipline + write-set-aware G3 + atomic close), but real. Fixed in the same run: the fused advance now counts a live `kind:'gate'` member before opening the next writer, order-independent, matching the other two seams. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-644-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (GENERATED aggregator, canonical + regenerated ×3 forge ports via `npm run sync:editions`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **Completed the OPT-2 `metric_paths` shape check — it now refuses absolute-path, backslash, and bare-existing-directory entries, closing the defense-in-depth residual #639 deferred — #640.** #639 tightened OPT-2 to refuse directory-shaped (`bench/`), glob (`bench/*.js`), and `../`-aliasing `metric_paths`, but left three shape classes the declared_write_set freeze-wall (`plan-validator.js` ~:1396-1432) already refuses unmirrored on OPT-2: **absolute-path** (`/`-prefix + Windows drive-letter `C:`, reason `absolute_path`), **backslash-separated** (reason `backslash_in_path`), and **bare existing-directory** (a slash-less name resolving to a real dir, `fs.statSync(...).isDirectory()` in try/catch with skip-on-throw, reason `bare-existing-directory`). OPT-2's `shapeReason(p)` now mirrors those EXACT freeze-wall check forms (not hand-rolled) with the same precedence — the existing dir-shape/glob/`../` check first, then absolute BEFORE backslash (so `C:\foo` reports `absolute_path`, not `backslash_in_path`), then the bare-dir `statSync` — recomputing a local `optRoot = opts.root || process.cwd()` (the freeze-wall's `freezeRoot` is block-scoped elsewhere); all refusals stay `OPT-2:`-prefixed with a per-path reason tag. RED-first walkthrough fixtures (3 refuse + a real-`Makefile` root-file accept-control, exercising the `statSync`-file-vs-dir branch genuinely). Inert defense-in-depth today — backslash dies at dispatch on POSIX; bare-dir/absolute are backstopped by the runtime per-node barrier + the OPT-5 change-gate metric reproduction — surfaced by #634's own gates + the #638-639 bundle's review/adversary. The run's own `code-reviewer` + `adversarial-verifier` gates (the latter a 19-plant scratch battery incl. symlink-to-dir, `.git`-pinned root, and a nonexistent-path soundness negative-control) both passed; both raised the same cosmetic non-blocking note — for an entry BOTH absolute and dir-shaped, OPT-2's arm order reports the dir-shape reason where the freeze-wall reports absolute, refusal behavior identical (documented in D-640-01, not fixed). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-640-01.md`; `docs/api.md` OPT-2 bullet updated. Cross-edition (#307): `plan-validator.js` (GENERATED aggregator, canonical + regenerated ×3 forge ports via `npm run sync:editions`) + the walkthrough; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially, unwaived — serial mode).
- **Two hardening follow-ups this session's own gates surfaced — #638 (edition-sync `--check`/`--write` symmetry) + #639 (metric-optimizer OPT freeze-rule hardening).** (1) **#638** — `scripts/edition-sync.js` `runWrite` was made fail-closed + self-healing in #629 (create-on-missing across `COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS`), but `runCheck` (`--check`) still verified only `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS`, so it stayed green on a missing/drifted COMMON or byte-group mirror (caught only in-chain by `validate-script-sync`). Added `checkMirrors` (reusing `checkByteIdenticalGroup` from `validate-script-sync.js` — no hand-rolled copy) into `runCheck`, giving `--check` parity with `runWrite`; `runWrite` untouched, no mirror data file written; `test-edition-sync.js` gains a RED-first synthetic-fixture case + a no-false-positive real-tree case. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-638-01.md`. (2) **#639** — five `optimize(<id>)` OPT freeze-rule holes surfaced by #634's own `code-reviewer` and change-gate `adversarial-verifier`: **OPT-2** now requires `metric_command` (R1) and refuses directory-shaped, glob, and `../`-alias `metric_paths` that defeat evaluation-isolation (R2/R5, reusing `hasUnresolvableEntry` + a `..`-segment check); **OPT-1** now refuses duplicate and fenced-decoy `optimize(<id>)` blocks for one node (R3/R7, via a new fence-aware `optimizeHeaderCounts` that reads the same `sectionBody(content,'Meta')` body + header regex as `parseOptimizeContracts`, so counter and parser can never disagree). R6 (numeric hex/exponent forms) is documentation-only — the OPT-3/OPT-4 caps bind on the `Number()`-converted value, so no separate rule is needed. Each rule has an accept + typed-refuse walkthrough fixture (RED-first). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-639-01.md`. Accepted residual: an inert defense-in-depth completeness gap in the OPT-2 `metric_paths` shape check (bare-existing-directory, backslash, and absolute-path forms the write-set freeze wall separately refuses) is filed as **#640** — backstopped today by the write-set wall + the OPT-5 change-gate metric reproduction. Cross-edition (#307): `plan-validator.js` (GENERATED aggregator, regenerated ×4) + `edition-sync.js` + the two test surfaces; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially, unwaived — no `test-run-chains` flake occurred).
- **Three edition-guard blind spots + a vacuous manifest guard — #629, #637.** (1) **#629** — the cross-edition guard net (`scripts/validate-script-sync.js` + `scripts/edition-sync.js`) had three holes: `hooks/hooks.json` was EXCLUDED from parity on a stale rationale (its only real cross-edition difference is the compact-context script-name token, exactly what `CONFIG_HOOKS_FAMILY`'s normalization already handles) → added a `HOOKS_JSON_FAMILY` (root + gitlab + gitea) normalized by `normalizeHooksJson`; the byte-identical `config/agents.toml` triple was covered by no `BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS` entry → added it; and `edition-sync.js` `runWrite()` steps (b)/(c) copied only when the target already existed, silently skipping a newly-enrolled member with an ABSENT mirror while `validate-script-sync` redded "Missing files" → dropped the `existsSync` gate (create-on-missing via `syncIfDrift`, matching aggregator step (a)), so the enrollment loop is now fail-closed AND self-healing. The `checkNormalizedFamily`/`checkByteIdenticalGroup` primitives are behavior-preserving refactors of the old inline loops (verified line-by-line by review + adversary; no `hooks.json`/`agents.toml` data file rewritten — they were already in parity). The change-gate adversary confirmed each guard bites on real planted drift (including an over-normalization probe: renaming a non-compact-context `.sh` hook token still reds). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-629-01.md`. A pre-existing `--check`/`--write` asymmetry the adversary noted (`edition-sync --check` covers only `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS`, while `validate-script-sync` covers the COMMON/byte-group mirrors in-chain) is tracked as **#638**. (2) **#637** — the #630 Layer-1 manifest block `fn-closure-audit` carried content_tokens `['<!-- PIN: closure-audit -->', 'closure-audit']` whose second token is a SUBSTRING of the first (the marker), so a marker-preserving *interior* gut of that finalize block stayed green — a gap surfaced by #630's OWN change-gate adversary (a closed loop). Added `sink_incomplete` as a distinctive interior content_token (verified present on all six finalize surfaces, anchoring the closure-audit interior prose, not a marker substring) + a RED-proof case in `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` that guts the interior while keeping the marker and asserts the checker reds. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-637-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `validate-script-sync.js` + `edition-sync.js` + `required-blocks.js` + `test-route-reachability.js` (root-only, no forge ports); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **Single-sourced the cross-runtime dispatch pins so runtime-dead prose is fenced — #636 (completes #627's descoped fix#2).** The **Codex-dispatch** effort/v2/v1 block is dead prose on the 3 Claude `plan-run` commands; the **Teammate-Mode** block is dead prose on the 3 Codex `plan-run` SKILLs — yet both were machine-pinned on all six surfaces (route-reachability `T5b`/`T14` + the `#606` teammate and `#611` unconditional-mandate families, mirrored across the four `validate-*-contracts.js`), so #627 could not fence either without redding the contract chains. Each block is now single-sourced to the surface that actually executes it, and every contract assertion relocated to track it: the Codex-dispatch block is fenced out of the 3 commands (preserving the always-live `Pass dispatch.nonce … Instruct the role to:` tail — the two forge commands had that sentence *fused into the block start*, a splice hazard handled per-file), the Teammate-Mode block fenced out of the 3 SKILLs; `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` moves the `T5b` array → SKILL-only and `T14` → command-only; across `validate-workflow-contracts.js` + its byte mirror `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` + `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` + the gitlab/gitea contract validators, the command-side `T5b` assertion is deleted, the `#606` family shrinks → command-only, and the **`#611`-fork family shrinks → SKILL-only** (the gitlab/gitea shared `[command, SKILL]` loops split three ways: symmetric families stay, `#606` → command-only, `#611`-fork → SKILL-only). Two `PROVENANCE_BAN`-safe semantic markers `<!-- PIN: teammate-mode -->` / `<!-- PIN: codex-dispatch -->` now delimit the fenced blocks (they also set up #630's Layer-2 slot boundaries). **The load-bearing correction** — proven by the shaping run's adversary — is that the `#611`-fork SKILL-only shrink MUST land in all four validators (+ byte mirror); omitting it (the first-draft map's shape) reds all four chains, because `on EVERY dispatch, tiered or not` would be asserted on a command that no longer contains it. The deliberate gitea/gitlab `mr|pr)` finalize-sink contract pins were explicitly left untouched. Design record: `docs/investigations/2026-07-08-630-636-routing-generation-seam.md`; decision record: `docs/decisions/D-636-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): the six `plan-run` surfaces + the four contract validators + the byte mirror; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **A routing-surface prose cluster: skeleton re-bloat, a false rolling-topup promise, and a stale speculation card — #627, #623, #628.** (1) **#627 (ships PARTIAL)** — `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` and its five sibling routing surfaces had re-bloated to ~480 lines against the ~150-line skeleton target (`docs/conventions.md` / D-445-01), with card content duplicated inline. Four of five sub-fixes landed: fix#1 restubbed the wait-budget/escalation/writer-kill-safety ladder to the `join-protocol.md` card pointer (commands only — the SKILLs correctly keep the pinned full Join Protocol inline) and the speculative-open paragraph to the `speculative-open.md` pointer; fix#3 compressed the Goal-Attestation block and moved its enum to `docs/api.md`; fix#4 prefixed the `$CLAIM_JS` resolver so the crash-recovery and workflow-next snippets are self-contained; fix#5 replaced rotted line-number self-cites with section-cites and reunited a split sentence. **fix#2 (runtime-dead-prose fencing) is DESCOPED to #636**: the Codex dispatch and Claude Teammate-Mode blocks are machine-pinned on all six surfaces (route-reachability T5b/T14, mirrored in all four `validate-*-contracts.js`), so fencing either out of its non-native runtime turns those chains RED — honest fencing needs relocating the cross-runtime pins (cross-edition script logic this prose bundle excluded). Adversarially verified behavior-preserving: every removed line has a live card home, every machine-pinned token survives on all six surfaces. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-627-01.md`. (2) **#623** — three routing surfaces (`commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md`, `agents/workflow-planner.md`, `docs/plan-run-cards/frontier-batch.md`) falsely promised that a fan-out wider than `FANOUT_CAP` "drains via rolling top-up as members close"; rolling slot-level top-up is a READ-frontier behavior only (a live write lane-group's membership / `write_union` / baseline are fixed at group formation). Corrected all three to scope rolling top-up to read frontiers and to state that a wide WRITE frontier runs in fixed group waves (each wave forms a new group paying its own synthesizer merge + barrier). Chose prose honesty over implementing member-admission into a live group (deferred — high blast radius on the group-identity invariants for an efficiency-only gain). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-623-01.md`. (3) **#628** — the `frontier-batch.md` card predated the speculative-auto default and still framed speculation as consent-only, and carried a freeze-illegal directory-shaped write-set example. Added the three-tier speculation rows (`auto` default-on / `consent` opt-in / `off` serial) to the card and `agents/workflow-planner.md`, replaced the example with a freeze-legal exact-path pair (`api/routes.js`, `cli/main.js`), and reworded `docs/plan-run-cards/README.md`'s speculative-open row to the three-tier framing (a trivial-inline finalize edit — `docs/**` is the barrier-invisible allowband — clearing the directory-wide grep AC that no frozen-plan node's write set covered). Prose-only: zero script/gate/behavior change. Cross-edition (#307): the six `#400` routing surfaces (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILLs) + card/agent docs; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **A release-greenness fail-open and the load-sensitive chain-runner test flake — #632, #635.** (1) **#632** — `kaola-workflow-release.js`'s `chainReceiptGreenness` returned `{green:true}` when the receipt's `chains` array was EMPTY or missing (zero chains verified was indistinguishable from all-green) — the SECOND fail-open consumer of this pattern (the #618 fix only closed the plan-validator's `--finalize-check` gate). Added a `chains_empty` guard returning `{green:false, reason:'chains_empty'}` in the correct precedence slot (`chains_unverified > chains_stale > chains_empty > chains_red`), mirroring the #618 precedent, across all four `release.js` editions. Also corrected a stale comment that wrongly claimed greenness "blocks `--cut`": `--cut` is INFORMATIONAL-ONLY (it cannot gate on a green receipt that doesn't exist yet at cut time — the offline pre-cut check runs before the online `npm test` that produces the receipt); greenness is surfaced only as a `chain_warning` at `--verify`. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-632-01.md`. (2) **#635** — `scripts/test-run-chains.js`'s signal-death assertions (T26/T27/T28) were load-sensitive flakes: they raced a real `process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL')` against the runner's per-chain timer, so under system load the child was reaped as `SIGTERM`/`timed_out` first and a different subset of assertions failed each run — forcing `--accept-known-red …:635` chain-receipt waivers on every prior release/finalize. Made the signal delivery DETERMINISTIC via an in-process seam (a monkey-patched `spawn`/`spawnSync` that returns a canned signal-death for a reserved sentinel command, so those assertions never create a racing subprocess), keeping one real-subprocess case with a class-only `exitCode === 1` assertion. `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js`'s own signal→exitCode mapping (the correct #618 fix) was NOT touched. Adversarially verified load-insensitive (22 runs, 19 under heavy induced CPU load calibrated to flake the pre-fix harness 4-in-6, all identical/green) — this bundle's own finalize four-chain receipt runs GENUINELY green with no `:635` waiver.
- **The sink/close pipeline could report success on a failed close, and `stale-worktree-cleanup` could permanently delete unmerged work — #619, #620, #631.** (1) **#619** — every issue-close site now verifies the live forge state on the SUCCESS path, not only in the catch: `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js`'s legacy (`postMergeCleanup`) close, its `--sink` `closeOne`, the bundle loop, and `kaola-workflow-claim.js`'s close-helper (`closeIssueIdempotent`, via a new un-memoized `probeIssueClosedLive` — reusing the memoized pre-close probe would have replayed a stale `open` verdict and made every genuine close register as failed). An `exit-0-but-still-open` close now buckets `failed` and yields a typed `sink_incomplete` refusal + exit 1 on BOTH the legacy and `--sink` paths (the legacy path finally mirrors the #497 `--sink` closure pattern). `push_upstream` proves `branch@{u}` parity and records a typed `push_upstream: failed` (not a false `stepDone`) on non-parity. The structurally-dead `worktree_sync` SINK_STEP (it always ran after the merge step removed the worktree, so its scan found nothing and recorded a no-op) was removed; its worktree-only project-dir copy now happens inline in the `merge` step (stage-to-tmpdir before removal, land into mainRoot after checkout only when the branch doesn't already track `kaola-workflow/<project>/` — a self-caught checkout-collision regression was fixed to this stage-then-land form). (2) **#631** — the sink receipt gains a NEW ADDITIVE field `published_head`, stamped at the closure gate once the live tip resolves as genuinely published (post-rebase); `branch_head` is UNCHANGED (still stamped once at receipt init — load-bearing for the #518 cycle-identity guard, never mutated). `verify-sink` / `cmdVerifySink` now prefers `published_head`, falling back to `branch_head` only for legacy receipts — fixing a false `impl_commit_not_ancestor` on a cleanly-rebased sink. (3) **#620** — `stale-worktree-cleanup` no longer force-deletes (`git branch -D`) a closed-issue branch unconditionally: it proves `git merge-base --is-ancestor <branch> <defBranch>` before `-D`, otherwise attempts the SAFE `git branch -d` (git refuses genuinely-unmerged work) and buckets the branch as `skipped_unmerged` with its tip SHA — never destroying committed-but-unmerged work (the exact #617 data-loss end-state this tool exists to remediate). The unconditional-`-D` `removeBranch` helper is now reachable only from the consented-discard `cmdRelease` path. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-619-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` + `kaola-workflow-claim.js` (canonical + codex twin, byte-identical; gitlab/gitea hand-ports).
- **Three adaptive-scheduler correctness fixes in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`, plus an in-run repair the run's own adversarial gate caught — #621, #622, #633.** (1) **#621** — the #590 baseline-first ordering (record the barrier baseline BEFORE flipping a ledger row to `in_progress`) was applied only to `runOpenNext`, leaving two other openers exposed: `runCloseAndOpenNext`'s fused advance and `runReopenNode` could each crash mid-operation and strand a row `in_progress` with no baseline. Both now record the baseline while the row is still `pending` (refusing `baseline_failed` with the row left pending on failure), and `runReopenNode` is idempotent across its own crash window (`alreadyAtTarget`). (2) **#622** — reads and leg-contained write lane-groups were mutually exclusive (`write_node_exclusive` blocked ALL read opens while any lane-group member was live), forcing mixed `[reads, writes]` frontiers into two serial waves. Relaxed to a leg-contained invariant (`liveHasLeglessWrite`) so a read co-opens alongside a live write group, backed by a new last-member merge fence (`merge_awaits_read_drain`, zero-mutation) that holds the group's octopus merge until live reads drain; STRICT exclusivity is preserved for legless serial writers and main-session gates. (3) **#633** — the lane-group synthesizer octopus merge refused with git's "untracked working tree file … would be overwritten by merge" (a structural untracked-parent-stub-vs-tracked-leg-evidence collision, not a real content conflict, hit live in a prior run). Fixed by seeding+committing each member's `.cache/{node-id}.md` evidence stub as a TRACKED file on the parent branch before legs branch off (new `legMirrorPath` helper), and making `runCloseNode`'s evidence read prefer a lane-group member's own leg copy when present (falling back to the parent, byte-identical to pre-fix for every non-lane-group case). **In-run repair (R4):** the #622 relaxation initially exposed a hole where a speculative write (the #596 speculative-open path) could form its own `lane_group` descriptor while another group was still live, silently replacing it in `running-set.json` and orphaning the displaced member's committed leg work; the run's `adversarial-verifier` gate reproduced it live and refused, and it was fixed in the same node by a `lane_group_live` exclusion in `runOpenReady`'s speculative-write branch (a speculative write is never selected while any write lane-group is live — it waits and opens normally once the group drains, preserving the single-live-descriptor invariant). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-622-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports, regenerated via `edition-sync.js`).
- **Six agent profiles had the evidence-persistence contract backwards relative to the executor, and `security-reviewer` could self-edit the very code it was reviewing — #625.** `implementer`/`synthesizer`/`tdd-guide` (the WRITE-role agents) previously told the model to RETURN its evidence for the orchestrator to persist, while `adversarial-verifier`/`code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer` (the READ-ONLY gate roles, plus both `agents/profiles/higher/` variants) told the model to WRITE its own `.cache` evidence file directly — exactly backwards from the canonical contract in `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md`. All three write-role profiles now self-write their evidence into the executor-seeded `.cache/{node-id}.md`, reading and preserving the seeded `evidence-binding:` header verbatim rather than altering it; all three gate-role profiles (plus higher-tier variants) now RETURN their verdict/findings block in final-message text for the orchestrator to persist via `record-evidence --stdin`. Separately, `security-reviewer`'s tool grant dropped `Write`/`Edit` (previously `["Read","Write","Edit","Bash","Grep","Glob"]`, now `["Read","Grep","Glob","Bash"]`, matching `code-reviewer`'s read-only set) — a self-review integrity hole where the reviewer could edit the code under review — with its remediation prose reframed from "provide/verify remediation" to routing a fix to the appropriate role (`fix_role=security`) instead of self-editing. Also dropped a stale duplicate Phase-1 `knowledge-lookup` cross-reference sentence from `agents/workflow-planner.md`, and fixed two dead route names in `agents/build-error-resolver.md`'s "When NOT to Use" table (`refactor-cleaner` → `implementer`, `architect` → `code-architect` — neither prior target is an installed role). Cross-edition (#307): the six agent `.md` profiles + the corresponding 18 `.toml` frontmatter files across all three plugin trees (`plugins/kaola-workflow{,-gitlab,-gitea}/agents/`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **Two vendored agent profiles hardcoded toolchain assumptions absent from most repos — #626.** `agents/tdd-guide.md`'s coverage-gate step unconditionally mandated `npm run test:coverage` with an 80%+ requirement, and `agents/doc-updater.md`'s Codemap Workflow unconditionally assumed a `scripts/codemaps/`/TypeScript toolchain (`npx tsx scripts/codemaps/generate.ts`, `madge`, `jsdoc2md`) — both false assumptions on a repo without that tooling. `tdd-guide` now runs the coverage command and applies the project's coverage target only where the repo actually exposes one, otherwise falling back to verifying via the project's recorded `validation_command`; `doc-updater` now runs a Detection step first (checking for `scripts/codemaps/` and/or `docs/CODEMAPS/`) and only regenerates codemaps when that tooling actually exists, otherwise reconciling the repo's real doc surfaces (README, CHANGELOG, `docs/*.md`, `.env.example`) against the diff with skip-with-reason instead of inventing structure. `docs/agents-source.md`'s Local Overrides section documents both divergences from upstream.
- **An issue could close on the forge while its ~3000-line implementation never actually reached the default branch — #617.** A live incident closed a GitHub issue whose merge sink died between its `closure` step and its final `push_main` step, because `closure` ran three steps too early in `SINK_STEPS`; the `remote-closed-after-publish` invariant that exists precisely to catch this had been declared in `CLOSURE_INVARIANTS` since #164 but never wired to any evaluation. `SINK_STEPS` now runs `closure` LAST, after `push_main`; `checkClosureInvariants` gained an optional `implRef`/`sinkTarget` pair that runs `git merge-base --is-ancestor` and records `remote_closed_after_publish: 'verified'|'failed'` on the receipt (a caller that omits both, e.g. `cmdFinalize`'s merge-lane, leaves the check a no-op); `sink-merge`'s `closure` step additionally re-resolves the branch's current tip and re-runs the ancestor check as a hard gate immediately before closing any issue, refusing loudly (`remote_closed_after_publish_unverified`, non-zero exit, no issue closed) on failure instead of trusting the ordering alone. `cmdFinalize` now derives its own merge-lane close deferral from the durable `sink:` field in `workflow-state.md` (defaulting toward deferral on an unreadable/absent field) instead of resting solely on the caller remembering `--keep-worktree`. A new read-only `kaola-workflow-claim.js verify-sink --project P` audit subcommand lets an operator independently check, after the fact, that a project's recorded implementation commit is an ancestor of the default branch and that no worktree/branch/active-folder residue lingers. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-617-01.md`.
- **Both forge-codex (GitLab/Gitea) finalize SKILLs were missing their entire adaptive four-gate prerequisite block, leaving a dangling `validator_script` reference reachable on an adaptive-path run — #624.** The `workflow_path: adaptive` branch and its script-enforced `--resume-check`/`--gate-verify`/`--barrier-check`/`--verdict-check` barrier existed only in the canonical (github-codex) finalize SKILL; both forge SKILLs jumped straight from the fast-path branch to the Chain-Receipt Gate section, so an adaptive-path run on either forge reached finalize with no instruction to run the script-enforced pre-merge barrier at all. The canonical block is now ported verbatim (modulo forge-specific `validator_script` paths) into `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md` and `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md`. Separately, all three Claude finalize commands (`commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` plus its GitLab/Gitea counterparts) said the barrier was "script-enforced by three gates — run all three" in prose directly above a code block that already invoked all four gates — a stale count an agent trusting the prose could act on by silently skipping `--verdict-check`; corrected to "four gates — run all four" in all three. Both forge contract validators (`validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js`, `validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts.js`) gain a new pin asserting the finalize SKILL carries the `workflow_path: adaptive` marker plus all four gate flags, so this class of drift is now machine-caught. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).
- **A killed test chain could still be reported green, and an empty chain-receipt passed the finalize gate vacuously — #618.** In `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js`, a child process terminated by a signal (an external OOM-kill, an operator `SIGKILL`, not necessarily our own per-chain timeout) could map to `exitCode: 0` in both the sync (`spawnSync`) and async (`spawn`) dispatch paths, since a pure signal death raises no `error` and falls through the prior exit-code ternary's false-green branch; both paths now map any signal-terminated child to `exitCode: 1` unconditionally and record the terminating signal name (`signal: "SIGKILL"` etc., `null` on a normal exit) on the chain for diagnosability. In `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js --finalize-check`, a fresh, HEAD-bound receipt whose `chains[]` array was empty (zero chains verified) previously passed the gate, since the red-chain filter is vacuously empty over an empty array; it now refuses with a typed `chains_empty` reason, precedence-ordered between `chains_stale` and `chains_red`. The previously-unwired `test-run-chains.js` suite is now part of the `test:kaola-workflow:claude` npm chain. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-617-01.md`.

## [6.20.3] - 2026-07-04

### Fixed

- **`plan-run`'s non-speculative serial-degrade now surfaces WHY it degraded — #616.** Follow-up to #615: on the speculative-write co-open path, an excluded write already carried `speculativeWriteExcluded: { reason: 'parent_dirty', ... }`, but the normal (non-speculative) co-open path's degrade to a single serial write was silent — a persistently broken or misconfigured `--parent-clean-check` fence could serialize every write frontier forever with no telemetry to notice it by. `runOpenReady` (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) now threads `serialDegradeReason: 'parent_dirty'` onto that path's SUCCESSFUL-open return, set only when `parentCarriesProductionDirt()` actually caused the degrade (the same `--parent-clean-check` fence result is captured once and reused for both the group-formation gate and the label, avoiding a double fence-subprocess spawn); the pre-existing single-write-node / `!legCoupled` / `groupCeiling < 2` / `!grp.ok` degrade causes remain byte-identical (field absent). Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

## [6.20.2] - 2026-07-04

### Changed

- **Project literature reframed around loop engineering.** The README opening, Philosophy lead-in, and a new "loop vocabulary, mapped to mechanisms" table now position Kaola-Workflow in the loop-engineering vocabulary (agent loop, verification loop/grader, exit condition, circuit breaker, durable state, outer loop, harness), each term mapped to the existing mechanism that backs it; `/goal` is framed as the outer loop, and the `CLAUDE.md` Project Overview and `docs/architecture.md` openings carry the same one-line reframe. The GitHub repository description was updated to match. Prose-only: zero script/gate/behavior change, no routing surfaces touched.

### Fixed

- **`plan-run`'s running-set scheduler no longer deadlocks a mixed serial + lane-group plan at the last-member group close — #615.** A lane-group co-open (two or more disjoint write nodes opened together in isolated legs) could form over a parent worktree that already carried out-of-allowband production dirt — uncommitted files left behind by already-closed SERIAL write siblings (serial nodes never commit; commits are finalize-owned). That group's last-member close then hit a two-horned deadlock: the parent-clean fence (`--parent-clean-check`) refused `parent_dirty` on the uncommitted serial file, while committing that file to clear the fence made the merge commit carry it, landing it outside the group's declared union at the commit-based group barrier (`write_set_overflow`) — two structurally contradictory requirements on the same paths, with no way to close cleanly. A new `parentCarriesProductionDirt` precondition (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) now gates lane-group formation before it happens, reusing the SAME `--parent-clean-check` fence the last-member close already runs (so the two checks can never classify a parent differently) and failing closed on any non-`pass` result. It applies at both sites that can form a group: the normal co-open path now degrades to opening a single serial write when the parent carries dirt, and the speculative-write co-open path now excludes write candidates from that open instead (the write simply waits for its gate normally). No change to the parent-clean fence itself, the commit-based group barrier, or the finalize-owned-commit contract for serial nodes — a pure-parallel or group-first plan carries no prior production dirt, so no currently-safe parallelism is lost. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-615-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

## [6.20.1] - 2026-07-04

### Fixed

- **The six finalize surfaces no longer contradict their own shipped consumer-mode Validation Gate trade with an unconditional "full test suite + coverage >= 80%" mandate — #614.** The three Claude finalize commands and three Codex finalize SKILL packs already implement a consumer-mode Validation Gate where the agent owns verification via `.cache/final-validation.md` plus the plan's `validation_command`, accepting a focused test set in place of project coverage tooling — but several spots in those SAME files (the Step 1 instruction block, Operational Guardrails, the Validation Delegation Policy, and the Step 2 acceptance criteria / condensed SKILL line) still stated an unconditional "run the full test suite and confirm coverage >= 80%" mandate, directly contradicting the trade already shipped. The final-validation instruction is now explicitly dual-mode: self-host (npm) keeps the four-chain receipt gate unchanged, while a consumer repo runs the plan's `validation_command` once or cites fresh prior evidence, with the coverage target no longer a universal mandate. Prose-only: zero script/gate/behavior change — `scripts/`, `--finalize-check`, `run-chains.js`, and `sink-merge.js` are byte-identical. Cross-edition (per the four-chain gate convention): the three Claude finalize commands + the three Codex finalize SKILL packs (per the six-surface propagation rule); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

## [6.20.0] - 2026-07-04

### Added

- **Codex dispatch gains a JOIN PROTOCOL — every dispatch card carries a tier-derived wait budget, `reconcile-running-set` positive-confirms writer safety before adopting a reclaimed writer, and delegation outcomes are typed — #611.** A live Codex-edition adaptive run (#598's authorization half was already solved) showed the OTHER half was missing entirely: 24 spawns, 8 `interrupt_agent` kills at a median ~4 minutes — every victim's own rollout showed it actively executing seconds before the kill, none wedged — 36 of 47 `wait_agent` calls were reflex-timeout busy-polls, zero `close_agent` calls all session, `send_message` "still there?" probes went unanswered and directly preceded kills, and a killed in-place writer leaked a stray file outside its declared write set that the orchestrator had to hand-clean. The Codex SKILL packs specified the spawn side only; nothing told the model that role nodes legitimately run 10–30 minutes. Six design arms close the gap. **A — wait budget.** `waitBudgetMinutes(model)` derives `wait_budget_minutes`/`wait_budget_source` from the node's tier (`reasoning`→40, `standard`→20, an absent/blank/unrecognized tier→a concrete 20-minute `role_default`, never null), and every dispatch card (`open-next`/`open-ready`/`close-and-open-next`) now carries it — a `running` agent is never interrupted before its budget elapses. **B/C — long-poll join loop + escalation ladder** (prose-only, propagated to all six plan-run routing surfaces): one long-timeout `wait_agent` per iteration, drain every completed member via `list_agents` before re-waiting, `send_message` status probes prohibited (structurally unanswerable by a busy agent, and the motivating run's probes all preceded a kill); on budget expiry, `followup_task` → a ~5-minute grace window → `interrupt_agent` plus a further `followup_task` for partial evidence → reclaim as the documented LAST resort. **AC5 — typed delegation outcomes.** A closed-vocabulary column-0 `delegation_outcome: completed | returned_partial | interrupted_unresponsive | interrupted_obsolete` evidence line (absent ⇒ `completed`) replaces a free-text "it stalled so I did it myself," enforced by `checkEvidenceShape` ahead of every role branch. **D — writer kill-safety.** `reconcile-running-set` now diffs every WRITER member leaving the live set (rolled back / capped out / stale) against its declared write set via `--barrier-check`, BEFORE the existing `--drop-base` loop, and emits a per-writer verdict (`writerReconciliation: [{node_id, verdict, reason, outOfWriteSet}]`, plus a top-level `writerHalt`). This shipped POSITIVE-CONFIRMATION and fail-closed only after an in-run adversarial-gate refutation: the first implementation let a resultless/unrecognized barrier result — `shellNode` never throws, so a SIGKILL'd/jetsam-killed/crashed/non-JSON/missing-validator check returns a truthy, result-less `{exitCode:N}` — fall through to a terminal `adopt`, silently keeping unverified writer dirt, reachable via exactly the jetsam kills this repo already documents on constrained hosts. The repair inverts the classifier: `adopt` now requires an EXPLICIT `result: 'pass'|'ok'` (or the vacuous `no_barrier_base` — a writer that crashed before it ever wrote under tracking); every other shape, including a resultless or unrecognized result, halts with `barrier_unverifiable`. The issue asked for a typed `adopt|revert|halt` output; the shipped classifier emits only `adopt|halt` from reconcile itself (non-destructive by design — it never auto-deletes), with `revert` retained as the separate, already-sanctioned `revert-overflow` remediation the orchestrator invokes on a `halt`, never something reconcile performs inline. **E — `fork_turns:"none"` is now an unconditional mandate** on every Codex v2 dispatch call (previously scoped to tiered nodes only), since the dispatch card is self-contained by contract regardless of tier. **F — frontier discipline + preflight bounds report.** All six plan-run routing surfaces gain same-turn back-to-back frontier spawns, one join loop per frontier, and a width formula counting only RUNNING members (a controlled probe on codex-tui 0.142.5 confirmed a finished-but-unclosed agent does not hold a concurrency seat, and the running cap IS enforced at spawn time with a verbatim `collab spawn failed: agent thread limit reached` refusal — the reactive remedy, not proactive early closure); the Codex preflight/doctor and installer additionally report the effective v2 slot budget and wait-timeout bounds (`max_concurrent_threads_per_session[_source]`, `effective_subagent_width`, `min_wait_timeout_ms`, `max_wait_timeout_ms`, `default_wait_timeout_ms`), gated on `multi_agent_v2_enabled` (`null`/`not_applicable` when v2 is off); an absent/non-positive configured thread count falls back to an OBSERVED default of 4 (`source: 'observed_default'`, per the issue's own controlled-probe evidence, not a published Codex default), while the three wait-timeout fields have no fabricated fallback and read `null` unless explicitly configured. A new reference card, `docs/plan-run-cards/join-protocol.md`, documents the full mechanics. Deliberately NOT built: a proactive heartbeat/progress-file protocol (zero genuinely-wedged agents were observed in the motivating run; the budget+ladder bounds a true wedge's cost to one node's budget) or a new adaptive-node subcommand (the existing `reconcile-running-set` was extended, per reuse-before-adding). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-611-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` + `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports) + `kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js` (×4) + `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (×3) + the six plan-run routing surfaces + the four contract validators; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

### Changed

- **Plan tier tokens are now runtime-neutral — `NODE_MODEL_TIERS` is `{reasoning, standard}`, with `opus`/`sonnet` accepted as legacy aliases — #610.** Three prior rulings deliberately kept `{opus, sonnet}` as the portable cross-edition plan `model`-column vocabulary, but that ruling predated the current state: no edition consumes the tokens as literal model names at dispatch anymore (Codex maps them to per-spawn `reasoning_effort` `xhigh`/`high`, opencode to top/second effort variants, and Claude to the `Agent(model=…)` alias), so they were ranks in fact — this change makes them ranks in name. `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (the byte-identical ×4 drift anchor) renames `NODE_MODEL_TIERS` to `['reasoning','standard']` and adds `normalizeTier(token)` (legacy `opus`→`reasoning`, `sonnet`→`standard`), making `TIER_RANK`/`dispatchEffort`/`mapTier`/`dispatchEffortOpencode` alias-aware and adding `dispatchModelClaude(tier)` so the Claude executor mapping is mechanical, not prose; the plan-validator and `next-action` validate via the normalizer (the `model_invalid` message lists the neutral tokens and notes the accepted legacy aliases); the `resolve-agent-model` reasoning-floor check normalizes before comparing so `reasoning` satisfies the floor (agent frontmatter and `DEFAULT_AGENT_MODELS` intentionally stay Claude aliases — they feed `Agent(model=…)` directly). Handoff/orient/dispatch emissions additionally carry a runtime-native `model_display` sibling (`{claude, codex, opencode}` display strings) alongside the RAW tier token, so payload echoes read natively on every runtime — additive, never mutating the raw token. **Back-compat is absolute and was adversarially verified**: frozen/archived plans keep their bytes — legacy cells validate at parse with NO rewrite, `plan_hash` values reproduce unchanged, `--resume-check` stays green, and dispatch efforts are byte-identical (an independent OLD↔NEW differential harness plus a real archived-plan resume returned NOT-REFUTED with the new accept-set proven a strict superset of the old); a walkthrough legacy-alias fixture pins this permanently. The planner/routing prose surfaces author the neutral tokens with exactly one per-edition mapping sentence; `docs/opencode-edition.md`, `docs/api.md`, and `docs/architecture.md` are docked. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-610-01.md` (supersedes the portable-vocabulary ruling). Cross-edition (#307): schema ×4 + generated `plan-validator`/`next-action`/`adaptive-handoff`/`adaptive-node` ×4 each + `resolve-agent-model` ×4 + the six-surface prose; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially); opencode suite green separately (D-530-02).

### Fixed

- **The run-progress mirror now fails closed on an untrustworthy main root, instead of risking a fabricated `kaola-workflow/` tree in an unrelated checkout — #612.** `writeRunProgressMirror` (in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) previously wrote `<main-root>/kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/run-progress.json` off of whatever root its resolution heuristic produced; a misresolved git-common-dir fallback could point at a foreign checkout and fabricate a `kaola-workflow/<project>/` tree there. The mirror now only writes when `mainRoot` is trustworthy: either affirmatively resolved from the project's own `workflow-state.md` `main_root:` field, or a fallback root that demonstrably already owns the project (its `workflow-plan.md` is present there). Two new regression fixtures, `T612-escape` and `T612-legit`, were added to `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + `plugins/kaola-workflow/`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/`).

- **Two closure-audit timeout-probe walkthrough cases no longer race a real 300ms OS timeout, removing a flake under CPU contention — #613.** `testClosureAuditExecuteDetectionTimeoutPropagates` and `testClosureAuditExecuteLabelRemovalTimeoutBreaks` relied on a hung subprocess shim losing a wall-clock race against a real 300ms OS timeout-kill, which misfired when the host was under load. The timeout shims now self-terminate via `SIGTERM` deterministically instead of relying on the OS timeout-kill winning the race, and the label-removal case's must-succeed `issue list` call was given a contention-proof budget. Applied to `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` and the equivalent sibling timeout-probe cases in `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js` and `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js`.

- **Non-Claude runtime narratives no longer speak Claude model nouns — the Codex prompt surfaces and the generated opencode edition are purged of "Opus"/"Sonnet"-as-model prose, with machine enforcement — #609.** On a Codex adaptive run the session dialogue repeatedly surfaced vocabulary that means nothing on that runtime ("the workflow-planner (Opus)", "opus ~= 5x sonnet", "You stay on Sonnet"): two vocabularies were conflated — the B1 plan-column tier tokens (a portable contract, addressed by #610 above) and B2 descriptive prose treating Claude model names as the runtime's models. The B2 class is now purged: the Codex TOML profiles (`workflow-planner`/`synthesizer`/`contractor` + `config/agents.toml`) and the adapt/plan-run SKILL packs across all 3 plugin trees speak tier/effort vocabulary (reasoning tier → per-spawn `reasoning_effort` `xhigh`; standard tier → `high`), keeping only tier-token mentions phrased as ranks; the opencode generator's root cause is fixed — `renderAgent()` never passed agent BODIES through a rewrite, so canonical Claude prose leaked verbatim into `.opencode/agent/*.md` — via a pure `rewriteClaudeModelNouns()` helper (exact-phrase, case-sensitive rewrites; idempotent by construction) applied in `renderAgent()` and `transformCommandBody()`, with the `test-opencode-edition.js` S2 exemption NARROWED to a body-wide case-sensitive `\b(Opus|Sonnet)\b` sweep over all generated output (the guard now forbids what it previously tolerated). The six `workflow-init` surfaces gain an explicit constraint: the synthesized consumer-repo "Kaola Workflow" section MUST use runtime-neutral tier vocabulary, never vendor model nouns (a consumer-repo CLAUDE.md block leaks into every runtime that reads that repo). Machine enforcement: negative B2-noun assertions in all four contract validators (each scanning its own edition's prompt surfaces, with the B1 rank-token mentions exempted), the init-constraint needle, and `test-route-reachability.js` kept in lockstep — a reintroduced noun reds the corresponding chain. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially); opencode suite green separately (D-530-02).

- **`run-chains.js` receipts now persist `timed_out` per chain, and a killed chain's failure summary names the exact remedy — #608.** The per-chain kill ceiling was 900000ms (15 min, #512); a live run on a constrained host exceeded it, producing a `chains_red` failure that was structurally indistinguishable from a genuine test regression without re-reading raw process output. `resolveTimeoutMs`'s default is raised to 1800000ms (30 min), and the already-computed internal `_timedOut` marker (previously stripped before persisting, alongside `_output`) is now PROMOTED into the receipt as `timed_out: true|false` per chain entry — absent on a legacy receipt reads as `false`, so no reader needs to change. The non-JSON failure summary line now labels a timed-out chain inline (e.g. `claude (TIMEOUT at 1800s — raise KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS or investigate a hang)`), letting an operator scanning stderr — not the JSON receipt — tell a timeout from a genuine red at a glance; the plan-validator's `chains_red` operator hint does the same for a `--finalize-check` caller, naming `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS` only when a red chain actually timed out (an unrelated red chain keeps the existing generic hint). The refuse/pass decision itself (`redChains.length` check) is byte-for-byte unchanged — this is hint/observability text only, no barrier-logic change. RED-first: `scripts/test-run-chains.js` grew 117 → 125 assertions (three new-default fallback cases, a hung-mock-chain receipt+stderr case, and a green-chain `timed_out: false` case). Cross-edition (#307): `scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` + `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (canonical + codex byte-twin + both forge ports — the run-chains port is hand-rename-normalized per `validate-script-sync.js`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-608-01.md`.

- **`main-session-gate` acceptance windows now carry a runtime write fence — an in-worktree out-of-band write during an open gate is denied by default, and the gate's own evidence must attest how any probe it ran was provisioned — #607.** A `main-session-gate` (the non-delegable acceptance check, e.g. a GPU/visual confirmation) is read-only by design, but nothing previously stopped the gate itself — or a stray tool call during its window — from authoring or deleting product files in the parent worktree: the close-time per-node barrier is blind to a write-then-delete inside that window (a net-zero diff), so a gate-authored file could vanish leaving no trace. Three layers close this, disclosed as a single design in `docs/decisions/D-607-01.md`: **Layer 1 (upstream provisioning, prose-only)** — the planner-authoring surfaces and the plan-run routing surfaces now mandate that any instrumentation a gate needs (a probe script, build wiring) is authored by an UPSTREAM WRITER node inside its own declared write set, with the plan stating the durability decision (durable/committed/env-gated vs ephemeral with downstream-owned deletion); the gate itself never authors or deletes files. **Layer 2 (runtime write fence)** — `open-next` and the `close-and-open-next` fused advance now record an opened `main-session-gate` into `running-set.json` as a minimal `kind:'gate'` entry (a new state channel, audited against every existing running-set consumer to confirm zero regression to write co-open, slot accounting, or reconcile roll-forward), and the write-lane hook (`hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh`) gains a new rule (c) — DEFAULT-ON, opt-out via `KAOLA_GATE_WINDOW_FENCE=0` — that denies (exit 2) an in-worktree out-of-band `Write`/`Edit` while any `kind:'gate'` node is open, carving out the workflow bands, the `.kw/` band, member worktrees, and a co-open writer's own declared lane as legal. **Layer 3 (close-time evidence token)** — a `main-session-gate`'s `.cache` evidence must now carry a column-0 `instrumentation: none | <node-id>` token attesting whether it ran any provisioned probe and, if so, naming the upstream writer that authored it; the named node must exist in the ledger as a writer (non-empty declared write set) — absence is refused alongside the existing missing-verdict shape check, converting silent gate-authored drift into an affirmative false attestation instead of silence. Honest residual gaps, disclosed in the decision record rather than silently accepted: Bash-mediated writes are NOT covered by the hook (Write/Edit tool-call interception only — the pre-existing close-time barrier remains the backstop there); the pre-existing `.cache/`-anywhere carve-out is unchanged (not a #607 regression); and a crash mid-gate leaves the fence STICKY (`reconcile-running-set` no-ops on a lone open gate rather than clearing it) — an intended fail-closed tripwire, not a bug, clearing only when the run resumes and closes the gate, or via the manual `KAOLA_GATE_WINDOW_FENCE=0` opt-out. Propagates across the six planner-authoring and plan-run routing prose surfaces (#400); an adversarial-verifier gate ran a real-repo fence-bypass / false-fence / fail-open-preservation / cross-edition battery and returned `verdict: pass` with 0 blocking findings (4 disclosed LOW notes, recorded above or accepted as design tradeoffs). Cross-edition (#307): `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex byte-copy + both forge ports) + `hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh` (byte-identical ×4) + the 13 planner/plan-run prose surfaces + validator/route-reachability needles; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-607-01.md`.

## [6.19.0] - 2026-07-03

### Added

- **Claude dispatch posture: agent-teams enablement is now detected and reported (report-only), and the plan-run surfaces teach teammate-mode orchestration — classic subagents remain the default path — #606.** The Claude runtime has two dispatch transports: classic synchronous subagents (always available) and the experimental agent-teams mechanism (named full-session teammates with a shared task list and a `SendMessage` mailbox), toggled by the binary `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` flag (session env or a settings `"env"` block; verified not model-gated). Mirroring the settled Codex dispatch-posture pattern: `install.sh` gains a read-only `detect_claude_dispatch_posture()` helper (env probe first — authoritative when present — then a read-mode-only scan of `~/.claude/settings.json`, `$PWD/.claude/settings.json`, `$PWD/.claude/settings.local.json` `env` blocks) and reports `claude_dispatch_posture: teams | classic` in the post-install summary — NON-FATAL, and it NEVER writes user-owned config (the install test asserts the settings file byte-unchanged, making the report-only boundary mechanical); the `classic` remediation LEADS with the always-available classic path and only then qualifies agent teams as experimental + flag-gated, and the three `workflow-init` COMMAND surfaces gain the same classic-led posture note in the config-audit region OUTSIDE the byte-paired CLAUDE.md template block (the three `kaola-workflow-init` SKILL packs stay byte-unchanged). All six plan-run routing surfaces gain a verbatim-identical `#### Teammate-Mode Dispatch` subsection beside the announcement contract: named spawns (name = node id), mailbox returns, `SendMessage` for the existing bounded repair nudges instead of re-dispatching, a sync-spawn exception, the REQUIRED one-nudge idle-race discipline (an idle notification is not a deliverable and carries no ordering guarantee; send exactly one request, then wait), and the closing rule that teammate mode changes the transport, never the contract — classic stays the documented default. Load-bearing non-goals held by construction and review: NO script, gate, barrier, or scheduler reads the flag for any decision (zero behavior change; a classic-only session runs with no degradation). Machine guards: needles in all five contract validators + a route-reachability all-six-surface pin; PROVENANCE_BAN clean. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-606-01.md`. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **Derived root-visible run-progress mirror — the live Node Ledger advances only in the worktree until sink, so the main root now gets a generated `run-progress.json` snapshot — #605.** During a linked-worktree adaptive run, every successful ledger-mutating subcommand (open-next, open-ready, close-node, close-and-open-next, reconcile-running-set, reopen-node, repair-node, write-halt/clear-halt) additionally writes `<main-root>/kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/run-progress.json` — `{plan_hash, updated_at, op, node_ledger:[{id,role,status}], in_progress, all_done}` — AFTER its ledger write succeeds, resolving the main root via the existing worktree↔main linkage seam, never from cwd. The mirror is strictly derived and NON-AUTHORITATIVE: a write failure degrades to a `run_progress_mirror: failed` warn field in the op envelope (never a refusal, never a nonzero exit — proven by a fail-open walkthrough oracle against an unwritable `.cache/`), no script ever reads it back for decisions, the embedded `plan_hash` makes staleness detectable, it is absent when no worktree is linked (serial in-repo runs are already root-visible), it is archived/removed with the rest of the project `.cache/` at finalize, and the root `workflow-plan.md` stays byte-frozen as the authority anchor. The six plan-run routing surfaces gain the required close-echo line (`{node-id} → complete; opened: {next-id|—}`) so transcripts carry progress even on hosts with no plan widget, and `docs/workflow-state-contract.md` documents the mirror alongside the ROADMAP.md generated-mirror rule. Cross-edition (#307): generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

- **Dispatch visibility announcement contract — the orchestrator now identifies itself at run start and announces every subagent spawn/return with node id, role, and task name — #604.** Observed on a Codex-edition run: children displayed only host-assigned random nicknames (`agent_role: null`), the supervisor rendered as bare `/root`, and the operator could not tell which role was delegated to whom. All six plan-run routing surfaces now carry three verbatim announcement formats in the Dispatch step — run-start (`plan-run orchestrator: driving {project} — {N} nodes; each role subagent will be announced at dispatch.`), pre-spawn (`→ dispatching {node_id} · {role} as subagent task "{task_name}" (model {model|default}, effort {effort|inherit})`), and on-return (`← {node_id} · {role} returned: {verdict or one-line outcome}`) — plus an inline-fallback announcement aligned with the existing gate-role degradation notice. Prose + machine guards only (no script changes): one contract-validator needle per format line in every edition validator plus route-reachability coverage, so removing any line from any surface reds the corresponding chain. Cross-edition (#307): all four chains green (run sequentially).

### Fixed

- **`--summary` on the open-bearing adaptive subcommands now carries the dispatch card essentials — an orchestrator following the canonical invocation no longer dispatches blind — #602.** The plan-run surfaces' canonical invocations embed `--summary`, which collapsed `open-next`/`open-ready`/`close-and-open-next` output to the single line `summary: ok` — hiding the `dispatch` card the very next SKILL step depends on, while the drill-into-the-envelope instruction fired only on `result: refuse`. Observed live on a Codex-edition run: all seven spawns went out with no `agent_type`, improvised task names, and no per-node reasoning effort — every child ran as the `default` profile under a host nickname (the scripts had computed the correct card every time; it just never reached the orchestrator in-band). The summary emission path now appends one machine-parsable segment per opened node — `opened=<node-id> role=<role> task=<codex_task_name> mode=<codex_dispatch_mode> effort=<codex_reasoning_effort|inherit>` (one segment per member on batch opens; leg paths stay in the envelope) — and default no-`--summary` `--json` output is proven BYTE-identical by a walkthrough oracle. The six plan-run surfaces correct the false return-shape claim, extend the drill rule to require the card before EVERY dispatch (not just on refuse), and add an explicit no-improvisation prohibition (never invent a task name, omit the agent type, or drop the effort tier because the card was not in view); contract-validator needles pin the new prose per edition. Cross-edition (#307): generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four chains green (run sequentially).

- **Codex dispatch mode: the preflight-detected `v2-task-name` mode now reaches the runtime dispatch cards — #603.** `resolveCodexDispatchMode()` consulted only `ctx`/env, and nothing ever set either, so on a host correctly detected as v2-capable at install/preflight every runtime card still said `v1-thread-id` and the role-bearing `codex_task_name` was computed but steered away from use. Startup now accepts `--codex-dispatch-mode v2-task-name|v1-thread-id`, value-validated BEFORE any claim mutation (typed `invalid_codex_dispatch_mode` refusal with zero mutation on any other or newline-carrying value) and persisted as an optional `codex_dispatch_mode` field in the `## Sink` block under the same `assertNoNewline` anti-injection fence as `worktree_path`; `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` reads the field once and threads it into the ctx of all THREE `buildDispatch` call sites (open-next, fused advance, open-ready batch) with the resolution precedence unchanged (ctx → env override → `v1-thread-id` fail-closed default — absent field and non-Codex editions produce byte-identical cards). The six Codex startup SKILLs (`kaola-workflow-next`/`kaola-workflow-adapt` ×3 editions) thread the preflight detection into the startup call; `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` untouched; `docs/workflow-state-contract.md` documents the field. Cross-edition (#307): `claim.js` ×4 (codex twin byte-identical, forge ports hand-adapted) + generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four chains green (run sequentially).

- **The Claude edition now installs the `synthesizer` agent — `install.sh`/`uninstall.sh` `REQUIRED_AGENTS` listed 14 of 15 roles — #600.** The plan-run merge-conflict recovery dispatches a reasoning-class `synthesizer` to resolve a real same-file conflict by intent, and the Codex/opencode runtimes already ship all 15 profiles, but the Claude installer's roster omitted the role (the plan-validator agent-registration-gap check only fires when a plan ADDS a new agent file, so the addition slipped both arrays), leaving `subagent_type: synthesizer` unresolvable in a Claude-runtime consumer repo on exactly the path that exists to protect correctness. `synthesizer` is appended to both `REQUIRED_AGENTS` arrays: a fresh `./install.sh --forge=github` now deploys 15 agents, a reinstall over an existing 14-agent install self-heals via the managed-file add, and uninstall cleans the file up. No `agents/profiles/higher/synthesizer.md` override is needed — `agents/synthesizer.md` frontmatter already pins `model: opus` in both profiles (the deployed copy is rewritten to `model: inherit` and the emitted `.kaola-agent-models.json` manifest carries the real `opus`, verified live in a sandboxed-$HOME install/uninstall round-trip). RED-first: `scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js` gained the `synthesizer` roster assertion plus a manifest→`opus` assertion, failing pre-fix with the never-deployed file; cross-edition roster parity on the 15-role set is covered by the union of the existing edition chains once the claude chain asserts it. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

### Changed

- **The Codex dispatch-posture remediation now LEADS with the always-available, officially documented in-session explicit ask; the `ultra` reasoning-effort route is offered second, qualified as undocumented and server-gated — #601.** The #598 posture report led with `model_reasoning_effort = "ultra"` (config.toml, then per-session `codex -c model_reasoning_effort=ultra`, then the in-session ask), but post-release verification established that the official Configuration Reference documents `model_reasoning_effort` only up to `xhigh` and per-model `ultra` availability is governed by server-delivered model metadata — on plans whose picker tops out at xhigh, remediations 1–2 are not actionable, while the officially documented subagents contract (Codex spawns sub-agents on explicit request) always is. The wording — authored once and propagated consistently — now leads with "explicitly ask for sub-agents/delegation/parallel work in-session" and qualifies the effort route with "if your Codex exposes an ultra reasoning effort for your model/plan (undocumented as of codex-tui 0.142.5 — check the /model picker)": the installer ×3 and preflight ×4 byte-groups (byte-identical within group, `validate-script-sync.js` green), README's Codex section, `docs/api.md`, and the six `workflow-init` config-audit surfaces (command+SKILL byte-pairs ×3 editions). Behavior-preserving: `deriveDispatchPosture` and all posture derivation untouched, the report/WARN stays non-fatal, the version-guard note is kept, and every previously pinned substring (`model_reasoning_effort = "ultra"`, `codex -c model_reasoning_effort=ultra`, `0.142.5`, proactive-suppresses-remediation) is retained. RED-first order assertions (the in-session ask must precede the effort clause on fresh non-proactive output) were added to the codex walkthrough and both forge suites. Ride-along: `install-opencode.sh`'s header comment AND usage text are corrected to the 5-file adaptive-core command set — the retired `auto` command was still listed (the usage-heredoc instance was surfaced by the in-run review gate and fixed before finalize); `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` green (499 assertions). Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

## [6.18.0] - 2026-07-03

### Added

- **Speculative-open (`speculative_open_policy: consent`) now admits write-bearing nodes, not just read-only ones — a write member bets on an open gate WITH a provisioned leg and is discard-only on failure — #596.** D-419-02 (Part 4, #439) shipped speculative gate-betting for read-only descendants only, and explicitly DEFERRED the write half — the stated reason was rollback complexity against the shared PARENT worktree (a `git checkout <baseSha> -- <path>` revert-in-place, fragile for a brand-new file that does not exist at the baseline SHA) and an unsettled blast-radius question. That premise is now obsolete: the per-member LEG machinery that shipped independently since (#437/#542) already isolates every co-opened write node in its own `.kw/legs/<project>/<node-id>` worktree, so a write's declared changes never touch the parent tree at all. This graduates write eligibility onto the SAME `consent` bet with NO new merge, containment, or barrier code. **Static eligibility** (`next-action.js`): a write-bearing node joins `speculativePending` under the existing structural conditions (pending, not already ready, single unsatisfied dep = an open gate) PLUS three write-axis guards — its declared set is exactly resolvable (`hasUnresolvableEntry`, reused from the `--parallel-safe` coarse-relaxation guard and newly exported from `plan-validator.js` for this purpose), it declares no PROTECTED file (`classifier.isProtected`), and it is not the plan's unique sink. **Runtime open** (`adaptive-node.js`): a new `selectSpeculativeWriteGroup` re-verifies exact-path disjointness against every live writer via the SAME `--parallel-safe` predicate normal write co-open uses, excluding only the overlapping candidate(s) (`speculativeWriteExcluded: {reason:'overlaps_live_writer', nodeIds}` — disjoint siblings still open); survivors open WITH a provisioned leg unconditionally — even a lone candidate forms a size-1 lane group, reusing the existing group-of-one degeneration at close (no new merge code) — and count against the WRITE fan-out cap, never the read cap; a host with no leg capability (`KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0`) excludes ALL write candidates (`speculativeWriteExcluded: {reason:'no_leg_capability'}`), read speculation unaffected. **Close** is unchanged (the existing `gate_not_complete` fence + per-leg-barrier → group-barrier → merge path apply verbatim). **Discard is DISCARD-ONLY for a write member** — a deliberate asymmetry with the read half's KEEP-or-discard operator review, since a write built on a refuted premise is rework risk rather than reusable evidence: `discard-speculative` skips the now-inapplicable in-lane revert step, tears down the leg (worktree + branch + leg-base ref, via the existing `teardownLeg`), purges stale evidence, and clears the `lane_group` entry if this was its last live member (envelope gains `legTornDown`/`evidenceDiscarded`/`groupCleared`). **`reconcile-running-set` gains a crashed-speculative-write arm**: a crashed write member rolls FORWARD only on a CONFIRMED gate `verdict: pass` at reconcile time, else rolls BACK via the existing drop path (idempotent, folded into the pre-existing `rolledBack` array — no new response field) with the same evidence purge. Default `speculative_open_policy: off` remains byte-identical; the reserved `auto` policy value (a distinct, still fully-deferred automatic-activation axis) is untouched by this change. Cross-edition (#307): `kaola-workflow-next-action.js`, `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twins + both forge ports) regenerated, plus the `hasUnresolvableEntry` export from `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`; `scripts/test-next-action.js` grew 97 → 103 assertions and `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` grew 1248 → 1310 (eleven real-git integration cases covering the happy/close/pass/fail paths, the runtime overlap re-check, no-leg-capability exclusion, fan-out cap accounting, and three distinct crash-repair windows); both an adversarial-verifier gate and a code-reviewer gate passed with zero blocking findings; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-596-01.md`.

- **Write-frontier dispatch cards now carry each member's own leg path/branch — no more laneGroup cross-reference needed to route a co-opened write leg — #591.** Write-leg isolation is discipline-dependent: when a `parallel_safe` write frontier co-opens, `provisionLeg` creates a real per-member `.kw/legs/<project>/<node-id>` worktree (default-on), but `buildDispatch()` built each member's `dispatch` object with only the shared `working_dir` — the member's own leg path was recoverable only by cross-referencing the separate top-level `laneGroup` descriptor against member ids, so the single most safety-critical routing fact for a co-open write dispatch lived outside the per-member card the orchestrator is told to use. `buildDispatch()` now conditionally attaches `dispatch.leg_path` / `dispatch.leg_branch` — mirroring the existing `goal_line` conditional-attach pattern — ONLY when the caller supplies them; `runOpenReady()`'s `opened[]` map reads each member's provisioned leg (`legs[n.id]`) and threads it straight into that member's own `buildDispatch()` call. The serial/read path passes neither field, so its `dispatch` shape stays byte-identical to pre-#591 (a new raw-string byte-identity guard pins zero `leg_path`/`leg_branch` occurrences on that path). All six routing surfaces (the 3 Claude commands + the 3 Codex SKILL packs) and the `docs/plan-run-cards/frontier-batch.md` frontier card were reworded identically (byte-diff-confirmed across all six changed regions) to dispatch each leg from its own member's `dispatch.leg_path`/`dispatch.leg_branch` rather than requiring the laneGroup cross-reference; the `laneGroup`/`laneGroup.legs` descriptor is retained for group-level observability only, and no provenance was added to any of the six agent-facing prompt surfaces. RED-first (a `LEG-PROVISION-ON` extension asserting each opened member's `dispatch.leg_path`/`dispatch.leg_branch` equalled its provisioned leg failed with `undefined` pre-fix — 6 failing assertions). Cross-edition (#307): `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin byte-identical via `sync:editions`) + both forge ports regenerated; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially); `node scripts/test-route-reachability.js` green (185 assertions). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-591-01.md`.

- **Write co-open width/mix coverage extended past width-2 — and a real `max_concurrent` write-cap defect found and fixed along the way — #588.** The write co-open machinery (lane groups, legs, the commit-union synthesizer) is the most heavily tested subsystem in the repo, but every existing test exercised exactly two legs (`A`,`B`) — no ≥3-leg octopus merge, no write-frontier `FANOUT_CAP` interaction, no mixed read+write ready-frontier case, and the running-set scheduler path's task-mirror fail-open contract (already covered on the retired batch path) was untested. Four new real-git cases were added to `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`, each asserting durable artifacts (ledger rows, `running-set.json`, worktree/branch existence, `rev-list --parents` octopus counts, `diff-tree` vs the declared write union) rather than stdout sentinels: **(a)** a 3-leg disjoint co-open (A/B/C) — 3 legs provisioned, per-leg barriers (A,B deferred, C last), an octopus merge with EXACTLY 4 parents (feature + 3 legs), a passing union barrier, all 3 complete + legs torn down — pinned correct, no defect. **(b)** a 5-wide disjoint write antichain — co-opens exactly 4 (the write `FANOUT_CAP`) and queues the 5th, correctly — **but the running set recorded `max_concurrent` as the READ cap (8) instead of the WRITE cap (4)**, corrupting `reconcile-running-set`'s crash-resume roll-forward ceiling: a write group could be reconciled forward to more members than co-open ever opens. Fixed by hoisting the group's actual ceiling into a new `laneGroupCeiling` local (`resolveFanoutCap` folded with `--max`, already ≥2 whenever a group forms) and recording `maxConcurrent = groupForm ? laneGroupCeiling : <existing read-cap logic>` — read/serial paths are unchanged. Post-fix, pinned correct: `max_concurrent==4`; the legs manifest covers exactly the 4 opened members; reconciling a crashed mid-open (all 4 rows `opening:true`) rolls all 4 forward and preserves `max_concurrent==4`; after the width-4 group closes (a 5-parent octopus: feature + 4 legs) the queued 5th opens serially (a single write, no group, no leg). **(c)** a mixed frontier (2 read members + a disjoint write pair) — `open-ready` opens ONLY the reads, the writes stay pending, and a second call while the reads are live returns `reason:'write_awaits_drain'` — pinned as deliberate (not a defect): co-open-by-default is scoped to write‖write frontiers, and read‖write co-scheduling would be a new capability, out of scope here. **(d)** forced the durable task-mirror write to fail (`workflow-tasks.json` made a directory, `EISDIR`) on both `open-ready` and `close-node`: both still return `result:'ok'` with `taskMirror.status=='failed'` while the ledger/running-set/legs advance normally — the fail-open contract now holds on the running-set scheduler path too. Suite grew 1127 → 1219 assertions (+92 total: +79 #588, +13 #591, landed in the same commit). Cross-edition (#307): `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + codex twin byte-identical) + both forge ports regenerated (`kaola-workflow-next-action.js` confirmed unchanged — no #588-discovered fix needed there); `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` gained a backward-compatible `makeLaneRepo` `extraMembers` parameter (byte-identical when absent); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-588-01.md`.
