- **Freeze-time disjointness proof now catches three blind spots that let two parallel write legs freeze GREEN yet collide — #587.** The write-set disjointness proof (`classifier.disjointWriteSets`) and the plan-validator freeze-hygiene arms were literal-string + coarse-area based, leaving three cases where two `parallel_safe` legs proved "disjoint" at freeze but collided later (a mid-run typed refusal, a merge conflict, or — for the allowband case — silently both-applied at the synthesis merge with no barrier net at all). **(1) Barrier-invisible allowband.** `CHANGELOG.md` / `README.md` / `docs/**` are a deliberate barrier allowband, so two parallel legs could both edit them undetected by both the freeze proof and the runtime barrier. A new `parallel_allowband_collision` freeze refusal now fires when ≥2 legs of a parallel group — fan-out members (group arm) OR independent antichain siblings (antichain arm, which fires regardless of shared ancestor because the allowband is barrier-invisible) — each declare an allowband docs surface; allowband writes must be declared on exactly ONE leg of any parallel group. Serial-run barrier invisibility is intentionally unchanged. **(2) Cross-node case collision.** The cross-node area/path comparison in `disjointWriteSets` and the plan-validator antichain exact-clobber check now case-fold unconditionally (over-blocking on Linux is the safe direction per the existing hygiene philosophy; the primary dev box is macOS), so two parallel legs declaring `Src/x.js` vs `src/x.js` refuse at freeze instead of clobbering one physical file on a case-insensitive FS. `normalizeRepoPath` and the same-node `case_collision` guard are deliberately NOT globally folded (that would corrupt display/reasoning strings). **(3) Glob tokens.** A new `glob_in_path` freeze-hygiene refusal (`* ? [ ] { }`) matches the existing dir / `..` / trailing-slash / backslash refusals, so `**/*.md` refuses at freeze rather than surfacing late as a runtime `write_set_overflow`. Each new refusal carries a typed emit-envelope reason (no caller string-matching). RED-first tests plus false-positive CONTROLs (genuinely-disjoint parallel still freezes GREEN; allowband on exactly one leg allowed; even 0/2 still passes) in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`. Cross-edition (#307): plan-validator canonical + codex twin byte-identical and the two forge ports regenerated; classifier hand-ported to all four editions; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-587-01.md`.

### Removed

- **The dead `batch_active` mutual-exclusion guard is removed in full — nothing has produced `active-batch.json` since the parallel-batch retirement, and the guard is now provably unreachable — #594.** D-586-01 (the parallel-batch retirement) deliberately kept the `batch_active` probe/refusal in `adaptive-node.js`'s mutual-exclusion guard prologue as backward-compat crash detection, flagging its eventual removal as a future call "once its diagnostic value is judged to no longer outweigh its maintenance cost." This lands that call. A repo-wide grep confirmed **zero writers** of `active-batch.json` anywhere in `scripts/`/`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/` — every reference was either the mutual-exclusion guard itself or a historical design-lineage comment. Removed: `probeCoordination`'s `active-batch.json` read; `readCoordinationState`'s `batchOpening`/`batchActiveState`/`batchLive` fields and their use in `serialLive`/`collisions`; `coordinationRefusal`'s `batch_active` arm; the `'batch'` entry dropped from every `excl` array (`open-next`, `close-and-open-next`, `open-ready`); the `batch_active` `OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY` entry. The scope was **expanded to the sibling `active_batch_exists` plan-repair-reopen arm** (`reopen-node` / `repair-node`) — the same dead-manifest-guard family, verified equally unreachable — while the parallel **running-set** exclusion arm (`scheduler_active`) is LIVE and untouched. **Deliberately kept**: `orient`'s read-only `active-batch.json` legality reconstruction (`batch_topup_incomplete`, the AC#5 batch member-set arm) — contract-bearing, byte-identical for every producible state (the manifest is always `null`), and out of this removal's declared scope. No natural failing unit test exists for pure dead-code removal; the proof is grep-absence in live scheduler code (post-removal: `batch_active`/`active_batch_exists` appear only in explanatory comments) plus the full suite and walkthrough staying green — the ~5 fixtures that exercised the guard were converted to their surviving `scheduler_active` sibling or retired as unproducible. An opus adversarial-verifier gate independently confirmed the removal is behavior-neutral for every producible state (verdict: pass, 0 blocking findings; one non-blocking LOW noting the deliberately-kept `orient` boundary). Landed together with #593 in the same commit (both edit `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`). Cross-edition (#307): generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4 (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports) regenerated; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-594-01.md`.

- **The vestigial `parallel-batch` subsystem is retired — the running-set scheduler owns the frontier path — #586.** `scripts/kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports) and `scripts/test-parallel-batch.js` are deleted (7,183 lines), and every registry/wiring reference is scrubbed: `edition-sync.js` `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS` (12→10 forge ports), both `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` copies, `validate-script-sync.js` `COMMON_SCRIPTS` (25→24), all five contract validators (presence/pin/OPERATOR_HINT assertions), the `package.json` claude chain, and the two test-suite `require()` sites (`test-adaptive-node.js` S-293cc/S-CC blocks, `test-bundle-state.js`). **Rationale:** the subsystem was OFF the live executor path — nothing shelled it at runtime (its matches in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` are prose comments); the running-set scheduler (`open-ready` / `close-node` / `reconcile-running-set`) superseded it as the live frontier engine, including default-on disjoint write co-open, so **no runtime parallelism is affected** (parallel-batch's own write isolation had already been excised — it could only serial-degrade writes). Within the dead subsystem a 2026-07-02 audit found four defects that made fixing it pure maintenance burden with zero runtime consumers: the `seal` transition had no crash-safe `'sealing'`-class marker (a crash window produced a false `orphan_member_set_mismatch` whose recommended `reconcile` repair could not fix it); crash-retry double-applied the compliance row (no `complianceRowExists` guard, unlike the live close path); `reconcile --abort` was not crash-atomic (an interrupted abort resurrected the batch being aborted); and the operator card `docs/plan-run-cards/frontier-batch.md` documented a CLI that never existed (`--batch-id` / `--nodes` / `--evidence-file` / `--ledger` — the real `main()` accepted only `--project`/`--json`/`--node-id`/`--max`/`--abort`). **The card is REWRITTEN, not deleted:** "frontier unit" is a live concept machine-pinned on all six routing surfaces, so `frontier-batch.md` now documents the real running-set CLI — every subcommand/flag/reason-code verified against the actual arg parsing (anti-fabrication) — and the six routing surfaces' card description was updated consistently. The live scheduler files are untouched (comment-only design lineage and the vestigial `batch_active` guard deliberately remain). An opus review gate passed with zero findings at any severity. Cross-edition (#307): registries/edition trees touched → all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-586-01.md` (retirement, issue-authorized and user-ratified; mirrors the ADR-0008 excision precedent).

### Fixed

- **Codex dispatch: the runtime's local configuration is now a VERIFIED part of the install contract — an install can no longer say "ok" while the runtime model-refuses every subagent spawn — #598.** A full Codex-edition adaptive run completed with ZERO dispatches (every role, including the gate roles, ran inline with self-issued `verdict: pass`) because (RC1) the Codex runtime's `MultiAgentMode` is effort-gated (`model_reasoning_effort = "ultra"` → `proactive`; anything lower → `explicitRequestOnly`, whose injected developer message outranks SKILL prose and refuses spawns; no working config key for the mode itself — verified on codex-tui 0.142.5) and (RC2) the SKILL delegation probe only checked the project-local `.codex/agents/kaola-workflow/`, so a `--global` install at `~/.codex/agents/kaola-workflow/` recorded a false `local-fallback-tool-unavailable`. Fixes: a `deriveDispatchPosture` derivation (features absent/false → `none`; effort `ultra` → `proactive`; else `explicitRequestOnly`), authored once and mirrored byte-identically across the installer (×3) and preflight (×4) groups — the installer now REPORTS the effective dispatch posture + the exact remediation (set `model_reasoning_effort = "ultra"`; per-session `codex -c model_reasoning_effort=ultra`; or an explicit in-session "use subagents" request) before its final `status: ok`, and the preflight/doctor gains additive `dispatch_posture`/`model_reasoning_effort`/`multi_agent_enabled`/`dispatch_posture_warning` fields with an attestation-style NON-FATAL warn (never reds an otherwise-good install/preflight; version-guarded — the effort→mode coupling is 0.142.5 CLI behavior); the design boundary is REPORT, never silently write user-owned effort config (cost/latency is the user's call). The delegation probe in all six Codex next/adapt SKILLs now accepts BOTH the project-local and the global path. Gate-role degradation is now LOUD: all six plan-run routing surfaces carry a `## Gate-Role Degradation Notice` — when dispatch is unavailable, the executor posts a run-start notice naming the gate roles that would self-review and routes `adversarial-verifier`/`code-reviewer` through the consent-halt valve instead of silently recording a self-issued `verdict: pass` (an inline gate reviewing its own writer-context is no gate). Contract-validator needles (×3 editions, incl. the root Claude command) machine-guard both the global-path acceptance and the degradation notice so a partial propagation reds the chain; the `workflow-init` config-audit guidance (command+SKILL byte-pairs ×3) no longer reports "features enabled" alone as dispatch-ready. An opus adversarial gate drove a 69-cell posture truth table + a real installer/preflight A/B non-fatality battery — zero false-proactive cells, zero exit-code regressions. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-598-01.md`.

- **`selectSpeculativeWriteGroup` now fails CLOSED on an indeterminate `--parallel-safe` result — a validator subprocess failure can no longer let speculative write candidates open unchecked — #599.** The speculative-write open-time disjointness re-check looped over `ps.overlapping || []`, so a `--parallel-safe` result that was non-ok WITHOUT a well-formed `overlapping` array (validator subprocess crash, garbled JSON, or a structured refuse such as `node_not_found`) excluded nothing and every candidate proceeded to open — fail-OPEN, asymmetric with `tryFormLaneGroup` (the normal co-open callsite of the same predicate), which treats any non-ok result as conservative and degrades to a single serial write. Now `Array.isArray(ps.overlapping)` branches: a well-formed non-ok (even an empty array) keeps the existing per-pair exclusion; anything else excludes EVERY candidate — no speculative open. Found by the issue-596 review gate as a LOW with three redundant nets behind it (freeze-grammar disjointness, per-leg isolation, merge-conflict fail-closed); fixed so both callsites of the one predicate share one error posture. RED-first: `T599-1a` (subprocess crash `{exitCode:1}`) and `T599-1b` (garbled JSON `{exitCode:0}`) reproduced the fail-open on pre-fix code; `T599-1c` pins the untouched well-formed-non-ok posture (test-adaptive-node 1310→1314). This run also served as the first live end-to-end exercise of the #596 speculative-write machinery (consent-gated open → `gate_not_complete` fence → gate-pass leg merge), recorded in #597. Cross-edition (#307): generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-599-01.md`.

- **`acquireProjectLock` no longer orphans an empty lockfile on a payload-write failure — no more wrong-flavor `scheduler_locked` refusals — #595.** A `writeFileSync`/`fsyncSync` failure immediately after a successful `openSync(lockPath, 'wx')` left the just-created lockfile on disk EMPTY: the held-lock marker (`_heldSchedulerLock`) is set only after the write+fsync+close sequence succeeds, so neither the caller's `finally` release nor the module-level exit hook ever cleaned it up. A follow-up `acquireProjectLock` in the same process then hit `EEXIST` on the empty/corrupt payload and was refused `scheduler_locked` (fresh-mtime classification) — a wrong-flavor refusal that only cleared after the 24h `LANE_STALENESS_MS` mtime fallback flipped it stale, meaning a genuinely transient write failure could brick a project's scheduler for up to a day. The fix wraps the payload write in the existing outer `catch`: when `fd !== undefined` (we own the file THIS call just created via `'wx'` — `fd` is reset to `undefined` only after the full sequence succeeds), best-effort `closeSync` then best-effort `unlinkSync(lockPath)` before rethrowing, instead of just closing the fd and leaking the file. **No-takeover invariant preserved**: the unlink is reachable ONLY inside the branch gated on this call's own `fd`, never on the pre-existing `EEXIST` branch (a foreign holder's lock, guarded by `if (!(err && err.code === 'EEXIST')) throw err;` and never touching `fd`) — a foreign holder's lockfile can never be opened via our `'wx'` in the first place, so `fd` can never be set to it. RED-first: a new `T-595-orphan` fault-injection unit test monkey-patches `fs.writeFileSync` to throw once immediately after a real `openSync(lockPath, 'wx')` succeeds, and against the pre-fix code reproduced the bug exactly (the lockfile stayed orphaned; the follow-up acquire was refused `ok:false` instead of `ok:true`, crashing on `follow.release is not a function`). Post-fix: 0 failures, the lockfile is gone after the injected failure, and a follow-up acquire in the same process succeeds cleanly (4 new assertions); every pre-existing T-585 lock test (acquire/release, live-refuse, stale-refuse, the real-subprocess stale-race harness) stayed green with no regression, confirming the planted dead-pid holder's lockfile in those tests stays byte-untouched. Cross-edition (#307): the byte-identical-×4 `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` drift anchor (canonical + codex twin + both forge ports, confirmed byte-identical via `cmp`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-595-01.md`.

- **`sink-merge --sink` no longer skips closure for a no-primary bundle sink — a bundle invoked with only `--issue-numbers` now actually closes every member — #592.** A bundle sink invoked with ONLY `--issue-numbers A,B` (no primary `--issue`) tripped the closure step's gate (`args.issue != null`) false, so the entire close loop was skipped — yet execution still fell through to `stepDone('closure')`, and the sink-receipt/JSON output reported `closure: done` / `status: sinked` having closed zero issues. Because `resume` treats a `done` step as already satisfied, the miss was permanent: re-running `--sink` never retried closure. Observed live on bundle-587-589. The gate is now `!OFFLINE && (args.issue != null || args.issueNumbers.length > 0)`; the primary close still runs only `if (args.issue != null)`; the bundle-member loop bound widened from `> 1` to `> (args.issue != null ? 1 : 0)` so every member closes when there is no primary. `receipt.closed_issues` (sorted) is now recorded on the SUCCESS path too (previously written only alongside a `partial` failure), so a resume can verify what already closed rather than silently skip the step. Single-issue and bundle-with-primary invocations are behaviorally unchanged; the `sink_incomplete`/`step: closure` fail-closed refuse is unchanged. Fixed across all four sink-merge editions (canonical `scripts/kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js`, the byte-identical codex twin, and both `plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/scripts/` forge ports). RED-first coverage in `scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js` (now 118 tests), `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js`, and `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js`. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-592-01.md`.

- **Adversarial-verifier majority-refute now breaks even-count ties toward refuted — #589.** `verifyVerdictBlock` computed `majorityRefute = refutes * 2 > verdicts.length`, so a 2-instance fan-out with verdicts `[refuted, pass]` gave `2 > 2` = false and PASSED, outvoting the lone genuine refuter — in direct tension with the subsystem's own "refuted-if-uncertain" burden inversion (a 1-1 split IS the uncertain case). The comparison is now `refutes * 2 >= verdicts.length` (a strict majority is required to PASS). Odd-width behavior is unchanged (2/3 refute still refutes; 1/3 still passes; 0/2 still passes); only the even 1/2 tie flips to refute. The existing typed reason (`fanout majority-refute`) is unchanged, so structural callers need no update. RED-first (`[refuted, pass]` now refutes) plus a CONTROL (`[pass, pass]` still passes). Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-589-01.md`.

- **Scheduler mutual exclusion is now enforced by an O_EXCL project lock — concurrent invocations of the running-set scheduler can no longer lose updates — #585.** The pre-#585 coordination guard (`probeCoordination`/`coordinationRefusal`) was **advisory-only**: a pure read of state files followed by an in-memory refusal decision, no lockfile/flock/O_EXCL/PID check anywhere in the mutation path, and `writeFileAtomicReplace` is atomic per file (fsync+rename) but performs a blind `renameSync` with no compare-and-swap against the on-disk version. Two concrete lost-update races followed directly: **concurrent `open-ready`×2** both read `running-set.json` as null and both opened the same frontier (double-dispatch); **concurrent `close-node`×2** each read the whole plan, spliced a different completed member's row, then rewrote the entire file — the second rename clobbered the first member's `complete` flip back to `in_progress`. A genuine two-process interleave harness (`raceN`: a `node -e` orchestrator `Promise.all`-spawning real CLI subprocesses on one project) reproduced both deterministically pre-fix (5/5 trials each). The fix adds a project-scoped **O_EXCL scheduler lock** (`kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/scheduler.lock`), acquired at the `adaptive-node.js` `main()` CLI boundary for exactly the mutating (`SPLIT_GUARDED_SUBCOMMANDS`) subcommand set — before the layered guard prologue and the entire dispatch body — and released in a `finally` (belt-and-suspenders: a module-level `process.on('exit')` hook also drops a crash-orphaned lock). Read-only subcommands (`orient`, `mirror-project`, `record-evidence --verify`) stay lock-free. **The first implementation shipped auto-takeover for a dead/crashed holder and was refuted by the in-run adversarial gate**: the takeover tail (re-read-confirm-stale → unlink → retry `wx` once) executes a stale VERDICT made *before* the unlink, so two concurrent takers holding the same stale verdict both unlink-and-reclaim — empirically 6/500 double-acquisitions at 2 concurrent takers and 111/500 at 6, on the real function. The architect repaired this by REMOVING auto-takeover entirely (the issue's acceptance criteria explicitly permit "stale takeover OR typed operator hint," so the fail-closed disjunct was available): `acquireProjectLock` now only CLASSIFIES a held lock's holder (`isStaleLock` — dead same-host PID via `process.kill(pid,0)` ESRCH, or an old/corrupt cross-host payload via `LANE_STALENESS_MS`) and NEVER unlinks another process's lock; contention refuses with a typed `scheduler_locked` (live holder) or `scheduler_lock_stale` (dead/aged holder) reason, the latter's `operator_hint` naming the exact holder (subcommand/pid/host/since) and the one-session manual recovery (`rm "<lockPath>"`, then re-run). Re-verification after the repair re-attacked the SAME harness that broke round 1 — 0/500 double-acquisitions at N=2 and N=6 (was 6/500 and 111/500) — and confirmed structurally that the only lock-creation path is `openSync('wx')` and the only unlink sites are a holder's own release/exit-hook, with no code path from a stale verdict to an unlink. Residual accepted risks (non-blocking): PID reuse yields a wrong-flavor refusal (`scheduler_locked` instead of `scheduler_lock_stale`) until the recycled-pid process exits; a create-then-write-failure orphan gets a wrong-flavor refusal for up to `LANE_STALENESS_MS` before the mtime fallback flips it stale; all recover via the same manual `rm`. Serial single-orchestrator behavior is byte-unchanged (zero contention ⇒ clean acquire every time; all 1078 pre-existing assertions + the walkthrough stay green). Suite grew to 1127 assertions (RED-first: the two races reproduced deterministically pre-fix; the repair added a dedicated `T-585-stale-race` proof asserting ZERO acquisitions across 6 concurrent callers against a stale lock in every trial). Cross-edition (#307): schema (byte-identical ×4) + generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-585-01.md`.

- **Serial `open-next` now records the per-node barrier baseline BEFORE flipping the ledger row to `in_progress` — mirrors the running-set scheduler's crash-safe ordering — #590.** `runOpenNext` previously wrote the plan (ledger row → `in_progress`) and only then shelled `commit-node --start` to record the baseline; a crash in that window left a node `in_progress` with NO baseline on disk, and because `reconcile-running-set` does not cover the serial path (there is no running set to reconcile), a later close dead-ended with the validator's `no_barrier_base` refusal. `runOpenReady` already did it in the safe direction (baseline on disk in Phase 2, before the single plan write in Phase 3) — the serial path predated that ordering and was never retrofitted. The fix moves the `commit-node --start` shell (+ its `baselineOk` check) above the `spliceLedgerNode` in_progress flip: a baseline failure now returns `baseline_failed` with the ledger row still PENDING (a clean, idempotent re-open — re-invoking `open-next` re-records the baseline), and an orphaned baseline on a later flip failure is harmless (`recordBase` is idempotent/overwriting on the next open) — never again a ledger row stranded `in_progress` with no baseline. A new `no_barrier_base` entry was also added to `adaptive-node.js`'s `OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY` (mirroring the pre-existing plan-validator hint of the same name), naming the idempotent `open-next` re-invoke as the repair for the close-time dead-end. RED-first: direct-call tests over `runOpenNext`'s injected seams asserted baseline-before-flip on both the failure and success paths and failed against the pre-change write-first code (the row was left `in_progress` with no baseline on a simulated failure); now green. Serial-path behavior is otherwise byte-identical — the reorder touches only the sequencing of two existing writes, no new state or schema. Cross-edition (#307): generated `adaptive-node.js` ×4; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-590-01.md`.

### Changed

- **`speculative_open_policy` gains the reserved `auto` tier and the freeze-time default flips `off` → `auto` — speculation (read AND leg-contained write) is now auto-granted under the structural net, with no per-run consent ceremony — #597.** The `auto` tier joins the legal set (`['off','consent','auto']`; `SPECULATIVE_OPEN_POLICY_REFUSED_AT_FREEZE` is now empty; unknown values still refuse `speculative_policy_unsupported` at freeze). At `auto`, `open-ready` acts on the `speculativePending` set with NO `--speculative-consent` (the flag is accepted as a no-op there; at `consent` it is still required — that tier's behavior is unchanged; `off` stays inert and byte-identical). Every #596 safety condition holds identically at `auto` (exact-path disjointness vs live writers, PROTECTED, sink, resolvability, leg capability, caps, the `gate_not_complete` close fence, discard-only writes) — `auto` removes the CEREMONY, never a safety condition, and an opus adversarial gate proved byte-equal refusal parity at `auto` vs `consent` across every condition. **Resume safety:** `parseSpeculativePolicy` keeps returning `off` for an ABSENT `## Meta` field — the absence fallback is decoupled from the default — so plans frozen before this change resume with their frozen posture, never retroactively `auto`; instead, the freeze transaction now MATERIALIZES an explicit `speculative_open_policy: auto` line into `## Meta` when the author omits it (hash-covered, after the refuse-gates, with the governance-ack recomputed on the materialized content — frozen plans are self-describing). Speculative discards now write telemetry (node id / role / gate via the provenance log) so the economics of `auto` are observable per run, and an indeterminate `--parallel-safe` result at speculative open-time is labeled `parallel_safe_indeterminate` instead of the misleading `overlaps_live_writer` (the fail-closed exclusion behavior is unchanged). This deliberately SUPERSEDES the D-419-02 invariant that per-run speculation consent is captured by the user and never auto-granted — ceremony only; every mechanical invariant is untouched (operator-directed 2026-07-02) — the third rung of the same precedent ladder as the disjoint-write co-open default-ON flip and the shared-infra consent retirement. Precondition satisfied and recorded in the issue before starting: #596 was exercised live end-to-end in project `issue-599` (speculative leg open → `gate_not_complete` fence → gate-pass merge). The SIX plan-run routing surfaces + the speculative-open card describe the new posture (serial waiting on a gate is now the degraded mode) and the card gains two operational-gotcha recipes from the live exercise (`serial_node_live` on a fused-serial-opened gate; `leg_base_unreachable` on parent commits landing while a leg is open); the planner's write-speculation authoring rubric (+ its three parity-locked `.toml` twins, `DISCARD-ONLY` parity token added) now encourages topology that exposes speculation. Cross-edition (#307): schema byte-identical ×4 + four GENERATED_AGGREGATORS (validator, handoff, next-action, adaptive-node) ×4; `test-adaptive-handoff.js` 107→116, `test-next-action.js` 103→113, `test-adaptive-node.js` 1314→1330, `test-agent-profile-parity.js` 24→27; all six walkthroughs green; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-597-01.md`.

- **Write co-open eligibility now relaxes the `coarse` class BY DEFAULT — two exact-file-disjoint cross-edition antichains sharing a top-level area (e.g. both under `plugins/`) now co-open instead of always serial-degrading — #593.** The `--parallel-safe` disjointness proof classified a write pair sharing a non-`SHARED_INFRA` top-level area as `coarse`, and the `writeOverlapRelaxable` predicate only relaxed that class when the operator opted in with BOTH `write_overlap_policy ∈ {disjoint, coarse}` AND per-run `--write-overlap-consent` — so an authored-parallel frontier whose legs happened to share an area (the common case for this repo's own cross-edition ports, which all live under `plugins/`) always serial-degraded unless an operator manually consented every time. `coarse` now relaxes BY DEFAULT under the SAME retained net that already governs the sibling `shared-infra` class (D-546-G2): **NET-1** a post-dominating `code-reviewer`/`synthesizer` gate covers the relaxed legs, and **NET-2** neither set contains a PROTECTED concrete file. A new `hasUnresolvableEntry` guard is the one addition: if either set carries a directory-shaped (trailing `/`) or glob (`* ? [ ] { }`) token, exact-path disjointness is unprovable — those tokens could physically overlap despite comparing string-distinct — so that pair keeps today's coarse-area refusal. `exact` (the same path, or a case-collision) still NEVER relaxes at this seam, at every tier, unconditionally. `write_overlap_policy` and `--write-overlap-consent` remain PARSED for frozen-plan back-compat but are now VESTIGIAL at this seam — they neither enable nor block the coarse relaxation (an explicit joint invariant test pins that `policy:off` + no consent relaxes byte-identically to `policy:coarse` + consent). Classifier verdict purity is preserved: `classifier.disjointWriteSets` is byte-unchanged (still emits `red`/`kind:'coarse'`) — the downgrade lives ONLY at the `writeOverlapRelaxable` callsite, so every verdict-only reader (`scanClaimedOverlap`, the G-SEL-4 select-arm check, the freeze-time concurrent-sibling antichain loop) is unaffected and the walkthrough's freeze-time coarse "must ask" behavior stays green unchanged. RED-first real-git coverage in `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: the AC1 happy path (coarse exact-disjoint co-open → legs → per-leg barriers → octopus merge → union barrier, no consent) failed pre-fix (`overlapping_write_sets`, serial-degrade) and passes post-fix; further cases pin NET-1/NET-2 enforcement (no override by policy/consent), `exact`/case-collision never relaxing, the directory/glob resolvability fallback, and the `KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0` serial byte-identity floor. `scripts/test-commit-node.js`'s two pre-existing `T463-FLOOR` tests (which pinned the OLD consent-gated coarse behavior) were re-pinned to assert the new default-relax floor (`ok`/`relaxed[kind:'coarse']`, not a vacuous pass) — their sibling floors (gate-absent, docs-only-gate, `exact`, `PROTECTED`) are unchanged and still refuse. Landed together with #594 in the same commit (both edit `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`; `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` carries the `writeOverlapRelaxable`/`hasUnresolvableEntry` change itself). Cross-edition (#307): generated `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` and `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (canonical + codex twins + both forge ports) regenerated via `edition-sync.js --write`; `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` grew 1223 → 1248 assertions (covers both #594's fixture updates and #593's new coverage); `scripts/test-commit-node.js` (123 assertions) and `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` green; an opus adversarial-verifier gate independently attacked NET-1/NET-2 enforcement, the resolvability fallback, and verdict purity — all WITHSTOOD (verdict: pass, 0 blocking findings). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-593-01.md`.

## [6.17.0] - 2026-06-30

### Added

- **Codex tiered dispatch now fails closed unless child-session metadata proves the requested effort — #582.** The adaptive schema's Codex `dispatchEffort()` still maps `opus → codex_reasoning_effort:"xhigh"` and `sonnet → codex_reasoning_effort:"high"`; only absent/blank model tiers resolve to `null`/`role_default` and intentionally inherit the base profile/session default. Post-merge local validation showed V2 `reasoning_effort:"high"` children still recorded `turn_context.effort:"xhigh"` on this machine, so plan-run command and Codex SKILL surfaces across all six routing copies now require a fresh child-session JSONL proof for the exact requested effort before either V2 or V1 tiered dispatch. Missing, stale, or failing proof refuses before dispatch with `codex_effort_override_unavailable` instead of silently inheriting. Regression coverage pins the descriptor mapping and the six-surface proof contract; API docs and decision record `docs/decisions/D-582-02.md` document the fail-closed rule and the local refutation.

- **Codex dispatch now carries stable task-name identity, direct per-spawn effort, and complete standalone role metadata — #581.** Standalone Codex role TOMLs now mirror the managed config block's `description` and `nickname_candidates` metadata, and installer/preflight schema checks derive those fields from `config/agents.toml` so stale or partial profiles are rejected instead of silently installed. Adaptive dispatch descriptors now include `codex_dispatch_mode` and `codex_task_name`: v1 remains the stable default and is documented as a thread-id fallback, while v2 task-name mode uses a sanitized node-id-plus-role task name when explicitly enabled. Planner-selected Opus nodes now carry direct per-spawn `reasoning_effort: "xhigh"` guidance instead of the retired parent-session workaround; plan-run command/skill surfaces, README, validators, and focused tests were updated together. Cross-edition (#307): generated/byte-identical surfaces are in sync and the full four-chain gate is required before release. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-581-01.md`.

- **Forge `active-folders` ports now share a machine-enforced state-field parity contract — the next #579-class silent forge-port miss is caught by a test, not luck — #580.** #579 added three shared engine `workflow-state.md` fields (`main_root`/`session_marker`/`claim_ts`) and had to hand-align all four edition `active-folders` ports to parse them; nothing pinned that alignment, so the *next* shared field added to canonical could silently miss a forge port again — the four `npm test` chains assert claim-record **shape**, not an end-to-end field round-trip, so they would not catch it. This lands the missing contract. **(A) Single source of truth.** A frozen `SHARED_STATE_FIELDS` constant (the 13 shared engine fields — `issue_number, phase, issue_numbers, status, bundle_id, closure_policy, next_command, branch, worktree_path, sink, main_root, session_marker, claim_ts`) is added and exported in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`, the forge-neutral 4-tree drift anchor already pinned byte-identical by `validate-script-sync.js`; because the four schema copies are byte-identical, every edition sees the same list and a future shared field is declared in ONE byte-mirrored place. **(B) Behavior parity gate.** New root-only `scripts/test-active-folders-field-parity.js`: for EACH of the four editions it `require`s that edition's `active-folders` module, builds a temp `workflow-state.md` populating every `SHARED_STATE_FIELDS` key with a distinct, type-appropriate sentinel, calls the cross-edition-uniform hermetic entry `readActiveFolders(root, { excludeClosedIssues: false })` (NOT the forge-only `parseStateFile`), and asserts every shared field is surfaced. The gate is **non-vacuous**: sentinels are chosen to differ from each field's parser default (`sink` default `merge`, `status` default `unknown`, numeric fields positive ints, `issue_numbers` a multi-element int array) so a *missing* field is distinguishable from a *surfaced* one — deleting any shared field from a port reds the gate, and appending a new key to `SHARED_STATE_FIELDS` reds any edition not yet parsing it. **(C) Wired into all four chains** (`test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` each append `&& node scripts/test-active-folders-field-parity.js`), so a missing field reds the #307 cross-edition obligation regardless of which chain runs. **No port edits:** an authoring-time audit confirmed all 13 shared fields are already parsed in every edition (#579 aligned the last three), so the four `active-folders` ports are deliberately untouched and the gate keys only on the strict shared intersection — per-edition fields (gitlab `mr_*`/`project_id`, gitea `full_name`/`pr_*`) are deliberately NOT pinned. RED-first (the gate was authored before the constant existed → failed to resolve `SHARED_STATE_FIELDS`; adding the constant turned it green at 61 assertions) with an explicit regression-fire proof; an opus adversarial gate independently confirmed non-vacuity (per-field sentinel-vs-default separation, zero vacuous assertions), byte-identity ×4, and the four-chain wiring. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-580-01.md`.

- **Concurrent same-repo sessions are hardened: co-tenant clean-check, a single main-root authority, and live-vs-stale lane classification — #579.** Two Kaola-Workflow sessions sharing **one repo checkout** collided before the git merge (which already worked) — the workflow treated the *other* lane's bookkeeping as a blocking mess and lost track of which checkout was "home base." All the load-bearing mechanics live in the **shared `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS`** that every edition byte-mirrors, so the fix is engine-level (Claude, Codex, GitLab, Gitea; reused by opencode), with no per-edition special-casing. **Three moves + a lane classifier.** **Move 1 — co-tenant clean-check.** The "is the worktree clean?" gate (claim-time `treeDirty` in `claim.js`, and `assertCleanWorktree`/`assertWorktreeClean` in `sink-merge.js`) now DISREGARDS `kaola-workflow/*` and `.kw/*` paths this run does NOT own (another lane's scratch space) while STILL failing on real uncommitted code — a second lane can start and finalize next to a first lane without the spurious "dirty main" refusal. The run's OWN `<project>` folders are never exempted, shared durable state (`.roadmap/`, `ROADMAP.md`, `config.json`, `archive/`) stays strict, and the fail-closed posture is preserved (an unverifiable `git status` is still treated as dirty). Implemented as `PARKED_LANE_PREFIXES` + `parsePorcelainPaths` + `isParkedLanePath` in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`, applied as a *secondary* filter that only ever narrows which known-clean-of-real-code states pass. **Move 2 — single main-root authority.** The "where is main?" resolver (`getCoordRoot`/`mainRootFromCoord`), previously re-implemented once in `claim.js` and again in `adaptive-node.js` (and a third `mainRootFromCoord` in `sink-merge.js`), is collapsed into ONE shared helper (`resolveMainRoot`) in the byte-identical 4-tree anchor `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`; the claim RECORDS the resolved root as a new `main_root` field in `workflow-state.md` so the executor reads it back instead of re-deriving and possibly diverging when launched from a linked/detached worktree (the authority-split that jammed a real two-session run is eliminated, with a `getMainRoot` fallback for pre-#579 state). **Move 3 — merge protocol UNCHANGED.** `ffMergeLoop` is byte-identical: first finisher merges, a later finisher rebases-and-retries, a TRUE content conflict halts-and-asks a human (never auto-resolved); a lane cleans up its own branch + worktree + `kaola-workflow/<project>/` folder only AFTER its own merge lands. **Lane classifier.** A four-bucket `classifyLane(lane, ctx)` (`mine` / `live` / `stale` / `ambiguous`) in `kaola-workflow-classifier.js` with a PER-LANE precedence ladder: an explicit per-issue resume instruction beats a blanket co-tenant prompt signal beats a liveness heuristic beats ask. The liveness signal is a **minimal seatbelt** — `session_marker` + `claim_ts` stamped into the claim record at claim-time ONLY (no periodic refresh, no tuning machinery), with a single conservative `LANE_STALENESS_MS = 86_400_000` (24h) constant: a non-owned lane with no explicit-resume and no co-tenant prompt signal classifies a *fresh* marker → `ambiguous` (ask, don't stomp a likely-live lane) and an *old/absent* marker → `stale` (resumable leftover). The three fields are stamped in `writeState`, surfaced by `kaola-workflow-active-folders.js` (`parseStateFile`/`readActiveFolders`) in all four editions, and consumed in `claim.js` `cmdStatus` (annotates each active folder with `lane_bucket`) and `cmdResume` (auto-selects the run's own lane instead of refusing `resume_ambiguous`). Forge-neutral co-tenant guidance was added to the `issue-scout` agent (disjoint-issue selection: a `lane_bucket: live` lane's issue is treated as occupied) and the adapt/finalize command + skill surfaces, provenance-free. Authored and verified via the adaptive path; two gate-caught implementation defects were repaired and re-verified before merge (an own-session classify path that was fed the wrong `ctx` shape, and the two forge `active-folders` ports that initially did not parse the new fields back) — both now covered by end-to-end integration assertions in the canonical + forge test suites so neither can silently recur. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (incl. the new two-lanes-in-one-checkout simulation) green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-579-01.md`.

### Fixed

- **Codex preflight now recognizes current Multi-Agent V2 config syntax and the install guide audits effort-safe dispatch without silently rewriting global Codex config — #584.** The byte-identical `kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js` copies now parse V2 readiness only from the `[features]` / `[features.multi_agent_v2]` config scope, accepting the legacy boolean flag, current inline object form, and table form while failing closed for missing, false, malformed, duplicate, or top-level values; `[notice].suppress_unstable_features_warning` remains warning posture only. The Codex init skills/commands and README now direct agents to run a read-only config audit, distinguish feature state, profile/cache freshness, warning suppression, and agent limits, and apply a minimal `~/.codex/config.toml` delta only with explicit user authorization. Regression coverage spans the root install-rendering test plus GitHub/GitLab/Gitea Codex preflight fixtures; live doctor verification on the current machine reports `dispatch_mode: "v2-task-name"` and `multi_agent_v2_enabled: true` for object-form config. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-584-01.md`.

## [6.16.0] - 2026-06-30

### Added

- **`sync-opencode-edition.js --check` now enforces that `templates/opencode/plugins/*.js` equals the `PLUGIN_SCRIPTS` allowlist — the installer glob and the sync allowlist can no longer silently drift on a future second plugin — #578.** After #577 gave the opencode hook-adapter plugin a tracked canonical source under `templates/opencode/plugins/`, two consumers of that directory disagreed on which files count as plugins: `install-opencode.sh` deploys `templates/opencode/plugins/*.js` via a **glob** (every `.js`), while `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js` regenerates `.opencode/plugins/` from an **explicit allowlist** (`PLUGIN_SCRIPTS`), copying and asserting parity only for allowlisted files. Today only one plugin exists, so glob and allowlist resolve to the same single file and agree — there was **no live defect** (hardening / future-proofing only). The latent gap: a second `.js` added under `templates/opencode/plugins/` **without** registering it in `PLUGIN_SCRIPTS` would be deployed by the installer glob yet stay invisible to `sync --write`/`--check` — re-opening the "unmanaged plugin" class #577 closed, for the second file. The fix adds a **set-equality guard** in `runCheck()`: after the existing per-script parity loop, it asserts the set of `*.js` files in `templates/opencode/plugins/` (`CANON_PLUGINS_DIR`) equals `PLUGIN_SCRIPTS`. The new direction is unregistered-on-disk (the complement of the existing registered-but-missing loop) — any on-disk `.js` absent from the allowlist pushes a mismatch through the same `mismatches` channel, so `--check` exits non-zero with `unregistered plugin '<file>' present in templates/opencode/plugins/ but absent from PLUGIN_SCRIPTS — add it to the allowlist`. Once `--check` enforces equality the templates dir can never hold an unregistered `.js`, so the installer glob and the allowlist are provably equal and `install-opencode.sh` is deliberately left untouched (the issue's chosen option over coupling the installer to the allowlist). A RED-first `A11-allowlist` assertion in `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` proves the guard fires: it asserts clean-state set-equality, then drops a transient unregistered `.js` into the templates dir, runs `sync --check`, asserts a non-zero exit naming the file and `PLUGIN_SCRIPTS`, and removes the probe in a `finally` block (tree restored even if an assertion throws). opencode is an additive edition (D-530-02): this diff touches no `npm test` / plugin-edition / forge-validator file, so it carries **no #307 four-chain obligation** — its gate is `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (499 assertions, exit 0; an opus accuracy gate independently confirmed the guard fires and stays green from tracked sources in a clean worktree). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-578-01.md`.

### Fixed

- **opencode hooks plugin now has a tracked canonical source — fresh-clone install + clean-worktree tests no longer silently fail — #577.** The opencode-edition hook adapter `.opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js` had **no tracked source anywhere** and was **not generated** by `sync-opencode-edition.js` — it existed only as an untracked, gitignored artifact in an already-installed tree, so a clean checkout / fresh git worktree could not reproduce it. Two verified consequences: **(1) install bug** — `install-opencode.sh` deployed the plugin by copying from the repo's OWN `.opencode/plugins/*.js` with a silent `2>/dev/null || true`, a self-referential copy that no-ops on a fresh clone (yielding an opencode install with no pre-commit / write-lane / dispatch-log / compaction hooks); **(2) test bug** — `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (A11/P1/G1/H1) failed in any clean checkout / fresh worktree (the suite reads `REPO/.opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js` directly and installs it into temp dirs), passing only on an already-installed repo and masking the gap. The fix tracks a canonical source OUTSIDE the gitignored `.opencode/` tree and threads it through sync + install + test: **(A)** new tracked `templates/opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js` (byte-identical to the live adapter); **(B)** `sync-opencode-edition.js` gains a `writePlugin()` step (mirrors the existing `writeHooks()` byte-copy) that copies `templates/opencode/plugins/*.js` → `.opencode/plugins/` on `--write` and asserts parity on `--check` (a missing/drifted regenerated plugin is a parity failure); **(C)** `install-opencode.sh` now deploys from the tracked `templates/opencode/plugins/` (not the self-referential `.opencode/plugins/`) and **drops the silent `2>/dev/null || true`** so a missing plugin is a loud install error; **(D)** `test-opencode-edition.js` self-provisions via `sync --write` before its assertions and adds an `A11-canon` assertion (the tracked source exists and the regenerated artifact is byte-identical to it) so the gap cannot silently reopen. Verified by running the opencode suite inside a fresh git worktree — the bug's exact failing environment — and watching it go green from tracked sources with no manual `.opencode/` seeding (496 assertions, exit 0). opencode is an additive edition (D-530-02): this diff touches no `npm test` / plugin-edition / forge-validator file, so it carries **no #307 four-chain obligation** — its gate is `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-577-01.md`.

## [6.15.0] - 2026-06-29

### Added

- **The "no provenance in agent-facing prompts" convention is now machine-enforced — #576 (follow-up to #575).** #575 stripped design-rationale provenance from the prompt surfaces but left the rule **prose-only** (a `CLAUDE.md` rule + a `docs/conventions.md` section) and explicitly deferred the lint/contract guard. This lands that guard plus the last stragglers the manual #575 sweep missed. **(A) Guard.** A `PROVENANCE_BAN` check is added to all five contract validators and the additive opencode suite, each scanning ITS OWN edition's agent-facing prompt surfaces with one shared banlist regex `/#\d{1,4}|D-\d{3}-\d{2}|\bINV-\d+|ADR[ -]\d{2,4}|\b(?:PR|MR|AC)#\d+/` — banning issue refs (`#NNNN`), decision IDs (`D-NNN-NN`), bare invariant tags (`INV-NN`, the #575 miss), `ADR `-space/`ADR-`dash citations (the other #575 miss), and forge-request refs (`PR#/MR#/AC#`); the failure message names `file:line` + the offending token and points at `docs/conventions.md`. Per-edition placement: `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` (claude, `agents/*.md` + `commands/*.md`) and its byte-identical codex copy; `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` (codex `agents/*.toml` + `skills/*/SKILL.md`, mirroring the existing `PHASE_NUMBER_BAN` loop); the gitlab + gitea validators (their `agents/*.toml` + `commands/*.md` + `skills/*/SKILL.md`); and `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (the regenerated `.opencode/agent` + `.opencode/command` mirrors) with 17 new positive/negative assertions. **Allowed (verified not flagged):** numeric placeholders (`#N`/`#<issue>`/`#<n>`), runtime target-issue vars (`KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUE=N`, `--target-issue <N>`, `Closes #<issue>`), and grey-zone audit/gate short-labels (`G1`/`G3`/`H5`/`AC7`/`M4`). **(B) Stragglers stripped:** bare `INV-17` → `validator-derived` in `agents/workflow-planner.md` + the three byte-identical `workflow-planner.toml`; example issue numbers `#42`/`#47`/`#53` → `#N` placeholders in `commands/workflow-next.md` (+ both forge ports) and the three `kaola-workflow-next/SKILL.md`; `# Workflow Plan — issue #142` → `#<N>` in the three `kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md`. After the strip the guard's live scan matches ZERO across every prompt surface. Completeness + meaning-preservation (incl. the `INV-17` reword and the agent-profile-parity FEATURE_TOKENs) were independently re-derived by an opus adversarial-verifier grep-oracle (0 banlist hits even under broader-than-committed patterns; no validator left a surface unscanned). Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` + route-reachability green; opencode is additive (D-530-02) and its suite is green (494 assertions). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-576-01.md` (supersedes the "machine enforcement is deferred" note in D-575-01).

## [6.14.0] - 2026-06-29

### Fixed

- **docs/terminology: swept three out-of-template stale "phase" mentions left after #572 — #573.** #572 re-grounded the injected `KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE` block on the adaptive DAG-of-roles model but deliberately scoped itself to that injected region; three residual stale mentions living **outside** the template (not reached by the `extractClaudeTemplate` phase-ban, and violating no #572 AC) are now corrected: **(NB1)** the `workflow-init` Active-Folder-Init fallback narration "proceed with local **phase artifacts**" → "local **workflow artifacts**" (3 `commands/workflow-init.md` surfaces — claude/gitlab/gitea); **(NB2)** the byte-parity-locked AGENTS.md redirect-block category label "*All other guidance — **workflow phases**, scripts, conventions, gotchas …*" → "**the workflow**, scripts, conventions, gotchas" across all 6 init surfaces (3 `commands/workflow-init.md` + 3 init `SKILL.md`) plus the repo's own dogfooded `AGENTS.md` — every surface changed identically so the redirect-block byte-identity assertion in `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` stays green; **(NB3)** `docs/architecture.md` "**full** — … ; **the default** and the answer to every doubt" → "an install-time opt-in (`--with-full`), **not the default**", correcting framing left stale by #538 (adaptive is the unconditional default; the accurate "6-phase ladder" description is retained); and **(NB4)** the related `README.md` SemVer-policy line "`MINOR`: backward-compatible **workflow phases**, agent roles, …" → "**workflow capabilities**, agent roles, …" (a durable category noun — "agent roles" already follows, so reusing "roles" would be redundant). Terminology-only; no script-logic, schema, or contract change. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially).

### Changed

- **Design-rationale provenance stripped from agent-facing prompts — #575.** The agent-facing prompt surfaces — role agent definitions (`agents/*.md` + the three editions' `agents/*.toml`), the workflow commands (`commands/*.md` + the two forge ports), and the skill packs (`*/skills/*/SKILL.md`) — had accumulated **design-rationale provenance**: hardcoded GitHub issue refs (`#NNN`, 636 occurrences), decision-record IDs (`D-NNN-NN`, 22), invariant tags (`[INV-NN]`, 4), ADR citations (`(ADR 0004)`), and ~18 whole historical defect/pattern *clauses* (e.g. `(the #291 defect pattern)`, `(the #254 router-rewrite parity defect)`). These cite *why a rule was introduced* — useful as maintainer history, but pure noise to the agent at dispatch time, burning context/tokens on every spawn for zero operational value. All of it is removed from the prompt surfaces across all four editions, **with no rule meaning changed** — every edit excises only the provenance, leaving the imperative rule intact; the opencode edition is regenerated from canonical. **Kept** (verified not provenance): runtime target-issue variable forms (`KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUE=N`, "issue N", `Closes #<issue>`), illustrative user-command examples, the `fast`/`full` six-phase path names and `Phase N` vocabulary, and all functional tokens. A new **`CLAUDE.md` Non-Negotiable Rule** ("Keep provenance out of agent-facing prompts") plus a `docs/conventions.md` section codify the convention so it does not creep back; a durable lint/contract guard is deferred to a focused follow-up. The claude-edition contract validator (`scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` + its codex byte-mirror) had its sole provenance-bearing needle re-pinned to the rule (`Question-shaped & bug-shaped issues`) rather than `(#486)`. Meaning-preservation + completeness were verified by an independent opus adversarial-verifier gate (every diff hunk read; residual-provenance grep-oracle = zero modulo the allowed forms; editions byte-symmetric). Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` green; opencode is additive (D-530-02) and its suite is green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-575-01.md`.

## [6.13.0] - 2026-06-27

### Fixed

- **`workflow-init` no longer injects stale 6-phase vocabulary into a consumer's `CLAUDE.md`; parity is now fully enforced — #572.** The injected `## Kaola-Workflow` block (the `KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE-START/END` region) still taught the **retired 6-phase-as-default** model — numbered `Phase 1/2/3/4` boundaries, "phase files", a "current phase file", `fast-summary.md`/bare `.cache/` as the default durable artifacts — which directly contradicted **#538 (adaptive is the unconditional default)**, onboarding every freshly-initialized repo with vocabulary that no longer matches the runtime it gets. The block is **re-grounded on the adaptive DAG-of-roles model**, phase-free: it states the model in one line (the `planner` authors and freezes `workflow-plan.md`; the executor runs it node-by-node via the running-set scheduler), names nodes by **function** (read/research → `code-explorer`/`knowledge-lookup`; strategy/blueprint → `planner`/`code-architect`; execution → `tdd-guide` test-first or `implementer` for refactors/scaffolding/config with no natural failing test; gates → `code-reviewer`/`adversarial-verifier`; docs → `doc-updater`), repoints durable-state bullets at `workflow-plan.md` (its `## Node Ledger`) + `.cache/{node-id}.md` instead of "phase files", and demotes `fast`/`full` to an explicit install-time opt-in aside. The exact `KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE-START/END` markers and the non-template prose (`MANDATORY` sentinel, redirect blocks, the Codex-note outside the markers) are preserved. **Parity is now fully enforced** (previously only half-enforced): `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` gains a phase-number ban (`/Phase\s+\d/`) + phase-file/artifact ban over all 6 init surfaces (3 `commands/workflow-init.md` + 3 init `SKILL.md`) plus a GitHub≡GitLab≡Gitea cross-forge content-parity assertion (modulo the single forge-noun line, #309); `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` gains the same phase-ban + parity for the regenerated opencode edition (modulo the `Claude Code agents → subagents` runtime-noun transform). The pinned init durable-state `assertConcept` needles in all four contract validators (claude + its #274 byte-mirror, codex, gitlab, gitea) drop `fast-summary.md`/bare `.cache/` and add `workflow-plan.md` + `## Node Ledger` + `.cache/{node-id}.md`; the unrelated fast-path `fast-summary.md` needles on the classifier/`workflow-next` surfaces are left untouched (the fast path remains an opt-in). RED→GREEN tdd-guide cycle (the phase ban is the driving failing test) and G1-reviewed. Cross-edition (#307): all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` green; opencode is additive (D-530-02) and its suite (`node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`) is green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-572-01.md`.

### Changed

- **Codex agent profiles are now GLOBAL by default — install once, every repo (Claude-edition parity) — #571.** Codex lifecycle hooks were already global (`~/.codex`, #447); this gives the **agent profiles** the same treatment. Three coordinated changes: (1) `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` gains a self-documenting **`--global`** flag (targets `os.homedir()/.codex` regardless of cwd/arg-order, position-robust like `--with-fast`/`--with-full`); the positional `projectRoot` form (`"$PWD"`/`"$HOME"`) is retained as an optional **project-local override** — nothing removed, fully back-compatible. (2) The preflight gate `kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js` now accepts **either** a fresh global `~/.codex` scope **or** a valid project scope, and **fails closed when neither is valid** — implemented as a global-first short-circuit (a fresh global scope passes with `scope: 'global'` before any project inspection, so no redundant project-local copy is autofix-installed); the load-bearing `scopeIsFresh = s.exists && !scopeIsStale(s)` predicate and every existing typed refusal (`--no-autofix` stale, `installer_failed`, `autofix_unsafe`, `schema_version_unsupported`) are unchanged — the short-circuit only *widens* the PASS set. Autofix continues to target project-local only (a per-repo gate never silently writes the user's machine-wide `~/.codex`). (3) The three Codex init SKILLs are now **scaffolding-only** — they no longer mandate a per-repo `install-codex-agent-profiles.js "$PWD"` agent install; `workflow-init` only scaffolds the project (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/roadmap/docs) and points to the one-time `--global` install/upgrade, exactly like the Claude edition. The contract-pinned installer-locator find-path is preserved; the 3 edition contract validators gain additive regression locks (assert the `--global` invocation; forbid the retired per-repo `"$PWD"` mandate). The preflight gate now consults the ambient `~/.codex`, so the preflight test surfaces were retrofitted with a hermetic temp `HOME` for deterministic scope assertions. Adversarially verified (15+ scope-corruption scenarios, zero fail-open) and G1-reviewed. Cross-edition (#307): preflight is byte-identical across all four trees and the installer across the three plugin trees; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (run sequentially) + `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` green. opencode is additive (D-530-02) and unaffected. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-571-01.md`.

## [6.12.0] - 2026-06-27

### Added

- **`/workflow-init`: three new Non-Negotiable Rules seeded into consumer `CLAUDE.md`.** The init bootstrapper now writes three additional binding rules alongside the existing five: (1) *Verify facts, don't fabricate* — do not guess API/library behavior, interfaces, or signatures; confirm against documentation, source, or a run, and never claim to understand code, errors, or requirements that have not been verified; (2) *Reuse before adding* — search for an existing equivalent before writing a new interface; (3) *Escalate irreversible changes* — do not unilaterally make hard-to-reverse changes or alter a user-owned contract (public API, schema/data migration, dependency or build-tooling swap, deletion of working capability) without stating the decision + evidence and getting confirmation. Rule 3 deliberately gates on the **reversibility / contract-ownership** axis — consistent with "escalate values, not facts" and the consent-halt valve (#44/#287) — rather than the ambiguous "architectural change" label, so it tightens accuracy without eroding agent autonomy or the planner-owns-claim boundary. Cross-edition (#307): propagated to all six init surfaces (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs) and the opencode edition regenerated from canonical; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green; opencode suite green.

## [6.11.1] - 2026-06-26

### Fixed

- **routing (prose): consumer repos are no longer pointed at the self-host-only `run-chains.js` at the plan-run→finalize seam — #570.** The plan-run §5 "All done" step and the finalize pre-contractor step previously instructed the agent to run `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` **unconditionally**. That script only runs the maintainer edition chains (`npm run test:kaola-workflow:*`) and is meaningful solely in the Kaola-Workflow self-host; in a consumer (non-npm) product repo it can only refuse `chains_config_missing`. The runtime guard held, but the prose led a consumer-repo agent to reach for a maintainer-only tool and momentarily conflate it with the project's own `validation_command` (observed in a real downstream run: the agent launched run-chains, got the refusal, then pivoted). Both operational steps now branch explicitly on self-host detection (presence of `test:kaola-workflow:*` scripts in `package.json`): self-host runs `run-chains.js` → `chain-receipt.json` as before; a consumer repo runs the plan's `## Meta` `validation_command` and records `.cache/final-validation.md` with a column-0 `verdict: pass` (the existing `--finalize-check` consumer auto-detection, #475), and does **not** invoke `run-chains.js`. No script-logic change — the `chains_config_missing` refusal stays as the defense-in-depth backstop. Cross-edition (#307): prose propagated to all SIX routing surfaces (#400) for both plan-run and finalize (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-570-01.md`.

## [6.11.0] - 2026-06-26

### Removed

- **`kaola-workflow-auto` autopilot driver removed in full — supersedes #443 (D-443-01) / D-420-01 Part 1.** The autopilot (`/kaola-workflow-auto` command + `kaola-workflow-auto` SKILL ×6 surfaces; `kaola-workflow-autopilot.js` ×4 script editions; `test-autopilot.js`) is deleted. It overlapped ~90% with `/workflow-next`, which already drives one issue/bundle scout → adaptive-claim → plan-run → finalize to closure. Its only distinguishing capability — a cross-issue *goal loop* over multiple bundles — both (a) contradicted `workflow-next`'s explicit one-issue-per-invocation Completion Contract (which forbids "next issue in line" continuation) and (b) was not even in-process (the driver finalized one bundle, returned `goal_progress`, and relied on operator re-invocation — functionally identical to re-running `/workflow-next`). Worse, its standalone `claim` stage collided with the adaptive path's planner-owns-claim contract (#287/#44): following the skill literally double-claimed the issue, yielding an off-nominal `owned`-on-fresh-claim that `workflow-next` avoids by deferring the claim to the planner. Removed all references: `AUTO_COMMAND`/`AUTO_SKILL` from `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical), the `COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES` entries in `validate-script-sync.js`, the `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` entry in `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` (×2), the route/skill pins in `test-route-reachability.js` + all four `validate-*-contracts.js` (GitLab/Gitea command+skill counts 12 → 11), the opencode-edition wiring (`install-opencode.sh` + `test-opencode-edition.js`), and the `## Autopilot Driver` section in `docs/architecture.md`. The `issue-scout` `backlog_empty` output shape is retained (it serves `/workflow-next` too); only its autopilot-specific prose was neutralized. D-443-01 marked Superseded. Cross-edition (#307) — all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green; opencode suite green.

## [6.10.0] - 2026-06-24

### Changed

- **observability (LOW, additive, fail-open): the dispatch-log SubagentStart hook now records the dispatched model in `dispatch-log.jsonl` — #566.** Each JSONL line gains `model_planned` (always resolved from the agent manifest via `kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js`) and `model` (the runtime-supplied tier, codex CLI only; empty for Claude Code `SubagentStart` and opencode). `model` is inherently best-effort and may be empty, while `model_planned` is always resolved for a known role. Dual-field and payload-agnostic (no branching on payload shape), fail-open (exit 0 always), backward-compatible (existing readers key on `agent_type`). No new gate, no schema migration. Cross-edition (canonical + codex byte-twin + gitlab/gitea hook copies — four chains green).
- **observability (LOW, fail-open): the dispatch-log hook now locates the model resolver across every install layout, so `model_planned` populates under opencode too — #567.** #566 hard-coded the resolver as `dirname(dirname($0))/scripts`, which only matches the four plugin editions (where `scripts/` is a sibling of `hooks/`); under opencode the hook lives at `<root>/hooks/` while support scripts live at `<root>/kaola-workflow/scripts/`, so `model_planned` came back empty on every opencode layout (global / project / dev self-host). The hook now searches the known layouts in order (`$_KW_ROOT/scripts`, `$_KW_ROOT/kaola-workflow/scripts`, `${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/opencode}/kaola-workflow/scripts`), fail-open (exit 0 always), byte-identical across all four hook copies with no forge-forbidden `./scripts` literal. RED→GREEN walkthrough (`testDispatchLogResolverResolvesUnderOpencodeLayout567`). Cross-edition (canonical + codex byte-twin + gitlab/gitea hook copies — four chains green).
- **observability (LOW, fail-open): the dispatch-log hook now captures role-agent spawns under opencode worktree posture by scanning the resolved repo's linked worktrees — #568.** The #338 dual-root scan logged each spawn under the hook's cwd-toplevel AND the dispatched agent's cwd-toplevel, covering the case where the agent runs **in** the linked worktree. Under opencode worktree posture the inverse holds: the role agent runs in the MAIN repo while the active `workflow-state.md` is worktree-resident, so both roots resolved to main (no active project there) and M1/M2 attestation was blind to every role spawn (only the script-written claim/contractor backfills landed). The hook now ALSO enumerates the resolved repo's linked worktrees (`git worktree list --porcelain`) and appends under each worktree's active project, deduped by root path so an in-place run still logs EXACTLY once and the #338 worktree case stays single-log. Edition-neutral (plain `git`, no forge tokens), fail-open (exit 0 always), byte-identical across all four hook copies. RED→GREEN walkthrough (`testDispatchLogCapturesWorktreeResidentActiveProjectFromMainCwd568`). Cross-edition (canonical + codex byte-twin + gitlab/gitea hook copies — four chains green).

## [6.9.0] - 2026-06-21

### Fixed

- **sink-merge (MED, fail-open, bounded): the #552 lane-group backstop now ALSO guards the LEGACY (non-`--sink`) main-advance path — #561.** `lingeringLaneGroupRefusal` was wired only into the `--sink` transaction (`sinkPreflight`); the legacy `main()` ffMergeLoop advance (the in-place merge sink that finalize routes through by default) ran every other precondition but never the lane-group check, so an in-place adaptive run that left a residual `lane_group` in `running-set.json` (the #552 crash-window: surviving legs' committed work NOT on the branch) could advance main with code missing. Added the SAME typed `lingering_lane_group` refusal (pure read, zero mutation, FIRST in the legacy precondition block) reusing the already-defined helper — no new mechanism. Bounded: the #552 runtime fixes (ledger `isLast` + reconcile self-heal) run on every close regardless of sink path, so this is the defense-in-depth belt the legacy path lacked. The de-engineering hypothesis (is the legacy path dead?) was REFUTED — finalize invokes it by default. RED→GREEN walkthrough (live + archive dual-location). Cross-edition (canonical + codex byte + gitlab/gitea hand-ports) — four chains green.
- **sink-merge (MED, destructive fail-open, pre-existing): the `--sink` merge step now runs `assertWorktreeClean` before force-removing the linked worktree — #562.** The legacy path guarded its `git worktree remove --force` with `assertWorktreeClean` (#496/#506) but the `--sink` path force-removed with NO clean precondition — a `--sink` run on a worktree carrying uncommitted work could destroy it (data-loss asymmetry). Hoisted the EXISTING `assertWorktreeClean` into `sinkPreflight` as a typed `worktree_dirty` refusal (throws on dirty OR unprobeable → exit 1, zero mutation, worktree intact; resume-safe when already removed). RED→GREEN walkthrough (dirty worktree refuses + survives; clean worktree not over-refused). Cross-edition — four chains green.
- **closure-audit + claim (LOW, fail-open): `isDirty()` and `archiveDirDirty()` now fail CLOSED on an unprobeable `git status` — #563.** The same `catch (_) { return false }` anti-pattern #557 fixed in `treeDirty`, still present in `closure-audit.js isDirty()` (both arms) and `claim.js archiveDirDirty()`. Flipped to `return true` (an unverifiable tree reads DIRTY; a genuinely-ABSENT path still reads not-dirty). `archiveDirDirty` is the load-bearing one — a probe fault no longer mis-reports a crashed finalize as `already_finalized`. A no-new-seam source-invariant guard (`testProbeHelpersFailClosed`) locks both flips against fail-open regression across all editions (chosen over a behavioral test that would need new production seams — precedence #3). Cross-edition — four chains green.

### Changed

- **cross-forge guard (LOW, prospective): the #553 `canonicalOnly` exclusions are now machine-guarded — #564.** `test-validate-script-sync.js` now asserts each `canonicalOnly` name (`ghExec`, `projectHasAdaptivePlan`) has ZERO code-context references in EITHER forge tree (gitlab/gitea), so a future `canonicalOnly` entry naming a genuinely cross-required export goes RED instead of silently re-opening the #550 crash class. Codex (a COMMON byte-copy that legitimately uses both names) is deliberately NOT scanned. The soundness invariant that was manual since #553 is now mechanically enforced.
- **docs/hygiene (LOW): stale #542/#364 comment + dead-import residuals scrubbed — #565.** (1) `adaptive-node.js` `runOpenReady` header still framed write co-open as `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT`-gated though its body is `parallelWritesDefaultOn`-driven (default-on since #542/D-542-01) — rewritten to match, plus the sibling serial-fallback note and a stale `:3976`→`:4034` line pointer. (2) Removed the dead `resolveLaneContainment` destructure at `adaptive-node.js:44` (never invoked; the schema definition/export stays load-bearing). (3) Corrected the CLAUDE.md + `frontier-batch.md` framing that called `parallel-batch.js` "shelled by adaptive-node" / "the running-set scheduler" — the running-set scheduler proper is adaptive-node's `open-ready`/`close-node` (the live `enterBatch` route); parallel-batch provides the batch-manifest mechanics, write-role isolation excised (#364/ADR-0008). adaptive-node is a GENERATED_AGGREGATOR (regen ×4) — four chains green.

### Changed

- **cross-forge: the #550 forge-classifier export-superset guard is generalized to a FAMILY over every divergent forge hand-port that participates in a cross-script require — #553.** `validate-script-sync.js`'s `FORGE_CLASSIFIER_EXPORT_SUPERSET` (classifier-only) becomes `FORGE_EXPORT_SUPERSET_FAMILY` covering classifier + claim + sink-merge + roadmap + repair-state + active-folders + closure-audit, so a future cross-required export omission fails CLOSED at `validate-script-sync` instead of TypeError-ing on a failing path no green chain hits (the #550 crash class). Each entry reuses the proven `require()`+`Object.keys` superset mechanism; a new `canonicalOnly` field SUBTRACTS genuinely edition-specific canonical exports (`ghExec` — the forges call `glab`/`tea` directly; `projectHasAdaptivePlan` — the forges use `isAdaptiveWorkflowState`/`routeAdaptive`), verified NOT cross-required so the exclusion cannot re-open the crash class. The genuinely-missing-but-defined forge exports it surfaced are added: claim's `isSafeBranchArg`/`assertSafeBranchArg`/`assertNoNewline`/`classifyWorktreeError`/`removeBranch`/`postAdvisoryClaim`, roadmap's `cmdGenerate`, repair-state's `repairStateContent` alias. New `test-validate-script-sync.js` (28 assertions, wired into the claude chain) locks the mechanism (family coverage, `canonicalOnly` subtraction, RED-provable drift catch). `FORGE_CLASSIFIER_EXPORT_SUPERSET` + `forgeClassifierExportDrift` stay exported (backward-compat).

### Fixed

- **roadmap (MED): the `generate` CLI can now close the last issue — #554.** `cmdGenerate` called `guardAgainstMissingRoadmapSource` (keyed on source COUNT==0) BEFORE `regenerateRoadmap`, so a present-but-EMPTY `.roadmap/` (the close-last-issue state) was WRONGLY refused, while the in-process `regenerateRoadmap` (whose narrow guard keys only on dir-MISSING) correctly empties the mirror. Dropped the redundant pre-check call (the function + its module.exports entry are KEPT — two contract validators pin its tokens), so `cmdGenerate` relies on `regenerateRoadmap`'s narrow guard as the single source of truth. The dir-MISSING refusal stays enforced. RED→GREEN CLI close-last-issue walkthrough test; cross-edition (canonical + codex byte + gitlab/gitea hand-ports).
- **forge (MED, fail-open): forge sink-merge no longer silently swallows a missing-export TypeError from the lazy `archiveProjectDir` require — #555.** The finalize step's bare `catch (_) {}` masked ALL errors (including the #550 cross-edition export-drift class) into a silent skip of the project archive. Narrowed to re-throw `TypeError`/`ReferenceError` (programmer errors) across canonical + codex + both forge sink-merge copies, swallowing only the expected archive-already-exists idempotency case. The live crash path is independently closed by the #553 export-superset guard (which now enforces `archiveProjectDir` stays exported); this is the belt-and-suspenders.
- **CI guard (LOW, defense-in-depth): the #547 allowband freshness guard now derives its scan set from the actual chain definitions instead of a hardcoded list — #560.** `test-validation-allowband.js` hardcoded a 5-file `VALIDATOR_FILES` array, so a NEW chain validator reading a real doc would be unscanned (the latent gap #547 warned about). It now parses the four `test:kaola-workflow:*` package.json chains and keeps the contract VALIDATORS (`validate-*.js`) — which read + assert on real-repo prose — while excluding `test-*.js`/`simulate-*.js` (they use `docs/**` strings as tmpdir FIXTURES; scanning them would false-flag, empirically 8 such files). Fail-closed floor (always re-adds the legacy set, so a parse miss can only ADD coverage). Adds derivation/superset controls + a non-fatal advisory for segmented-`path.join` doc reads the literal scan can't see (17 assertions, claude-chain-only — no cross-edition obligation).
- **claim (LOW, fail-open): `treeDirty()` now treats an unprobeable `git status` as DIRTY, not clean — #557.** `catch (_) { return false }` was the same "unverifiable → assume safe" fail-OPEN that #496 removed for the destructive worktree path, left on the in-place-branch + discard gates. Flipped to `return true` (fail-closed) so those gates refuse on an unverifiable tree; added a `KAOLA_WORKFLOW_FORCE_STATUS_FAIL` [TEST ONLY] seam + RED→GREEN walkthrough test. Cross-edition (canonical + codex byte + gitlab/gitea hand-ports).

- **adaptive/self-host: the chain-receipt freshness check is re-keyed from the commit-SHA pin to a content-addressed code-tree hash, and the planner now records the validation command once — #547 Stage 2 (D-547-01).** This finishes #547 (Stage 1, commit `60877d75`, shipped the prose "cite-don't-rerun" discipline and DEFERRED the mechanical half). Right-sized per the design precedence (accuracy #1, efficiency #2, cheapest-sufficient-mechanism #3): it extends the existing `.cache/chain-receipt.json` rather than adding a subsystem, and does NOT touch the #475 consumer final-validation gate (D-475-01 stands).
  - **Freshness re-key (the demonstrated waste).** `plan-validator --finalize-check` (self-host branch) keyed `chains_stale` on `receipt.headSha !== HEAD`, so ANY new commit after the chains ran — including a commit touching only inert docs (narrative/ADRs) or workflow-state, whose code tree is byte-identical — forced a full four-chain re-run (~30 min, #551). It now compares a new `codeTreeHash` (`computeCodeTreeHash`: `sha256` of the filtered `git ls-tree -r` over the landable `snapshotWorktree`) against a recompute over the current tree. A legacy receipt lacking the field falls back to the headSha pin (fail-closed). `run-chains.js` records `codeTreeHash` via the SAME exported helper the gate recomputes, so producer and gate never disagree.
  - **Accuracy-critical band (precedence #1).** A naive "exclude all `*.md`/`docs/**`" hash is a confirmed correctness bug for this repo — the four chains' contract validators read and assert on `docs/api.md`, `docs/workflow-state-contract.md`, `docs/agents-source.md`, root `README.md`, root `CHANGELOG.md`. `isValidationInvisible` therefore excludes the #424 narrow allowband + the whole `kaola-workflow/` state tree but SUBTRACTS those test-consumed files (`SELF_HOST_TEST_CONSUMED`), which stay CODE. A new guard `test-validation-allowband.js` scans the chain validators for allowband-member prose reads and fails closed if any is not kept-as-code, so the list can never silently drift.
