A self-hosting fork extends the set at runtime via the `validation_test_consumes: a/b.md` `## Meta` field (no predicate fork).
  - **Record-once validation command (the omitted Stage-1 half).** New `validation_command: <cmd>` `## Meta` field (`parseValidationCommand`, reader-only/hash-covered) — the planner records it once at freeze; nodes and Finalization reuse it instead of re-deriving a per-node command. Authored in `agents/workflow-planner.md` (+ the three `.toml` twins, parity-pinned), consumed via the `plan-run` (×6 routing surfaces) and `finalize` (×3) Validation De-Duplication blocks, plus the opencode generated tree.
  - **Cross-edition:** `plan-validator.js` (4-edition generated/COMMON) + `run-chains.js` (codex byte-twin + two rename-normalized forge ports) + the 6 plan-run / 3 finalize prose surfaces + the planner `.md`/`.toml` triple + opencode. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (sequential) + `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`. New RED→GREEN walkthrough scenarios prove a docs-only commit stays fresh and a code (or test-consumed-doc) commit refuses `chains_stale`.

### Changed

- **adaptive: `orient` now auto-surfaces dispatch-fidelity telemetry, and the planner profiles describe write co-open as default-on — #558 + #559.** Two parallelism-observability/authoring-guidance audit findings.
  - **#558 (everConcurrent auto-surfaced):** `runOrient` (`adaptive-node.js`) now emits `dispatchFidelity: {maxSimultaneousOpen, everConcurrent}` derived from the durable `node-timings.jsonl` on every run, so a regression to silent serialization auto-surfaces on a REAL run (the #472 close-criterion) instead of only via a hand-run probe. Additive field, fail-closed (absent telemetry ⇒ zeroed trace, never a refuse — telemetry never blocks a lifecycle transition). RED→GREEN `test-adaptive-node.js` + `docs/api.md` note.
  - **#559 (stale co-open prose):** the `workflow-planner` profile family (`agents/workflow-planner.md` + the three `.toml` twins + the opencode mirror) said disjoint-write nodes "co-schedule under lane containment" / "Serial is the DEGRADED fallback (… lane containment off …)" — stale since #542/D-542-01 retired `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` as the co-open gate. Rewritten to "disjoint-write antichains co-open in isolated per-leg worktrees BY DEFAULT; serial only for overlapping/uncertain writes, a no-worktree host, or the `KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0` kill-switch." Residual `DORMANT` comments in `adaptive-node.js` (`runCloseNode`/`closeGroupMember` headers + the leg-primitive export) scrubbed to the #542 default-on reality. `test-agent-profile-parity.js` gains the `KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES` curated token to lock the rewrite across all three twins. (The six routing surfaces already carried #542-current prose — no #400 obligation.)

### Fixed

- **adaptive (LOW, fail-open): the self-host finalize gate no longer downgrades to the weaker consumer gate when `package.json` is present-but-unreadable — #556.** `plan-validator --finalize-check`'s repo-kind discriminator wrapped the git-toplevel probe AND the `package.json` read+parse in ONE outer `try` that set `selfHostNpm=false` on ANY throw — so a present-but-unreadable (EACCES/EIO) or corrupt-JSON `package.json` on a genuine self-host repo silently fell through to the weaker `final-validation` consumer gate (a fail-OPEN that an agent-written `verdict: pass` could then satisfy). It now distinguishes THREE states: `package.json` ABSENT (ENOENT) ⇒ genuine consumer (#475, unchanged); PRESENT-but-unreadable/unparseable ⇒ a typed `repo_kind_undetermined` refusal (exit 1, zero mutation) rather than the weaker gate; readable-with-no-chain-script ⇒ consumer. New operator-hint + RED/GREEN `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (#556 f: corrupt pkg → `repo_kind_undetermined`; #556 g: ENOENT stays consumer). Cross-edition (`plan-validator.js` generated ×4 + COMMON byte).
- **adaptive (HIGH, fail-open): a crash in `closeGroupMember`'s two-write window no longer silently loses a write-parallel leg's committed work or false-reports `allDone` — #552 (D-552-01).** On the DEFAULT write-parallel path (#542 co-open), a lane-group member closes in two non-atomic writes: flip its ledger row to `complete`, then record it in `lane_group.closed_members` + drop it from the running set. A crash BETWEEN them left a member ledger-terminal but absent from `closed_members`; the genuine last member then read the stale `closed_members`, computed `isLast=false`, DEFERRED forever, and never ran the synthesizer/group barrier — so the other legs' committed work was never merged, while `allDone` (pure-ledger) + a lane_group-blind sink went GREEN and advanced main with code missing. Three coordinated fixes:
  - **Ledger-derived last-member detection** (`closeGroupMember`): "last" is now derived from the authoritative ledger (every other member id is ledger-terminal), with `closed_members` kept only as a union fallback. The crash-consistent ledger is the source of truth, so the genuine last member still synthesizes.
  - **Reconcile self-heal + leg retention** (`runReconcileRunningSet`): a close-direction terminal member of a SURVIVING lane group is EXCLUDED from leg teardown (its committed leg work survives for the synthesizer) and re-added (deduped) to `closed_members` so the optimization field re-converges with the ledger. A whole-group drop still tears down all legs.
  - **Sink-side `lingering_lane_group` fail-closed backstop** (`sinkPreflight`, ×4 editions): a clean group completion DELETES the `lane_group` key, so a residual key at sink time means unmerged legs. `sinkPreflight` (pure read, zero mutation, before the dirty-tree scan) reads `running-set.json` from BOTH the live and post-finalize archive `.cache/` and refuses (exit 1, main not advanced) on a non-empty `lane_group`. `next-action`'s `allDone` stays pure-ledger by design (#272) — the teeth live at the one irreversible point, not the aggregator.
  - **Cross-edition:** `adaptive-node.js` (generated ×4) + `sink-merge.js` (COMMON byte claude↔codex + gitlab/gitea divergent hand-ports) + RED-provable unit tests (`test-adaptive-node.js`: ledger-isLast + reconcile-heal) + walkthrough sink-backstop tests (canonical + gitlab + gitea, live + archive dual-location). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (sequential).
- **opencode-edition: the `--global` install is no longer dead, reinstall/uninstall actually reset to adaptive-only, and the global hooks plugin can find its scripts — a 9-fix correctness pass (F1–F9) on the opencode edition installer + sync + plugin.** An adversarial audit of the opencode edition surfaced 27 confirmed findings collapsing to 9 distinct fixes; all are opencode-edition-only (no #307 four-chain obligation per D-530-02 — validated by `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`, 363 → 489 assertions).
  - **F1 (critical): `--global` deployed to a NESTED `~/.config/opencode/.opencode/{...}` that opencode never scans — the entire global install was dead.** `copy_tree` now takes a scope-correct `layout_root`: project → `<project>/.opencode/{...}` (unchanged); global → the config root **directly** (`${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/opencode}/{agent,command,plugins,hooks}/`). `opencode.json` still lands at the config root. (Authoritative basis: opencode's docs — the config dir *is* the global ".opencode equivalent".)
  - **F2 (high): `copy_tree` was additive-only and never pruned** — a narrowed/reset opt-in left orphaned `fast`/`phase1-5` command files on disk. It now PRUNES kaola-owned command files not in the EFFECTIVE set before re-copying, so a bare reinstall converges to adaptive-only (the real "reset" half).
  - **F3 (high): the global hooks plugin couldn't find its hook scripts** (`findRoot`/`hookPath` walked only the project tree). `hookPath` now also probes the plugin's own sibling `../hooks/` (via `import.meta.url`) and `$OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR/hooks/`, project candidates still first (fail-open preserved).
  - **F4 (high): no uninstall path existed.** New `install-opencode.sh --uninstall` (honors `--target`/`--global`) removes only kaola-deployed artifacts by source-tree filename, preserves the user-owned `opencode.json`, and surgically resets `installed_paths:[]` in the shared config (other editions unaffected). (`uninstall.sh` stays forge-scoped; opencode is an additive runtime, not a forge.)
  - **F5 (low): the command partition was a fail-OPEN denylist** — a future canonical command would silently deploy by default. It is now a fail-CLOSED allowlist (`ADAPTIVE_CORE_COMMANDS`); an unrecognized command is skipped + warned. Tests assert exact-set-equality + partition exhaustiveness.
  - **F6 (low): a dead `transformCommandBody` strip** (`/downgrade to full path \//`) matched nothing after #538 rewrote canonical to "NEVER downgrade to fast/full"; removed. A22 now POSITIVELY asserts the guard + no un-`NEVER`'d fallback wording.
  - **F7 (low): the Path Intent section strip was keyed to the volatile step number** (`Startup Step 0a-1`); re-keyed to the stable "Path Intent" title so a canonical renumber cannot silently leak the path-selection switch. New A22 body-literal canaries back it.
  - **F8 (low): `sync --check` never validated `opencode.json`** despite being billed as the parity gate; it now compares the committed config to the neutral renderer.
  - **F9 (medium): `seed_kaola_config` was python3-dependent** (a python3-absent host left opt-in files on disk while `installed_paths` went unrecorded); reimplemented in node (already a hard dependency). New tests cover `--global` layout + global-tree leak invariant (G1), support-script deployment (S1), prune-on-reset (P6), uninstall round-trip (U1), idempotent reinstall (I1), direct `hookPath` global-resolution (H1), and non-command surface locations. (`node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`: 363 → 493 assertions.)

## [6.8.0] - 2026-06-20

### Added

- **docs/init: the Codex `kaola-workflow-init` SKILL packs now document the `--with-fast` / `--with-full` install-time path opt-ins, closing the discoverability gap #538 deferred at its n6 sync-group barrier (#549).** #538 shipped the `--with-fast`/`--with-full` partition across the three runtimes but deferred the Codex init prose because the `skills/kaola-workflow-init/SKILL.md` files have byte-identical `commands/workflow-init.md` peers that must move together (#301 sync-group constraint). This run lands the opt-in discoverability prose on those surfaces so a Codex user is told how to install `fast`/`full` (default install ships adaptive-only; the flags UNION on re-install, never remove; uninstall→reinstall resets to adaptive-only). The flags themselves already worked (#538); this is the missing documentation.

- **classifier/run-chains: the four-chain `npm test` gate now retries a TRANSIENT per-chain fault GRANULARLY (only the failing chain, bounded), and an offline run is held to a determinism guard so a flake cannot masquerade as a determinate green/red (#550).** A single chain that fails for a transient-infra reason (the #519 transient class — TLS handshake timeout / rate-limit / DNS / connection reset / ETIMEDOUT / 5xx) is re-run in isolation rather than collapsing the whole gate, and the offline-determinism guard refuses to treat a non-reproducible result as a determinate verdict. Accuracy precedence #1 is preserved: a genuine red still refuses; only a positively-identified transient is retried, and the retry budget is bounded.

### Changed

- **issue-scout: the scout is now the SOLE backlog reader on `/workflow-next` — the router no longer pre-reads or pre-ranks the roadmap/backlog before dispatching the scout, removing the double-read divergence where the orchestrator's own glance could disagree with the scout's roadmap-priority ranking (#545).** Building on #524 (the scout ranks by roadmap priority/drive-order first), #545 makes the scout the single source of truth for backlog selection on the next-issue path: the orchestrator dispatches the scout and consumes its `priority_basis` verdict rather than forming a competing pre-read. This keeps issue selection an agent decision (CLAUDE.md §"Agent Owns Reasoning") and eliminates the silent-substitution risk of two rankings disagreeing.

- **adaptive path: a cite-don't-rerun de-duplication discipline — an already-produced read frontier / probe result is CITED, not re-dispatched, on the adaptive path; the durable receipt for it is deferred (#547).** When a read-only investigation (probe / lookup / verifier) has already produced a result earlier in the run, the orchestrator cites that prior evidence instead of re-running the same frontier — removing the wasted re-dispatch (a real makespan + token cost, CLAUDE.md §"Maximize Workflow Efficiency"). The durable de-dup receipt (a persisted citation artifact) is deferred to a follow-up; this run lands the discipline as authoring guidance.

- **sink: `runTestGate` is now consumer-aware — on a consumer (non-npm) repo it gates on the consumer `.cache/final-validation.md` `verdict: pass` artifact instead of assuming the self-host four-chain receipt, so a downstream consumer's `--sink` is not blocked on a `test:kaola-workflow:*` chain it does not own (#548).** Mirrors the dual-mode `--finalize-check` (#475): self-host (npm) gates on the `chain-receipt.json` four-chain receipt; consumer gates on the column-0 `verdict: pass` final-validation artifact. This keeps the workflow self-sufficient (CLAUDE.md §"CI/CD Is Not a Gate" / D-475-01) — a consumer run completes on a repo with no `test:kaola-workflow:*` scripts and no degradation.

- **parallelism: a file-disjoint SHARED-INFRA write frontier (same shared area — e.g. two `scripts/` files — exact-file-disjoint) now co-opens as isolated parallel legs BY DEFAULT under the retained gate+PROTECTED net — NO `write_overlap_policy:coarse` and NO `--write-overlap-consent`; consent + the `write_overlap_policy` tier now gate ONLY a genuine NON-shared coarse-area overlap, and exact-file overlap stays blocking (#546, G2).** Superseding the D-542-01 "shared-infra overlap ⟹ consent" classification clause (accuracy-first DECISION B), `writeOverlapRelaxable` in `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` now relaxes the `shared-infra` class by default, gated by a single RETAINED safety net at every class/tier: (NET-1) a `synthesizer`/`code-reviewer` gate post-dominates the relaxed legs themselves (leg-scoped `gateUncovered` over the `--nodes` set), and (NET-2) no PROTECTED concrete file (lockfiles / `CHANGELOG.md` / `ROADMAP.md` / install manifests / archive-roadmap artifacts / the `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` ×4 anchor) is in either set. The consent ceremony bought no correctness the structural net (exact-file-disjoint by construction + post-dominating gate + per-leg `.kw` worktree barrier) does not already provide. The runtime co-open consequence in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` is unchanged in mechanism: leg-provisioning fires under `groupForm && legCoupled` (`legCoupled = parallelWritesDefaultOn`, default TRUE, #542); a shared-infra frontier simply now reaches `groupForm` without consent because the validator's `--parallel-safe` re-check relaxes it. The non-shared coarse-area overlap stays consent-gated (`write_overlap_policy ∈ {disjoint,coarse}` + `--write-overlap-consent` + the net), and exact-file overlap never relaxes here (the runtime `merge_conflict` barrier owns real reconciliation). The #498 co-open ⟺ legs-provisioned invariant is preserved. See `docs/decisions/D-546-01.md` (D-546-G2, supersedes-in-part D-542-01 on the shared-infra-classification axis only).

- **editions: the `--with-fast`/`--with-full` install-time opt-in partition (#538) now spans ALL three runtimes — Codex + opencode join Claude, so a default install on every edition ships adaptive-only, with `fast`/`full` reachable only via explicit opt-in; the opencode edition is also made standalone (zero `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`~/.claude/` path leaks — folded #544-run-discovered) (#543).** Two coupled scopes sharing one behavioral-parity goal, shipped as a two-lane adaptive DAG (Codex research-upfront; opencode test-first) → dual opus code-review gates → doc sink. **(1) Codex partition (Option A — installer config-writer).** The Codex runtime legality gate was ALREADY shipped and enforced across all editions (`kaola-workflow-claim.js:849-863` → `path_not_installed`, reading `installed_paths` via `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`), so the ONLY gap was the install-time writer. `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (×3 byte-identical triplet: codex/-gitlab/-gitea) gains `--with-fast`/`--with-full` arg parsing (`process.argv.includes`, position-robust; `--enable-adaptive` warn+ignore; unknown args ignored) + a pure-JS `seedKaolaConfig(homeDir, withFast, withFull)` UNION writer that read-modify-writes the shared edition-agnostic `~/.config/kaola-workflow/config.json` `installed_paths` — byte-semantically mirroring `install.sh:712-734` D4 (canonical order `["fast","full"]`; only `{fast,full}` tokens retained; `parallel_mode` setdefault "auto"; `delete enable_adaptive`; WARN-first on corrupt/non-object config, never throws; write-temp-then-rename). Called after `updateHooks()`; exported for unit-testability. NO `plugin.json` change (the `"skills":"./skills/"` assertion ×3 validators holds), NO skill/profile conditional-removal (`kaola-workflow-fast` SKILL.md is validator-mandatory; the 15 agent profiles are role-based not path-based), NO version bump. **(2) opencode partition + standalone fix (folded #544-run-discovered).** `install-opencode.sh` gains the same `--with-fast`/`--with-full` partition (R1 EFFECTIVE UNION + D2 conditional copy + D4 `seed_kaola_config`), deploys support scripts to the opencode-native `${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/opencode}/kaola-workflow/scripts` (not `~/.claude/`), and the header/docs replace the "scoped out" deferral. `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js transformCommandBody` rewrites the `kaola_script()` resolver + agent-profile prose to opencode-native paths (a `rewriteClaudeScriptPaths` pass), so the regenerated `.opencode/` tree (14 files) has ZERO `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`.claude/kaola-workflow` tokens (was 146); the generator STILL emits all 12 commands (the partition is install-time COPY selection, not generator-time trimming — existing reachability assertions stay green). **Verification:** all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307 — codex installer triplet byte-identity enforced by `validate-script-sync.js`); `test-opencode-edition.js` 363 assertions green (incl. the absence assertion A); 10 Codex `installed_paths` partition sub-cases pass (default→`[]`, opt-ins, UNION-never-removes, reinstall-reset, `enable_adaptive` migration, WARN-first corrupt/non-object, `parallel_mode` setdefault); opus code-review gates n7 (opencode) + n8 (Codex) both PASS, 0 blocking. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-543-01.md`. Cross-edition behavioral parity: Claude/Codex/opencode now all default adaptive-only, all write the shared `installed_paths` config byte-identically. **Deferred (filed as follow-up):** the Codex `skills/kaola-workflow-init/SKILL.md` opt-in prose ×3 — blocked at the n6 barrier by a sync-group constraint (#301: the SKILL.md files have byte-identical `commands/workflow-init.md` peers that must move together); the `--with-fast`/`--with-full` flags work without it, the discoverability docs are filed for a follow-up.

- **opencode-edition: the two-tier effort mapping is now CONTRACT-KEYED (top/second ↔ opus/sonnet resolve by the model's API contract, not its brand name), unknown providers get a safe top/second default instead of silently de-tiering, and model switches are a documented re-sync rather than a silent collapse (#544).** Three defects in `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-identical cross-edition drift anchor) are fixed. (1) **Contract-keying:** `PROVIDER_EFFORT_TABLE` → `CONTRACT_EFFORT_TABLE` keyed by API contract (`anthropic` / `openai` / `google` / `default`), with a new `contractForProvider(providerId)` mapping provider ids → contracts. The effort knob now follows the contract: anthropic-contract (incl. **GLM-5.2 via z.ai**, which is served under the Anthropic API contract) → the `thinking` budget shape (`{type:'enabled',budgetTokens}`), NOT `reasoningEffort`; openai-contract → `reasoningEffort` (`xhigh`/`high`); google-contract → `reasoningEffort` (`high`/`low`); the safe default → `reasoningEffort` (`high`/`medium`). **Variant names are preserved** (`max`/`high` for GLM) — only the options payload flips, so `test-adaptive-node.js` Cases 4/5/6/7/9 stay green. (2) **No more silent de-tier for unknown providers:** `effortForProvider()` returns `CONTRACT_EFFORT_TABLE.default` (a real top/second split) for an unrecognized provider id instead of `null`; the load-bearing falsy-guard (`if (!id) return null`) is preserved so claude/codex dispatch stays behavior-inert. (3) **Resilience to model switches:** opencode's variant schema is model-scoped (no model-agnostic namespace, no per-call override), so the generated config's variant definitions are unavoidably pinned to the seeded model id; the runtime dispatch path (`dispatchEffortOpencode` → `KAOLA_OPENCODE_INHERIT_MODEL`) already re-resolves on every dispatch (never de-tiers the envelope), and the config-side re-sync is now documented prominently — the generated-config header comment, `docs/opencode-edition.md` (new "Switching models" subsection + contract-keyed table + GLM `thinking` example), and `install-opencode.sh` echo all carry the `KAOLA_OPENCODE_INHERIT_MODEL=<new> node scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js --write-config --adapt` re-sync command. The committed neutral `opencode.json` is unchanged (byte-identical). Decision recorded in `docs/decisions/D-544-01.md`. TDD RED→GREEN: `test-opencode-edition.js` S1 flipped to assert the Anthropic-contract `thinking` budget + a new S1-contract block + A12-unknown flipped to assert the safe default (300 assertions). Cross-edition (#307, the schema is the ×4 anchor): all four `test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green; `validate-script-sync.js` byte-identity green; `sync-opencode-edition.js --check` parity green.

### Fixed

- **adaptive path: a cluster of #546 per-node lifecycle correctness fixes — barrier, evidence-path, speculative-revert, running-set drain, and receipt-path defects (#546).** The #546 run hardened several per-node lifecycle seams in `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` / the plan-validator: the barrier check, the evidence-file path resolution, the speculative-discard revert target, the running-set drain on close, and the receipt-path resolution. (These accompany the #546-G2 parallelism change above; the G2 co-open is the headline behavioral change, these are the supporting correctness fixes landed in the same run.)

### Documentation

- **README: restructure to lead with principles/methodology, then runtime + forge support; opencode is now a first-class runtime.** The top-level flow is now Philosophy → Overview → Workflow roles → Installation → Usage → reference → Release versioning (moved out of the mid-document user path to the end). Added a **Runtimes and forges** matrix making the two install axes explicit (Claude Code / Codex / opencode × GitHub / GitLab / Gitea), a dedicated **opencode** install section (`install-opencode.sh` + the two-tier reasoning-effort model mapping `mapTier`/`PROVIDER_EFFORT_TABLE`), and reframed "Vendored Claude Code agents" → "Workflow roles" as a shared cross-runtime role set (Claude vendored agents / Codex `.toml` profiles / opencode `.opencode/agent`). Updated the tagline + "What you get" to name all three runtimes. Tightened the densest implementation-detail blocks (collapsed five `#NNN` paragraphs into one with a `docs/decisions/` pointer) and fixed two stale "under the ON switch" references retired in #538. Docs-only; no asserted README strings changed — `validate-vendored-agents.js` and `validate-workflow-contracts.js` green.

## [6.7.0] - 2026-06-20

### Changed

- **parallelism: planner-proven-disjoint (`parallel_safe` antichain) write frontiers now co-open as isolated parallel legs BY DEFAULT — the four-way activation conjunction is retired for the disjoint case; serial becomes the host-limited / opt-out fallback (#542).** The previous gate forced almost every `implement` frontier serial: co-opening a proven-disjoint write antichain required `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` **and** `KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION` **and** `--write-overlap-consent` **and** `write_overlap_policy:coarse` — all defaulting OFF, so no ordinary run ever reached the parallel path even though the planner already proves the antichain disjoint at freeze time. Now `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical drift anchor) adds `PARALLEL_WRITES_ENV='KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES'` + `parallelWritesDefaultOn(env)` (default **TRUE**; only `0`/`false`/`no` flips it off), and `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` keys co-open on that default instead of the recipe: `legCoupled = parallelWritesDefaultOn(process.env)`, the co-open gate drops its `containment &&` precondition (now `if (legCoupled && writeNodes.length >= 2)`), close-path member detection keys on `running0.lane_group` (dropping `resolveLaneContainment`), and the orphan sweep is gated on `parallelWritesDefaultOn`. **The four-way toggle conjunction is no longer required for the disjoint case** — per-leg git-worktree isolation (#463 / ADR-0010) + the mandatory synthesizer reconcile are the correctness net, not an operator remembering to flip flags. **Overlap stays consent-gated**: genuinely-overlapping (non-disjoint) writes still require `--write-overlap-consent` **and** `write_overlap_policy != off` (the default-on flip does NOT relax the overlap case; the planner's disjointness classification is the discriminator — disjoint ⟹ default co-open, coarse/shared-infra overlap ⟹ consent, exact-file overlap ⟹ refuse, unchanged). **Serial = host-limited / opt-out fallback**: `KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0` (or a host that cannot provision per-leg worktrees) degrades every write frontier to the byte-equivalent serial `write_node_exclusive` path. The default is **activated at install across the three runtimes (Claude, Codex, opencode) × the three forges (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea)** via the shared schema constant — no per-edition special-case lane. The **#498 invariant is preserved**: co-open ALWAYS provisions legs (`groupForm ⟺ legCoupled ⟺ legs`) — the legless attribution-blind union barrier is never taken. The write-lane hook (`hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh`) is deliberately unchanged (env-gated defense-in-depth; per-leg worktree isolation is the real containment). Also folded in: the runtime-label fix (#2 — neutral runtime labels in the generated surface instead of Claude-specific tier nouns) and opencode `install-opencode.sh` config parity so the opencode runtime inherits the same default-on disjoint co-open. See `docs/decisions/D-542-01.md` (supersedes-in-part D-419-01 / D-419-02 Part 2 / D-500-02 on the default-posture axis only — kernel model, per-leg mechanism, and emit-envelope contracts stand unchanged).

- **finalize + opencode-edition: two #539 finalization-summary follow-ups — (1) #540 purges the 3 stale inline `(Step 0a-1)` references #539's opencode path-flip (mechanism B) left in the GENERATED `.opencode/command/workflow-next.md` (~L72/L159/L464); (2) #541 forwards `--base` to the whole-plan `--barrier-check` call so shared/multi-issue-branch finalization passes BOTH gates end-to-end — landed as one same-scope bundle (#540, #541; `bundle-540-541`).** **(1) #540 (opencode-only enhancement, area:workflow-router; additive — D-530-02).** Post-#538 the "Step 0a-1 — Path Intent" step no longer exists, but #539's generation-time strip left 3 dangling inline `(Step 0a-1)` / `or Step 0a-1` refs in the generated command. Mechanism: extended the opencode-only strip-transform in `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js` (`transformCommandBody`: `text.replace(/ \(Step 0a-1\)| or Step 0a-1/g, '')`) to strip the inline refs at generation time; added content-reachability assertion A22 to `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (the generated `workflow-next.md` must contain no `Step 0a-1`; TDD RED→GREEN, test count 283→284). **opencode-only / additive (D-530-02): NO canonical `commands/*.md` touch, NO #307 four-chain obligation.** Verified via `test-opencode-edition.js` + `sync-opencode-edition.js --check` (drift-free). **(2) #541 (canonical cross-edition bug, area:scripts; carries the #307 four-chain).** The finalize command prose ran the plan-validator's whole-plan `--barrier-check` (the adaptive prerequisite) WITHOUT `--base`; on a shared/multi-issue branch it diffs `main...HEAD` and refuses `foreign_archive` (prior issues' archived folders sweep in). The validator's whole-plan `--barrier-check` already accepted `--base` (`plan-validator.js:2161`/`2198`, default `origin/main`) but the prose never forwarded it. Fix: source `--base` from the `KAOLA_FINALIZE_BASE` env var (mirroring #539's `--finalize-check` `--base` sourcing in `cmdFinalize`, `claim.js:2028-2035`), **default unset → byte-equivalent** (the empty-array `"${BARRIER_BASE_ARG[@]}"` idiom expands to zero args), applied IDENTICALLY across the **4 surfaces** holding the call (canonical `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md`, github-Codex `plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md`, GitLab + Gitea `plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md`; the GitLab/Gitea SKILL twins are excluded — no adaptive barrier branch). The per-node `--barrier-check` anti-laundering `--base` REJECTION (`plan-validator.js:2162-2165`) is **unchanged** — the same structural asymmetry as #539's `--finalize-check` `--base` (a per-node barrier cannot be laundered via `--base`). **Canonical `commands/*.md` edit propagating to the codex/gitlab/gitea twins → CARRIES the #307 four-chain obligation** (`npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}`, run sequentially). Both were filed by #539's finalization-summary (R1→#540, R2→#541); landed as one same-scope bundle (`bundle-540-541`) on the shared `feature/opencode-support` branch.

- **finalize + opencode-edition: two additive #539 surfaces — (1) `cmdFinalize --base <ref>` forwards a cross-edition attribution base to the whole-plan `--finalize-check`, unblocking per-issue finalization on a shared/multi-issue branch; (2) the opencode runtime edition becomes adaptive-by-default at the router level via a generation-time transform (mechanism B, additive — no four-chain obligation) (#539).** **(1) finalize `--base` (cross-edition, `scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js` ×4).** `cmdFinalize` now forwards a `--base <ref>` to the plan-validator's whole-plan `--finalize-check` attribution sweep, sourced from the `--base <ref>` flag and/or the `KAOLA_FINALIZE_BASE` env var (flag wins), **defaulting to unset** (→ the validator's `main` default — byte-equivalent to today for branch-per-issue runs, so existing tests stay green). This unblocks per-issue finalization on a **shared/multi-issue branch**: pass `--base <project-merge-base>` (or `--base HEAD` for an in-place run whose own changes are already verified by the chain receipt) so the sweep attributes only the project's own diff instead of every change accumulated on the shared branch. The per-node `--barrier-check` anti-laundering guard is **unchanged** — `--base` is structurally rejected with `--node-id` (feeding base = a node's post-write tree would empty the diff and neuter the gate, the #368 vacuous-pass hole), so a caller cannot launder a per-node barrier with `--base`. **(2) opencode edition path-flip (mechanism B, additive).** On the opencode runtime edition, **adaptive is now the unconditional default** — the Path Intent / adaptive-switch-resolution step (`## Startup Step 0a-1 — Path Intent`) and the adapt repair-loop "downgrade to full path" auto-fallback wording are **stripped at generation time** by an opencode-only transform in `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js` (`transformCommandBody`); canonical `commands/*.md` is **untouched** (the transform runs only inside the opencode `renderCommand` output, so it cannot collide with #538's in-flight canonical edits). `install-opencode.sh` path-selection opt-ins (`--with-fast`/`--with-full`) are **scoped out**: the opencode installer is standalone and deploys the **full** command set, so a user gets adaptive-by-default router behavior regardless of which commands are present — the install-time command-set partition is orthogonal to the (already-delivered) router-prose flip, and full `--with-fast`/`--with-full` parity rides a later issue without colliding. Per the additive-edition rule (D-530-02), this opencode-only diff triggers **no** #307 four-chain obligation; its own suite (`node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`) covers it.
- **router/installer: adaptive is now the UNCONDITIONAL default — the adaptive on/off switch (`enable_adaptive`) and every automatic fallback between paths are retired; `fast`/`full` become install-time opt-ins via `--with-fast` / `--with-full` (#538).** The orchestrator no longer deliberates the workflow path on every run: `commands/workflow-next.md` Step 0a-1 loses its entire "Path Intent" switch-resolution sub-step and Branch A (the switch-OFF menu + the auto-fast trivial-fix rubric + the level-4 structure question), collapsing to a single unconditional flow — a no-keyword run goes straight to adaptive with **no path-selection step**. The switch axis flips from "adaptive on/off" to "which extra paths are installed": the config field `enable_adaptive: <bool>` becomes `installed_paths: <array>` (a subset of `["fast","full"]`; adaptive is implicit-always and never in the array; default `[]`). `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical) replaces `resolveEnableAdaptive`/`WORKFLOW_PATHS_NO_ADAPTIVE`/`ENABLE_ADAPTIVE_{FIELD,ENV}` with `resolveInstalledPaths(config)` + `INSTALLED_PATHS_FIELD`, and `isLegalWorkflowPath(value, installedPaths)` makes adaptive legal unconditionally. `kaola-workflow-claim.js` (+ 3 forge ports) refuses a `KAOLA_PATH` naming a non-installed path with the typed `path_not_installed` (`result: refuse`) — never a silent adaptive substitution and never a crash (#44/AC7) — renames the bundle refusal `target_set_not_adaptive` → `bundle_requires_adaptive`, collapses `cmdAuthoringAllowed` to always-allowed, and defaults all three claim-default sites to `adaptive`. `install.sh` ships adaptive-only by default, adds `--with-fast`/`--with-full` (re-install UNIONS `installed_paths`, never removes; the stale-file cleanup spares installed paths), warns-and-ignores the retired `--enable-adaptive`, and `uninstall.sh` removes the shared config so reset = uninstall→reinstall (back to adaptive-only). `kaola-workflow-adapt.md`'s exhaustion floor drops "downgrade to full path" — adaptive failure routes to bounded planner repair → discard+restart → stop+ask, never an automatic downgrade. The #400 routing prose change propagates across all six surfaces (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs) + the forge command/full/fast-entry copies; the route-reachability contract (`test-route-reachability.js` + the four `validate-*-contracts.js`) is re-pinned to the new vocabulary. Cross-edition (#307): schema byte-identical ×4, claim/classifier forge ports rename-normalized, contract-validator byte pair — all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. See `docs/decisions/D-538-01.md` (supersedes `0007-adaptive-default-under-switch-on.md`).
- **opencode-edition: landed all five #530-audit follow-ups as one adaptive bundle (#534, #532, #535, #536, #533), closing the audit's defect/decision table for the additive opencode runtime edition.** Five issues, one frozen DAG (n1→n2→n3 sequenced for the shared generated-command + test surface; n4/n5 independent; opus code-review gate; all-or-nothing sink), executed worktree-isolated on `workflow/bundle-532-533-534-535-536`. **#534** — rewrote the three leaked Claude-specific constructs the generator (`scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js transformCommandBody`) was emitting verbatim into the generated `.opencode/command/*.md`: a structurally-scoped `Agent(`→`task(` dispatch-card rewrite (anchored to the `subagent_type=` card opener, so prose/inline-code/headings cannot match), a `Claude Code agent(s)`→`subagent$1` prose rule, and the `## Agent Model Badge`→`## Effort Variant Resolution` heading rename; 12 files regenerated, 0× residual leaks, generator `--check` parity green. **#532 (D-530-01)** — added a 20-assertion content-reachability block (A15–A21) to `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`, asserting PIN/literal wiring-token PRESENCE across the 6 generated commands (mirrors `test-route-reachability.js` T5–T11 on the opencode surface; GREEN-on-arrival characterization suite, deliberately NOT joining the forge T-sets per D-530-01); test count 223→243. **#535** — plugin portability: threaded opencode `sessionID`/`callID` into the dispatch-log `agent_id` (`.opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js`), hardened A11's `node --check` against Node <22.12 via a transient `.mjs` copy (no new tracked ESM infra — the committed-`package.json` path is blocked by the nested `.opencode/.gitignore`), and added a structural `reasoningEffort:'max'` config-shape assertion (S1); 243→244. **#536** — decoupled `test-claim-hardening.js` from a contributor's global `$HOME` config: `kaola-workflow-classifier.js`'s `parallel_mode` bypass now honors a `KAOLA_FORCE_CLASSIFY=1` test override (mirrors the existing `KAOLA_CLASSIFIER_*` env-seam idiom; production bypass semantics preserved since real users never set it), byte-mirrored to the codex twin; the full claude chain is green UNDER A HOSTILE `parallel_mode:on` config (TDD RED→GREEN). **#533** — made the opencode edition discoverable: README `### Choose an edition` entry, `docs/README.md` index link, and a CLAUDE.md Documentation Map + Validation Policy (D-530-02) note clarifying opencode is an additive runtime edition (not a forge; not wired into `npm test`/`edition-sync`/`install.sh`/the SIX routing surfaces; an opencode-only diff triggers no #307 four-chain obligation). Verification: opus code-review gate `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; `test-opencode-edition.js` (244), the full `test:kaola-workflow:claude` chain, `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`, `validate-script-sync.js` (byte-twin parity), and `sync-opencode-edition.js --check` all green. **Tracked follow-up #537**: the planner/generated-commands still surface Claude-specific `sonnet`/`opus` tier LABELS (the opencode edition should present neutral effort-tier names) and the dispatch envelope resolves `opencode_variant: null` — filed as a distinct opencode-edition surface defect (not a blanket rename, since claude/codex consume those tokens as literal models). The gitlab/gitea classifier ports carry the same `parallel_mode` bypass and need the `KAOLA_FORCE_CLASSIFY` override mirrored in a separate forge-edition follow-up.
- **audit(opencode): the opencode runtime edition (added additively on `feature/opencode-support`, commit 77e88c38) is verified solid, reliable, and correctly aligned with the claude/codex/gitlab/gitea editions — as a deliberately *additive* runtime edition, not forge machinery (#530).** A #486 Case-A investigation DAG (3 concurrent read-only probes → planner synthesis → adversarial verifier + main-session E2E → doc converge) audited all 10 dimensions of the issue checklist. Findings: the edition is correct-by-construction — byte-identical generation parity (`test-opencode-edition.js` A6), complete reasoning/top-tier set (`topTierRoles()` = canonical-opus ∪ `agents/profiles/higher/` = 6 roles; standard = 9), hooks adapter covering all 4 canonical behaviors with matching `tool_input.command`/`tool_input.file_path`/`agent_type` payloads, a `kaola_script()` resolver that works end-to-end for opencode consumers with no `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`, and a schema-valid `opencode.json` (the provider-agnostic neutral template is the committed canonical form; the `--adapt` personalization is user-owned and must NOT be committed — A7). **No regression**: all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green under a clean HOME (n3 verifier). Two decisions recorded: **D-530-01** — extend `test-opencode-edition.js` with content-reachability assertions (an empirically-verified gap: a `transformCommandBody` edit stripping PIN/literal wiring tokens passes all 223 assertions, since A6 compares generated↔renderer and A9 is existence-only), rather than joining the forge-scoped T4–T11 T-sets; **D-530-02** — opencode stays additive (NOT wired into `npm test`/`edition-sync`/`install.sh`), because it is a runtime-not-forge edition (5 independent signals) and the `node --check` ESM fragility (fails under Node <22.12 without `"type":"module"`) would couple a non-forge portability quirk into forge Finalization. The strongest evidence for end-to-end reliability: this audit run IS a live opencode cycle (claim → planner-authored+frozen plan → plan-run node lifecycle → state persisted). Deliverables: `docs/audits/opencode-edition-audit.md` + `docs/decisions/D-530-01.md` + `docs/decisions/D-530-02.md`; concrete defects (content-reachability gap, docs discoverability, leaked `Agent(`/Claude prose, ESM portability) filed as follow-up issues. No code change in this run.
- **tests: the four `test:kaola-workflow:*` chains now run CONCURRENTLY on a multi-core host, cutting the cross-edition `npm test` makespan ~49% (measured 23.4 min → 12.0 min, 1.95× on an 18-core box); a constrained host falls back to serial (#529).** `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` replaces its serial `spawnSync` dispatch loop with a **core-count-gated** concurrent `spawn` pool. The four chains are independent OS process trees (separate `$TMPDIR`, read-only config — proven race-safe + attribution-preserving in #528/D-528-01), so on a host with core headroom they overlap and the makespan collapses from sum-of-chains toward **max-of-chains** (the slowest single chain — here claude at ~720s, with codex/gitlab/gitea running entirely in its shadow). This is the gated opt-in D-528-01 anticipated but declined to ship: rather than prove a universal ≤4-core win (which an 18-core host cannot), the dispatch **gates on core count** — `resolveConcurrency(env, cpuCount, chainCount)` stays serial below 8 cores (the ≤4-core case D-528-01 could not prove safe), else runs `min(chains, floor(cores/2))` concurrently — so a constrained host is structurally never at risk and the multi-core dev box gets the win now. Override with `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_CONCURRENCY` (`auto` | `serial` | `<N>`). Preserved exactly: canonical `KNOWN_CHAINS` receipt ordering despite out-of-order completion (re-sort), per-chain `exitCode`/`duration_ms`, the `--accept-known-red` waiver, a clean aggregate exit, the 900s per-chain timeout (a manual `setTimeout`→kill, since `spawn` has no `timeout` option), and the 6-field receipt schema; the serial path stays the byte-equivalent default/fallback. A failed chain's captured output is surfaced (concurrent runs aren't watchable live). Cross-edition (#307): codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea rename-normalized; all four chains green. See the amendment in `docs/decisions/D-528-01.md`.
- **tests: revisited cross-chain (C1) parallelism on the four-chain `npm test` frame that D-526-01 left as "SAFE but FUTILE" — the ideal win is real and attribution is structurally clean, but the contended-host win cannot be measured on an 18-core box, so serial four-chain dispatch stays; no concurrent test runner ships (#528).** D-526-01 refuted the walkthrough-internal dispatcher (C3) and labelled cross-chain parallelism (C1) futile *for the single claude chain*. #528 reopened C1 on the broader gate — running `claude && codex && gitlab && gitea` concurrently instead of serially (serial Σ ≈ 25-28 min, concurrent ideal ≈ max-of-chains ≈ ~10 min). This run was a #486 Case-B read-only shaping investigation with **real timed experiments**. It established that the four-chain frame is genuinely **not** futile the way single-chain C1 was: the ideal-core makespan win is a real ~460-860s, comfortably above the realistic ~232s (light) / ~395s (heavy) serial-jitter bar, and chain-level first-failure attribution is **structurally clean** — 0 mismatches across 5000 trials over all 24 completion orderings through a `KNOWN_CHAINS.indexOf` re-sort (the receipt consumers are set-wise and order-independent regardless), so whole-chain granularity avoids the C3 misattribution that sank the prior candidate. **But the make-or-break — whether that win survives contention on a ≤4-core host — cannot be settled on the available 18-core machine:** macOS has no hard core-cap (no taskset/cgroups/cpuset), and empirically background load did not invert the win and the ≤4-core oversubscription-inversion regime was un-reproducible, so every favorable number is the forbidden non-transferable upper bound. Under accuracy precedence #1 (an un-settleable affirmative win-claim is refuted, not "probably fine"), the ship-it verdict is not finalizable here. **No code change** — the deliverable is the decision record `docs/decisions/D-528-01.md`, which records the measurements and an explicit reopen condition: a benchmark on a contended ≤4-core host (real runner / constrained VM / cores-disabled box, not an 18-core dev box) showing a median four-chain win decisively exceeding 2× the serial-jitter band while preserving deterministic ordered chain-level attribution. The zero-risk makespan lever available today is **selective execution** — `run-chains.js` already honors `--chains <list>` / `--only`, so a contributor who touched only some editions runs only those chains. The `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS` ceiling (D-512-01, 900s) remains the correct operational guard.

### Fixed

- **opencode-edition: the generated opencode command/agent prose no longer leaks Claude-specific `opus`/`sonnet` tier-label words, and a declared node `model:` now resolves a concrete `opencode_variant` for the active provider instead of falling through to `null`/`role_default` (#537).** Two disjoint opencode-edition-local surfaces, fixed as parallel siblings → shared opus code-review gate → sink. **Surface 1 (prose leak, a #534-class leak):** every `opus`/`sonnet` tier-label occurrence in `.opencode/command/*.md` + `.opencode/agent/workflow-planner.md` is produced by `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js`; a STRUCTURALLY-SCOPED rewrite of the five generator string constants (`OPENCODE_BADGE_BLOCK`'s `mapTier` line, the three `transformCommandBody` rewrite strings, and `opencodeAgentSuffix`) replaces the tier-label words with neutral labels (`reasoning-tier`/`standard-tier`), then `sync-opencode-edition.js --write` regenerates the 10 commands + the planner agent. The `mapTier(tier, provider)` sentence becomes self-describing without Claude nouns. A new S2 content-reachability block in `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (mirroring the #532 A15–A21 style, section-scoped + word-bounded so canonical `(Opus)` model-name mentions and "octopus" prose are NOT false hits) asserts the neutral labels are present and the tier-label words absent; acceptance grep `rg -wn "opus|sonnet" .opencode/command/` → 0. **Surface 2 (dispatch variant unresolved):** `dispatchEffortOpencode` in the ×4 byte-identical `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` drift anchor now resolves a concrete `opencode_variant` (e.g. `max`/`high`) for the active provider when a node declares `model: opus|sonnet`, via a new pure `resolveOpencodeProvider(env)` reading `KAOLA_OPENCODE_INHERIT_MODEL` (the established opencode inherited-model convention `detectInheritModel`/`parseModelProvider` already consume) — closing the silent `role_default` gap where `buildDispatch` never populated `ctx.opencode_provider`. New D451 cases 6–9 in `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` cover the env-resolved concrete variant; the existing null-branch (cases 1–3) and explicit-provider (cases 4–5) cases stay green. **Canonical-vocabulary preserved (the architectural constraint):** `NODE_MODEL_TIERS = Object.freeze(['opus','sonnet'])` and `TIER_RANK` are UNCHANGED — claude/codex editions consume `opus`/`sonnet` as literal model names via `dispatchEffort` (the codex twin), NEVER `dispatchEffortOpencode`, so both surfaces are behavior-inert in those editions; only the opencode DISPLAYED surface translates, never the internal tokens. The frozen plan's own `model:` column keeps `sonnet`/`opus`. **Verification:** `test-opencode-edition.js` 278 assertions green; `test-adaptive-node.js` 1018 assertions green; `validate-script-sync.js` 25 byte-identical groups green (the ×4 schema copies remain byte-identical); `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` EXIT 0. Opencode edition is additive (D-530-02) — NOT wired into `npm test`/`edition-sync.js`/`install.sh`, so this opencode-only diff triggers no #307 four-chain obligation (its own suite, `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js`, is the gate). CI/CD is not a gate (#501).

## [6.6.2] - 2026-06-18

### Changed

- **tests: resolved the #523-deferred test-suite parallelism design track — serial execution is the right permanent posture; no concurrent test runner ships (#526).** D-523-01 deferred the one remaining test-chain makespan lever (race-safe parallelism) to a dedicated, risk-bounded design effort. This run instantiated that effort as a #486 Case-B read-only shaping investigation (probe → assume → adversarially falsify → converge) with **real timed experiments**, and refuted it. The leading candidate (a walkthrough-internal N-worker dispatcher over the 231 self-contained scenarios) delivered a best-case *warm* makespan win of only **4.45s / 14.4% — inside the measured ~24.5% serial jitter band** (median 43.22s, stddev ≈3.9s ⇒ a >7.8s bar), **inverted to ~2× slower under oversubscription** (N=36 on an 18-core host; a 4-core CI/laptop enters that regime at N=12), and **lost deterministic first-failure attribution** (naive "first-to-complete" mis-attributed the failing unit in 4/5 reps); it also carries the largest #307 six-copy-walkthrough propagation surface. The race proxy was clean across 26 reps but only proves process-per-scenario filesystem isolation (already structurally evident), not a real dispatcher's in-process safety. Under accuracy precedence #1 (a flaky gate is the most expensive outcome, strictly worse than a slow-but-correct one), no candidate proved **both** race-safety **and** a decisive contended-host win, so serial stays. **No code change** — the deliverable is the decision record `docs/decisions/D-526-01.md` (continuing the D-523 series), which records the measurements and the explicit two-part reopen condition. The `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS` ceiling (D-512-01, 900s) remains the correct operational guard for the genuinely-slow-but-passing chain.

### Fixed

- **tests: the gitlab/gitea `adaptive-node` `orient` smoke tests no longer leak an untracked `kaola-workflow/nonexistent-{gl,gt}-445-test/.cache/` scratch tree into the working tree on every chain run (#527).** The `--summary` smoke test (the #446 anchor) ran `orient --project nonexistent-{gl,gt}-445-test --json --summary` from the repo cwd; `orient`, though documented read-only, materializes `kaola-workflow/<project>/.cache/orient-envelope.json` even for a nonexistent project, so each `test:kaola-workflow:gitlab` / `test:kaola-workflow:gitea` run left two untracked scratch project folders behind that the chain never cleaned up — surfaced during the #524 four-chain finalize gate, where the contractor's staging guard excluded them and they were cleaned manually before sink. Fix (test-local, narrowest blast radius): each test now runs its `orient --summary` spawn in a `$TMPDIR`-rooted cwd (via the file's own `tempRoot(...)` helper) and removes it in a `finally`, so the read-only-orient scratch lands in tmp and is cleaned up even on assertion failure; the shared `orient` source (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) is untouched (direction c was rejected for blast radius — it would alter the byte-mirrored `--summary` envelope-cache block in all four editions). The `--summary` sentinel assertion (`summaryOut.startsWith('summary:')`, #446) is preserved unchanged. Cross-edition: applied as mirror twins to the gitlab + gitea test ports (`plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/scripts/test-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-scripts.js`, identical modulo the forge noun); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307).

- **issue-scout: the scout now ranks candidate issues by roadmap priority/drive-order FIRST — scope-cohesion and actionability operate as within-tier tiebreaks only, so a lower-priority cluster can never displace the active frontier (#524).** Before this fix, the `issue-scout` agent profile scored candidates on cohesion + actionability alone, with no roadmap-priority axis. This caused live mis-rankings (observed on `vrpai-cli`): the scout picked adjacent environment/SDK issues (vrpai-cli 82, then 652) over the vrpai-cli `488/502/561` epic frontier that the roadmap drives, rationalizing them as "the closest actionable proxy" — a silent substitution that violated the drive-order the `### Project rules` guardrails and master-epic `Next Step` ordering encode. Fix: the scout profiles (`agents/issue-scout.md` + the three `.toml` byte-identical forge ports) now make priority/drive-order the **hard tier** — the scout extracts `### Project rules` guardrails and the per-issue `next_step:` ordering emitted by `kaola-workflow-roadmap.js` as its primary signal; scope-cohesion and actionability rank only *within* that priority tier. A documented `### Project rules` guardrail (e.g. "X must not preempt the correctness frontier Y") is honored as a hard constraint, not a suggestion. When the top-priority frontier issue is genuinely blocked or unverifiable, the scout now says so EXPLICITLY (a "frontier blocked because…" basis statement) and only then falls to the next-priority actionable item — replacing the prior silent actionable-proxy substitution. A new required output field `priority_basis` reconciles the pick against the roadmap (sub-fields: `frontier`, `pick_vs_frontier`, `guardrails_honored`) and is consistent across the `.md` and `.toml` output surfaces. Pure agent-instruction-prose change; no code path. Cross-edition: the scout profile edit propagates to all four editions (the `.toml` ports are byte-identical to the prose blocks governed by them); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains apply (#307). See `docs/decisions/D-524-01.md`.

## [6.6.1] - 2026-06-18

### Fixed

- **codex/hooks: the installer no longer writes a top-level `$schema` into `~/.codex/hooks.json` — Codex's strict hooks parser rejected the whole file, silently dropping every Kaola-Workflow Codex hook; latent since #284 (#525).** The Codex agent-profile installer (`install-codex-agent-profiles.js`) carried a top-level `"$schema": "…claude-code-settings.json"` editor-hint into the written `~/.codex/hooks.json`. Codex's hooks-config parser is strict (Rust serde, `deny_unknown_fields`) and accepts **only** a top-level `hooks` key, so it rejected the entire file at parse time (`failed to parse hooks config …: unknown field $schema, expected hooks`) — dropping *all four* managed Codex hooks (compact-resume, pre-commit guard, write-lane, subagent-dispatch-log). `$schema` was introduced in #284 and #325 R2 *hardened* the carry (with tests asserting the installed file ships `$schema`), so Codex hooks had **never** actually loaded since #284. The premise that Codex's `hooks.json` schema equals Claude's `settings.json` schema is false on this point — `settings.json` tolerates `$schema`, Codex's `hooks.json` does not. Claude is unaffected: its hooks merge into `~/.claude/settings.json`, which accepts `$schema`. Fix: `mergeHooks()` now emits exactly `{ hooks }` — dropping the top-level carry. This both stops introducing `$schema` **and self-heals** an existing `~/.codex/hooks.json` a prior install poisoned (the installer reads the old file with `JSON.parse`, which tolerates `$schema`, then re-writes it clean). The R3 orphan-sweep and non-managed-entry preservation are intact. The `$schema` line is also removed from the three `config/hooks.json` templates for hygiene. Verified empirically against the real `codex doctor` (codex-cli 0.140): the stripped `{ hooks }` file — including entry-level `id`/`description`/`matcher`/event names — parses with no warning, confirming no second rejection one field deeper. The #325-R2 `$schema`-carry assertions in the codex/gitlab/gitea test suites are flipped to assert the corrected contract (output has only the `hooks` key; an existing `$schema` self-heals). Cross-edition: `mergeHooks` applied byte-identically across the three installer copies; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307). Mission #524 (issue-scout roadmap-priority gap) was filed alongside but is not addressed here.

## [6.6.0] - 2026-06-18

### Fixed

- **sink: `archive_commit` no longer commits the transient crash-resume journals (`sink-receipt.json` / `sink-fallback.json`) into the default branch on every direct `--sink` cycle (#520).** The archive_commit step staged the whole archive band with a directory-wide pathspec (`git add -- 'kaola-workflow/archive/<project>/'`), sweeping the disposable transaction journals into `main` and forcing a recurring manual cleanup commit after each sink. These journals are crash-resume scratch paper — they must persist on disk (read off the working tree by the #429 crash-resume path and the #484 freshness guard) but must never be tracked. Fix: **both** the `git add` and the `git commit -- <pathspec>` in archive_commit now carry `:(exclude)kaola-workflow/archive/<project>/.cache/sink-receipt.json` and `:(exclude).../sink-fallback.json` pathspecs — the commit-side exclude is load-bearing because `git commit -- <pathspec>` re-sweeps an already-tracked, modified journal independently of the index. The on-disk journal is untouched, so crash-resume still works (`testSinkTransactionCrashResume` stays green). New RED→GREEN tracked-status coverage (after a clean sink, `git ls-files` shows no committed journal while the on-disk file survives) in `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (`testSinkTransactionCleanEndToEnd`) plus the GitLab/Gitea sink tests. Cross-edition: applied byte-identically to the codex twin and rename-normalized to the GitLab/Gitea sink ports; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307). A follow-up (#521) investigated hardening the regression test to also bite an add-only half-fix and proved that bite is unreachable via the live flow — see the #521 entry.

- **sink: documented that the `archive_commit` commit-side `:(exclude)` is defensive against an unreachable state; no regression test is constructable for it (#521).** Follow-up to #520. A git-level reachability matrix + scratch probes proved an add-only half-fix (dropping only the commit-side exclude) is behaviorally indistinguishable from the full fix in every state the live `--sink` flow can reach: the commit-side exclude only matters for an *already-tracked, modified* journal at archive_commit, but a pre-tracked archive band makes `archiveProjectDir` suffix the destination to `<project>.archived-<ts>/` (`claim.js:1824-1825`), so no non-receipt change lands in the commit pathspec and `archive_commit` never fires with a tracked journal; a clean band leaves the journal untracked, and `git commit -- <pathspec>` never sweeps an untracked file. The **add-side** exclude is the load-bearing fix for the real recurring bug; the commit-side exclude is correct *defensive* code guarding a state the current flow cannot produce. Resolution (per the accuracy-first "document the contradiction, do not reframe-to-pass" discipline): rather than ship a green-but-vacuous test, an inline `#521` comment now marks the commit-side exclude defensive-only in all four sink-merge editions so it is not dropped as redundant. Comment-only across the four editions (codex twin kept byte-identical); zero behavior change.

- **finalize: `cmdFinalize` now gates on the existing `--finalize-check` before making any irreversible side effect — the archive commit, worktree removal, or issue close can only proceed when the gate passes (#522).** Before this fix, the archive commit was unconditional: `cmdFinalize` called `archiveProjectDir` regardless of whether the adaptive-path validation gate had been satisfied. The `--finalize-check` gate (in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`) enforces dual-mode validation — self-host (npm) mode requires a `chain-receipt.json` bound to HEAD that is fresh and all-green; consumer (non-npm) mode requires `.cache/final-validation.md` with a column-0 `verdict: pass`; both modes run the attribution sweep (B). This gate was intended to be run by the contractor (Step 8c) before committing. However, the contractor is a subagent dispatched via a Bash tool call: `run-chains` takes ~15 minutes for the `claude` chain, which exceeds the Bash 600s ceiling, so the call was auto-backgrounded and the subagent could not await it across turns — the receipt was never produced and `cmdFinalize` committed with no verified receipt (a fail-OPEN of a fail-CLOSED gate). This was observed live during the #520 run. Fix: `cmdFinalize` (all four `claim.js` editions) now shells `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js --finalize-check --json` immediately before `archiveProjectDir`. The gate fires unconditionally for both the `--keep-worktree` worktree path and the in-place path, when a `workflow-plan.md` is present (adaptive run); non-adaptive runs (no plan) are unaffected. On any non-`pass` result, `cmdFinalize` returns `{ result: 'refuse', reason: 'finalize_gate_unverified', inner_reason: <inner>, operator_hint: <hint>, errors: [...] }` with exit 1, leaving no archive commit. Procedure change (n2): receipt *generation* via `run-chains` moves to the main session/orchestrator (which can await the long backgrounded run); the contractor's Step 8c is demoted from generate to verify-or-fail-closed — propagated to `contractor.md` + 3 `contractor.toml` + the 6 `#400` finalize surfaces. `plan-validator.js` and `run-chains.js` are unchanged; only the caller gates. Cross-edition: the gate is applied byte-identically to the codex twin and the GitLab/Gitea claim.js ports; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307). See `docs/decisions/D-522-01.md`.

## [6.5.0] - 2026-06-17

### Fixed

- **enforcement coverage: three fail-closed guards / cross-edition parity contracts that the green `npm` suite did not actually enforce are now machine-enforced (#505).** A safety guard the test suite never executes is worthless — these close that class. **(1) FOREIGN_ARCHIVE staging guard** (the Phase-6 archive-pollution guard, bash-in-markdown in `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` + the two forge mirrors) had ZERO coverage — `npm test` never runs bash-in-markdown, and it already shipped a fail-OPEN regression once (#294). Now a substring pin in all four contract validators asserts each edition's guard text is present, plus a `test-bash-block-guards.js` case extracts the real Staging Guard block, runs it against a foreign `archive/<other>/` band, and asserts fail-closed (`exit 1`). **(2) T5 route-reachability self-disarm** — the 6-surface (#400) `<!-- PIN: frontier unit -->` propagation check in `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` blocked only `if (anyHasPin)`, so removing the pin from all six surfaces passed silently; the else-branch is now a hard `assert(anyHasPin, …)`. **(3) Forge shared-function-presence guard** — the hand-ported `claim`/`sink-merge`/`classifier`/`roadmap`/`repair-state` forge ports had no static shared-function parity check; the gitlab/gitea contract validators now assert the shared safety-function names are present in each edition's ports (generalizing the #492 pin). Each validator pins its OWN edition only; the root↔codex validators stay byte-identical. Every new assert was opus mutation-bite-gated — a mutation was demonstrated to turn the relevant chain RED, then reverted. Cross-edition: all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307).

- **classifier/claim: the `gh`-fetch error-class axis now partitions by stderr error-class, not by process exit code — a transient-infra fault escalates; a genuine negative refuses (#519, foundational axis fix).** Before this fix, every non-zero `gh`/`glab`/`tea` exit in the classifier and at the claim-time `probeIssueState` sites was collapsed to the same determinate `target_unavailable` / `result: refuse` verdict regardless of whether the failure was a TLS handshake timeout, a DNS resolution failure, a rate-limit 429, an ETIMEDOUT, a connection reset, or a genuine 404 / "Could not resolve to an Issue" / closed-issue response. A transient infra blip therefore aborted a claim on a genuinely-claimable issue — the live false-negative this fix corrects. The new partition: a **transient-infra class** (TLS handshake timeout / rate-limit / DNS "could not resolve host" / connection reset / ETIMEDOUT / 5xx) routes to the existing `indeterminate` / `escalate` valve (`target_(set_)indeterminate`, `result: escalate`); a **genuine negative** (404 / "Could not resolve to an Issue" / confirmed-closed) stays a determinate `target_(set_)unavailable` / `result: refuse`; an **unrecognized clean-nonzero** defaults to refuse (escalation requires positive infra evidence). Applied uniformly at four sites: `getRepoOwnerName`/`gh repo view`, classifier `gh issue view`, `claim.js classifySubprocessError`, and `probeIssueState` — plus the claim-comment detection site. `probeIssueState` gains a NON-BREAKING additive discriminant: `.state` is unchanged (`'unavailable'` for genuine/unknown, `'open'`/`'closed'` unchanged); a new `transient: true` field is added only for transient failures, routed to `escalate` only by the claim-acquisition callers; `closure-audit.js` and probe-memo readers are unaffected. Applied uniformly across all four editions (`scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js` + classifier sites ×4). See `docs/decisions/D-519-01.md`.

- **classifier: forge classifiers' exit-0-with-malformed-body now converges on `indeterminate` (not `target_unavailable`), mirroring root's in-`try` SyntaxError path (#510, forge parity fix).** The forge classifiers' `_st` guard returned `indeterminate` only on exit-non-zero; an exit-0 with an unparseable or empty body fell through `parseJson`'s silent swallow and produced a determinate `target_unavailable` — a wrong verdict for a structural fetch anomaly, diverging from root's behavior where `JSON.parse(raw)` inside the fetch `try` block correctly routes a `SyntaxError` as transient. Fix: when `parseJson` swallows a malformed or empty exit-0 body (returns `null`/`undefined`), the forge classifier `_st` guard now returns `indeterminate` rather than falling through to a determinate verdict. Root needed no change. Affects the gitlab and gitea classifier editions. See `docs/decisions/D-519-01.md` § Known limitations / F3.

- **tests: gitlab+gitea end-to-end claim-flow tests now pin a genuine-negative `clean_nonzero` (real 404 / "Could not resolve to an Issue" stderr) → `result: refuse`, not a generic "gh exits 1" (#511, forge determinate-refuse test-pin).** The #507/#519 axis fix changed the routing of an unrecognized `clean_nonzero`: it now defaults to refuse (positive infra evidence required for escalate). Forge end-to-end tests that previously used a generic "gh exits 1" stub no longer unambiguously cover the determinate-refuse path. Fix: the gitlab and gitea claim-flow tests now stub a GENUINE-negative stderr pattern (real 404 / "Could not resolve to an Issue") so the `clean_nonzero` → `result: refuse` route is pinned and will fail if the axis logic regresses. `scripts/test-kaola-workflow-gitlab-claim.js` + `scripts/test-kaola-workflow-gitea-claim.js`.

- **finalize: bundle `cmdFinalize` now reports `remote_issue_closed: close_pending` (not `partial`) on the merge/keep-worktree lane when all members are still open pre-push, aligning bundle deferral semantics with the single-issue path; the genuinely-mixed post-push partial-failure case still reports `partial` (#508).** `scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js` ×4 editions + `scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js`. See `docs/decisions/D-508-01.md`.

- **sink: `sink-merge` now probes live issue state after `push_main` on a keep-open run and reopens the issue if GitHub's commit-keyword auto-close fired, recording `remote_issue_closed: reopened_after_autoclose` — a deterministic guarantee that a keep-open run stays open regardless of forge-side auto-close (#517).** `scripts/kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` ×4 editions + forge sink tests. See `docs/decisions/D-517-01.md`.

- **sink: `loadOrInitReceipt` now stamps `branch_head: <branch-tip-sha>` at init and reinitializes on a fresh cycle when the on-disk receipt's `branch_head` is absent, mismatched, or not an ancestor of the current tip — preventing a stale prior-cycle all-done receipt from producing a false `status:sinked` without a real merge; the #484 ancestry backstop is preserved (#518).** `scripts/kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` ×4 editions. See `docs/decisions/D-518-01.md`.

- **adaptive-node: three integrity hardenings to the per-node lifecycle aggregator (#498, #499, #516).** All in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (canonical + the codex twin + the two forge ports, regenerated via `edition-sync.js`). **#498 (HIGH) — write co-open no longer engages on `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` alone.** Previously the co-open gate fired on lane-containment alone, leaving `legs=null`, so the close path ran the attribution-blind snapshot union barrier — which passes a cross-member overwrite when leg-isolation is off. Fix: `runOpenReady` now factors a single `legCoupled = resolveLegIsolation(env) && opts.writeOverlapConsent` used at BOTH the co-open gate (`containment && legCoupled && writeNodes ≥ 2`) and the leg-provision gate, establishing the invariant `groupForm ⟺ legs provisioned ⟺ safe close path`; a `groupCeiling < 2` case now serial-degrades (honoring `KAOLA_FANOUT_CAP=1`). Both off-combos (containment-only, leg-isolation-only-without-consent) serial-degrade; only the full conjunction co-opens. The deeper union-barrier rewrite in `plan-validator.js` is deliberately NOT taken (over-engineering, per the issue). Mutation-verified non-vacuous. **#499 (HIGH) — the serial `open-next` path now has the `plan_hash` integrity gate** the fused/batch paths already had: `runOpenNext` passes `integrity: true` to its mutation-guard prologue, so a tampered frozen plan is refused (`plan_integrity_failed`, zero mutation) on the serial resume path too. Proven against the REAL validator subprocess (freeze→tamper→refuse), not a mock. **#516 (LOW) — the dispatch packet's `evidence_file` is now project-qualified** (`kaola-workflow/<project>/.cache/<id>.md`) so a role subagent no longer mis-resolves the bare `.cache/<id>.md` to the worktree root → spurious `write_set_overflow`. The misleading plan-run prose (the write-parallelism activation now correctly states the full conjunction) is updated across all 6 plan-run surfaces, and the `#499` recovery card `docs/plan-run-cards/resume.md` is rewritten to cite the REAL `orient` fields (`resumeCheck.ok`, `inProgressNode`, `cacheState`, `requires_redispatch`, `allDone`) instead of fabricated ones. Cross-edition: all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307); new RED→GREEN coverage in `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`.

- **routing: adaptive is now a non-bypassable default under an ON switch — a reciprocal front-door guard, the sibling of `authoring-allowed` (#515).** Under an ON adaptive switch the path is contract-determined (adaptive is the default; `fast`/`full` are explicit user escapes only — #254), and adaptive *authoring* was already hard-guarded at the front door by `kaola-workflow-claim.js authoring-allowed` (#235). But there was **no reciprocal guard** refusing a `fast`/`full` claim under an ON switch reached *without* an explicit opt-in, so the contract was **bypassable** by any agent reaching the fast/full surface directly (not via `/workflow-next`) — and even when it landed on adaptive, the agent could burn tokens *selecting* a contract-determined path. Two complementary, owner-endorsed levers (cheapest-sufficient #3; Agent-Owns-Reasoning #44 — the guard validates/fails-closed, never selects; planner-first #287 — a front-door switch check, not a planner gate; accuracy #1 — every boundary preserved): **(1) Script guard.** `claimProject` (all four claim.js editions) now returns a typed `path_requires_explicit_opt_in` refusal when `adaptiveEnabled && pathWasDefaulted`, where `pathWasDefaulted = !args.workflowPath && !process.env.KAOLA_PATH`. Because `requestedPath = args.workflowPath || process.env.KAOLA_PATH || 'full'`, a *defaulted* path always collapses to `'full'` — so "defaulted fast" is unconstructable and no `requestedPath` membership test is needed. Three boundaries preserved and tested: switch-OFF default→full stays intact (Branch A relies on `|| 'full'`; the guard is gated on `adaptiveEnabled`); explicit `KAOLA_PATH=fast|full` / `--workflow-path fast|full` under ON stays allowed (the router exports `KAOLA_PATH` on every switch-ON route, so the legitimate "full path" escape passes); adaptive + bundle claims pass `--workflow-path` explicitly and are unaffected. The refusal mirrors the existing `workflow_path_refused` family shape, forge-neutral, with the edition-correct issue local (`issueNumber` root/codex, `issueIid` gitlab/gitea). **(2) Prose floor.** The switch-ON contract ("adaptive is the default; path selection is a non-decision; do NOT orient / deliberate / advisor-consult to choose a path, and do NOT self-route to fast/full on issue size; fast/full require an explicit user path-name escape") is added to the 12 fast+full-entry surfaces (#400 six-per-path), carrying a `<!-- PIN: adaptive-default-contract -->` comment + the `path_requires_explicit_opt_in` literal, machine-pinned by a new fail-closed **T11** block in `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` so it cannot drift. **Test-hermeticity fix:** the guard refuses *every* defaulted bare `startup` under an ON switch (not just the bypass), which exposed that all six walkthrough harnesses non-hermetically inherited the developer's `~/.config` switch state; each now defaults `KAOLA_ENABLE_ADAPTIVE='0'` (explicit switch-ON sub-tests still win), making the suite deterministic regardless of local config. New RED→GREEN coverage in `scripts/test-claim-hardening.js` (6 cases). Cross-edition: touches all four editions; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green (#307). See `docs/decisions/D-515-01.md` and `docs/conventions.md` § Switch-ON path guard.

## [6.4.0] - 2026-06-16

### Fixed

- **run-chains: the per-chain `spawnSync` kill timeout is now configurable via `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS` (default raised 600000 → 900000), so a passing-but-slow chain is captured instead of false-killed (#512).** `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` hardcoded `timeout: 600000` (10 min) on each chain's `spawnSync`. The `claude` chain now runs ~574s standalone — only ~26s under the ceiling — so with `spawnSync` overhead and environmental jitter it tipped over 600s and was killed mid-run (`exitCode:1, duration_ms:600028`), and the finalize chain-receipt gate (`plan-validator --finalize-check`) then false-blocked `chains_red` for a chain that actually passes. Fix: a new exported pure helper `resolveTimeoutMs(env)` reads `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS` and returns it when it parses to a positive integer, else the **900000** (15 min) default; a non-numeric/zero/negative value falls back to the default (mirrors the `KAOLA_GH_REMOTE_TIMEOUT_MS` parse-or-default pattern). Unlike the forge-API timeout there is **no upper clamp** — a long-running *local* test suite is not a remote-hang risk, so a larger ceiling only makes capture safer. The `.cache/chain-receipt.json` **receipt schema is unchanged** — the knob governs only the kill ceiling. The "speed up the chain" alternative is deferred: the issue records the ~574s slowness is not yet root-caused (genuine suite growth vs. environment), so diagnosing it is the larger, uncertain effort while raising the timeout is the cheapest sufficient capture fix (rationale in `docs/decisions/D-512-01.md`). Cross-edition: the canonical edit applies byte-identically to the codex twin and rename-normalized to the GitLab/Gitea ports (all four kept in lockstep, enforced by `validate-script-sync.js`); new RED→GREEN unit coverage in `scripts/test-run-chains.js`.

- **docs/comments: two cosmetic comment nits from the #500 build review, zero behavior change (#514).** (1) `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (+ the 3 forge ports): the reworded L2 comment framed `working_dir` as staying parent-side "until Slice 3", presenting Slice 3 as still pending — but Slice 3 shipped (#463 AC18 PASS) and "working_dir stays parent-side" is now permanently true (routing is via the surfaced `legPath` manifest + dispatch discipline, not by overwriting the field); reworded to "routing into legs landed in Slice 3 (#463 AC18)". (2) `scripts/test-route-reachability.js`: the T9 block-header comment read `<!-- PIN: speculative-open -->` while the assertion correctly checks (and still checks) `<!-- CARD: speculative-open -->`; the descriptive comment was corrected to `CARD:`. Comment-only — the 4 `adaptive-node.js` editions stay byte-in-sync via `edition-sync.js` (regenerated ports), and `test-route-reachability.js` (146 assertions) + `test-edition-sync.js` (29) stay green. All four editions.

- **roadmap: `generate` no longer silently overwrites a populated mirror with the "No active work" placeholder when `.roadmap/` is empty or missing (#502).** The missing-source guard previously refused only when the entire `.roadmap/` directory was absent; an empty-but-present directory (per-issue sources deleted, only `_rules.md`/`.DS_Store` remaining) slipped through and `generate` replaced the active-work mirror with the placeholder row. Fix: the CLI `generate` path (`cmdGenerate`) now refuses when the per-issue source count is 0 while the prior generated mirror still has active rows, and the in-process `regenerateRoadmap` path (called on finalize/closure) refuses when `.roadmap/` is missing while the mirror has active rows — both fail-closed and reported as `failed`/mirror-preserved by their callers, never a silent wipe. The legitimate close-last-issue empty-out (directory present, last source removed) still empties the mirror cleanly. All four editions.

- **claim: `resume` without `--project` no longer silently selects the alphabetically-first active folder (#503).** Previously `cmdResume` fell back to the first active folder under ambiguity — a hidden script selection of which issue to resume, violating the agent-owns-selection contract (#44). Fix: when `--project` is absent and more than one active folder exists, `resume` emits a typed `resume_ambiguous` refusal listing the candidate folders and forcing the caller to name one. The single-active-folder back-compat path and the explicit `--project` path are unchanged. All four editions.
