- **fast: fast-path Finalization now has a fail-closed compliance backstop, symmetric with the full path (#504).** The fast `summary-write --verdict PASSED` path previously accepted any compliance array without validation, synthesized a fabricated-green `code-reviewer | invoked` row when compliance was omitted, and never enforced delegated review — so a self-review, an `N/A`-without-reason row, or an omitted code-reviewer row could pass fast Finalization with no adversarial review performed. Fix: `runSummaryWrite` now calls the shared `unresolvedCompliance(content, state)` helper and refuses (`fast_compliance_unresolved`) on any unresolved compliance row; the default code-reviewer row is now neutral/pending instead of fabricated-green; and a scope-aware guard requires a real delegation status on multi-file write sets. The shared `repair-state.js` helpers are unchanged, so the full path does not regress. The mandatory-delegated-code-reviewer rule and neutral-default-row guidance propagate across all six fast surfaces and six finalize surfaces (3 commands + 3 SKILLs each). All four editions.

- **sink: `assertWorktreeClean` is now fail-closed on a transient `git status` probe fault (#496).** Before this fix, if the `git status --porcelain` probe that guards `git worktree remove --force` threw (e.g. a held `index.lock`, `EAGAIN`, `EMFILE`), the exception was **silently swallowed** and the worktree was treated as clean — meaning destructive worktree removal could proceed and destroy uncommitted work. Fix: a transient probe fault is now treated as DIRTY (unprovable-clean ⇒ typed refusal). One bounded retry absorbs a momentary fault before refusing; a second consecutive failure throws with a clear operator message identifying the transient fault and instructing resolution before re-running `sink-merge`. Present in all four editions (canonical GitHub / Codex byte-identical twin + hand-ported divergent GitLab and Gitea sink ports).

- **sink: hard `push_main` and `closure` failures no longer report `status:sinked` (#497).** Before this fix, if `git push origin <defBranch>` failed after a successful local FF-merge, or if issue-close failed for one or more members of a bundle, `sink-merge` only emitted a stderr warning and continued — recording the step as `done` in the receipt and falling through to `{result:"ok", status:"sinked"}` exit 0. A re-run then skipped the already-`done` step and never retried. The deliverable could be permanently un-pushed, or the issue permanently un-closed, while the receipt read success. Fix: on a hard `push_main` failure, `sink-merge` records `push_main:"failed"` in the receipt, does NOT `stepDone("push_main")`, and emits `{result:"refuse", reason:"sink_incomplete", step:"push_main", push_main:"failed", …}` with exit 1 (branch preserved for retry). On a hard `closure` failure (at least one issue genuinely could not be closed), it records `remote_issue_closed:"partial"` and `failed_issue_closures:[…]` in the receipt, does NOT `stepDone("closure")`, and emits `{result:"refuse", reason:"sink_incomplete", step:"closure", remote_issue_closed:"partial", closed_issues:[…], failed_issue_closures:[…], …}` with exit 1. A successful close path is byte-equivalent to the prior behavior. Present in all four editions.

- **sink: `assertWorktreeClean`'s OUTER `git worktree list` probe is now fail-closed too (#506).** Companion to #496, which hardened the inner `git status --porcelain` probe. The separate outer `git worktree list --porcelain` probe that locates the linked worktree still had a fail-open `catch (_) { return; }` — a transient fault there returned as if there were no worktree to guard, skipping the entire clean-check before the destructive `git worktree remove --force`. Fix: distinguish "no worktree for this branch" (the loop falling through with no matching block — a legitimate skip) from "the probe itself failed" (cannot prove ⇒ fail closed). A probe fault now retries once (bounded) and then throws a typed refusal rather than silently returning. Present in all four editions (canonical GitHub / Codex byte-identical twin + hand-ported divergent GitLab and Gitea sink ports).

- **classifier: boundary-2 CLI-fetch transient faults now escalate instead of masquerading as `target_unavailable` (#507).** Follow-up to #495, which hardened the boundary-1 `claim.js → classifier` subprocess spawn. The classifier's internal `gh`/`glab`/`tea` issue-fetch `catch` discarded the error and collapsed every fetch failure to a determinate `target_unavailable`, so a one-off CLI blip was indistinguishable from a genuine "issue gone". Fix (faithful to #495's three-bucket taxonomy): the fetch error is now classified (`spawn_fault` / `killed` / `clean_nonzero`); transient classes are retried with bounded backoff (N≤2); a `clean_nonzero` exit is determinate and is NOT retried (still `target_unavailable`); a persistent transient fault emits `verdict: indeterminate`, which the existing #495 envelope and the forge forward-compat handlers route to `result: escalate` (pause and ask the user). Edition-uniform across all four classifiers (canonical + Codex byte-twin + GitLab + Gitea ports).

- **claim/startup: the adaptive starting contract no longer dies at the front door on an opaque, un-retried, transient classifier-subprocess fault (#495).** Previously `kaola-workflow-claim.js startup --workflow-path adaptive` swallowed every non-`status===2` `execFileSync` failure of the classifier subprocess into one static `target_unavailable` / `"classifier failed (subprocess error)"` (`e.status`/`e.signal`/`e.stderr` discarded), so a one-off spawn fault, a timeout/kill, and a permanent packaging bug were indistinguishable — and one transiently-unclassifiable bundle member collapsed the whole set to `target_set_unavailable` before any folder, plan, or worktree existed, leaving a human restart as the only path forward. Fix applies the documented split (Scripts Own Atomicity; Agent Owns Reasoning; Escalate values-not-facts — #44/#287) to the front door: (1) **stop swallowing** — the subprocess error is classified into spawn-fault (`e.code` ENOENT/EAGAIN/EMFILE/ENOMEM) / timeout-kill (`e.killed`/`e.signal`) / clean-nonzero-exit (truncated `e.stderr` surfaced); (2) **bounded in-script retry** (N≤2, ≤3 total attempts) on the transient classes ONLY — a clean verdict, a clean non-zero exit, and `status===2` (`owned`) are never retried; (3) a **new typed verdict `target_set_indeterminate`** (single-target `target_indeterminate`) carrying **`result: escalate`**, genuinely distinct from the determinate `target_unavailable`/`target_set_*` family which now carry **`result: refuse`** (fail-closed, unchanged); (4) the bundle loop surfaces one indeterminate-after-retry member as `escalate` instead of collapsing the set. Agent-side routing across all six routing surfaces (the 3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs) plus the `workflow-planner` profile: `result: refuse` → hard stop; `result: escalate` → pause and ask the user (the front-door analog of the consent valve — at claim time there is no plan/ledger yet, so a plain consent-halt to the user, not an `adaptive-node write-halt` ledger marker). Machine-enforced by a new `test-route-reachability.js` pin across all 18 command/skill surfaces + a `target_set_indeterminate` `test-agent-profile-parity.js` FEATURE_TOKEN. The fix is `claim.js`-only because root/codex have a `claim.js → classifier subprocess` boundary (where the bug lives) while the gitlab/gitea editions run the classifier in-process (no such boundary) and take envelope-only `result`-field parity plus a forward-compat `indeterminate` handler (the boundary-1/boundary-2 rationale is recorded in `docs/decisions/D-495-01.md`); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Adversarial-verified (indeterminate-vs-determinate distinctness + no retry-bleed across 7 scenarios incl. the bundle path) and code-reviewed (forge-mirror hand-diff, boundary decision adjudicated sound). Known follow-up: the boundary-2 internal-CLI-fetch swallow (`classifier → gh`/`glab`) is edition-uniform across all four classifiers — the same defect class one layer down, deliberately out of #495 scope.

### Changed

- **adaptive: the `workflow-planner` now carries an authoring rubric for speculative-open-eligible topologies (#513; follows #500 L3).** #500 wired the speculative-open *mechanism* (plan-run skeleton, operator card, route-reachability T9) so it is reachable; #513 teaches the planner *when* to use it proactively. A new "Speculative-open-eligible shaping" rubric in `agents/workflow-planner.md` (+ the three `workflow-planner.toml` twins, byte-identical and forge-neutral) instructs the planner to set `## Meta: speculative_open_policy: consent` when a READ-ONLY node's sole unsatisfied predecessor is an in-progress gate very likely to pass — so the executor can `open-ready --speculative-consent` to overlap it ahead of the gate (a critical-path makespan win), with verdict:fail rollback. Authoring control is the `## Meta` key ONLY: eligibility stays runtime/validator-derived (the same INV-17 discipline as `parallel_safe`) — the planner never hand-adds a `speculative: true` row. Machine-enforced by a new `test-agent-profile-parity.js` needle across all three twins (parity assertions 15→18), with a worked in-grammar example added to the profile and `docs/plan-run-cards/speculative-open.md` (§ Authoring). Authoring-only — the #500 mechanism surfaces are untouched; no script/schema/runtime change. No live makespan probe yet — the rubric is the deliverable (the proactive win is realized when a future plan authors the shape). All four editions; see `docs/decisions/D-513-01.md`.

- **docs: investigated #500 (parallel-write makespan levers inert on the live path) and recorded per-lever wire-or-relabel decision inputs — no behavior change.** A Case-B (#486) read-only shaping run produced `docs/investigations/2026-06-16-500-makespan-levers-decision.md` and the pending-approval recommendation record `docs/decisions/D-500-01.md`. Findings (verified against live code, adversarially critiqued): **L1** (`write_overlap_policy` relaxation) — the makespan win is real but "cheap AND safe" is refuted (the cheap consent-forward at `adaptive-node.js:3351` decouples lane-formation from leg-provisioning and corrupts when `KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION=0`; the safe leg-coupled wire is non-trivial and the shared-infra path has never run end-to-end) → **GUARDED-WIRE-as-follow-up or near-free RELABEL floor**, the one genuine owner value-call; **L2** (`KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION`) — the "dormant" premise is factually stale (the per-leg engine is COMPLETE, #463 AC18 PASS); only the activation-recipe toggle names are missing from prose → **WIRE** (document the recipe + fix stale "DORMANT" comments); **L3** (`speculative_open_policy:consent`) — fully wired ×4 editions, least risky, prose omission was an acknowledged deferral → **WIRE the prose** (+ a separate planner-rubric follow-up for the proactive win). #500 stays OPEN; a re-planned build run implements the approved direction and closes it. Docs-only; no script, schema, or prose-behavior change.

- **adaptive: the three parallel-write makespan levers are now WIRED on the live path (#500; owner-approved, `docs/decisions/D-500-02.md` accepting `D-500-01 (existing)`).** Closes the build half of #500 (the shaping run above produced the recommendation). **L1 — `write_overlap_policy` relaxation, leg-COUPLED safe wire:** `tryFormLaneGroup` now forwards `--write-overlap-consent` into lane-group FORMATION only under `resolveLegIsolation(process.env) && opts.writeOverlapConsent` (mirroring the provisioning conjunction), so a shared-infra co-open can never form unless per-leg worktrees are actually provisioned — closing the #283/#303 clobber window the cheap (consent-alone) wire would have opened. A new shared-infra-coarse **end-to-end** test in `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` exercises the full relaxation→form→provision→synthesize→barrier path (previously only the disjoint-green path was covered); the coupling is adversarially **mutation-verified non-vacuous** (dropping `resolveLegIsolation` makes the toggle-off/consent-on case wrongly form a group). The disjoint-green co-open path is byte-identical (consent is irrelevant before the green short-circuit). **L2 — `KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION` honesty wire:** the full activation recipe (`KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` + `KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION` + `--write-overlap-consent` + `write_overlap_policy: coarse`) is now documented across the six #400 plan-run surfaces (the per-leg engine was already COMPLETE/live since #463 AC18 — only the recipe was undocumented), and the stale "DORMANT" code comments are reworded to ADR-0010 "containment, not construction". **L3 — `speculative_open_policy:consent` prose wire:** a new `docs/plan-run-cards/speculative-open.md` card + six `<!-- CARD: speculative-open -->` markers + a driver line make the (already runtime-wired) consent path reachable; the planner rubric that delivers the proactive win is deferred to #513. Fail-closed `test-route-reachability.js` T8/T9 pins (`leg-isolation-recipe`, `speculative-open`) machine-enforce the prose across all six surfaces. Cross-edition: `adaptive-node.js` regenerated to all four editions via `edition-sync.js`; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Adversarial-verified (coupling + e2e path) and code-reviewed (four-edition parity, forge-neutrality). Two LOW cosmetic comment follow-ups filed #514.

- **finalize: `closure-audit.js` is now wired into all six finalize-route surfaces as a defense-in-depth reconciliation sweep complementary to the #497 inline emit fix (D-497-01).** The inline fix catches failures at transaction time; `closure-audit` is the periodic broad sweep that catches drift across ALL archived runs — a closed issue whose label was never removed, a stale roadmap source, etc. The `<!-- PIN: closure-audit -->` token is machine-enforced by a fail-closed T6 assertion in `test-route-reachability.js` across all six surfaces (3 Claude finalize commands + 3 Codex finalize SKILLs). Wired by n2-wire-closure-audit; the canonical GitHub `kaola-workflow-finalize.md` command is the model surface (see `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` § "Sink result handling and closure-audit reconciliation sweep").

## [6.3.0] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed

- **sink: freshness guard — `--sink` no longer false-resumes a stale committed sink-receipt and reports `status:sinked` WITHOUT merging (#484).** A multi-slice / keep-open issue that reuses the same `project` + `branch` across slices commits an all-`done` `sink-receipt.json` into the *tracked* `kaola-workflow/archive/<project>/.cache/` tree; on the next slice `resolveSinkReceiptPath` falls back to that archived receipt, `loadOrInitReceipt` returns it verbatim with no freshness validation, and the step loop (`if steps[step] === 'done' continue`) **skips `merge` and `push_main`** and falls through to `{result:'ok', status:'sinked'}` exit 0 — **main silently not advanced and the deliverable lost** (observed live in a consumer-repo finalize; the script literally computes the branch-not-merged signal for `git branch -d` cleanup, swallows it, and still reports success). Fix: a **freshness guard** runs after the step loop and **before any teardown or success emission** — it asserts the feature branch tip **is an ancestor of the resolved default branch** (the merge actually applied); a non-ancestor (or a branch that no longer exists) is a stale / never-applied receipt → typed refusal **`stale_sink_receipt`** (exit 1), never a false `status:sinked`. OFFLINE-safe (the `merge` step merges into the *local* default branch, so ancestry holds regardless of `push_main`). RED→GREEN real-git regression in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (`testSinkRefusesStaleReceipt`): Scenario A (branch un-merged → refuses, main unchanged, deliverable not lost) + Scenario B (a stale all-`done` receipt whose branch genuinely landed still sinks — the guard is precise, not a false-positive). Present in all four editions; fixed in the canonical script (byte-synced to the Codex twin) and hand-ported to the divergent gitlab/gitea sink ports; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

- **adaptive: enforce the synthesizer reasoning-class floor on the live dispatch seam + machine-pin the `synthesizer` role across all six surfaces (#463 Slice 1 — closes two gaps the post-closure completeness audit caught in the shipped step-3 scaffolding).** The #463 step-3 reasoning floor was *defined but never enforced*: `resolve-agent-model` exported `REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES`/`isReasoningClass` with **zero consumers**, so a manifest/frontmatter override (or an explicit `inherit`) **silently lowered** the synthesizer to a non-reasoning tier. Now: (1) `resolve-agent-model` adds `enforceReasoningFloor(role, model)` + an opt-in `resolveAgentModel(name, {enforceFloor:true})` + a `--enforce-floor` CLI flag that turn a floor-role resolution below the reasoning class into a typed `reasoning_floor_violation` (thrown / non-zero exit, `operator_hint` carried); and (2) the production model-resolution seam **consumes it** — `kaola-workflow-next-action.js` (the aggregator that resolves the dispatch model for the executor) now enforces the floor on the **dispatchable frontier**, refusing `reasoning_floor_violation` fail-closed when a *ready* `synthesizer` resolves to a non-reasoning **effective** model (the authored `model` column OR the resolved default — an authored column bypasses `resolveModel`, so the effective model is checked). A plan may RAISE but never LOWER the floor; the bare default path (opus) is unchanged, and non-floor roles are never constrained. Separately, the `synthesizer` role token — present in all **six** (#400) plan-run prose surfaces but pinned by **none** of the contract validators (an AC11 gap) — is now token-pinned across all six: the 3 edition `kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` commands + the 3 Codex `kaola-workflow-plan-run/SKILL.md` packs (including the two forge-codex SKILLs, the historic #400 dead zone, proven unguarded by a drop-and-validate during review). RED→GREEN in both `test-agent-model-resolver.js` (the floor primitive + CLI) and `test-next-action.js` (FLOOR-1..4: the dispatchable-frontier enforcement, RED-proven non-vacuous); `resolve-agent-model` byte-identical ×4, `next-action` regenerated ×4; all four chains green. Adversarial review caught the original opt-in-with-zero-consumers hole (the verifier reproduced the silent downgrade through `next-action`) and the 4-of-6 pin gap — both fixed before merge. Refs #463 (the leg-provisioning + synthesizer-dispatch runtime is the remaining step-4 work; #463 stays OPEN until the AC18 live probe).

- **claim/sink: `--help` (and any unrecognized flag) is now a safe no-op on the destructive lifecycle scripts — no more silent fall-through to a full finalize+sink (#476; ADR D-476-01).** `--help`/`-h` on `kaola-workflow-claim.js` and `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` now prints usage and exits 0 with **zero side effects**; any unrecognized flag yields a typed `unknown_flag` refusal with **zero mutation**, *before* any destructive operation. Previously a help probe like `claim.js finalize --project X --help` or `sink-merge.js ... --help` was silently dropped and the subcommand **ran to completion** (close issue + merge + delete branch + remove worktree) — the root trigger of the painful KaolaTerminal issue-85 orphan, which **bypassed every existing safety hardening (#346/#427/#466)**. `--help`/`-h` is detected on the **raw argv before parsing** (so a value flag cannot *swallow* the help token — `--issue-numbers -h` is still caught), and value flags refuse to consume a `-`-prefixed next token, so an unknown flag positioned in a value slot falls through to the typed refusal rather than being silently eaten. The unknown-flag guard is a closed per-script flag allowlist (claim.js gates its value flags + the explicit boolean branches; sink-merge.js a `KNOWN_FLAGS` set incl. `--sink`/`--json`/`--root`); a known flag missing its value is NOT mis-flagged. Regression tests drive the **real subprocess CLI** (`test-claim-hardening.js`, incl. value-slot swallow of both `--help` and `-h`) and assert no archive / no merge / no branch deletion, plus that valid flags are not false-rejected. Byte-synced to the Codex twin + hand-propagated to the four divergent forge claim/sink ports (the forge-port propagation). Two rounds of adversarial review each caught a real HIGH (the `--help` then the `-h` value-slot swallow) — both fixed + RED-proven before merge.

### Added

- **adaptive: the parallel-write engine is COMPLETE — governance pins + Codex synthesizer profiles + leg-dispatch discipline prose + ADR-0010 + the AC18 live-harness probe (#463 Slice 6, the issue-closing slice; default-off, byte-identical when off).** Slices 1–5 built the runtime (leg provisioning, per-leg barrier, parent-clean fence, the commit-based union barrier, the synthesizer, the `merge_conflict` envelope); this slice closes the governance, prose, profile, and **release-gate** items and is verified by a recorded live probe. (1) **AC11 governance pins** — the four contract validators (canonical + Codex plugin byte-pair + GitLab + Gitea) now token-pin the three write-overlap anchors so a future edit cannot silently drop them: `REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES` (the synthesizer's non-lowerable Opus floor, `resolve-agent-model.js`), `WRITE_OVERLAP_POLICY_LEGAL` (the policy field, `adaptive-schema.js`), and `PROTECTED_BASENAMES` (the always-blocking set, `classifier.js`). (2) **Codex `synthesizer` agent profile ×3** — `plugins/*/agents/synthesizer.toml` (byte-identical across the three editions) mirroring `agents/synthesizer.md`; `test-agent-profile-parity.js` gains `REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES` to its curated tokens so the md↔toml twin parity is now *meaningfully* enforced for the new role (12 parity assertions). (3) **AC3 leg-dispatch discipline prose ×6** — the three statements (discipline-dependent isolation via **absolute `legPath`** briefs; **fail-closed containment, not construction**, with the parent-clean fence as the own-lane catch; the **K = 2** out-of-lane thrash bound) propagated to all six plan-run routing surfaces (3 commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs). (4) **ADR-0010** (`docs/decisions/0010-runtime-neutral-per-leg-worktree-isolation.md`) records the three settled decisions: this *fulfills* ADR-0008 (the reintroduction it tracked via the write-lane hook + per-node running-set scheduler) rather than reversing it; the `.kw/legs` sibling-substrate choice (gitignored, outside the workflow worktree so the parent `git add -A` never sweeps a leg); and the rejection of Claude-only `isolation:'worktree'` for runtime neutrality (→ containment, not construction). (5) **AC17 telemetry gap fixed** — the live probe caught that `leg_committed` was emitted *nowhere* (the S4 synthesizer refactor moved capture into `synthesizeLevel` without carrying the emission); `synthesizeLevel` now emits `leg_committed` per leg (the lifecycle `leg_opened → leg_committed → level_merged`), pinned by `SYNTH-DISJOINT-END-TO-END` (983 assertions). (6) **AC15 Codex write-time-DENY verification** — documented result: the write-lane hook is wired in the Codex `.codex/hooks.json` with its `exit 2` deny path, but Codex deny capability may differ / needs trust activation; this is *not* a blocker because the deny is only a coarse best-effort cross-lane fence in every runtime — the fail-closed guarantee rests on the runtime-neutral per-leg barrier + parent-clean fence + commit-union barrier (Slices 3–4), resolving the pre-#463 "deny may differ, flag stays off" TODO. (7) **AC18 live-harness probe (the release gate)** — `docs/investigations/2026-06-15-463-live-probe-and-verification.md` records two runs driven by the real scripts in throwaway repos with **real `Agent`-tool dispatches** (not the test harness): **Run 1** (disjoint) co-opened two `.kw` legs, dispatched two real agents concurrently in one message (each in-lane via absolute `legPath`), captured `everConcurrent: true` (`deriveMaxSimultaneousOpen` over the real timings) + the full `leg_opened/leg_committed/level_merged` telemetry, octopus-merged to a 3-parent M whose `diff(base→M)` equalled the declared union, and tore down clean (0 legs, 0 refs); **Run 2** (the first-ever `synthesizer` dispatch) constructed a same-file conflict (which is freeze-refused in a real plan, so a constructed harness is the correct vehicle), proved the mechanical octopus bails clean, dispatched a **real Opus synthesizer** that resolved by intent preserving both legs, and verified the agent-resolved M satisfies the same commit-union barrier (base + both legs ∈ ancestors(M), diff ⊆ union). Spike-de-risked (a throwaway conflict→synthesizer→barrier spike) + an advisor consult before the build; the advisor's framing held (real dispatch is the artifact, not rebadged tests; Run 2 is a constructed harness by design). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. **Deferred (filed follow-up #492):** the `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES` entry for the two forge classifier ports (pre-existing hand-maintained-port hygiene that needs a `os`-require + CI-comment reconciliation first; not a numbered #463 AC — AC8 is satisfied). **Closes #463.**
- **adaptive: the `merge_conflict` bounded-repair escalation envelope (#463 Slice 5 — the write-overlap repair contract; default-off, byte-identical when off).** With Slice 4 the synthesizer *raises* a conflict; this slice defines what happens *after*. **By design there is no new runtime producer or "K=3 code branch"** — routed *exactly* like `test_thrash`: a schema constant the orchestrator applies, **no script counter on the adaptive path**, because the COMMIT-based union barrier on the merge commit M (Slice 4) — not the counter — is the fail-closed gate, so a resumed run that re-counts attempts from zero only re-does work and can never land unverified work. (1) **Schema** (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`, byte-identical ×4): `MERGE_CONFLICT_REPAIR_LIMIT = 3`, mirroring `TEST_THRASH_LIMIT`. (2) **The AC10 escalation chain is now traceable** across the six (#400) plan-run routing surfaces (3 commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs) and the `repair-routing.md` card: a write-leg level's **first-detection** refusal — `member_vacuity` (a **no-op leg** — the "leg that produced no changes" producer the AC names, caught at the member's *own* close where its evidence / a `no_op:<reason>` declaration is visible), `write_set_overflow` (an overflow), or the synthesizer's **octopus bail** (a real same-file conflict, the deferred tier) — is repaired by its *own* recovery (re-dispatch the leg · `revert-overflow` · a reasoning-class **Opus**-floor `synthesizer` agent resolving by intent), re-running `close-node`; on the **K=3**rd failure the orchestrator escalates via `write-halt --reason merge_conflict` (a **RESUMABLE** consent-style halt — `clear-halt --reason consent` to resume, unlike `test_thrash`'s one-way escalation). (3) **`operator_hint`s** (`OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY`, the D-445-01 auto-decorator): `merge_conflict` (names the K cap + the `write-halt` escalation) and `leg_capture_failed` (a genuine `synthesizeLevel` git fault); `close-node` surfaces the offending `leg` top-level so the hint can name it. **No `synthesizeLevel` no-op-leg detector was added** — a detector there cannot see the evidence and would false-positive a sanctioned `no_op:` member; `member_vacuity` (leg-aware since Slice 4) is the correct producer, where the evidence is visible (#44). Real-git integration tests (`scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`, +33 assertions, 982 total): `S5-MERGE-CONFLICT-BAIL` (codifies the spike — a same-file conflict bails `merge_conflict`, the abort is clean by `git ls-files -u` empty + HEAD-unchanged + no `MERGE_HEAD`, the leg branches survive for repair), `S5-NOOP-LEG-VACUITY` (the no-op-leg producer is `member_vacuity` at the member close), `S5-NOOP-LEG-REPAIR-THEN-SUCCESS` (re-dispatch → `group_passed`), `S5-CAPTURE-UNCOMMITTED` (the script-owned capture is robust to an un-committing agent), `S5-IDEMPOTENT-RESUME` (re-synth over already-merged legs returns the SAME M, stable HEAD — no spurious merge, no false `merge_conflict`), `S5-REPAIR-LIMIT-CONSTANT` (the K cap is operator-visible in the hint), and `S5-MULTI-LEVEL` (AC7 — two sequential fan-out levels: level-1 → M1, level-2's legs branch off M1 → M2 with M1 in its ancestry). The conflict mechanism was de-risked in a throwaway spike (real same-file conflict + no-op-leg + idempotency through the actual `synthesizeLevel`) + an advisor consult before the build; the consult **corrected the initial design** (the spike-suggested `empty_leg` detector was removed once the test showed `member_vacuity` already catches a no-op leg leg-aware, and a detector would false-positive a sanctioned `no_op:` member). `adaptive-schema` (byte-identical ×4) + `adaptive-node` (GENERATED_AGGREGATOR) regenerated; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Refs #463 (Slice 6 = ADR-0010 + Codex deny doc + AC3 prose ×6 + Codex synth profiles ×3 + the **AC18 live probe that CLOSES #463**; #463 stays OPEN until that probe).
- **adaptive: the planner now natively handles question-shaped & bug-shaped issues — authoring HINTS, not a new mechanism (#486).** The adaptive path composes a task-shaped DAG for *any* shape of work, but the `workflow-planner` was steered to author a *build* DAG (implement → test → review) regardless of shape — so a **question without a settled answer** ("which approach?", "is X viable?", "why does Y happen?") got a fabricated build plan around an *unvalidated premise* (laundered into the frozen DAG, sailing through a green artifact-vs-plan verdict) or no natural composition at all. This is a missing *instinct*, not a missing capability: the classic open-question arc **probe → assume → adversarially critique → converge** maps 1:1 onto `CANONICAL_ROLES` + the `sequence`/`fanout` shapes with **zero grammar changes**. Adds concrete guidance to `agents/workflow-planner.md` (Claude) and the three byte-identical `plugins/*/agents/workflow-planner.toml` Codex profiles, plus a quick-reference `### Question-shaped & bug-shaped issues (#486)` subsection on **all six** (#400) `kaola-workflow-adapt` routing surfaces (the 3 edition commands + the 3 Codex SKILL packs). The hints cover: the arc + roles (read-only `code-explorer`/`knowledge-lookup` PROBE as a fan-out inheriting #472 concurrency; `planner` ASSUME with explicit falsification tests; `adversarial-verifier` CRITIQUE — a separate subagent, read-only-but-has-Bash so for a bug it **runs the existing reproduction**, riding the existing majority-refute barrier; `planner`/`synthesizer` CONVERGE); the **freeze-once split** (Case A shape-knowable → author the whole DAG up front or `select(<group>)`; Case B shape-depends-on-findings → a short read-only shaping run, then RE-PLAN as a fresh run with a new `plan_hash`, no in-place thaw); the **bug flavor** (reproduction-as-falsification-criterion, the verifier's **"root cause or symptom mask?"** check, and **cannot-reproduce-after-a-bounded-probe → `consent`-halt, never a guess-fix**); and **escalate values not facts** (the existing `write-halt --reason consent` valve — no new gate, `decision:ask` stays advisory, planner-first #44/#287 intact). Worked in-grammar examples (Case A, the read-only critic fan-out, the bug diagnosis→re-plan→fix flow) are documented in `docs/investigations/2026-06-15-486-question-shaped-issues.md` and **machine-proven in-grammar** by `testQuestionShaped486` in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (zero grammar changes — pure composition over existing roles/shapes). The 6-surface propagation is **machine-enforced**: `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` pins the hint token across all six `adaptSurfaces486` (a 4-of-6 gap fails the chain). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Deferred (recorded): in-place DAG thaw, `findings_ready → replan` fusion, an answer-only non-merge terminal, a producer-keyed verifier reasoning floor, and a write-capable throwaway **spike** shape. Closes #486.

- **adaptive: the SYNTHESIZER execution + the COMMIT-based union barrier + the parent-clean fence + the dependency-level commit barrier + the singleton fast-path (#463 Slice 4 — the write frontier now genuinely fans out into per-leg worktrees and reconciles them; default-off, byte-identical when off).** With this slice the dormant legs go LIVE: at a ≥2-wide write level the orchestrator routes each member's writes into its own `.kw` leg worktree, and at the last-member close the scheduler **drains → synthesizes → commits → advances HEAD**. (1) **The B1 fix — union barrier on the merge COMMIT, not a working-tree snapshot.** `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` `--group-barrier` gains `--merge-commit M --project P`: instead of `snapshotWorktree(root)`, it diffs the legs' shared branch-point → **M** (`diff-tree base M`). A throwaway spike proved why this is load-bearing — a working-tree snapshot (`read-tree HEAD` + `git add -A`) **sweeps in a floated own-lane slip that never made it into M**, false-greening lost work; the commit diff measures only-committed content. The base is the legs' branch-point **commit** (anchored ref, cross-checked against the manifest #368), never the group's `snapshotWorktree` **tree** baseline (wrong object type for the ancestor checks). Subset (`diff⊆union`) catches **escapes**; **per-leg-head ancestor inclusion** (`base ∈ ancestors(M)` + every `legHead ∈ ancestors(M)`) catches **omissions** — a dropped leg whose committed work would be silently lost on teardown (new refusals `merge_base_unreachable`, `leg_omitted_from_merge`, `leg_baseline_split`, `merge_commit_invalid`). (2) **The parent-clean fence** — new `--parent-clean-check [--project P]`: a floated own-lane slip lands a relative-path write in the *parent* (invisible to the per-leg barrier, self-exempt from the write-lane hook (the mechanism)). The fence refuses `parent_dirty` iff a **production** (non-exempt) path is dirty in the parent before the merge — it **reuses the exact barrier allowband** (the `isWorkflowArtifact`/`isBarrierInvisible`/`isTestLike` predicates, lifted to module scope) so the perpetual plan/ledger/`.cache` churn of a never-commits-per-node run **never trips it** (a blanket `git status --porcelain` empty would ship the slice inert). It scans with `--untracked-files=all` (adversarial review caught this): default porcelain collapses a wholly-new untracked dir to a single `?? dir/` line, so a non-exempt file masked by an exempt-named new subdir — e.g. a foreign-archive write `kaola-workflow/archive/<other>/leak.js` collapsed to `?? kaola-workflow/archive/` — would be classified by the collapsed dir name and evade the fence; `-uall` lists every file so each path is classified precisely (`PARENT-CLEAN-CHECK-UALL`, RED without the flag). (3) **The synthesizer execution** (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` `synthesizeLevel`): script-owned capture-commit of each leg, then a **mechanical octopus merge** of the disjoint legs into the feature branch (no agent — the clean #463 win), with a real conflict (the deferred overlapping tier) bailing to `merge_conflict` (`merge --abort`, no advance; the Opus resolver + K=3 repair is Slice 5). `closeGroupMember` composes fence → merge → commit-union-barrier on the last-member close when legs are live; a legless group keeps the **byte-identical** snapshot group barrier. The leg-aware vacuity guard (`memberInLaneChanges`) now probes the **leg** (uncommitted + committed-vs-base) so routed work is not read as vacuous. (4) **The dependency-level commit barrier**: the merge commit M **is** the HEAD advance, so the next level's legs branch off M; lane-group state stays live until the synthesizer completes (level N+1 cannot open mid-synthesis). (5) **The singleton fast-path** (AC8): a single-write-node level forms no lane group (`tryFormLaneGroup` needs ≥2) → no leg, no synthesizer → the normal per-node barrier in the parent, overhead-free. Real-git integration tests (`scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`, +30 assertions): `SYNTH-DISJOINT-END-TO-END` (octopus merge → M, HEAD advance, `diff base→M == union`, `level_merged` telemetry, teardown), `SYNTH-PARENT-DIRTY-FENCE`, `PARENT-CLEAN-CHECK-DIRECT` (churn-clean vs production-dirty), `SYNTH-OMISSION` (the no-silent-loss inclusion catch), `SYNTH-UNION-ESCAPE`, `SYNTH-MERGE-COMMIT-ARG-VALIDATION`, `SYNTH-SINGLETON-FAST-PATH`; the three Slice-3 close-path tests were updated to the live-routing model (writes into legs). `barrierCheck` refactored to delegate to the lifted predicates (behavior-identical; 940 adaptive-node assertions still green). `plan-validator` + `adaptive-node` regenerated ×4 (GENERATED_AGGREGATORS); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. The B1 mechanism was de-risked in a throwaway spike + an advisor consult before the build (the advisor caught: the fence must reuse the allowband or ship inert; the union barrier needs per-leg-head inclusion or it permits silent loss; do not build the pre-fan-out commit in this slice). A 4-lens adversarial review then caught two real defects, both fixed + RED→GREEN-tested: (a) the parent-clean fence missed a non-exempt file masked by a wholly-new untracked dir's porcelain collapse → `--untracked-files=all` (`PARENT-CLEAN-CHECK-UALL`); and (b) a **silent-loss** vector — `close-and-open-next` (the serial close path) has no `isMember` routing, so closing a live lane-group member out-of-band there would skip the synthesizer and orphan the leg's committed work; fixed with a **narrow lane-group-member fence** (`refuse scheduler_active`, keyed on lane_group membership, NOT a broad `runningSetLive` excl which would false-fence a legitimate per-node running-set close) so a member closes only via `close-node` (`SYNTH-SERIAL-CLOSE-FENCED`). Refs #463 (Slice 5 = `merge_conflict` producers + K=3 bounded repair; Slice 6 = ADR-0010 + AC3 prose ×6 + Codex synth profiles + the AC18 live probe that CLOSES #463; #463 stays OPEN until that probe).
- **adaptive: the per-leg write-isolation barrier + tamper-resistant leg-base anchor (#463 Slice 3 — the write-isolation half of the parallel-write engine; default-off, byte-identical when off).** Adds `--leg-barrier --node-id ID --project P --leg-root PATH [--expect-base SHA]` to `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`: it snapshots the leg worktree (`--leg-root`, the spike-proven leg-rooting — a leg is a linked worktree sharing the object DB/refs, so `snapshotWorktree(legRoot)` + `diff-tree` resolve against the shared store) and diffs it against the leg's branch-point over the node's **own** declared write set (per-node mode), refusing an out-of-lane leg write via the existing `write_set_overflow` arm. It answers "did THIS leg stay in its declared lane" and is **DISTINCT** from the group/union barrier (which measures the parent / the merge commit — the B1 fix in Slice 4); the leg-barrier never substitutes for it. **Anti-laundering (#368 pattern):** a free `--base` is the vacuous-pass hole the per-node barrier refuses — feeding base = the leg's post-write tree empties the diff — so the base is resolved **only** from a ref anchored at provision (`refs/kaola-workflow/leg-base/<project>/<id>`); `open-ready` now anchors that ref (fail-closed: a leg whose base ref did not anchor rolls back + refuses `leg_provision_failed`, so it can never brick its later barrier with `no_leg_base`), `teardownLeg` deletes it alongside the worktree+branch, and `--leg-barrier` cross-checks the running-set manifest SHA (`--expect-base`) against the ref (`barrier_base_mismatch` on tamper), with an ancestor backstop (`leg_base_unreachable` for a forward/unrelated base). `--project` is passed **explicitly** (mirrors the provision-side `legBaseRef` key — never derived from the plan path) so producer/consumer ref names cannot drift into a silent `no_leg_base`. The barrier is **pre-wired** into `close-node`'s group-member close (runs per closing member that has a leg, before the group barrier); in this slice production legs stay **empty** (write routing + the parent-clean fence + the write-lane hook are Slice 4, where they gain semantic content), so on a real run it passes trivially — the integration tests are what exercise it with **real** leg writes. New typed refusals `missing_leg_root`/`missing_project`/`leg_root_invalid`/`no_leg_base`/`leg_base_unreachable` (each with an `operator_hint`). Real-git integration tests (`scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`, +33 assertions): `LEG-BARRIER-{REF-ANCHORED, IN-LANE, OVERFLOW, COMMITTED, VACUOUS-BASE, NO-REF, ANCESTOR-BACKSTOP, ARG-VALIDATION, LEG-ROOT-INVALID, CLOSE-PATH-GATES, CLOSE-PATH-CLEAN, CLOSE-PATH-FLAG-OFF, TEARDOWN-DROPS-REF}` — covering committed-in-leg detection, the adversarial post-write `--expect-base` cross-check, the end-to-end ref-resolves (the producer/consumer name agreement), and a RED-proven close-path gate (neutering the wiring fails `CLOSE-PATH-GATES`). The cwd-rooting mechanism was de-risked in a throwaway spike first (the existing `--barrier-check` CLI **cannot** be cwd-tricked to root at a leg — `root` derives from the plan path, not cwd — which is why the new explicit-`--leg-root` entry point exists). `plan-validator` + `adaptive-node` regenerated ×4 (GENERATED_AGGREGATORS); all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Refs #463 (Slice 4 = route writes into legs + synthesizer merge + the merge-commit union barrier + parent-clean fence; #463 stays OPEN until the AC18 live probe).
- **adaptive: DORMANT per-leg `.kw` worktree provisioning + telemetry + leg-aware reconcile/teardown (#463 Slice 2 — the first runtime course of the parallel-write engine; gated entirely OFF by default).** Adds the leg-worktree machinery the synthesizer commit barrier (Slice 4) will run on: under `KAOLA_LEG_ISOLATION` **and** a per-run `--write-overlap-consent` flag **and** a formed lane group, `open-ready` provisions one git worktree per write-leg member at `<mainRoot>/.kw/legs/<project>/<node>` on branch `kw/legs/<project>/<node>` off the feature-branch HEAD (mirroring `claim.js`'s `git worktree add` discipline — `assertSafeLegBranch` flag-injection guard, idempotent dangling-branch reuse, partial-failure rollback in Phase 1 before any ledger flip), records `{legPath, legBranch, baseline}` per leg under `running-set.json`'s `lane_group.legs`, and emits a `leg_opened` timing event. **Legs are DORMANT this slice** — `working_dir` stays parent-side (writes are NOT yet routed into legs; that, the per-leg barrier, and the merge-commit union barrier are Slices 3–4). Leg-aware crash repair: `reconcile-running-set` tears down a dropped/cappedOut/closed/stale member's leg (worktree-remove **before** branch-delete — the sink-hang-family order) and an orphan sweep (hoisted above the `no_running_set` return, gated on the toggle) reclaims legs even after a crash lost `running-set.json`; `close-node` tears legs down on clean group completion before clearing `lane_group` (so nothing leaks on the modal happy path). **The non-negotiable safety property is byte-identity when off**: absent the toggle+consent there is no `lane_group.legs` key, no worktree, no timing, zero git calls — proven by a three-way flag-off test (`LEG-FLAG-OFF-BYTE-IDENTITY`) plus real-git integration tests (`LEG-PROVISION-ON`, `LEG-CLEAN-COMPLETION-NO-LEAK`, `LEG-CRASH-LOST-MANIFEST-RECLAIM`, `LEG-ORPHAN-SWEEP`, `LEG-DANGLING-BRANCH-REUSE`; +67 assertions). Two rounds of adversarial review each caught a real HIGH — an unbounded, unreclaimable leg leak on clean group completion **and** on crash-before-manifest-write (both the same root cause: the sweep was gated on a manifest absent in exactly the orphan cases) — both fixed (close-node primary teardown + manifest-independent toggle-gated hoisted sweep), RED-proven, and re-verified NOT-REFUTED with real git. All four chains green. Refs #463 (Slice 3 = route writes into legs + the per-leg/parent-clean barrier; #463 stays OPEN until the AC18 live probe).

- **adaptive: `synthesizer` WRITE convergence role + grammar + reasoning-class model floor (#463, sequencing step 3 of the write-overlap mechanism).** Registers the `synthesizer` role — the WRITE convergence node of a parallel write fan-out that `depends_on` every leg, declares the **union** of the legs' write sets, and is post-dominated by a real `code-reviewer` (G1). Added to `CANONICAL_ROLES` + `WRITE_ROLES` (NOT `IMPLEMENT_ROLES`, NOT `GATE_VERDICT_ROLES`): `producesCode()` is therefore true for a union-declaring synthesizer, so the existing G1 machinery **requires** a `code-reviewer` after it. It is never a leg co-member (its union exact-overlaps every leg; because it `depends_on` each leg the antichain disjointness loop skips those ordered pairs, so the overlap is grammar-legal only as a convergence node — no validator-loop change needed). `resolve-agent-model` pins a reasoning-class default (`synthesizer: 'opus'`) and adds a `REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES` set + an `isReasoningClass()` helper (a non-reasoning tier is a refusal — the *enforcement* lands at dispatch in the step-4 scheduler; step 3 establishes the floor). New local agent profile `agents/synthesizer.md` (Opus; dispatched only on a real merge conflict — disjoint legs merge mechanically). The discriminating **AC11** test proves `legs→synthesizer(union)→code-reviewer→sink` **freezes** and the same minus the reviewer **refuses** via `gateUncovered`. Like steps 1–2 this is a default-inert foundation: the runtime that PROVISIONS legs, DISPATCHES the synthesizer, and RAISES `merge_conflict` (the per-leg `.kw` worktree scheduler + commit barrier) is the step-4 remainder, plus the AC18 live-harness probe. The core mechanism — concurrent leg dispatch into isolated `.kw` worktrees + mechanical disjoint merge + commit-based union barrier + parent-clean fence + teardown — was validated end-to-end in a throwaway 2-leg probe before this lands.

### Changed

- **finalize: consumer (non-npm) repos gate on the agent's `final-validation.md`, not a re-executed chain receipt (#475, split from #464; Pure option A, owner-decided; ADR D-475-01).** `plan-validator --finalize-check` is now **dual-mode by repo kind**, auto-detected by whether `package.json` declares any `test:kaola-workflow:*` script. A **self-host (npm)** repo keeps the #432 chain-receipt gate **byte-unchanged** (`chains_unverified` > `chains_stale` > `chains_red`). A **consumer (non-npm)** product repo (e.g. a Swift/Xcode app — the KaolaTerminal issue-85 case) does **NOT** run `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` at all: the agent **owns verification** (#44), so the gate is its recorded `.cache/final-validation.md` (present + a column-0 `verdict: pass`, reusing the #251 vocabulary) — fail-closed `final_validation_unverified` (absent) > `final_validation_failed` (no `verdict: pass`). Freshness leans on the existing **attribution sweep** (run for both modes), NOT a `validated_head` equality (which would false-refuse every correct finalize per #324 AC3 — the finalize node records validation *before* committing its own docs/CHANGELOG). The v6.2.0 `kaola-workflow/chains.json` consumer escape hatch is **RETIRED** — `resolveChains` is now self-host-only (`run-chains.js` refuses `chains_config_missing` in a consumer repo, which is EXPECTED). Prose carve-out across the finalize/contractor surfaces (canonical `kaola-workflow-finalize.md` + the three Codex finalize `SKILL.md` + `contractor.md` + the three `contractor.toml`), self-guarded by a new `final-validation.md` / `final_validation_unverified` pin in every finalize contract validator (canonical + codex + gitlab + gitea — closing the prior zero-pins drift gap). RED-first walkthrough proves the consumer fail-closed path; the self-host receipt path stays green. The recorded #44 tradeoff: this trusts agent-recorded evidence — a focused validation may miss a regression outside its set, and in-write-set churn after `final-validation.md` is recorded is not caught by the sweep (the self-host `chains_stale` analog is deliberately not re-introduced) — accepted because the agent owns verification (see ADR D-475-01).

### Added

- **adaptive: `merge_conflict` typed HALT reason (#463, sequencing step 2 of the write-overlap mechanism).** Enumerates the fourth `ESCALATION_MARKERS` member (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js`, byte-identical ×4) — the typed, fail-closed outcome for an unresolvable write-leg convergence that the upcoming per-leg synthesizer commit barrier (steps 3–4) will raise after its bounded-repair cap. Unlike `test_thrash` (a one-way escalation to the full path), `merge_conflict` is a **resumable consent-style halt**: `write-halt --reason merge_conflict` flows through the generic escalation branch (records `escalated_to_full: merge_conflict` + the durable `consent_halt: pending` marker, so the run's halt fence catches it exactly like a consent halt), and `clear-halt --reason consent` now strips **both** the ledger marker **and** the `merge_conflict` cause, so a resolved conflict resumes adaptively with clean state (no lingering escalation marker). Audited every `ESCALATION_MARKERS`/escalation reader — `autopilot` (`checkRunHalts` already surfaces it via the `consent_halt` ledger marker), `repair-state` (`routeAdaptive`'s OR-combined `readDurableConsentHalt` already surfaces it as `consent-halt-surface`), and `fast-advance`/`fast-audit` (whose fast-path `ESCALATION_TRIGGERS` list is consumed independently of `ESCALATION_MARKERS` and never carries `merge_conflict`, an adaptive-only outcome) need no change. The `merge_conflict` token is propagated to all SIX (#400) plan-run prose surfaces — the three edition `kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` commands (top-level + gitlab + gitea) and the three Codex `kaola-workflow-plan-run/SKILL.md` — and machine-pinned by **every** contract validator that pins `test_thrash` (`validate-workflow-contracts.js` + its Codex byte-mirror, `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js`, and the forge `validate-kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}-contracts.js`), so the cross-edition parity is self-guarding. The runtime that *raises* `merge_conflict` (the scheduler legs + synthesizer) and the live-harness probe remain the OPEN remainder of #463.

## [6.2.0] - 2026-06-14

### Added

- **adaptive: dispatch-fidelity seam — the executor no longer silently serializes a planner-authored parallel read frontier (#472, sequencing step 1; the live-concurrency proof is the OPEN remainder).** A forensic sweep found that the read-axis scheduler stack (#303/#375/#377/#438/#439) shipped the *scheduler* (it marks N rows `in_progress`) but never a *dispatcher* — across 21 traced runs `everConcurrent` was false in every one, because `open-next` silently single-opened `readySet[0]` even at an independent ≥2 frontier and `enterBatch` was advisory (read by no script, gated behind a "when in doubt, use serial" card). This is a **fidelity** fix (run the plan as authored), **not a width mandate** (width stays the planner's scope-driven call). Three parts: (1) `runOpenNext` (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) now mirrors `orient` / `close-and-open-next` — at a fresh INDEPENDENT ≥2 delegable frontier (auto-pick, no `--node-id`) it returns `{enterBatch:true, frontier:[...]}` with **zero mutation** instead of single-opening one node (a width-1 frontier or a dependency chain still single-opens serially; an explicit `--node-id` is exempt; any write in the mix still serial-degrades via the #463/#437 path). (2) The six `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` prose surfaces now make **concurrent one-message dispatch the DEFAULT** — on `enterBatch:true`, run `open-ready` then dispatch the frontier's role agents in ONE assistant message (the only thing that yields real concurrency; one-per-turn is itself a serial barrier) — and the `frontier-batch` card's "when in doubt, use serial … parallel is an optimization, not a requirement" framing is retuned to "run the authored width; serial is the *degraded* path only." (3) A new `deriveMaxSimultaneousOpen()` derives `{maxSimultaneousOpen, everConcurrent}` from the **existing** durable `node-timings.jsonl` `opened`/`closed` events (a pure interval sweep — no redundant counter; recording "open-ready opened N" would be circular since open-ready opens N by definition), so a real run can **prove** the authored width ran concurrently. `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` adds T472-DIVERT (≥2 → enterBatch, no ledger flip) / T472-SERIAL (width-1 single-opens) / T472-NODEID (explicit target exempt) / T472-TELEMETRY (overlapping → everConcurrent true; serial → false; same-ts hand-off not over-counted). Sequenced after #463 (shared files, no disjoint lane). Also corrects the #463 docs (`docs/api.md` + the #463 CHANGELOG entry above) to describe the gate as **leg-scoped**, matching the shipped R1 fix (they previously said "whole plan"). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. **#472 stays OPEN:** green chains prove the *seam* exists; only a recorded live run with `everConcurrent:true` proves the authored width actually dispatched concurrently — the same "shipped-the-capability-without-a-concurrency-trace" trap that left this dormant through five prior closures, so it is not closed on chain-green alone.
- **validator: `write_overlap_policy` — `--parallel-safe` no longer refuses a coarse-area-overlapping but EXACT-FILE-DISJOINT write frontier when it is gated (#463, sequencing step 1, the original narrow AC as a strict subset).** The original #463 ("relax `parallel_safe` coarse-area blocking for concrete disjoint write sets") is delivered as the first, default-off, fully-gated slice of the redesigned mechanism. New `## Meta` field **`write_overlap_policy`** (hash-covered; legal `off` (default = today's PREVENT behavior byte-for-byte) / `disjoint` / `coarse`; `exact` is designed-but-refused at freeze, `write_overlap_policy_unsupported`) — the WRITE-side knob, DISTINCT from #439's read-side `speculative_open_policy` (writes clobber where reads do not). `classifier.disjointWriteSets()` gains an **additive `kind ∈ {exact, coarse, shared-infra}`** field alongside the UNCHANGED `verdict`/`reasoning` (the verdict stays pure — `scanClaimedOverlap`, the #232 antichain loop, and the G-SEL-4 select-arm check keep reading `verdict` and ignore `kind`); a new `classifier.isProtected()` marks concrete files (lockfiles, `CHANGELOG.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, install manifests, archive/roadmap artifacts, the ×4 `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` anchor) that **stay blocking at every tier even when their area relaxes**. `--parallel-safe` (the scheduler's co-open check) now downgrades a coarse/shared-infra (exact-file-disjoint, non-PROTECTED) overlap from refuse to **ok** ONLY when a single shared predicate holds: the active `write_overlap_policy` tier authorizes the class (`disjoint` relaxes coarse; `coarse` additionally relaxes shared-infra) **AND** the per-run, never-persisted `--write-overlap-consent` carrier is present **AND** a `code-reviewer` gate post-dominates **the relaxed legs themselves** (leg-scoped `gateUncovered` over the `--nodes` set — NOT a whole-plan `producesCode` check, which is vacuously-empty for docs-only legs; the adversarial-verifier R1 fix); the refusal envelope surfaces a `relaxed[]` audit list. An **exact-file** overlap NEVER relaxes here (it demands real reconciliation — the deferred runtime merge_conflict barrier). At `off` / no-consent / no-gate / exact / PROTECTED, **today's `overlapping_write_sets` refusal stands verbatim** (the fail-closed floor). Propagated across all four editions (schema byte-identical ×4; `plan-validator.js` rename-normalized; the divergent hand-maintained gitlab/gitea `classifier` ports hand-propagated with the same `kind`/`isProtected`, gitlab parity verified live). `scripts/test-commit-node.js` adds T463-AC (the original AC relaxes) + the floor cases (no-consent / policy-off byte-identity / no-gate / exact / PROTECTED) + T463-FREEZE (`exact` refused at freeze) + T463-PURITY (AC13: `kind` is additive, every `verdict` unchanged; `isProtected` true/false). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. **Scope:** this is sequencing step 1 only — the redesign's runtime mechanism (the `merge_conflict` HALT, the `synthesizer` write role + reasoning-model floor, the per-leg `.kw` worktree scheduler + dependency-level commit barrier) and the live-harness AC18 probe remain; **#463 stays OPEN** as code-incomplete + AC18-pending.
- **scripts/adaptive: the D-419 read-overlap speculative-execution kernel — a read node may now optimistically run ahead of an OPEN gate dependency, behind a default-off, consent-gated policy (#439).** The slice from `feat/439-speculative-read-overlap` is completed into a working kernel. A read-only node whose ONLY unsatisfied dependency is a currently-open gate (a `code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer`/`adversarial-verifier`/`main-session-gate` in_progress) is now *speculative-eligible* — it can be opened betting the gate will pass, and rolled back to the anchored baseline if it fails. **Mechanical eligibility** (`kaola-workflow-next-action.js`): an additive `speculativePending` set (read-only + own status pending + not already ready + exactly one unsatisfied direct dep, which is an in_progress gate); keyed on the new shared `GATE_VERDICT_ROLES` schema vocabulary. The key is **emitted ONLY at `speculative_open_policy:consent` and omitted entirely at the default `off`**, so next-action output (and the open-time `gate_not_complete` branch that reads it) is byte-identical to pre-#439 off-policy. **Activation requires BOTH** the hash-covered `## Meta` field `speculative_open_policy: consent` (default `off` = today's behavior byte-for-byte; `auto` refused at freeze — write-overlap optimism is deferred to #463) **and** the per-run, never-persisted `open-ready --speculative-consent` carrier. **Runtime** (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`): `open-ready --speculative-consent` fans the speculative read frontier out only when the normal frontier is empty (the gate is the sole blocker), stamping each running-set entry `speculative: true` ([INV-25]); `open-next` of a gate-blocked read node refuses the typed `gate_not_complete` (dependency-unsatisfied slot) pointing at the consent path; a **close-time guard** then holds a speculative member (`gate_not_complete` at close) until its gate is `complete` — so a speculative node cannot vanish to `complete` ahead of its bet and lose its review pointer + discard handle (the concurrent work already happened; only the formal `complete` is deferred; never deadlocks since the gate is an upstream dep that closes independently); a new `discard-speculative --node-id N` rolls a speculative member back GC-safely (ledger reset → revert in-lane writes to the anchored baseline SHA → `--drop-base` → remove from the running-set; composes with #424's `drop_base_window_open` lock and #434's no-re-snapshot posture; for a read node the in-lane revert is a no-op — the revert is the SHARED primitive #463's write-leg rollback reuses); and a gate that closes with a **failing** verdict surfaces `speculative_review_required` (on BOTH the `close-node` and `close-and-open-next` paths) naming the speculative members that bet on it (non-blocking, informational — per-node verdicts stay informational per #328; the operator KEEPs or `discard-speculative`s each). `discard-speculative` is registered in the #466 worktree-authority split-guard set. Default `off` ⇒ no `speculativePending` consumer, no speculative open ⇒ byte-identical to today across all four editions. `scripts/test-next-action.js` (SPEC-1…6: eligibility + the policy-off byte-identity omission) and `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` (T439-1…10 real-git-repo subprocess — consent open + marker, no-flag/policy-off inert, open-time `gate_not_complete`, discard reset+removal, `not_speculative` refuse, verdict-fail→review, verdict-pass→no-review, close-time guard holds a speculative node ahead of its gate, and the held-then-reviewed coherence). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. (The per-leg plan-run prose for the speculative open discipline rides the combined prose pass per the issue; #463 is the write-overlap half that shares this kernel.)
- **contract+docs: contractor-free routing for the migrated fast/full transitions is now machine-enforced, and the docs describe the current script-owned boundary (#459, ADR 0004 capstone for #456/#457/#458).** The four contract validators now fail if a `subagent_type="contractor"` dispatch returns to any migrated command surface (`kaola-workflow-fast` + `kaola-workflow-phase1..5`, all three command editions) or if contractor delegation prose returns to the migrated Codex SKILLs (`research`/`ideation`/`plan`/`review`/`execute` must be fully contractor-free; the `fast` SKILL keeps only its allowed finalize-exception boundary note). The dispatch-pattern check (not a bare-word grep) deliberately leaves the allowed references untouched — `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md`, `agents/contractor.md`, and historical ADR/docs text. Registration-coverage assertions pin every full/fast transaction script (+ forge ports) in the install manifest so a manual install cannot silently omit one and fall back to a now-nonexistent contractor handoff. Docs corrected to current behavior: `docs/api.md` (the adaptive aggregators are called directly by the main session per #272, not "run by the contractor"; the Contractor Agent section is narrowed to Finalization-only), `docs/architecture.md` (the lean-orchestrator boundary now states the contractor owns the Finalization mechanical block only, with fast/full/phase-4/adaptive transitions script-owned and main-session-direct), `docs/workflow-state-contract.md` (script-owned transition map), and `docs/decisions/0004-script-owned-mechanical-transitions.md` (a status-update note recording that every non-finalization seam has landed; Phase 6 remains the sole documented contractor exception). No script behavior changed; this is enforcement + documentation. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.
- **scripts/full: `kaola-workflow-phase4-advance.js` — the full path's Phase 4 (Execute) progress/task/failure-ledger transitions are now script-owned, off the `contractor` subagent (#458, ADR 0004).** A new typed transaction script owns the per-task execute-loop bookkeeping the contractor previously did: `init-progress` (stamps `phase4-progress.md` from `phase3-plan.md` — one `## Tasks` row and one `tdd-guide executor task N` compliance row per Phase 3 `### Task N:` block, all `pending`; Build Status `clean`; empty Failure Routing Ledger; create-only/idempotent), `open-task --task N` (`workflow-state.md` pointer → `step: delegate-task` / `task: N` before the `tdd-guide` dispatch), `record-failure --task N --stdin` (appends one Failure Routing Ledger row from the orchestrator's verbatim classification/route, deduped on re-run), and `close-task --task N --stdin` (marks the task `complete`, fills Files Modified, flips its `tdd-guide executor task N` compliance row to a RESOLVED status, sets Build Status + `Last Updated`, advances the pointer to the next task or Phase 5), plus a read-only `orient`. The main session still owns ALL judgment (task dispatch, result verification, failure classification + route selection, the validation-passed verdict); the script transcribes the verbatim outcome and mutates files only. Like full-advance (#457), the `tdd-guide executor task N` rows are `delegation_policy`-aware (`delegate` → `subagent-invoked`, etc.) and `close-task` **self-validates the whole compliance table against the real `repair-state.unresolvedCompliance(content, stateContent)` phase 4→5 boundary gate when closing the LAST task** — failing closed (`unresolved_compliance`, zero mutation) rather than advancing into a crossing the resume/finalize router would reject. Crash-safe write order (progress file before state pointer); idempotent on resume (a true no-op re-run does not even bump `Last Updated`); `## Sink` preserved byte-for-byte. Every value written into a markdown-table cell is sanitized (a literal `|` in an orchestrator-supplied task name / file / ledger field is rewritten to the non-delimiter fullwidth `｜` so it cannot shift columns and corrupt the table that both this script and `repair-state.taskRows()` parse), and the Build-Status section write uses a function replacer so a `$1`/`$&` token in a build note is not interpreted as a regex backreference. The script is rename-normalized per edition (the lone repair-state `require`; routes are KW-split), registered in `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` + `validate-script-sync.js` (`COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES`). All 6 Phase-4 prose surfaces migrated off `contractor` (the `kaola-workflow-phase4` command ×3 editions + the `kaola-workflow-execute` Codex SKILL ×3 editions). New `scripts/test-phase4-advance.js` (46 assertions, incl. the two-arg boundary gate under `delegation_policy: delegate` + explicit-row self-validation refusal + ledger dedupe + idempotent timestamp + piped-task-name and `$`-token-in-build-status corruption guards). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.
- **scripts/full: `kaola-workflow-full-advance.js` — the full (6-phase) path's Phase 1/2/3/5 checkpoint + phase-file transitions are now script-owned, off the `contractor` subagent (#457, ADR 0004).** A new typed transaction script owns the deterministic mechanical seams that previously delegated to `contractor` in the full path: `phase1-complete` (the `workflow-state.md` checkpoint after Phase 1; refuses if `phase1-research.md` — the session's research synthesis — is absent; never authors it), `phase2-finalize --stdin` (authors `phase2-ideation.md` from the orchestrator's verbatim Selected Approach + Approaches Evaluated + Out of Scope, then advances the pointer to Phase 3), `phase3-finalize --stdin` (authors `phase3-plan.md` from the verbatim blueprint + task list, keeping the per-task `- Write Set:` lines the parallel-overlap classifier reads, then advances to Phase 4), and `phase5-finalize --stdin` (authors `phase5-review.md` from the verbatim verdict + finding lists, refusing a `review_status` that is not `PASSED`/`PASSED WITH FOLLOW-UPS`, then routes to Finalization), plus a read-only `orient`. Every authored phase file renders a RESOLVED `## Required Agent Compliance` table (from the orchestrator's `compliance` packet rows, or a policy-aware default) AND is **self-validated against the real `repair-state.js` `unresolvedCompliance(content, stateContent)` phase-boundary gate before any write** — the script `require`s the same-edition repair-state and fails closed (typed `unresolved_compliance` refusal, zero mutation) rather than emit a phase file that the downstream resume/`/next`/finalize router would reject. The default compliance status is `delegation_policy`-aware (`delegate` → `subagent-invoked`, `local-authorized` → `local-fallback-explicit`, `tool-unavailable` → `local-fallback-tool-unavailable`, else `invoked`) so an omitted-compliance run passes the delegation cross-check that production always applies. The main session still owns ALL judgment (research synthesis, approach selection, blueprint completeness, review verdict, severity triage); the script emits typed JSON only and never dispatches a role, asks the user, judges severity, re-selects, designs, or invents a write set. Crash-safe write order (phase file first, `workflow-state.md` pointer last); every subcommand is idempotent/resume-safe and preserves an existing `## Sink` block byte-for-byte (surgical field replace, not whitelist-reconstruct — the rich claim-time sections survive); a non-array `compliance` or bad packet is a typed refusal with `operator_hint`. The per-issue roadmap `init-issue` stays a direct command-prose call (it resolves the forge-correct roadmap script per edition) rather than being duplicated in the transaction. The script's command/skill ROUTE names are KW-split (forge-neutral, so they keep their un-prefixed names across editions); its lone `require` of repair-state is rename-normalized per edition (the codex copy is byte-identical to canonical, the gitlab/gitea ports carry `kaola-<forge>-workflow-repair-state.js`), registered in `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` + `validate-script-sync.js` (`COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES`). All 24 full-path prose surfaces migrated off `contractor` to direct script calls (Phase 1/2/3/5 commands ×3 editions + research/ideation/plan/review Codex SKILL packs ×3 editions); `contractor` now remains only for Phase 6 finalization. New `scripts/test-full-advance.js` (67 assertions: orient step derivation across the full ladder, `state_missing`/`research_missing` refusals, checkpoint + `## Sink` byte-preservation, each phase file passing the real **two-arg** `unresolvedCompliance(content, stateContent)` gate as production calls it, `delegation_policy: delegate` default rows + explicit-row self-validation refusals, orchestrator-supplied compliance, `bad_compliance`/`bad_packet`/illegal-verdict/`unresolved_compliance` refusals, idempotent re-runs, a rich claim.js-shaped state surviving the full Phase 1→5 chain, and the rename-normalized edition-token shape). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.
- **scripts/fast: `kaola-workflow-fast-advance.js` — the fast path's deterministic mechanical transitions are now script-owned, off the `contractor` subagent (#456, ADR 0004).** A new typed transaction script owns the six fast-path mechanical seams that previously delegated to `contractor`: `plan-setup` (.cache + `step: plan` checkpoint), `plan-capture --stdin` (`fast-summary.md` `IN_PROGRESS` stub from the orchestrator-approved write set + acceptance command, then state pointer → execute), `execute-setup` (`step: execute` + the `tdd-guide` dispatch descriptor), `acceptance-run` (runs the recorded acceptance command, returns `exit_code`/`passed`/`evidence_path`/`repeat_count` facts only — writes no consequence), `acceptance-consequence --decision proceed|escalate` (PROCEED → `step: review` + `fast-summary.md` `REVIEW`; ESCALATE → `workflow_path: full` + `/kaola-workflow-phase1` routing + bare `ESCALATED`), and `summary-write --verdict PASSED|ESCALATED --stdin` (terminal `fast-summary.md`, written once, PASSED → Finalization), plus a read-only `orient`. The main session still owns ALL judgment (fast eligibility, approach ambiguity, PROCEED vs ESCALATE, acceptance sufficiency, review verdict); the script emits typed JSON only and never dispatches a role, asks the user, judges severity, chooses escalation, or invents write sets. Crash-safe write order (cache/evidence → `fast-summary.md` → `workflow-state.md` pointer last); every subcommand is idempotent/resume-safe and preserves an existing `## Sink` block byte-for-byte; missing packet fields are typed refusals with `operator_hint`. The script is FORGE-NEUTRAL (command/skill route names are KW-split so the rename-normalizer cannot mangle them) and ships byte-identical across all four editions, registered in `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` + `validate-script-sync.js` (`COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES`). All six fast-path prose surfaces migrated off `contractor` to direct script calls (the 3 commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs); `contractor` now remains only for Phase 6 finalization. New `scripts/test-fast-advance.js` (69 assertions: clean setup, idempotent re-run of every subcommand, `## Sink` byte-preservation, plan-capture refusals, acceptance-run facts-only, proceed→review, escalate→full + em-dash trigger, summary-write PASSED→Finalization, crash-order/stale-pointer fixture, corrupt-status refusal, no-contractor-dispatch on all six surfaces). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

### Fixed

- **scripts/adaptive: `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` now fails loud when an adaptive lifecycle call is run from the main root while a worktree is linked, instead of silently splitting authority (#466).** The adaptive executor's per-node lifecycle subcommands resolve the project folder — `workflow-plan.md`, the `## Node Ledger`, `.cache/<node-id>.md` evidence, and the barrier baselines — **cwd-relative** (`getRoot()` = `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`). Because `/workflow-next` starts the orchestrator shell at the main repo root and `kaola-workflow-plan-run` only pinned `Working directory:` on the *agent* dispatches, a straightforward run executed the whole lifecycle from the main checkout — so the ledger / plan / evidence / baselines became main-authoritative while the role agents' edits were worktree-authoritative, a split that stayed invisible until finalization derailed onto a fragile manual path (reported from a downstream consumer repo after a bundle adaptive run). Two coordinated fixes: (1) a `main()` **pre-dispatch split guard** — when a `worktree_path` is recorded for the project AND that worktree exists on disk but a MUTATING lifecycle subcommand (`open-next` / `open-ready` / `close-node` / `close-and-open-next` / `reconcile-running-set` / `write-halt` / `clear-halt` / `reopen-node` / `revert-overflow` / `repair-node` / `route-findings` / `record-evidence --stdin`) is invoked from the main root (`realRepoRoot === mainRoot`), the script refuses `worktree_authority_split` with **zero mutation** and an `operator_hint` to `cd` into the worktree; `orient` + `mirror-project` (the legitimately-main-root main→worktree copy) + `record-evidence --verify` are read-only and exempt, and native posture (no `worktree_path`, or a recorded-but-missing worktree) is unguarded so non-worktree runs are byte-unchanged. (2) the `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` prose now `cd`s into `$ACTIVE_WORKTREE_PATH` right after `mirror-project` and states the explicit **worktree-cwd contract**, so the happy path runs the whole lifecycle from the worktree and the guard is purely the fail-loud backstop. Propagated across all 4 script editions (canonical + codex byte-twin via `sync:editions`, gitlab/gitea rename-normalized forge ports) and all 6 plan-run prose surfaces (the 3 commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs). `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` adds T466-1…9 (REAL git-repo subprocess tests: guard fires on `open-next`/`open-ready`/`record-evidence --stdin` from main root with a linked worktree; native posture, missing-worktree, `orient`, `mirror-project`, `record-evidence --verify`, and lifecycle-run-from-the-worktree are all unguarded). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.
- **scripts/finalize: `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` no longer hard-codes the four npm edition chains, unblocking non-npm product repos (#464).** The finalization chain-receipt gate previously ran a fixed `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` set, so installing Kaola-Workflow into a Swift/Xcode (or any non-npm) repo produced a receipt with all four chains exiting `254` and finalization could not pass. The runner now resolves the validation chain set per repo: a repo-local **`kaola-workflow/chains.json`** (`{ "chains": [{name, command}, ...] }`) wins when present and is run via `spawnSync` (real exit codes) and recorded in the receipt; otherwise the built-in npm chains run, but only for the `claude/codex/gitlab/gitea` whose `test:kaola-workflow:<name>` script is actually declared in `package.json` (so the Kaola-Workflow self-host keeps its four chains and a `--chains claude` subset still works); otherwise the runner emits a typed `chains_config_missing` refusal and writes **no receipt** (instead of a misleading four-`254` receipt). `--chains` and `--accept-known-red` now validate against the resolved chain names (a repo-config name like `build` is valid), and the receipt records a `source` field (`repo-config`/`npm-default`). The `--finalize-check` chain gate was already name-agnostic (it iterates whatever `chains[]` the receipt records), so a configured chain set passes the gate unchanged — this is a one-script producer fix. Two fail-open holes (caught in adversarial review) are closed in the producer so the gate's trust is never falsely satisfied: an EMPTY effective chain set (e.g. `--chains ","`) now refuses `no_chains` with **no receipt** (a `chains: []` receipt would otherwise pass the name-agnostic gate with zero chains verified), and a partial-invalid `chains.json` (an entry missing `name`/`command`, a non-array `chains`, unparseable JSON) now refuses `chains_config_invalid` strictly rather than silently dropping the malformed entry and running fewer chains than declared. `kaola-workflow/chains.json` uses a slash so it stays edition-neutral; the script is rename-normalized across all four editions. `scripts/test-run-chains.js` adds T8–T12 (repo-config receipt is gate-acceptable, `chains_config_missing` refuses with no receipt, `resolveChains` precedence + strict validation, empty-effective-chains refusal, partial-invalid refusal — 60 assertions). Docs: `docs/api.md` § run-chains + `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` Chain-Receipt Gate document the config. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. (Follow-up: the `--finalize-check` gate itself has no `chains.length` guard, so a hand-crafted empty-`chains[]` receipt from any source would pass — a pre-existing cross-source hole in `plan-validator.js`, left for a coordinated fix since that file is under concurrent redesign.)
- **scripts/release: `kaola-workflow-release.js --cut` idempotent short-circuit envelope now carries `codex_version` / `codex_version_source` (#460).** After #455 the normal `--cut` success envelope reports `codex_version` and `codex_version_source` (`derived`/`explicit`), but the full-completion idempotent short-circuit (`runCut` returns early when the terminal `git_tag` step is already done for the requested version) omitted them — so a caller re-running an already-completed cut and reading `codex_version` from the `--json` result got `undefined`, even though the Codex manifests on disk were correctly at their resolved version. The short-circuit now backfills both fields from the `codex_resolution` receipt entry (added in #455) for the requested root version, falling back to the on-disk Codex baseline (`source: "persisted"`) for a legacy pre-#455 receipt that has no `codex_resolution` step, so the idempotent and normal success envelopes are shape-consistent. Observability/consistency only — no release mutation changes; the early path predates #455 (from #442). Propagated to all four edition release scripts; `scripts/test-release.js` adds T5d asserting the idempotent re-run envelope carries `codex_version`/`codex_version_source`. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

## [6.1.0] — 2026-06-14

### Removed
