- **codex: the `<role>-max` xhigh effort-variant profile matrix retired; the per-node tier drives base-role reasoning via session inheritance (#451, D-451-01).** Codex 0.139 has no per-spawn `reasoning_effort` override; instead a profile that OMITS `model_reasoning_effort` makes a spawned agent inherit the parent **session's** effort, and agent-config wins over the project profile (PR #14807). #451 retires the #405 matrix end-to-end and rebuilds per-node effort on that mechanism: (1) **schema** — `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical) drops `OPUS_ELIGIBLE_ROLES` + `variantProfileText` + their exports and adds `dispatchEffort(model)` → `opus ⇒ {codex_reasoning_effort:"xhigh", source:"planner_model"}`, `sonnet`/absent ⇒ `{null, "role_default"}`. (2) **dispatch** — `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4) `buildDispatch` emits `agent_type` (always the base role) + spreads `dispatchEffort(model)`; one builder flows to every dispatch caller (`scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` gains the `D451-DISPATCH-EFFORT` case, 752 assertions). (3) **base profiles** — the single top-level `model_reasoning_effort = "..."` line stripped from all 14 base `agents/<role>.toml` × 3 editions = 42 files (a pinned base effort would win over the session and render the feature inert — the load-bearing change). (4) **installer/preflight** — `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (×3) + `kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js` (×4) `validateProfileText` now treats `model_reasoning_effort` as OPTIONAL (present⇒legal, absent⇒valid); `generateMaxVariants` + `variantConfigBlock` + the `--generate-variants` branch deleted; the six `<role>-max.toml` names added to `RETIRED_PROFILE_FILES` so a machine that installed #405 prunes them on upgrade (user-owned `*-max` preserved via `extra_unmanaged`, never blanket-globbed). (5) **deletions** — 18 `<role>-max.toml` (6 roles × 3 editions) + 6 `[agents.<role>-max]` tables per `config/agents.toml`. (6) **contract validators ×3** — the `<role>-max` derivation/bijection guards + the README per-role reasoning-effort-table contract replaced by a FORBID (0 `*-max.toml`, 0 `[agents.*-max]`); the GitLab/Gitea install-count assert flipped 20 → 14. (7) **walkthroughs/tests** — the github-codex walkthrough's `test405MaxVariants` deleted + its three count asserts flipped 20 → 14; the two forge-codex walkthroughs drop the `model_variant_missing`/`<role>-max` SKILL-prose check; the two forge `test-*-workflow-scripts.js` flip their install-count asserts 20 → 14. (8) **plan-run SKILLs ×3** — the tier→`<role>-max` dispatch prose rewritten to the session-effort model (delegate to the base `dispatch.agent_type`; when `dispatch.codex_reasoning_effort` is non-null, raise the Codex session effort before spawning). Docs: `README.md` (the per-role effort table + `<role>-max` sentence → session-inheritance prose), `docs/api.md`, `docs/architecture.md` (roster "14 base + 6 -max = 20" → "14"), the three `kaola-workflow-adapt` SKILLs, and `docs/decisions/D-451-01.md` (the AC6 docs-based feasibility proof, the AC4 deviation, and the known session-effort-persistence tradeoff). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

### Fixed

- **scripts/release: `kaola-workflow-release.js --cut` now honors the independent Codex manifest series instead of forcing it equal to the root version (#455).** Previously `--cut --version X.Y.Z` set the three `.codex-plugin` manifests (and the README Codex lines) to the ROOT version, silently abandoning the deliberate dual-version scheme (root `5.x` ↔ Codex `3.x`, per #193; now `6.x` ↔ `4.x`) on every release. `--cut` now resolves a Codex version in its own series: derived by default from the current Codex baseline + the same bump-KIND as root (`6.0.0→6.1.0` ⇒ `4.0.0→4.1.0`; major ⇒ Codex major), or an explicit `--codex-version A.B.C` override. It never silently falls back to the root version: `codex_version_underivable` refuses (no last root tag and no override) and `non_monotonic_codex_version` refuses (target ≤ baseline), both in the pre-mutation block (no half-mutation); the JSON envelope reports `codex_version` + `codex_version_source` (`derived`/`explicit`). The cut also now bumps the 2 `.claude-plugin` manifests (gitlab/gitea) to the root version and updates the README "Claude Code command install, {edition} edition" lines (previously left stale, breaking `validate-workflow-contracts.js`). Codex-version resolution is crash-resume idempotent via a `codex_resolution` receipt step — a resume after the Codex bump but before the git tag reuses the persisted version (and skips the monotonic re-guard) instead of re-deriving against the already-bumped baseline, which had two faces: a README↔manifest mismatch on the derived path and a permanently-bricked resume on the explicit path. Propagated to all four edition release scripts; `scripts/test-release.js` rewrites the cut-success test to assert codex `!=` root and adds derive-major / explicit-override / `non_monotonic_codex_version` / `codex_version_underivable` (with no-mutation) / both crash-resume regression cases (94 assertions). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

## [6.0.0] — 2026-06-14

### Removed

- **adaptive/planner: per-node `FILE_CEILING` (6-file) validator wall removed; planner now groups cohesive write sets (#453).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical) drops the `FILE_CEILING` constant + export, and `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4: canonical + codex byte-twin + gitlab/gitea edition-named ports) drops the `n.writeSet.size > schema.FILE_CEILING` refusal — an adaptive node may now declare an arbitrarily large set of EXACT files in one node, so a planner can keep a semantically-cohesive write set (cross-edition mirrors, generated-aggregator siblings that must move atomically) together instead of artificially splitting it to fit a count. **Every other write-safety wall is unchanged and still binding:** exact-path shape refusals (directory / trailing-slash / glob / absolute / backslash / `..` / case-collision / bare-existing-dir → refused at freeze, #381/#388), read-only roles declare no writes, `generated_port_split` (#431), concurrent-sibling + select-arm write-set disjointness, the sync-group / agent-registration / forge-port-ordering union checks (now count-independent), and the per-node barrier's actual-write refusal (`write_set_overflow` / `unattributed_write`). The planner profile (`agents/workflow-planner.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors) and the three `kaola-workflow-adapt` Codex SKILL packs replace the "split a lane over six files into sequenced same-role nodes" guidance with the semantic-grouping rubric. The fast path's separate "absolute backstop of 6 files" is untouched and now explicitly fast-path-only. `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`: the A2 `FILE_CEILING` refusal fixture flips to a positive (a 12-exact-file node freezes in-grammar); the A3 22-path agent-registration fixture's node split is re-attributed to byte-group co-occurrence + forge-port ordering (count-independent; assertions unchanged). `docs/api.md` grammar + cross-references rescoped. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

### Added

- **scripts/adaptive: `operator_hint` on every typed refusal/halt envelope + plan-run skeleton/reference-card split ×6 (#445, D-445-01).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`, `kaola-workflow-commit-node.js`, `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`, and `kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` (×4 editions each) gain a module-level `OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY` — a `reason → templateFn(ctx)` map — so every typed refusal/halt envelope now includes `operator_hint`: one actionable sentence and the exact next command, generated at emit time from templates living IN each script (single source; no resident prose branches). Vocabulary contract: hints use only the sanctioned #424/#434 primitives (`revert-overflow`, `repair-node`), never `drop-base` laundering, and are forge-neutral (no `gh`/`glab`/`tea` tokens). Plan-run prose on the six surfaces (`commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs) reduced to a ~150-line loop skeleton (orient → dispatch → close → act on `operator_hint`); per-situation recovery branches (resume, governance, repair-routing, reopen-complete-node, frontier-batch) moved to five single-copy reference cards under `docs/plan-run-cards/` (indexed in `docs/README.md`). Pinned `frontier unit` literal and route-reachability contract survive verbatim in the skeleton. `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` + `test-commit-node.js` + `test-parallel-batch.js`: table-driven tests over each `OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY` verify every reason code emits a non-empty hint. Contract validators (×4 chains) pin that `operator_hint` is present in refusal output and that the skeleton's card-reference markers exist. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-445-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: `route-findings` subcommand + `--summary` envelope diet (#446, D-446-01).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions) gains (a) a `route-findings` subcommand that parses a gate node's evidence `finding:` lines into `.cache/findings-route.json` (`{ finding_id, file, owning_node, fix_role, status }` — `owning_node` resolved via write-set lookup over the frozen plan; `owning_node: null` is the plan-repair signal for a finding in a file no node declared); `close-and-open-next` auto-invokes it when closing a `VERDICT_ROLES` node so the orchestrator can route repairs from the compact descriptor without re-reading full evidence; and (b) a `--summary` flag on all four aggregators: prints a one-line `summary:` and writes the full JSON envelope to `.cache/<op>-envelope.json`; the interactive plan-run skeleton uses `--summary` and drills into the cached envelope only on `result: refuse`. Default output (full JSON) is unchanged; `--summary` is strictly additive. `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: three TDD fixtures — router fixture with 3 findings across 2 nodes + 1 unowned (routes + `null`-owner signal), summary-mode envelope byte-equality check, refuse-path envelope surfacing. All four chains green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-446-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: forge contract validators skip `//` comment lines in forbidden-token scan (#445 mid-run, n11e-fix-validators).** `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js` and `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts.js`: the `.js`-file forbidden-forge-CLI-token loop now filters `//`-prefixed comment lines before the `\bgh\b` / `\bglab\b` assertion. Fixes a false-positive where `OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY` documentation comments that spell out the forbidden-token constraint (e.g. `// - NO forge CLI token (gh / glab / tea) appears in any hint`) triggered the scan. Functional forge-binary detection is unchanged; the fallback-script assertion still tests the full unfiltered text.

- **scripts: `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` `--sink` mode — script-owned resumable worktree-sink transaction (#429, D-429-01).** Replaces the manual 8-step worktree sink choreography with a single `node kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js --sink --project …` invocation. `sink_blocked` preflight scans for foreign-dirt before ANY mutation — refuses with exact paths listed, zero writes committed; auto-stash applies only to `kaola-workflow/.roadmap/issue-N.md` entries in the sink's own `issueSet`. `sink-receipt.json` (atomic temp+rename) tracks each of the 9 sequenced steps (`stash_roadmap`, `push_upstream`, `merge_no_ff`, `worktree_sync`, `finalize_internals`, `close_issues`, `stash_restore`, `archive_commit`, `push_main`) with per-step `state:'done'` markers; a re-run of a killed transaction skips all done steps (the #296 pattern generalized). `push_upstream`/`push_main` tolerate already-pushed; `close_issues` uses `probeIssueClosed()` before each close; `archive_commit` skips an empty commit. `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` is a COMMON_SCRIPT: codex edition is byte-identical; gitlab/gitea forge ports are rename-normalized. `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs and `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs updated to route the worktree sink choreography to this mode (#400 ×6 surfaces). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-429-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: sanctioned repair primitives — `revert-overflow`, `repair-node`, `requires_redispatch` (#434, D-434-01).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions: canonical + codex twin + gitlab/gitea edition-named ports) gains three recovery-path primitives. `revert-overflow`: reads `outOfAllow` from `--barrier-check --json`, runs `git checkout <baselineSha> -- <exact-paths>` scoped to exactly those paths in the barrier root, appends a provenance-log entry, then re-runs the barrier to confirm cleared; injectable `gitCheckout` seam for testing. `repair-node`: the **anti-laundering primitive** — resets a `complete` writer back to `in_progress` while KEEPING the original `barrier-base-{nodeId}` intact (no `commit-node --start`, no re-snapshot); deletes only downstream gate baselines; returns `baselineReused: true` as explicit audit evidence. `orient` gains `requires_redispatch: true` for the case where a node is `in_progress` but its evidence file is absent or lacks the binding header — machine-signals the re-dispatch case (vs. the complete-evidence-but-no-commit crash case which re-runs the commit bracket only). `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: three new TDD fixtures (a/b/c), 641 assertions green (up from 623). `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs: resume/recovery-hint prose for all three primitives (#400 ×6 surfaces). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-434-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: lane-attributed disjoint write co-open with group-scoped close barrier behind `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` (#437, D-419 P2).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`, `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`, and `kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` (×4 editions each: canonical + codex twin + gitlab/gitea edition-named ports) implement the four settlements that fix the ADR D-419-02 Part 2 broken close-side story. (1) `tryFormLaneGroup` (`adaptive-node.js` L2522) + `runOpenReady` (`adaptive-node.js` L2550): under `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT=1`, a ≥2 disjoint write frontier is co-opened as a lane group — ONE shared group baseline recorded via `--record-base --node-id <group_id>`, a `lane_group` key written into `running-set.json` with `group_id`, `members`, `closed_members`, `baseline`, `write_union`, each member node entry stamped with `group_id`. Flag OFF ⟹ the existing `else { toOpen=[writeNodes[0]] }` serial write runs byte-identically (INV-6). (2) `closeGroupMember` (`adaptive-node.js` L2996): a non-last member's close runs evidence-shape + per-member in-lane vacuity (`memberInLaneChanges`) only — the diff barrier is DEFERRED; compliance row carries the literal `deferred_to_group` marker; response carries `barrier:'deferred_to_group'`. The LAST member's close shells the group barrier BEFORE closing, then clears `lane_group` and drops the group baseline on pass; response carries `barrier:'group_passed'`. New `member_vacuity` typed refusal when in-lane changes are absent with no `no_op:` declaration. (3) New `--parallel-safe --nodes A,B[,C] --json` flag (`plan-validator.js` L1627): read-only pairwise-disjointness check exposing the existing antichain pair-loop (exact-file + `classifier.disjointWriteSets`). `result:'ok'` ⟹ safe to co-open; `result:'refuse'` (reason `overlapping_write_sets`) ⟹ `open-ready` degrades to a single serial write. (4) New `--group-barrier --group-id <id> [--member <id>] [--skip-root-pin] --json` flag (`plan-validator.js` L1914): the group-scoped close barrier; diffs the group baseline → now over the UNION of all members' declared write sets via `barrierCheck` with `opts.groupMembers`; out-of-union paths hit the EXISTING rank-4 `unattributed_write` / `write_set_overflow` arm — no new reason code. Cross-lane runtime protection is advisory (the #376 hook is fail-open; the group barrier + `--finalize-check` attribution sweep are the teeth). `reconcile-running-set`: group survives iff ≥1 member survives; else `lane_group` cleared + group baseline dropped. **Flag-OFF invariant (INV-6, permanent):** `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` unset/0/false (the default) ⟹ `resolveLaneContainment(process.env)` returns false ⟹ every new arm is dead ⟹ serial behavior is byte-identical. Test coverage: `test-commit-node.js` 85 assertions (T-PS-1..5 `--parallel-safe` + T-GB-1..6 `--group-barrier` including T-GB-6 mutation/bite check); `test-adaptive-node.js` 623 assertions (T-OR-1/2 co-open/degrade, T-CL-1..5 deferred/last/sibling-stray/cross-lane-stray/vacuity, flag-OFF guards D437-OPEN-READY-FLAG-OFF / D437-CLOSE-NODE-FLAG-OFF-SERIAL); `test-parallel-batch.js` 214 assertions (T-PB-1 seal-non-regression + flag-OFF batch byte-identity). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-437-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: `max_concurrent` coordination-kernel field + INV-2/INV-7 byte-identity tests (#436).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions: canonical + codex twin + gitlab/gitea edition-named ports) gains an optional top-level integer `max_concurrent` in `running-set.json`, set at OPEN time as `min(cap, --max || cap)` — never at freeze time, never hashed. Absence implies 1 (fail-closed, legacy `open-next` default). `runReconcileRunningSet` caps its roll-forward re-opens at `ceiling − liveStable` (absent field ⟹ 1). `runCloseNode` and `runCloseAndOpenNext` empty-set fallbacks now spread `...running` so `max_concurrent` and any unknown top-level fields survive the rewrite (previously dropped). `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: three new assertions — **D419-INV2** (byte-identity: `open-next` never writes `running-set.json`), **D419-INV7** (crash-resume: reconcile honors the ceiling), **D419-CLOSE-FIELDSURVIVAL** (empty-set fallback preserves unknown fields). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Canonical spec: `docs/decisions/D-419-01.md` Part 1.

- **scripts/adaptive: scheduler-default-posture prose ×6 + planner rubric (D-419 P3, #438).** `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs (the 3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs — the #400 ×6 surface contract) updated with a new `Scheduler-default posture (D-419 P3)` block and a `Planner rubric (D-419 P3)` block: serial = `max_concurrent=1` (degraded mode, not the default); six concrete conditions under which serial applies; planner is rewarded for wide independent frontiers; `parallel_safe` is VALIDATOR-DERIVED — NEVER hand-authored. `agents/workflow-planner.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors (#422 token-pin pair): rubric paragraph added after "Author EFFICIENT DAGs" — rewards wide antichains, names serial as the degraded fallback, explicitly prohibits hand-stamping `parallel_safe` ([INV-17]). Pinned `frontier unit` literal retained at all six surfaces (verified by `validate-workflow-contracts.js` ×4). Forge-neutral: both `--forbidden-only` validators pass. `docs/architecture.md`: new "Coordination kernel" subsection documents serial-by-subsumption, `open-next`/`open-ready --max N` as two surfaces of one kernel, and absence-implies-1 semantics. Canonical spec: `docs/decisions/D-419-01.md` Part 3.

- **scripts/adaptive: `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` — machine-verifiable chain receipt for finalization (#432).** New SUPPORT script (×4 editions: canonical + byte-identical codex twin + two edition-named forge ports) shells each test chain via `spawnSync` (real exit codes, no shell-pipe aliasing) and writes `.cache/chain-receipt.json` with `{headSha, workTreeHash, startedAt, chains:[{name, exit}]}`. `--accept-known-red <name>:<open-issue>` records an auditable waiver for a known-red chain. `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4) `--finalize-check` gate adds three typed refusals: `chains_unverified` (no receipt on disk), `chains_stale` (receipt `headSha` ≠ current HEAD), `chains_red` (any chain `exit ≠ 0`, absent a covering waiver). Contractor Step 8c: contractor now invokes `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` and cites the receipt path as evidence, replacing the prior prose attestation. `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs and `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs updated with the receipt gate and run-chains dispatch prose (#400 ×6 surfaces). `docs/api.md`, `docs/conventions.md`, `docs/architecture.md`: receipt schema, new typed refusals, and "chain receipt is the only valid greenness evidence" convention. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-432-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: open-time evidence seeding + single-source `ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY` (#433).** `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4) exports `ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY` — the canonical per-role evidence token vocabulary — consumed by both the `checkEvidenceShape` validator and `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`'s open-time seeder, eliminating drift between the shape checker and what the agent is told to fill. `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4) now seeds `.cache/<node-id>.md` at `open-next` / `open-ready` / fused-advance time with a binding header (`evidence-binding: <id> <nonce>`) plus role-specific token stubs; the `opened` payload carries `evidence_file` and `required_tokens` for the dispatcher. `reopen-node` re-seeds the entire evidence file (fresh stubs, old body discarded), closing the #392 anti-replay gap that would have allowed a prior attempt's `verdict: pass` to survive a reopen via line-1-only nonce rotation. `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs updated with evidence-seeding dispatch prose (#400). Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-433-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: script-emitted `dispatch` descriptor in every adaptive opener + `record-evidence --verify` (#444, composing #433; #421 P1+P2).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions: canonical + codex twin + gitlab/gitea edition-named ports) gains `buildDispatch(nodeInfo, context)` — a single module-level builder called by ALL three openers (`runOpenNext`, `runOpenReady`, and the fused advance in `runCloseAndOpenNext`) so the `dispatch` sub-object in the `opened` payload is always field-complete and identical in shape across all three call sites (kills the #411 class where fused-advance descriptors diverged from serial-open descriptors). `dispatch` fields: `node_id`, `role`, `model`, `working_dir`, `declared_write_set`, `evidence_file`, `nonce`, `required_tokens`, `forge_rider`, `guards`. `deriveGuards(nodeInfo)` is script-owned: gate roles (`GATE_ROLES`) → `'read-only'`; `tdd-guide` → `'RED-fixture-in-$TMPDIR'`; write sets containing a generated-aggregator sibling → `'sync:editions'` (anchor-gated on `edition-sync.js` availability). New `record-evidence --verify` subcommand (`runVerifyEvidence`): reads `.cache/<node-id>.md` from disk with no stdin transit, delegates to the shared `checkEvidenceShape` + nonce-binding check, returns the same typed reason vocabulary (`evidence_shape_failed` / `evidence_stale` / `evidence_unbound` + `missingTokenClass`) as the close gate — no second token list, `ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY` is the single source. `--stdin` kept for one release (compat). `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs — the #400 ×6 surface contract): hand-written per-field dispatch assembly prose replaced with a single "render the script-emitted `dispatch` descriptor verbatim" instruction. `docs/decisions/D-444-01.md` authored. `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: five new assertions — **D444-DISPATCH-PARITY** (serial open == fused-advance dispatch shape), **D444-DISPATCH-OPENREADY** (batch opener == serial opener shape), **D444-VERIFY-ACCEPT** (`--verify` on well-formed file → `ok`), **D444-VERIFY-REFUSE-TOKEN** (missing required token → typed refuse per token), **D444-RECEIPT-PASSES-CLOSE** (receipt-only return passes when evidence on disk). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

- **scripts/release: `kaola-workflow-release.js` — one-transaction release aggregator (`--verify` / `--cut --version X.Y.Z` / `--push`) (#442, D-420 P4).** New maintainer script (×4 editions: canonical + byte-identical codex twin + gitlab/gitea rename-normalized forge ports) that CALLS the existing validators in place (no logic extraction). `--verify` derives the closed-issue set from git-log-since-last-`kaola-workflow--v*`-tag + CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` `#N` refs (offline → `verification: offline`, never a silent pass) and reads the #432 `.cache/chain-receipt.json` for the greenness signal; refuses `changelog_incomplete` with `missing:[n]`. `--cut --version X.Y.Z` requires an explicit version (`missing_version`), refuses non-monotonic versions vs the last release tag (`non_monotonic_version`), lockstep-guards the three `.codex-plugin` manifests (`lockstep_violation`), then atomically renames `[Unreleased]` → `[X.Y.Z] - <date>`, bumps the three Codex manifests + README + `package.json`, and creates the local `kaola-workflow--v` tag (tag-before-test); a step-receipt JSONL (`.cache/release-receipt.jsonl`, the #429 pattern) makes a re-run of a completed cut idempotent (`idempotent: true`). `--push` is the only remote-mutation path (push tag + forge release publish) and is forge-neutral — it names no forge CLI binary. The manifest paths and tag prefix are built from base-literals so the `validate-script-sync.js` rename-normalizer treats them as forge-independent data (the ports reference the real `plugins/kaola-workflow{,-gitlab,-gitea}/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` manifests and the one root `kaola-workflow--v` tag series). Registered in `COMMON_SCRIPTS` + the rename-normalized forge-ports family in `validate-script-sync.js`, but NOT in the install-manifest `SUPPORT_SCRIPT_NAMES` (a maintainer/dev tool on the `release-surface-drift.js` precedent, D-442-01 §6). `scripts/test-release.js` (claude chain): `changelog_incomplete` `missing:[n]`, `lockstep_violation`, `non_monotonic_version`, offline-verify receipt, cut-without-push-leaves-no-remote-mutation, `missing_version`, forge-neutral `--push`, step-receipt crash-resume, plus regression coverage for forge-port data-token preservation and double-cut idempotency. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-442-01.md`. Follow-up filed: #449 (version-blind `isStepDone` cross-version receipt bleed). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

- **scripts/finalize: `kaola-workflow-gap-sweep.js` — machine-gated run-gap capture at finalize (#435, D-435-01).** New SUPPORT script (×4 editions: canonical + byte-identical codex twin + two edition-named forge ports) that scans the per-project `.cache/` for run-discovered defect signals and gates finalization on their resolution. Scanner mode (`--project <P> --json`): reads `provenance-log.jsonl` (nodeIds with more than one `open` event → `in_run_repair`), `chain-receipt.json` (`accepted_red:true` chains → `deferred_red_chain`), and optional `.cache/run-gaps-manual.md` (`gap: <class> — <text>` lines → `manual:<slug>`); deduplicates by `(reasonClass, sample)`; writes `.cache/run-gaps.json`; emits `{result:'swept', project, sweptClasses, artifact}` (result is always `swept`, exit 0, empty sweep = `sweptClasses:[]`). Gate mode (`--check`): reads the artifact and the `## Run gaps` section of `finalization-summary.md`; each swept tuple must appear as `- <reasonClass> (<sample>): filed: #N` (tracks the gap in an open issue) or `- <reasonClass> (<sample>): noise: <reason>` (justifies as not worth tracking); emits `{result:'refuse', reason:'gaps_unswept', unmapped:[...]}` (exit 1) when any swept tuple is absent from the section; vacuously passes (exit 0) when `sweptClasses` is empty. Wired via contractor Step 8c.2 (after the #432 chain-receipt step) — NO edit to `cmdFinalize` (`kaola-workflow-claim.js`), mirroring the #432 run-chains pattern. Six finalize prose surfaces updated (3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILL packs — the #400 ×6 contract): `### Run-Gap Sweep Gate` subsection added after `### Chain-Receipt Gate` in each; `## Run gaps` line added to the `finalization-summary.md` template. Goal-follow-up-filing rule ("finishing an issue INCLUDES capturing its run-discovered defects; each gap filed or justified; silent deferral violates the goal + `gaps_unswept` refuses") propagated to `workflow-next.md` + the three next/adapt SKILL packs (six router surfaces per #400). Registered in `validate-script-sync.js` `COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES`; `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` (in both byte-paired copies); gitlab + gitea contract validators' `scriptFiles` + `installSupportScripts` lists. `scripts/test-gap-sweep.js` added to `package.json` `test:kaola-workflow:claude` chain. `docs/conventions.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/decisions/D-435-01.md` updated. Dogfooded on the #435 run itself. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green.

- **scripts/autopilot: `kaola-workflow-autopilot.js` — receipt-gated stage state-machine aggregator; `/kaola-workflow-auto` command (×6 surfaces: 3 Claude commands + 3 Codex SKILLs); ×4 script editions (#443, D-443-01).** New aggregator that drives the multi-bundle autopilot loop (scout → claim → plan → run → finalize) by READING merged receipt contracts — it dispatches nothing and names no forge CLI (lean-orchestrator boundary, #44). `next` subcommand: stateless function of (`--goal`, `--project`, on-disk receipts, optional `--scout-result <path>`) — reads the digest's last line + the relevant stage receipt and emits either a stage descriptor (`{stage, action, project, goal, inputs:{…}, receipt_path, repair?:{kind,node,paths}}`) or a typed stop. `digest` subcommand: appends one JSONL transition line to `kaola-workflow/<project>/.cache/autopilot-digest.jsonl` (append-only crash-resume; last non-empty line + stage receipt → resume point on restart). Both subcommands exit 0 on a clean descriptor or stop; exit 1 on an internal or argument error. Six typed stop reasons — all ground-truth-field-driven, never prose-grepped: `goal_satisfied` (cmdFinalize stdout `closure_receipt.goal_check === 'satisfied'`, #441), `backlog_empty` (scout JSON `backlog_empty === true && recommended_bundle === null`), `consent_halt` (state `escalated_to_full: consent` or ledger `consent_halt: pending`, #440), `security_halt` (state `escalated_to_full: security` without a concurrent consent marker, #440), `typed_refusal` (a `barrier_failed` envelope carrying #440 triage, or a claim/handoff/validator `{result:'refuse',reason}` envelope), `repair_limit` (two same-node `barrier_failed` events after a `repair_applied` digest entry under `KAOLA_AUTOPILOT_REPAIR=auto`). Receipt seams the driver gates on: #429 sink-receipt `steps.push_main === 'done'` (finalize-complete witness), #432 `chain-receipt.json` (tests-green witness — every chain `exitCode === 0 || accepted_red === true`), #441 `goal_check` (advisory `satisfied|absent`), #440 triage `class` + `proposed_repair`. Repair consent model (`KAOLA_AUTOPILOT_REPAIR ∈ {ask(default), auto}`): mechanical-class = exactly `{add_to_write_set, write_set_swap}` (auto-applicable under `auto`; bounded 1 auto-repair per node; 2nd same-node barrier_failed → `repair_limit`); `revert_overflow` and `unclassified` always halt even under `auto`; the autopilot surfaces a `repair` descriptor for the orchestrator to apply — it never edits the plan itself. `readPlanAllDone` ledger parser uses `$(?![\s\S])` (not `\Z`) in the section boundary regex so the last ledger section is captured correctly by the JS `String.prototype.match` engine. `agents/issue-scout.md` + 3 TOML twins gain the `backlog_empty` output shape (`{backlog_empty:true, recommended_bundle:null}`) as an alternative top-level envelope to `{recommended_bundle:{…}}`. Script is forge-neutral (zero forge-CLI tokens); ×4 editions: canonical `scripts/kaola-workflow-autopilot.js` + byte-identical codex twin + two edition-named forge ports (gitlab/gitea). Registered in `COMMON_SCRIPTS` + `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES`; `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` in `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` (both byte-paired copies). `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4) gains `AUTO_COMMAND='/kaola-workflow-auto'` + `AUTO_SKILL='kaola-workflow-auto'` exports. `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` + 4 `validate-*-contracts.js` pin `/kaola-workflow-auto` + SKILL presence ×6. `scripts/test-autopilot.js` covers the full stage walk, digest replay, all six stop reasons (fixture-driven), repair=ask vs repair=auto boundary, `backlog_empty` round-trip, and forge-neutrality body scan. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-443-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: consent-halt triage payloads — classified `class` + structured `proposed_repair` on write-halt AND barrier_failed envelopes (#440, D-440-01).** `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4 byte-identical) gains `WRITE_SET_OVERFLOW_SUBTYPES` (`lockfile_write`, `mirror_write`, `count_bump`) with literal-pattern tables. `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4 generated-aggregator) gains `classifyOverflowSubtype()` — narrows `write_set_overflow` in the `barrierCheck` reason envelope (rank 3, same arm as `write_set_granularity`, never a new top-level family). `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 generated-aggregator) gains `computeTriage(barrierOut)` that derives `{ class, testDelta?, proposed_repair? }` and attaches it to BOTH `runWriteHalt` returns AND `barrier_failed` closes (one shape on both channels, D-440-01 settlement 5). `proposed_repair` is the structured `{ kind, node, paths[] }` vocabulary (#434 primitives); computed-and-attached, never auto-applied. `--triage-json <path|->` CLI flag on `write-halt`. `testDelta` from chain-receipt when present, else node-evidence RED/GREEN lines. Unknown class degrades to `class: unclassified` (non-blocking). `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js`: T-440-A/B/C/D assertions (660 total). All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-440-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: goal-conditioned bundles — `KAOLA_GOAL` → issue-scout → hash-covered `goal:` Meta line → advisory `goal_check` receipt attestation in cmdFinalize (#441, D-441-01).** `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4) gains `parseGoal()` — reads `^goal:[ \t]*(.*)$` from `## Meta` via `classifier.sectionBody` (decoy-immune); reader-only, no gate; the `goal:` line is hash-covered by `computePlanHash` (tampering trips `plan_hash_mismatch` on `--resume-check`). `kaola-workflow-closure-contract.js` (×4 byte-identical) gains `goal_check: ['satisfied','unsatisfied','absent']` in `CLOSURE_RECEIPT_FIELDS` + `emptyReceipt()`. `kaola-workflow-claim.js` (×4: canonical + codex + 2 edition-named forge ports) gains `computeGoalCheck()` in `cmdFinalize` — v1: `KAOLA_GOAL` set or plan `goal:` line present → `satisfied`; else → `absent`; advisory in v1. `agents/issue-scout.md` + 3 byte-identical toml twins gain `## Goal Context` section + optional `goal_alignment: { aligned, reason }` output field. `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` + `commands/workflow-next.md` + 3 finalize SKILL packs: `### Goal Attestation (advisory, v1)` prose block. All four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-441-01.md`.

### Fixed

- **docs/codex: architecture and API hook-contract prose now match the #447 global-hook model (#452).** `docs/architecture.md` and `docs/api.md` no longer describe Codex lifecycle hooks as project-local `.codex/hooks.json` state; they now document `~/.codex/hooks.json`, the stable `~/.codex/kaola-workflow/{hooks,scripts}` home, project-local profiles/config only, and the legacy project-local cleanup caveat.

- **#449** `kaola-workflow-release.js` `isStepDone()` now version-keyed — cutting version B after version A in the same workspace without clearing the receipt no longer short-circuits on A's receipt rows and fabricates `result:ok` (the `version` field is now matched on every receipt lookup; `git_tag` row now stamps `version` too). All four edition release scripts updated.
- **scripts/adaptive: `.md` files no longer blanket-exempt from the per-node barrier (#424).** Previously any `.md` write was invisible to the barrier; a role agent could silently mutate `agents/workflow-planner.md` or `commands/kaola-workflow-*.md` without declaring those files in its write set. `isBarrierInvisible()` in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4) replaces the blanket exemption with a narrow allowband: `docs/**`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `README.md`, and `kaola-workflow/{project}/**` are still invisible (documentation surfaces expected to change); everything else requires explicit declaration. `--drop-base` is now window-locked with `drop_base_window_open` — attempting a drop-base re-snapshot while a node is `in_progress` is refused (prevents laundering via re-baseline). `--finalize-check` gains an attribution sweep: every file changed in the bundle diff must appear in at least one completed node's declared write set, or `unattributed_change` is returned. `root_mismatch` typed refusal added for plan-path root validation. `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/api.md`, `docs/conventions.md`, and `README.md` updated with allowband definition, new typed refusals, and the `.md files as production surfaces` convention. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-424-01.md`.

- **scripts: `archiveProjectDir` switched from rename to copy-then-verify-then-delete (#426).** `renameSync` was cross-filesystem-unsafe (EXDEV) and had no completion check before deleting the source. `archiveProjectDir` now uses `copyDir` → `verifyArchiveComplete` (confirms `workflow-state.md` exists in dest) → only then deletes the source. For linked-worktree finalization (`keepWorktree: false`), both the main-root live copy and the worktree live copy are deleted after the verified copy is in the archive. New `anchored_root` field added to the closure receipt. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-426-01.md`.

- **scripts: `cmdFinalize` now closes GitHub issues; both `cmdFinalize` and `sink-merge` use probe-before-close so they are independently idempotent (#427).** `cmdFinalize` added `closeIssueIdempotent(n, opts)` — probe-then-close, with re-probe on error and label-removal wrapped in its own try/catch — gated on `!keepIssueOpen && !OFFLINE && !args.keepWorktree`. Bundle runs drain all members; single-issue runs close the one issue. Closure outcome recorded in `closureReceipt.closure = { attempted, closed, failed, skipped_offline, kept_open }`. `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` (×4 editions) gained a matching probe-before-close guard: if the issue is already closed (e.g. by `cmdFinalize`), the redundant close call is skipped instead of erroring. `scripts/kaola-workflow-closure-contract.js` (×4): `anchored_root` added to `CLOSURE_RECEIPT_FIELDS`; `roadmap-residue-clean` added to `CLOSURE_INVARIANTS`. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-427-01.md`.

- **scripts: `reconcileRoadmapForClosure` now removes `.roadmap/issue-N.md` from BOTH main and worktree roots (#428).** Previously only the main-root source was removed; a stale copy in the worktree checkout could be swept into the implementation commit via `git add -A`. Extended to iterate over both roots, returning `roadmap_removed_by_root` (per-root list) and `roadmap_residue` (sources that survived — should be empty on clean closure). Post-build attachments `closureReceipt.roadmap_removed` and `closureReceipt.roadmap_residue` record the outcome. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-428-01.md`.

- **scripts/adaptive: three-point bundle coherence guard — `target_set_mismatch` at claim, `bundle_state_incoherent` at handoff and orient (#430).** Hand-edited or silently-collapsed `workflow-state.md` bundle fields previously passed all checks silently. Three guards added: (1) `cmdStartup` bundle path refuses `target_set_mismatch` if persisted `issue_numbers` ≠ claimed `--target-issues`; (2) `kaola-workflow-adaptive-handoff.js` (×4 editions) refuses `bundle_state_incoherent` in `runHandoff` if `bundle_id` is present but `issue_numbers` is absent or inconsistent; (3) `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions) refuses `bundle_state_incoherent` in `orient` on the same condition. `agents/workflow-planner.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors and `commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs (#400): `target_set_mismatch` added to bundle refusal tables, coherence guard described. `scripts/test-bundle-state.js`: 12 new assertions (tests d/e) covering orient-level incoherent-hand-edit detection. Decision record: `docs/decisions/D-430-01.md`.

- **tests: `test-bash-block-guards.js` red on main since 58ffd81 — fixture repaired and no-plan scenario added (#423).** Scenario-A fixture now writes a minimal `## Node Ledger` (with `| id | status |` header) after `workflow-state.md` so the Step-8a `ledger-compare` guard finds a parseable `--source` plan and exits 0. New Scenario-D asserts the Step-8a block exits 0 and still mirrors staged renames when no `workflow-plan.md` is present (full/fast-path projects). `agents/contractor.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors: added a `[ -f "$PLAN_PATH" ]` presence-check around the ledger-compare call so the guard is a no-op on full/fast-path projects (emits `ledger_compare_skipped: no_plan` and continues); 17/17 assertions pass; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:*` chains green.

### Added

- **adaptive/validator: `ledger_header_invalid` freeze-wall + `--repair` normalization (#425).** `validatePlan()` in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (×4 editions) now refuses a plan whose `## Node Ledger` section is present but whose header row lacks `id`/`status` columns (aliases `node`, `node_id`, `node-id`, `state` detected) with typed error `ledger_header_invalid`, naming the found columns. `reconcileLedger()` normalizes aliases to canonical `id`/`status` under `--repair` and reports `header_normalized: true` (hash-safe: ledger is outside `plan_hash`). Freeze-only — in-flight plans are never bricked retroactively. `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js` (×4 editions): `spliceLedgerNode` emits a structured `diagnostic` field on `node_not_in_ledger` when the `## Node Ledger` section is present but lacks the `id` column, surfacing `detected_columns`, `required_columns`, and a remediation hint. `agents/workflow-planner.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors: pins the `| id | status |` ledger header template with an explicit rule naming `ledger_header_invalid`. All four walkthrough editions: `testAdaptiveLedgerHeaderInvalid425` + `testCodexLedgerHeaderInvalid425` + gitlab/gitea equivalents assert the freeze-wall and `--repair` normalization.

- **adaptive/validator: `generated_port_split` freeze-wall (#431).** `validatePlan()` (×4 editions): a per-node write-set wall (after FILE_CEILING) refuses `generated_port_split` when a node declares `scripts/<base>` where `<base>` ∈ `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS` (from `scripts/edition-sync.js`) without also declaring the codex twin and both forge ports in the same node. Anchor-gated: silently inert when `edition-sync.js` is absent (forge/codex/consumer installs). `agents/workflow-planner.md` + 3 `.toml` mirrors: adds the aggregator-coupling rule subsection (correct declaration example included). `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` + 3 Codex SKILL packs (×4, #400): adds the "Generated-aggregator forge ports in the diff" paragraph explaining why a node that edits a canonical GENERATED_AGGREGATOR generates forge-port diffs at review time. All four walkthrough editions: `testAdaptiveGeneratedPortSplit431` + codex/gitlab/gitea equivalents assert split-shape refusal and bundled-shape pass. Three decision records: `docs/decisions/D-423-01.md`, `docs/decisions/D-425-01.md`, `docs/decisions/D-431-01.md`. `docs/api.md`: two new freeze-wall error code entries + `node_not_in_ledger` diagnostic field entry.

### Changed

- **Codex lifecycle hooks now install globally into `~/.codex`, decoupled from per-repo `workflow-init` (#447).** `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (the three byte-identical Codex editions) writes hooks to `~/.codex/hooks.json` + `~/.codex/kaola-workflow/{hooks,scripts}` instead of `<project>/.codex/`, while agent **profiles** stay project-local (`<project>/.codex/agents/kaola-workflow` + the managed `[agents.*]` config block). Every installer run — a per-repo init or a global pack upgrade — force-refreshes the single global hooks file, so refreshing hooks after an upgrade no longer means re-running `workflow-init` in every repo, and the project-local-vs-global double-fire is eliminated. `uninstall.sh` cleans the global hooks symmetrically (profiles are still removed project-local). Hooks remain a one-time `/hooks` trust per machine (content-hashed; re-trust only after a hook's content changes; `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is the non-persisting per-run bypass). README + the `workflow-init` SKILL/command prose (×3 editions) and the install tests — `scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js`, the codex-edition `simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js`, and the two forge `test-*-workflow-scripts.js` (all under a temp `HOME`) — were updated to the global model; all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains green. Supersedes the prior unreleased project-local `/hooks` documentation pass.

## [5.16.0] — 2026-06-12

### Added

- **design(adaptive/auto): goal-driven automation — two ADRs and a runtime-grounded investigation document (#420).** `docs/decisions/D-420-01.md` (Part 1: autopilot loop over scout→claim→plan→run→finalize — confidence-threshold gating (`confidence:"high"` is the proceed gate; `medium`/`low` parks with typed `confidence_halt`), selection-aloud #44 invariant (announce before `cmdStartup`, never silent), typed stop conditions `goal_satisfied`/`backlog_empty`/`confidence_halt`/`error_halt`/`consent_halt`, orchestrator-level boundary (subagents cannot dispatch subagents, driver must live in main session); Part 3: goal-conditioned bundles — optional `goal:` line in `## Meta`, hash-covered for free (`computePlanHash` already hashes full `## Meta`), backwards-compatible (`parseGoal` reader, no gate change), goal flows to issue-scout as context, `goal_satisfied` beats `backlog_empty` as terminal, finalize AC-vs-goal prose attestation; 12 invariants [INV-1..INV-12]). `docs/decisions/D-420-02.md` (Part 2: enriched consent-halt payload — offending-paths arrays threaded from `barrierCheck` into `write-halt` return (never re-derived), mechanical class taxonomy extending the #406 typed `reason` envelope with `lockfile_write`/`mirror_write`/`count_bump` subtypes under `write_set_overflow`, ready-made plan-repair diff as suggestion (never auto-applied — repair-consent boundary from [INV-6]), symmetric `test_thrash` enrichment with `testDelta`, forge-neutral JSON payload, ×4 edition propagation; Part 4: release aggregator `kaola-workflow-release.js --verify/--cut` — `--verify` mutates nothing (re-validates first per adaptive-handoff SPAWN-1→SPAWN-2 discipline), `--cut` re-verifies and gates on fresh pass, `changelog_incomplete` typed refusal reports missing issue numbers (closes #417 gap), operator runs forge CLI (aggregator prints the forge-neutral command only — no `gh`/`glab`/`tea` in shipped code), ×4 edition propagation; 11 invariants [INV-13..INV-23]). `docs/investigations/2026-06-12-goal-driven-automation-design.md`: runtime-grounded analysis over four surfaces (autopilot pipeline, write-halt machinery, plan_hash coverage, release surfaces), citing real file/lines. `docs/README.md` decisions index updated. No production-script changes — design deliverable only; companion track #421 is separate.

- **design(adaptive/parallel): parallelism v3 — two ADRs and a runtime-grounded investigation document (#419).** `docs/decisions/D-419-01.md` (Part 1: one coordination kernel — serial = running-set `max_concurrent=1` by subsumption, not deletion; `open-next`/`close-and-open-next` retained as the `max=1` aliases; 25 invariants [INV-1..INV-25] bind all downstream implementation issues; [INV-2] byte-identity is the hard invariant; [INV-4] four ledger states only; Part 3: scheduler-default posture — reads fan out by default, serial is the documented degraded mode for writes when `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` is off, planner rubric updated to reward wide frontiers via `longestPathToSink`; [INV-15/16] six-surface propagation + four-chain green required for Part 3 prose). `docs/decisions/D-419-02.md` (Part 2: lane-attributed disjoint write parallelism — validator stamps derived `parallel_safe` on disjoint write-node antichains, never authored; `open-ready` lifts `write_node_exclusive` only for stamped pairs behind `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT`; [INV-11] #283 seal-vacuity guard carried forward in its post-#364 barrier-allowlist form; two layered out-of-lane failure modes: hook fast-fail at write-time, barrier ground-truth at close-time, no new `unattributed_write` reason; [INV-14] requires P1 scheduler max>1 arm; Part 4: consent-gated speculative gate overlap — `speculative_open_policy: off|consent` Meta field, default off; [INV-21] speculative = `in_progress` NEVER `complete` while gate runs; gate close is strict prerequisite for descendant's close via new `gate_not_complete` refusal; [INV-20] 5-step baseline roll-back discard; speculative WRITE overlap is DESIGNED-but-DEFERRED; [INV-23] requires P1 max>1 arm). `docs/investigations/2026-06-12-parallelism-v3-design.md`: runtime-grounded analysis comparing v2 vs v3, citing actual script locations. `docs/README.md` and `docs/architecture.md` updated with cross-references. No production-script changes — design deliverable only; companion tracks #420/#421 are separate.

- **scripts: `test-agent-profile-parity.js` — regression guard that goes RED when a feature token is added to an `agents/*.md` without mirroring it into all three `.toml` twins (#422).** Curated `FEATURE_TOKENS` list (currently `write_set_granularity`, `main-session-gate`); a token is only enforced for a profile when it appears in that `.md`, so unused tokens never cause a false RED. GREEN at HEAD; guards the #404 planner-gap class going forward.

- **validate-script-sync.js: agent-profile `.toml` triple byte-group and `config/hooks.json` forge-port family (#422, #418.1).** Twenty `.toml` basenames (14 base agents + 6 `-max` variants) are now programmatically added to `BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS` via `fs.readdirSync` on the codex agents tree — any new profile is auto-covered. A new `CONFIG_HOOKS_FAMILY` + `normalizeConfigHooks()` verifies the three plugin-tree `config/hooks.json` files are rename-normalized (identical except the `codex-compact-resume` session-start command path).

- **validate-*-contracts.js ×4: pin `test-agent-profile-parity.js` into the claude test chain (#422).** All four contract validators now assert that `scripts."test:kaola-workflow:claude"` includes `test-agent-profile-parity.js`, mirroring how `test-route-reachability.js` guards the route surface.

- **package.json: claude chain gains `test-parallel.js --self-test` and `test-agent-profile-parity.js` (#418.2, #422).** `test-parallel.js --self-test` (13 assertions) is appended after `test-parallel-batch.js`; `test-agent-profile-parity.js` runs before `validate-workflow-contracts.js` so the parity check precedes the validator that pins it.

### Fixed

- **tests: bare-remote sink branch-delete order is now asserted — `testSinkMergeBareRemoteDeleteOrder` in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (#414).** A git-shim wrapper traces invocation order and asserts `push origin --delete` fires before `merge-base --is-ancestor` before `branch -D`, plus verifies no spurious `branch-worktree-resolved` violation. Covers the #397.1 fix gap: zero test coverage for the ONLINE branch-delete choreography.

- **tests: `defaultBranch` probe-chain unit added to `test-claim-hardening.js` (#414).** Two assertions cover the symbolic-ref hit (origin/HEAD set to `trunk` → returns `trunk`) and the hardcoded-main fallback (no origin, no remote → all probes fail → returns `main`).

- **forge walkthroughs: adaptive `--freeze-checked` → `governance_ack_stale` smoke added to both gitlab and gitea walkthroughs (#418.5).** `testGitlabAdaptiveFreezeChecked` / `testGiteaAdaptiveFreezeChecked` run the fork plan-validator's SPAWN-1 (`--freeze-checked` validates without writing) and SPAWN-2 (`--freeze --governance-ack <stale-hash>` refuses with `governance_ack_stale`) contract against a mutated plan.

- **install-manifest: intentional per-forge exclusions enumerated in comment (#418.2).** Both byte-paired copies of `kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` now have a block comment enumerating every script intentionally absent from `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` (edition-sync, fixtures-orphan-legality, fast-audit, install-manifest self, release-surface-drift, gitlab/gitea install-codex-agent-profiles, ledger-compare).

- **gitea claim.js: #369 two-line clarifier comment restored at bundle-member-probe site (#418.4).** The gitlab twin carried both `// #369: truthful ONLINE token …` and `// (never \`skipped_offline\`, the OFFLINE-only token).`; the gitea twin was missing the second line. Now parity-matched.

### Fixed

- **docs: post-v5.15.0 staleness sweep — CHANGELOG backfill, CLAUDE.md/docs/README/plan-run surfaces updated (#417).** Prose-only sync across six surface groups: (a) CHANGELOG.md: backfilled commit 91d9e5e as a 5.15.0 Fixed entry (#385 barrier-open freshness token, #388 round-2 freeze shapes, #389 atomic freeze, #390 point-of-use model_invalid gate); added #401 Part 2 entry; amended existing #401 entry's deferred clause to past-tense-shipped; changed "Prose ×4" → "Prose ×6" (the two forge-codex plan-run SKILLs carry the nonce/evidence-binding prose, so ×4 was the #400 documentation gap). (b) CLAUDE.md: Key Scripts — added `kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` aggregator; extended adaptive-node bullet with `open-ready`/`close-node`/`reconcile-running-set` subcommands + #383 mutual-exclusion guard prologue; extended plan-validator bullet with `--freeze-checked`/`--governance-ack` + emit-envelope `reason` field; rewrote adaptive-handoff bullet to the post-#408 fused 2-spawn chain (`--freeze-checked` → `--freeze --governance-ack`). (c) docs/api.md: corrected the #390 "Point-of-use `model_invalid` gate" sentence — it said "fires at `--freeze` time" but the actual `computeNextAction`-site guard fires at `open-next`/dispatch, not at `--resume-check` (verified against `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:66-80`); updated `<role>-max` clause from "tracked follow-up" to LIVE (#405); added #388 round-2 shapes, #389 atomic-freeze, #390 point-of-use gate notes to the validator section. (d) docs/architecture.md: added per-node model-column cross-reference paragraph at end of `## Model Resolution` section (#382). (e) docs/workflow-state-contract.md: expanded `.cache/` inventory — added `running-set.json`, `active-batch.json`, `barrier-base-<id>` (node-start snapshot), `barrier-open-<id>` (HEAD-at-open record). (f) README.md: added #377 running-set scheduler paragraph in the parallelism section; added #405 `<role>-max` dispatch sentence in the Codex reasoning-effort section; added #409 stable hooks home note (`.codex/kaola-workflow/{hooks,scripts}`) in the Codex hooks section. (g) Six plan-run surfaces (3 commands + 3 Codex SKILLs): `description:` frontmatter updated from "dispatching one frontier unit at a time with per-node checkpoints" to "running-set scheduler; each frontier unit dispatched when its dependencies complete" (literal `frontier unit` preserved, contract-validator pin honoured); `(current Claude Code)` runtime-coupling parenthetical in the (b′) background-dispatch paragraph replaced with "when the harness supports background subagent dispatch" (runtime-neutral). No code or behaviour changes; four chains green.

- **adaptive/node: `close-and-open-next` now surfaces `nonce` in its `opened` payload — serial chains no longer get `evidence_stale` on the second close (#411 Bug A).** The fused-advance path omitted the `nonce` derivation present in `open-next` and `open-ready`; every caller binding the next node's evidence got `undefined`, causing a guaranteed `evidence_stale` refusal. Uses the same nested `recordBase.base` path as the other two open paths.

- **adaptive/node: `close-and-open-next` removes the closing node from `running-set.json` and refuses to close an unsealed parallel-batch member (#411 Bug B).** Running-set blindness caused `reconcile-running-set` to no-op with `not_opening` (the just-closed node stayed in the set → next `orient` saw an `orphan_multi_in_progress` mismatch). An `excl: ['batch']` prologue guard prevents the serial close path from racing a live batch.

- **install/manifest: `kaola-workflow-ledger-compare.js` added to `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` — the #399 ledger-regression guard is no longer silently disarmed on manual install (#412).** The file was implemented in #399 but never registered; a fresh manual install omitted it, making the Step-8a staler-ledger guard unreachable.

- **agents/codex: `workflow-planner.toml` ×3 gains #381/#404 exact-file write-set guidance; `contractor.toml` ×3 gains #399 sync-order guard reference — Codex and Claude editions now carry equivalent guidance (#413).**

- **commands/skills: plan-run X6 surfaces updated with fused `close-and-open-next` as a third nonce source (#411 prose).** The #392 evidence-binding prose previously named only `open-next` (serial) and `open-ready` (batch) as nonce sources; the fused advance is now the documented third source across all 3 Claude commands and 3 Codex SKILLs.

- **plan-validator: refuse absolute-path and drive-letter write-set tokens at freeze (typed reason: `absolute_path`) — guaranteed `write_set_overflow` at barrier prevented (#415).** The freeze wall accepted tokens such as `/abs/path` and `C:src/app.js` (drive-letter form); those tokens froze successfully but always triggered `write_set_overflow` at the per-node barrier because the barrier only resolves repo-relative paths. Added a pre-grammar check that recognises both forms (leading `/` and `[A-Za-z]:` drive prefix) and emits a typed `{ result:'refuse', reason:'absolute_path' }` refusal before writing the plan — the failure is now surfaced at authoring time rather than mid-run at the barrier.

- **plan-validator: correct `barrier_base_mismatch` recovery hint at both sites to steer toward `--drop-base` + `--record-base` or ref-restore; adds laundering-warning (#416 Part A).** The two `barrier_base_mismatch` error sites both said "re-run `--record-base`", which cannot converge: the idempotent-reuse branch returns early without re-anchoring the ref when a base file already exists. Both hints now steer toward `--drop-base` then `--record-base` (or ref-restore for a genuine ref-only loss), and add an explicit note that re-recording after work is done would launder the crashed attempt.

- **claim: exclude `skipped_offline` from `closePendingFinalize`; surface `probe_degraded` in receipt — forge-outage probe failure no longer silently downgrades `remote-members-closed` invariant (#416 Part B).** When `cmdFinalize` performed an online probe and the probe threw (forge outage), the `catch` branch recorded `'skipped_offline'` and then `closePendingFinalize` counted that as `close_pending`, silently downgrading the `remote-members-closed` invariant. `'skipped_offline'` is now excluded from the `close_pending` bucket; the finalise receipt surfaces `probe_degraded: true` so operators know a probe was attempted but the forge was unreachable.

## [5.15.0] — 2026-06-12

### Added

- **adaptive/validator: emit-envelope migration + typed barrier reasons + write_set_granularity classification + fused `--freeze-checked` freeze chain (#406, #404, #408).** Three validator-surface follow-ups land together on `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` + consumers. **#406 (#355 rest):** the validator's per-subcommand `{ ok:false, … }` shapes migrate toward the canonical `{ result, reason/reasonCode, … }` envelope without breaking the shells — Class A (`verifyGateExecution`/`verifyVerdictBlock`/`revalidateForResume`) **dual-emit** `{ result, reasonCode }` ALONGSIDE the established `ok` (and the human `reason` string the resume-check stderr / handoff / adaptive-node still echo), so every consumer (commit-node `gateVerify.ok`/`verdictCheck.ok`, the integrity-gate `resumeCheck.ok` reads across adaptive-node/parallel-batch/adaptive-handoff, ~148 walkthrough `.ok` asserts) keeps working; Class C (the standalone `--selector-check`/`--verdict-check` CLI arg-errors) migrate to `{ result:'refuse', reason }` (the 2 walkthrough `scJson.ok===false` asserts flip to `result==='refuse'` — the only deliberate consumer change); `barrierCheck` gains a typed `reason` carrying the highest-precedence matched family (`foreign_archive` > `sensitive_write_unreviewed` > `write_set_overflow` > `unattributed_write`, `null` on pass) plus surfaced arrays (`foreignArchiveHits`, `unattributed` — additive, no consumer reads it today). **#404 (#381 Part C, BUILD-SMALLER — auto-repair deferred):** `barrierCheck` sub-classifies a per-node overflow as `write_set_granularity` when EVERY out-of-allow file is a strict subtree of one of THIS node's OWN bare directory tokens (`src/`/`src`) — the mechanical granularity artifact — via structural literal-string-prefix detection (NO mutation, NO re-freeze, NO auto-repair); plan-run surfaces a per-class actionable consent-halt ("node declared the bare directory 'X' but wrote X/a.js X/b.js — re-author to the exact files and re-freeze") across all 6 prose surfaces (3 commands + 3 codex SKILLs) plus the per-reason message map for the other four families. The auto-repair machine (the issue's guards 3-7) is **permanently deferred**: an adversarial pass proved it unbuildable-safe — `revalidateForResume` only asserts `stored === computePlanHash(content)`, but `freezePlan→injectHash` re-stamps the hash over the just-mutated `## Nodes`, so any post-repair integrity gate validates the mutated plan against its own fresh hash and is always green (freeze is the only legitimacy oracle and cannot check itself). A planner/adapt-SKILL doc warning names the one shape the freeze wall cannot catch (a bare token that becomes a directory by write-time). **#408 (#366 deferred, Option A):** new `--freeze-checked --json` (validate + governance payload, no write) lets `adaptive-handoff` fuse its freeze chain 3→2 spawns — SPAWN 2 `--freeze --governance-ack <planHash>` re-validates, asserts the hash is unchanged (`governance_ack_stale` refuse + no write on a mid-spawn plan edit), writes atomically (#389), and folds `--resume-check` into its emission (`resumeOk`); governance byte-identical (refuse paths, decision:ask audit rows, PE write order). Plan-validator is COMMON (canonical→codex byte + forge hand-port via `propagate.py`); commit-node/adaptive-node/adaptive-handoff via `sync:editions`. Four chains green.
- **editions/adaptive: the forge-codex adaptive SKILL pack ships + a registry-driven route-reachability contract closes the dead zone (#400, #393b; closes the forge-codex #380/#369/#405 prose gap).** The gitlab/gitea Codex editions routed adaptive — the default path since #254 — to `kaola-workflow-plan-run` / `kaola-workflow-adapt` SKILLs that **did not exist** (the byte-identical schema emits the route, the forge `claim.js` routes adaptive unconditionally, but the forge `skills/` trees shipped neither SKILL). **Shipped (Option A):** `plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/skills/kaola-workflow-{plan-run,adapt}/SKILL.md` as forge-renamed mirrors of the canonical Codex SKILLs (the script-path prefix, the cache-probe twin, and the `{adaptive-node,commit-node,next-action,plan-validator,adaptive-handoff,parallel-batch,claim}` script names renamed to the `kaola-{forge}-workflow-*` ports; `adaptive-schema`/`resolve-agent-model` keep the canonical byte-identical name; gitlab=MR, gitea=PR). The forge-codex plan-run SKILLs **inherit** the #405 `<role>-max` tier→profile dispatch + `model_variant_missing` prose (this delivers #405's "all three codex editions" AC, which was blocked on these SKILLs existing) and the #392 evidence-binding nonce prose. **Route-reachability contract (#400 heart):** a registry-driven assert in all four contract validators (`scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` + the three `validate-kaola-workflow-{,gitlab,gitea}-contracts.js`) `require()`s the schema route constants (`PLAN_RUN_SKILL`/`ADAPT_SKILL`/`PLAN_RUN_COMMAND`/`ADAPT_COMMAND`) plus the static `next_skill`/`next_command` fallbacks and asserts **every receipt-emitted route resolves to an installed surface** (skill dir for codex, command basename for claude), with a content-reachability tier asserting a mirrored SKILL carries the command's wiring tokens. **Forge-codex dead-zone content ports:** `--issue-numbers`/`issue_numbers` wiring into the gitlab/gitea finalize SKILLs (#369/#393b — every forge-codex bundle finalize now closes + buckets every member, not just the primary) and the #380 adaptive-route + auto-bundle router restructure into the gitlab/gitea next SKILLs (the forge next SKILL had **zero** adaptive routing — no Branch-B switch resolution, no `workflow-plan.md exists -> kaola-workflow-plan-run` rung, no front-end route). Tests: `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` (per-edition receipt-emission → every route resolves, with a RED-proof against the pre-#400 dead-zone surface set + a content-reachability tier) and an adaptive-route scenario in both forge Codex walkthroughs (`simulate-{gitlab,gitea}-codex-workflow-walkthrough.js`, formerly 0 adaptive mentions). The 6-surface propagation rule (3 commands + 3 codex SKILL packs — the "×4" wording is the symptom of the dead zone) is documented in `docs/conventions.md` + `CLAUDE.md`. GitLab/Gitea skill counts bumped 9→11. Four chains green.

- **codex/adaptive: per-node model tier is live on Codex — generated `<role>-max` xhigh effort-variant profiles (#405, #382 deferred half).** Completes #382's deferred Codex half (the Claude editions already mapped the per-node `model` tier to the `Agent(model=…)` param). `OPUS_ELIGIBLE_ROLES` (planner, code-architect, tdd-guide, code-reviewer, security-reviewer, adversarial-verifier — the node-dispatchable roles where output quality is bounded by reasoning depth/gate per the planner rubric) and a deterministic `variantProfileText(base, role)` transform are defined once in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-identical anchor). A new `install-codex-agent-profiles.js --generate-variants` subcommand (exported `generateMaxVariants`) materializes **6 committed `agents/<role>-max.toml` profiles per codex tree (18 total)** — name=`<role>-max`, `model_reasoning_effort = "xhigh"`, developer_instructions verbatim — plus their `[agents.<role>-max]` blocks in `config/agents.toml`, because the github-codex bijection guard and the codex-preflight `^\[agents\.…\]` regex both require 1:1 file↔table (an install-only generated file would dangle the bijection). The variants install automatically via the unfiltered `copyAgentProfiles` copy loop. **Dispatch (SKILL prose):** the codex plan-run SKILL now selects `<role>-max` when a node's resolved `model` is `opus` on an eligible role; `sonnet`/absent stays on the base profile; a missing variant degrades to base with a visible `model_variant_missing: <role>-max → base` note (never blocks). **Guards:** a derivation assert in all three codex contract validators pins each committed `<role>-max.toml` to `variantProfileText(base)` (a hand-edit or drifted base reds the chain) and forbids a `-max` file for a non-eligible role; the github-codex README role-catalog excludes the machine-generated `-max` variants. **#340 parity counts bumped** 14→20 in the gitlab/gitea contract validators (`agentFiles.length`) and their test suites + the github-codex simulate (`tomls.length` / `manifest.roles.length`); github-codex stays enumeration-free (bijection) with the new derivation guard. Tests: `test405MaxVariants` (derivation parity, schema-valid, idempotent transform, fresh-install black-box) + count bumps. Four chains green.

- **install/editions: single-source install.sh SUPPORT_SCRIPT_NAMES/SUPPORT_HOOK_NAMES from one manifest (#407, #365 deferred half).** Closes #365's deferred "≤2 places to register a new shared script" goal. A new `scripts/kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js` exports `supportScripts(forge)` / `supportHooks(forge)`, derived from ONE canonical `SUPPORT_SCRIPTS` list + a rename-IFF-the-forge-port-exists transform (the edition-sync discipline) + a small set of per-forge-only adds (`forge.js`, `codex-compact-resume.js`) and one content-rename override (gitlab `sink-pr`→`sink-mr`). install.sh's three hand-maintained `SUPPORT_SCRIPT_NAMES=(…)` + `SUPPORT_HOOK_NAMES=(…)` case-block arrays (the exact triplication that fed the surface-map-undercount class) are replaced by a single PORTABLE `while IFS= read -r … done < <(node …)` loop (NOT `mapfile` — the install target may run macOS bash 3.2; matches the #363 pattern) per list, with an empty-array fail-loud guard (anti 5.4.0-silent-empty). Registering a new shared support script is now ≤2 places (the manifest, + a rename override only if its forge port is content-renamed). The manifest self-checks (`exit 2` on empty/invalid/unknown-forge emission). `validate-workflow-contracts.js` cross-checks every emitted name against the on-disk forge scripts/hooks dirs (closes surface-undercount at validate time) and re-targets the prior `assertIncludes('install.sh', …)` registration checks onto the manifest; the gitlab/gitea validators do the same. A `KAOLA_INSTALL_MANIFEST` env override keeps the #363 planted-typo fail-closed test non-destructive. Tests: `test-install-manifest-single-source.js` (per-forge emission parity, a planted new entry picked up with no install.sh edit, empty/invalid guards, no residual install.sh array literals) + 3-forge fresh-install verification (every manifest script lands in `$SUPPORT_DIR/scripts/`). `bash -n install.sh` green. Four chains green.

- **tests/parity: forge plan-validator refusal anchors + compact-context drift coverage (#401, Parts 1 + 3).** Closes two #347-drift-class gaps the #365 sync work left open (Part 2 — promoting the plan-validator into edition-sync generation — shipped (see `## [5.15.0]`)). **Part 1 (forge refusal matrix):** the gitlab/gitea walkthroughs exercised only `--freeze` happy paths, so a forge-side regression in the #381/#382 write-set + model refusals passed all four chains. A new `testGitlabPlanValidatorRefusalMatrix401` (+ gitea twin) drives the REAL forge plan-validator `--json` CLI (`runNodeRaw`, asserting `status===1` + parsed `result==='refuse'`) over a 3-fixture matrix — a directory-shaped `src/` entry (`/directory-shaped/`), a `src/../b.js` traversal (`/contains '..'/`), and a 7-column plan with model `haiku` (`/model_invalid/`) — plus a green anchor (exact files → `in-grammar`, `status===0`) proving no false-refusal. The gitlab/gitea contract validators pin the new function name. Mutation-checked: neutering a forge refusal reds the gitlab/gitea chain. **Part 3 (compact-context drift):** the gitlab/gitea `kaola-{forge}-workflow-compact-context.js` ports carried backticked `` `fast-summary.md` `` that canonical+codex did not (drift unguarded); the backticks are removed (all four now byte-identical), the stale `validate-script-sync.js` rationale (which falsely claimed compact-context was "Claude-only … no plugin-local copy is needed") is corrected, and the whole family is brought under coverage — canonical↔codex in a `BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS` entry and the forge ports under a new SELF-CONTAINED `RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES` mechanism in `validate-script-sync.js` (deliberately NOT edition-sync, to avoid colliding with #407's install.sh manifest and the deferred plan-validator promotion); `*-codex-compact-resume.js` ×3 (the no-root 3-tree family) is brought under the same rename-normalized coverage with no content edit. Mutation-checked: re-introducing a backtick or a body drift reds `validate-script-sync.js`. Four chains green.

- **editions/adaptive: plan-validator promoted into `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS` — forge ports now regenerated via `npm run sync:editions` (#401 Part 2).** Previously the plan-validator forge ports (`kaola-gitlab-workflow-plan-validator.js` / `kaola-gitea-workflow-plan-validator.js`) were hand-maintained; they are now generated from the canonical `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` via `sync:editions`, closing the #347-drift-class gap identified in the Parts 1+3 entry. Part 2 was deferred in the #401 Parts 1+3 entry pending the in-flight #404/#406 plan-validator edits; it shipped as commit 0c6e314 before the v5.15.0 tag. Four chains green.

### Fixed

- **release: tag-must-be-ancestor-of-HEAD guard (#402).** `validate-workflow-contracts.js`'s release gate checked the tag EXISTS but not WHERE it points, so a release stack rebased after tagging (an origin-advance during the sink — the live #281/#296/#328 shape) kept the tag on the orphaned pre-rebase commit while `npm test` stayed green (post-rebase content is identical → zero surface drift), and a `gh release create` on an UNPUSHED tag silently created the remote tag at the default-branch tip (a different commit). `release-surface-drift.js` gains a pure, injectable `tagAncestry(root, tagName, headRef, {tagTarget, isAncestor})` → `{ok, tagSha, reason}` (defaults `git rev-list -1 <tag>` + `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tag> HEAD`); it reds `ok:false reason:'tag_not_ancestor_of_head'` ONLY on a definitive non-ancestor, stays inert (`ok:true`) when the tag is absent (the existing tag-existence assert owns absence) or ancestry is indeterminate (a shallow clone / git error → `ancestry_indeterminate`). Wired inside the EXISTING tag/online/.git gate (after the tag-exists assert); the refusal names `git tag -f <tag> <commit>` + push-the-tag-before-`gh release create`. INERT on a legitimately-tagged release (the v5.14.0 tag IS an ancestor of HEAD — verified). README release checklist + `docs/conventions.md` now state: push the tag explicitly BEFORE `gh release create`, and re-point + force-push the tag after any rebase of the release stack. Tests: `test-release-surface-drift.js` (ancestor→ok / orphaned→reds / absent + indeterminate→inert) + walkthrough `testTagAncestorGuard402` (pure-fn injected). `validate-workflow-contracts.js` + `release-surface-drift.js` edition-synced canonical→codex. Mutation-checked: an orphaned tag reds. Four chains green.

- **install/codex: Codex hooks point at a stable, version-less home instead of the installer's run-time path (#409).** `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (all three codex trees) wrote `~/.codex/hooks.json` with hook commands substituting `path.resolve(__dirname, '..')` — wherever the installer physically ran — and copied **zero** hook scripts to a Codex-owned home. So a routine "reinstall from a throwaway `/tmp` worktree" baked a `/private/tmp/…` path that macOS later purged (live failure on 2026-06-11: every PreToolUse/Write/SubagentStart/compact hook fired exit 127), and even the plugin-cache route baked a version-pinned `…/3.14.0/…` path GC'd at the next release. The fix mirrors `install.sh`: the installer now **copies the hook-referenced scripts** (parsed from each edition's `config/hooks.json` template via the new exported `hookReferencedRelPaths` — auto-adjusting to the edition-named `kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-codex-compact-resume.js` basenames) **into a stable `~/.codex/kaola-workflow/{hooks,scripts}/` home** (the new exported `copyHookScripts`, write-temp-then-rename + chmod 0o755, fail-closed on a missing source) and substitutes **that** version-less dir into `__KW_PLUGIN_ROOT__` instead of `pluginRoot` (which stays the read SOURCE). Reinstall sweeps the prior stable `{hooks,scripts}` dirs first (no orphans). `uninstall.sh` removes the stable home. Regression test (RED against the un-fixed installer): install FROM a throwaway plugin-tree copy, DELETE the copy, then assert every `hooks.json` command still resolves to an existing executable, names no deleted-source/version-pinned path, and that reinstall sweeps a planted stale script — in the github-codex simulate + the gitlab/gitea test suites. Installer byte-identical ×3 (`validate-script-sync` group). Four chains green.

- **adaptive scheduler: the P1 mutual-exclusion spine — a unified guard prologue across every mutating subcommand (#383, #384, #385, #386, #387, #391, #392, #403 items 1/3/4).** The three coordination surfaces (serial `open-next`/`close-and-open-next`, the batch manifest `active-batch.json`, and the #377 running-set `running-set.json`) each enforced only their own invariant; every mixed sequence the plan-run prose invites landed in an `orphan_multi_in_progress` wedge whose repair looped. A new **layered guard prologue** now runs before every mutating subcommand body in a fixed order: **(1) integrity (#387)** — `open-ready`/`close-node` run the same `--resume-check` plan-integrity gate as `open-batch`/`top-up` (refuse `plan_integrity_failed`, zero mutation), so a tampered write node can no longer be reclassified read-only and fanned out; **(2) consent-halt fence (#391b)** — a durable `consent_halt: pending` refuses `halt_pending` in `open-next`/`open-ready`/`close-and-open-next`/`close-node`/`open-batch`/`top-up` (a resume/loop that skips `orient` can no longer sail through a halt); **(3) live-coordination mutual exclusion (#383)** — a shared pure `readCoordinationState` probe (`serialLive`/`runningSetLive`/`batchLive`, consistent with `crossCheckStatus` single_in_progress per the #293 agreement) refuses `serial_node_live`/`scheduler_active`/`batch_active` per a per-command matrix, so the union state is refused at creation; `reopen-node` gains the missing running-set arm; `close-and-open-next` never reports `opened` for an already-in_progress node (#383d). **#384:** `reconcile-running-set` gains a CLOSE direction (a ledger-terminal member stranded in an `open` set by a crash/RMW is dropped — `closed_member_dropped`; `orient` routes the wedge there via `running_set_close_incomplete` instead of looping), and the compliance-row append is idempotent per node (no duplicate `Required Agent Compliance` row on a re-close). **#385:** every rollback / close-direction drop shells `--drop-base` per node (`reconcile-running-set` + `parallel-batch reconcile --abort`), closing the #281/#296 stale-baseline trap inside the new #377 path. **#386:** the write-lane PreToolUse hook self-exempts the open write node's own lane (`kind:write`) so `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` no longer bricks every serial write node; the Bash-bypass posture is documented (barrier = ground truth) in ADR 0008. **#387/#391a:** `clear-halt` is re-runnable after a crash (state-first/plan-last reorder + a widened gate that also fires on a stranded `escalated_to_full`). **#392:** the close gate enforces an anti-copy/anti-replay `evidence-binding: <node-id> <nonce>` header (the per-open barrier-base SHA prefix surfaced by `open-next`/`open-ready`) — copied evidence refuses `evidence_unbound`, replayed/stale evidence refuses `evidence_stale`; absent-on-disk nonce skips the check (backward-compatible with the ~40 existing 3-arg callers). **#403:** `safeJsonParse` ignores a trailing non-object scalar (1); `open-next` surfaces `baselineReused` (3); the close paths emit a non-blocking `verdict_unparsed` warning for a verdict-bearing role's near-miss verdict (4).
