HARD INVARIANT preserved: with containment off, no running-set/batch, ≤1 in_progress, and no halt marker, `open-next`/`close-and-open-next` are byte-identical to today (each guard is vacuously-pass; regression + #293 cross-consistency tests pin it). Mutation-tested per guard. Prose ×6 (claude command + codex SKILL + gitlab/gitea commands + the two forge-codex plan-run SKILLs carry the nonce/evidence-binding prose — ×4 is the documented #400 symptom) instruct passing the nonce and echoing the binding header; reason codes in `docs/api.md`, the hook posture in ADR 0008. Aggregators edition-synced (codex + both forges); `write-lane.sh` byte-identical ×4.
- **finalize: contractor Step-8a ledger-regression guard + tribal recovery prose (#399).** The 2026-06-11 full-workflow audit reproduced live (at v5.14.0/ba094a2) a clobber where the contractor's Step-8a artifact mirror `cp -R`'d a STALER main `kaola-workflow/{project}/` over a finished worktree copy, resetting its `## Node Ledger` from `complete` back to `pending` so the archive would commit a pending-ledger plan. A new forge-neutral `scripts/kaola-workflow-ledger-compare.js` (no `kaola-<forge>-workflow-` token, so it byte-copies to codex unchanged — added to `COMMON_SCRIPTS`) exports `countComplete(planMdText)` (its own 5-line `## Node Ledger` regex parse, NOT plan-validator's `parseLedger`) + `compareLedgers(srcText, destText)` → `{safe, reason, sourceComplete, destComplete}`, returning `safe:false reason:'would_regress_complete_ledger'` IFF the dest is present AND strictly more complete than the source (STRICT `>`), and FAIL-OPEN (`safe:true`) on the legitimate first sync (dest absent/empty/no-ledger). The CLI (`--source --dest [--json]`) exits 0 safe / 3 unsafe / 1 usage. The guard is inlined BEFORE the `cp -R` in all four shell homes (agents/contractor.md Step 8a + the three finalize SKILLs), inside the existing `if [ "$ACTIVE_WORKTREE_PATH" != "$(pwd)" ]` gate: on a non-zero exit it echoes `REFUSED: main copy staler than the worktree ledger; sync worktree->main FIRST` and exits 1 (skips the clobbering copy). The three recovery rules previously living only in operator memory (rediscovered across the #293/#254/#328 runs) are now shipped prose in `contractor.md` + mirrored to the three finalize SKILLs: (1) sync order is worktree→main BEFORE the mirror; (2) the machinery never authors the implementation commit (surface it if missing, do not cover); (3) after a sink-merge rebase detour, repair the MAIN checkout named in the failure's `git -C` line, never `cd` the deleted worktree, force-with-lease. New `scripts/test-ledger-compare.js` (claude chain) + walkthrough `testLedgerCompareGuard399` cover the strict-`>` regression, the first-sync fail-open, equal-completeness idempotence, and both empty. `validate-workflow-contracts.js` pins the guard reference + the recovery phrase in `contractor.md`. ×4 editions (script byte-synced canonical→codex; the guard resolves the forge-neutral script from the base tree in the gitlab/gitea SKILLs); four chains green.
- **lifecycle: finalize/sink/claim hardening round 2 (#393a, #394, #395, #396, #397, #398, #403.2/.6/.7/.8).** A batch of finalize/sink/claim lifecycle defects found by the 2026-06-11 full-workflow audit, where the all-or-nothing/truthful-receipt/guard contracts were enforced at some sites and absent at equivalent ones. **#393a sink-merge member-set fallback:** `sink-merge` now derives the bundle member set from the live/archived state's `issue_numbers` when `--issue-numbers` is absent (the flag becomes an override; a flag/state mismatch WARNs flag-wins) — a bundle sink run without the flag no longer silently closes only the primary. `deriveMemberSet` returns `[]` for a true single-issue (no `issue_numbers:` line) so the `length>1` close-loop never trips — byte-identical single-issue output (no misfire; `member_source` surfaced in the JSON, and `writeState` now emits `issue_numbers:` only for a real bundle, `length>1`). **#394 exit-3 archived-lane:** the merge-impossible fallback chain now works on a project finalize already archived — `sink-merge` writes the `sink-fallback.json` receipt (with `resolved_default_branch` + the full member set) to the ARCHIVE `.cache` (was "skipping receipt write"); `claim.js sink-fallback` operates on the archived state; `sink-pr`/`sink-mr` read the archived folder, guard `appendSummary`, resolve `--base`/target from the default branch, and record `pr_url`/`mr_url` to a durable `sink-pr-result.json` BEFORE any throwable step (no more orphaned open PR/MR invisible to watch-pr). **#395 finalize crash-window:** `buildClosureReceipt` skips `undefined` so seeded `'failed'` defaults never vanish (receipt-field-survival root fix); a shared `reconcileRoadmapForClosure` is reused by both `archiveProjectDir` and the `cmdFinalize` source-missing backstop so the archived-but-roadmap-live state converges on finalize re-run (the previously non-convergent loop); the worktree variant cleans a surviving MAIN-root live copy on re-run; opt-in `--strict` (exit 4 on `closure_invariants.ok:false`, default exit 0 — D1). **#396 truthful receipts:** `cmdRelease` captures + reports `claim_label_removed` and warns on non-removal (was discarded → silent re-claim trap); the single-issue online path records `close_pending` not `skipped_offline` (#396.2); keep-open keys on the recorded `keep_open_requested` intent, not the mutable token (#396.3); a new `close_disposition: close_pending` qualifier suppresses the pre-sink `remote-members-closed` alarm on the merge lane (D2, #396.4 — it fired on every happy-path bundle finalize); the sink-merge close loop probes/classifies already-closed members as success and dedupes `--issue-numbers` (#396.5); watch-pr is bundle-aware and surfaces `probe_errors` (#396.6). **#397 race-recovery receipt:** sink-merge deletes the remote branch first then `-D` after verifying merged-into-default (the post-re-rebase `branch -d` refused on every successful race recovery → spurious `branch-worktree-resolved` violation); remediation text says `--force-with-lease` + states the worktree/cwd disposition; `defaultBranch` gains an offline-safe probe chain (symbolic-ref → remote show → ls-remote --symref → main); forge `fastForwardMain` literals removed (#397.4). **#398 guard parity:** `assertSafeBranchArg` throws at ALL branch-creation sites (provisionWorktree, in-place `checkout -b`, patch-branch — not just teardown); `writeState`/`patch-branch` refuse a newline/CR in durable fields (field injection); `worktree-finalize` commits a pathspec'd index (no longer sweeps a pre-staged unrelated file). **#403 polish:** bare `no_target` refusal carries a `reasoning` field (.2); keep-open sink-merge bundle members get explicit per-member comment + label removal (.6); `roadmap_staged_reconciled` records the #297 staged-orphan unstage (.7); a raw `worktree_error` is collapsed + classified into `worktree_error_class` (.8). `close_pending` enum + `close_disposition`/`keep_open_requested` plumbing in `closure-contract.js` (4-tree byte-identical). Mutation-checked guards; ×4 editions (canonical + codex byte-sync + gitlab/gitea hand-ports); four chains green.

- **adaptive/validator: freeze hardening — #388 round-2 write-set refusal shapes, #389 atomic freeze write, #390 point-of-use `model_invalid` gate, #385 baseline-freshness `barrier-open-<id>` token + `stale:head_advanced` warning (#385, #388, #389, #390).** Round 2 of the write-set refusal shapes (#388) adds missing error codes that the #381 first pass left out. The atomic freeze (#389) makes `plan_hash` stamping idempotent to concurrent writes (write-temp-then-rename). The `model_invalid` gate (#390) validates the `model` column value at the point-of-use (plan-run `open-next`) rather than at freeze-time, so a stale model name is caught before a node is dispatched. The baseline-freshness token (#385) uses `barrier-open-<id>` files created by `open-next`/`open-ready` to detect when HEAD advanced since the baseline was snapshotted, emitting `stale:head_advanced` as a non-blocking warning so the operator can decide whether to re-anchor. Shipped as commit 91d9e5e before the v5.15.0 tag.

### Changed

- **init: drop the obsolete local-Karpathy-skill discovery from `workflow-init` (#410, follow-up to #352).** The three `workflow-init` command editions still enforced a local Karpathy-skill find — an "Inputs" block that asked the agent to discover `../andrej-karpathy-skills/skills/karpathy-guidelines/SKILL.md`, fall back to `KARPATHY_SKILLS_PATH`, or *ask the user* where their local clone lives — plus a Step-2 sentence telling it to use that file "only to confirm" the working-principle bullets. This added friction for zero gain: the canonical 5 working-principle bullets are already hardcoded inline in the `KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE` under `## Non-Negotiable Rules` (the source of truth), and the codex `kaola-workflow-init` SKILL editions already inline them with no discovery dance. The discovery block + the Step-2 conditional are removed from all three command editions (`commands/workflow-init.md` + `plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/commands/workflow-init.md`); the Inputs section keeps only `Use $ARGUMENTS as optional project context.`, and the Step-2 tail keeps a plain "Keep the working-principle bullets concise." The byte-locked template region is untouched, the three editions stay byte-identical in the changed block, and no contract validator pinned the Karpathy text. Four chains green.

## [5.14.0] — 2026-06-11

### Added

- **adaptive: planner-determined per-node model tier — optional `{opus|sonnet}` model column with plan-beats-install resolution (#382; codex effort-variants deferred).** The `## Nodes` grammar gains an optional `model` column with a closed two-tier vocabulary `NODE_MODEL_TIERS = {opus, sonnet}` (no haiku), defined once in `adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-identical anchor). Applies the repo's *agent-owns-reasoning* principle (#44) to model selection: the planner is the only component that sees the task, so it picks the tier per node. **Parse:** `validatePlan.parseNodes` + `classifier.readPlanNodes` both read the column header-indexed with absent-column back-compat (the `selector_source` pattern) — every existing frozen plan/fixture stays valid byte-for-byte and hash-stable (`plan_hash` already covers `## Meta` + `## Nodes`, so assignments seal at freeze and survive `--resume-check`). **Resolution precedence (plan beats install):** `next-action.computeNextAction` now resolves `node.model || resolveModel(node.role)` at both descriptor sites — `--profile=higher` becomes the fallback for unassigned nodes; a deliberate downgrade (a cheap reviewer node under `--profile=higher`) is allowed. Because next-action is the single resolution point and every dispatch surface passes its per-node `model` through, the feature reaches serial and parallel dispatch automatically. **Parallel-path fix:** `open-ready` (the #377 running-set scheduler) no longer drops the model — `newNodes` carries it, the `opened` return stops hardcoding `undefined`, and `running-set.json` members **persist** `model` so `reconcile-running-set` roll-forward / crash re-dispatch keep the planner's tier. **Validator (freeze-time, minimal):** a non-empty cell outside the vocabulary → typed refusal `model_invalid`; a `main-session-gate` carrying a model → refusal (it is never dispatched as a subagent). Tests: T6b parse↔read parity extended to `model`; a new `open-ready` model-persistence unit (R12); walkthrough governance scenarios (valid tiers freeze green / `model_invalid` / gate-refusal). ×4 editions (adaptive-schema byte-identical; next-action + adaptive-node edition-sync-generated; plan-validator hand-ported; classifier codex-byte + gitlab/gitea hand-ported — the forge classifier ports are line-divergent so the one-line model field was hand-applied). The planner **model-assignment rubric** is documented in `agents/workflow-planner.md`, the three codex/forge `workflow-planner.toml` profiles, and the adapt SKILL; the plan-run dispatch prose (×4 + codex SKILL) now explicitly instructs passing the returned `model` on every `Agent(... model=…)` call; the grammar + typed refusals are in `docs/api.md`. Four chains green. **Deferred to a follow-up:** the Codex `<role>-max` xhigh effort-variant generation (§5 — 18 static `.toml` profiles across 3 trees + `config/agents.toml` registration + the #340 parity count bumps). On Codex the tier is parsed + validated but currently selects the role's base profile (graceful, zero regression); the full per-node model tier is live on the Claude editions.

- **tests/bundle: cover release/watch-pr bundle paths, probed-open receipts, and crash recovery (#371).** Closes the bundle-lifecycle test gaps the #328 surface left unexercised. New `test-bundle-finalize.js` scenarios (canonical chain): **watch-pr bundle MERGED** — asserts the per-member close buckets (`closed_issues`/`open_issues`) and the truthful `partial` token when a member is still open online (the planted-regression target: dropping a member from the receipt fails the chain); **watch-pr bundle CLOSED** — abandoned archive + every member's advisory claim cleared + `issue_numbers` preserved; **crash interleaving (a)** — `release` after a mid-claim crash clears EVERY member's advisory claim (idempotent recovery); **crash interleaving (b)** — a finalize re-run after the source folder is already archived (post-rename crash) is a graceful refusal, never an uncaught exception; **worktree-suppression posture** — in-place (`KAOLA_WORKTREE_NATIVE=0`) finalize provisions no `.kw/worktrees/`. The harness gains a `pr view` gh-mock route (configurable PR state) and a `sink:pr`/`pr_url` bundle-state variant. Combined with the existing release-loop test (#371-part), the #369 probed-open receipt test, and the claim-side smaller-branch coverage in `test-bundle-claim.js` (naming/sort, malformed-token `target_set_invalid_token`, classifier `target_set_red`, duplicate-block), the listed surfaces each have an asserting test. Forge bundle scenarios remain #342's scope. Four chains green.

- **adaptive: unified refusal/emit protocol + shared constructors (#355).** The adaptive scripts now share a framed-output + refusal contract via two helpers in `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-identical anchor): **`emit(obj)`** writes exactly one compact JSON line (single-line so the last-valid-JSON-line parser always round-trips it even after a stray log line), and **`refuse(reason, extra)`** builds the canonical `{ result:'refuse', reason, ... }` envelope (per-subcommand payloads carry extra fields additively). Builds on the session-1 framing fix (whole-payload-then-last-line `safeJsonParse` + reserved `exitCode` key in `commit-node`/`adaptive-node`/`parallel-batch`). **Bug fixed:** `kaola-workflow-task-mirror.js` emitted its `missing_arg`/`plan_not_found`/`plan_not_frozen` refusals on **stderr** while `shellNode` parses `err.stdout` only — so the reason was always lost and `refreshTaskMirror` degraded to a bare `'failed'`. Those refusals now `emit(refuse(...))` to **stdout** (exit 1 preserved, legacy `status` key kept for back-compat), and `refreshTaskMirror` surfaces the recovered `reason`. Documented as the canonical envelope in `docs/api.md` § Adaptive Refusal / Emit Protocol. Tests: `test-commit-node` Test 8 (stray-line round-trip + reserved-exitCode) + `test-adaptive-node` S1–S3 (emit/refuse units + end-to-end task-mirror-reason-via-shellNode). ×4 editions (schema byte-identical; task-mirror codex byte + forge-renamed ports; the schema require propagates verbatim since the schema is not forge-renamed); four chains green. The plan-validator's established per-subcommand `{ ok:false, ... }` shapes are kept as backward-compatible variants (not migrated — that would break consumers); migrating every remaining emission to the shared constructor is an additive follow-up.

- **editions: scripted edition sync — `sync:editions` generator + rename-normalized forge-aggregator parity (#365).** Cross-edition propagation of the adaptive aggregators is no longer hand-copy-validated after the fact (the #347 drift class — a producer ported to one edition but not the forge ports, green over all four chains). New `scripts/edition-sync.js`: **`--write`** (`npm run sync:editions`) regenerates the five forge **aggregator** ports (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-{adaptive-node,next-action,commit-node,parallel-batch,adaptive-handoff}.js`) from canonical via a **declared** rename map, then cp's the `COMMON_SCRIPTS` set canonical→codex and the byte-identical groups across editions; **`--check`** (now wired into the gitlab + gitea chains) recomputes each generated port from canonical and fails the chain on any byte mismatch — a hand-edit to a generated port, or a canonical edit not propagated, turns the chain RED at commit time. The rename map is **declared, not derived** (a `kaola-workflow-<X>` script base-name is renamed to `kaola-<forge>-workflow-<X>` iff a renamed port file exists — which auto-excludes the byte-identical `adaptive-schema`, and a bounded lookahead leaves `/kaola-workflow-<command>` slash-commands and the `kaola-workflow/` state dir untouched). Generated ports carry an `// @generated from scripts/<base> by \`npm run sync:editions\`` header. This normalized the prior hand-divergence where some aggregators forge-renamed their `usage:`/repair-hint strings and others did not. `scripts/test-edition-sync.js` covers parity-green, planted-1-line-edit-RED, idempotency, the adaptive-schema exclusion, no-over-rename of commands/state-dir, and the header. Reuses `validate-script-sync.js`'s exported `COMMON_SCRIPTS`/`BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS` (single source). Four chains green. **Deferred (follow-up):** single-sourcing the install.sh `SUPPORT_SCRIPT_NAMES` + per-forge validator lists from one manifest (the "≤2 places to register a new shared script" goal — a higher-risk install.sh change), and bringing the three 3-copy unguarded files (`install-codex-agent-profiles.js`, `*-codex-compact-resume.js`, `compact-context`) under the same coverage.

- **adaptive: parallelism v2 — event-driven per-node running-set scheduler (#377).** The post-#364 successor to the batch-as-a-unit machine: an additive per-node scheduler that opens and closes individual nodes against a `.cache/running-set.json` manifest so a DOWNSTREAM node unblocks PER NODE (not per whole frontier). Three new `adaptive-node.js` subcommands: **`open-ready [--max N]`** flips up to N ready-pending nodes (priority-ordered by `next-action`'s `longestPathToSink`), records per-node baselines, and two-phase writes the manifest (`opening` → flip ledger → `open`, crash-safe like `open-batch`); **`close-node --node-id`** runs the same evidence-shape → per-node `--barrier-check` → ledger-complete → compliance → selector-arm contract as the serial close, removes the node from the running set, and returns the newly-ready frontier; **`reconcile-running-set`** rolls a crashed `opening` set forward (rows that flipped) / back (rows still pending). The **#293 multi-in_progress legality re-keys to the running set** (`runOrient` + `parallel-batch` `crossCheckStatus` now accept in_progress rows that match the running-set node set as a valid per-node fan-out, and route a crashed `opening` set to reconcile; `orient` resume reconstructs the live set from it). **Safety / serial fallback:** with `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` off (the default + permanent fallback) the only concurrency `open-ready` creates is read-only fan-out (read-only nodes share the parent tree and never write); a write node opens **alone** (one at a time, never alongside a read or another write) — byte-identical to today's serial path (`open-next`/`close-and-open-next` are untouched; regression-guarded). The cross-lane write+read overlap the design envisions stays **dormant**, gated on the #376 lane-containment worktree primitive. `next-action.js` `computeNextAction` annotates each `readyPending` entry with `longestPathToSink` and orders the frontier by it (descending; document order tiebreak) — classic critical-path list scheduling; `readySet`/`nextNode`/`allDone`/`active` stay byte-unchanged. Adds `RUNNING_SET_NAME` (`running-set.json`) + `isReadOnlyNode`/`readRunningSet` to the public surface. State-level acceptance coverage in `test-adaptive-node.js` (R0–R11: read-only fan-out, write-alone serial fallback, write exclusivity, crash→refuse-reconcile, close+compliance+remove, last-node unlink, barrier-fail no-mutation, reconcile roll-forward/back, orient legality, opening-set reconcile, legacy open-next intact) + `test-parallel-batch.js` (running-set cross-check + 2-arg byte-identical regression guard). ×4 editions (adaptive-node + parallel-batch codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea forge-renamed ports); four chains green. (The ×4 plan-run prose event-driven-loop rewrite is the remaining wiring that makes the scheduler the default route.)

- **hooks/adaptive: write-lane containment PreToolUse hook — the enforcement primitive #320 was waiting for (#376).** A new `hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh` (PreToolUse `Write|Edit`, byte-identical ×4, forge-neutral) DENIES an out-of-lane write at the moment it happens instead of stranding finished work at seal-time (the #320/#364 leak). It is **fail-open everywhere unexpected** — a missing/`0` flag, a missing or malformed `.cache/running-set.json` manifest, unparseable stdin, or a non-git cwd all exit 0, so non-adaptive and serial sessions are never touched — and **dormant-but-correct** until the #377 per-node scheduler writes a real manifest of open write-nodes. With a manifest present and `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` enabled it enforces two deny rules: (a) a write inside a member worktree (`.kw/node/<projTag>/<id>`) outside that member's `declared_write_set` ∪ the workflow bands (`kaola-workflow/`, `.cache/`); (b) a write in the PARENT worktree matching any open node's declared lane (the leak shape). A new fail-closed `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT` flag (`resolveLaneContainment` in `adaptive-schema.js`, default false, successor of the retired #320 `resolveBatchCwdEnforced` shape) gates enforcement; the hook is wired into all four editions' `hooks.json` + Codex `config/hooks.json` and the three `install.sh` `SUPPORT_HOOK_NAMES` blocks. Honest layering (AC8): the hook intercepts `Write`/`Edit` only — Bash-mediated writes stay covered by the per-node `--barrier-check` own-lane allowlist + seal vacuity (hook = fast-fail containment; barrier = ground truth). The live-harness deny probe (AC1) is not walkthrough-satisfiable, so it ships as a **scripted harness** (`testWriteLaneHookGuard`) that drives the hook binary with crafted PreToolUse stdin and asserts fail-open + both deny rules + in-lane/workflow-band allow (mutation-checked), plus a `resolveLaneContainment` resolver unit and ×4 registration assertions. Four chains green.

- **adaptive/integrity: barrier-base SHA cross-check + `--drop-base` (#368).** The per-node barrier baseline is now cross-checked against its gc-anchored ref: `--barrier-check` refuses `barrier_base_mismatch` when the `.cache` base-file SHA differs from the anchored ref's SHA, or the ref is missing while the file exists — closing the trivial spoof (overwrite the base file with a fresh-snapshot SHA to empty a node's diff and neuter the blocking barrier). A new idempotent `--drop-base` deletes file+ref together; `reopen-node` shells it so a reopen leaves no dangling ref. New real-git integration test `scripts/test-barrier-base-integrity.js` (claude chain) + a reopen drop-base assertion. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **adaptive/contracts: producer-attested evidence tokens (#359).** The shape-gate evidence vocabulary moved into the PRODUCING agents' Output Contracts, so the tokens originate in the subagent's returned report and the orchestrator transcribes them verbatim instead of synthesizing them (the prior gap that degenerated the mechanical close gate into orchestrator self-attestation): `tdd-guide` (agents/*.md + 3 toml editions) gains a RED→GREEN evidence-block spec; `implementer` gains `verification_tier` — exactly one of `regression-green`/`build-green`/`smoke-integration`. The evidence-ownership contradiction is resolved: producing agents RETURN evidence in their final report and never self-write `.cache/` — the orchestrator records it via `record-evidence`, identical for serial and batch members. Emission-side pins (#290 pattern) in all four contract validators. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **adaptive: script-owned consent-halt lifecycle — `clear-halt` subcommand + fence-aware halt probes (#360).** New `adaptive-node.js clear-halt --project P --reason consent|security`: ONE typed transaction that removes the ledger `consent_halt: pending` marker AND the matching `escalated_to_full` state marker(s) (consent⇒security coupling: clearing consent clears both), replacing the prior two-file prose contractor lockstep (ADR 0004/0005). Typed refusal `no_halt_present` with zero mutation when no durable halt exists; `invalid_reason` otherwise. All halt probes now route through the LEDGER-SCOPED `readDurableConsentHalt` (adaptive-schema) — a decoy line outside `## Node Ledger` no longer forces a phantom halt (orient) or fakes idempotence (write-halt). plan-run prose ×4 replaces the contractor-lockstep block with the clear-halt call; validator pins ×4; RED→GREEN tests in test-adaptive-node.js. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **scripts: batched + memoized remote issue probes (#362).** `active-folders` gains a per-invocation issue-state memo (number→open/closed) shared by `issueIsClosed` AND `probeIssueState`, plus a single batched prefetch — `readActiveFolders` resolves every active folder's issue in ONE `gh issue list` (forge: `forge.listIssues`) before the per-folder filter, instead of N per-folder `gh issue view` round-trips; `claimProject`'s repeated readActiveFolders and `cmdStatus`'s double-probe reuse the cache. Cross-process reuse: the memo serializes to `KAOLA_ISSUE_STATE_SNAPSHOT` for the canonical subprocess classifier (the forge classifier is in-process and shares the memo directly). New `scripts/test-issue-probe-memo.js`: 3 issues cost 1 gh call; a seeded subprocess makes zero. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **adaptive: fused `--node-end` barrier subcommand — 4 validator spawns → 1 (#366).** The plan-validator gains `--node-end --node-id N`, running barrier-check + gate-verify + verdict-check + selector-check in ONE process / ONE plan parse (selector-check only when the node IS a selector source), emitting the same per-check payloads keyed in one envelope and keeping the #368 anchored-ref cross-check. `commit-node`'s per-node end-mode shells `--node-end` once instead of four separate validator spawns (synthesizing each sub-check's exitCode so `combineResults` is unchanged), with a fail-safe fallback to the legacy four spawns when the validator lacks the subcommand. Spawn-count test proves 4→1 (5 on the fallback path). The secondary `--freeze-checked` (handoff 3→2) is deferred — the handoff runs decision-hit governance between validate and freeze. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **adaptive/telemetry: per-node wall-clock telemetry — `node-timings.jsonl` (#373).** Best-effort sidecar: `adaptive-node` (open-next / record-evidence / close-and-open-next / write-halt) and `parallel-batch` (open-batch / seal-member / top-up) append one JSON line per lifecycle transition to `kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/node-timings.jsonl`. Append-only and never blocks a transition; real-tempdir test asserts opened/closed events. This is the honest source for wall-clock overlap claims (state-level tests never make them). ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **adaptive/plan-run: background member dispatch — make rolling top-up actually roll (#374).** A new (b′) "Background dispatch (design D2)" step in the plan-run batch-dispatch prose on all four surfaces: when the harness supports `run_in_background` Agent dispatch with completion notifications, each opened member is dispatched in the background and, on each completion, the orchestrator runs record-evidence → seal-member → top-up (while `status` reports `nextRoute:'top-up'`, per #322), dispatching the newly opened sibling — the rolling bounded dispatch #303 designed, finally reachable. Gang dispatch remains the documented fallback. Prose-only: the aggregators are already pure state machines built for exactly this loop. Verify wall-clock via #373's node-timings.jsonl.

- **adaptive: split runtime concurrency caps — `KAOLA_FANOUT_CAP_READONLY` (#375).** `adaptive-schema` gains `DEFAULT_FANOUT_CAP_READONLY=8` + `KAOLA_FANOUT_CAP_READONLY` + `resolveFanoutCapReadonly` beside the write cap; `parallel-batch` open-batch/top-up pick the cap by batch kind (read-only vs write-role); plan-run prose ×4 documents the split. A read-only batch of 10 opens 8 (test). ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **design-first deliverables: prose-dedup via install-time partial rendering (#367), optimistic lane concurrency (#378), `map(<group>)` dynamic fan-out (#379).** Three design docs recorded under docs/: the #367 dedup inventory (model badge ×27, kaola_script one-liner ×27, Prompt Defense Baseline ×16, …) + the chosen `commands/_partials/*.md` `{{> name}}` include mechanism reusing install.sh's line-renderer and #365's generator, with render-parity validation for canonical files; the #378 verdict matrix, 3-way-merge join, and typed `merge_conflict` escalation; the #379 G-MAP rules, expand-map, and resume/freeze/barrier folding. Implementation tranches deliberately sequenced later (#367 after #372/#365; #378/#379 pending the lane-containment primitive). docs-only; no chain impact.

### Fixed

- **adaptive: directory-granularity write-set entries now refuse at FREEZE instead of dying at the barrier (#381, Parts A+B; Part C deferred).** A `declared_write_set` entry naming a directory (`src/`, trailing slash) or carrying a `..` segment used to freeze in-grammar (it parses to a non-empty token, counts 1 toward `FILE_CEILING`, no shape check) but was **dead on arrival** at the per-node barrier — `barrierCheck` matches by exact membership (`declared.has(p)`), so a real write `src/foo.js` is never `declared.has('src/')` → guaranteed `barrier_failed` → the executor escalated a purely-mechanical authoring artifact to a ~45-minute human consent halt (field report: a codex-edition run on 2026-06-11). **Part A (freeze-wall):** `validatePlan`'s per-entry loop now emits two typed refusals — `directory-shaped` (token ends in `/` after `normalizeRepoPath`) and `contains '..'` — placed before the `FILE_CEILING` check, so the dead declaration is caught at the authoring gate, not deferred to a maximally-expensive mid-run halt. **Freeze-only:** `revalidateForResume` is untouched, so an in-flight plan frozen by a pre-#381 validator still passes `--resume-check` (no brick) and falls through to the unchanged consent route. Exact root-level / dot-leading files (`Dockerfile`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/x.yml`) are unaffected (the check keys on a trailing `/` only); the exact-file barrier semantics are **preserved** (no glob/prefix matching added — explicit non-goal, proven by a test that `barrierCheck` still refuses `src/foo.js` vs a `src/` declaration). **Part B (authoring prose):** `agents/workflow-planner.md` and the adapt `SKILL.md` now state the exact-file rule + the split-an-over-ceiling-lane-into-sequenced-nodes guidance (no FILE_CEILING raise, no directory grants). **Part C deferred:** the mid-run mechanical-repair lane (auto-narrowing a directory grant without a consent halt) was designed-then-deferred — an adversarial pass found it opens cross-lane-clobber (SHARED_INFRA prefix coverage), sensitivity-bypass (plan-global `hasSecReviewer`), hash-laundering, symlink/`..`-traversal, and #377 cross-lane-absorption holes that the freeze-time wall avoids entirely; a follow-up issue carries the full adversarial guard catalog as its acceptance criteria. ×4 editions (plan-validator canonical/codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea hand-ported, all 1563 lines); walkthrough coverage (directory + variant + `..` refusals; no-false-refusal; freeze-only/no-brick; exact-barrier-preserved); four chains green.

- **adaptive/bundle: all-or-nothing closure is now script-owned with truthful receipts + an invariant (#369).** #328's headline contract — a bundle run ends at ONE finalization that closes EVERY issue — was prose-only: `sink-merge` closed only `args.issue` (the primary), `cmdFinalize` merely PROBED members, and the receipts were structured so a partial closure was indistinguishable from success (a member probed `open` landed in neither `closed_issues` nor `failed_issue_closures`, and `remote_issue_closed` read `skipped_offline` even when online with members still open). Now: (1) **script-owned member closure** — `sink-merge` takes `--issue-numbers` and closes EVERY member of the bundle on the success path (the finalize choreography passes the set, ×4 surfaces), recording each in exactly one bucket. (2) **Truthful receipts** — every member is bucketed `closed_issues` / `failed_issue_closures` / `open_issues` (never silently neither); the online token is `closed` (all) or the new `partial` (some) — never the offline-only `skipped_offline`; `cmdFinalize` and the watch-pr MERGED arm both populate the per-member fields. (3) **Invariant** — a new warn-first-but-VISIBLE `remote-members-closed` closure invariant flags any member left in `failed_issue_closures`/`open_issues` while online, so `closure_invariants.ok` becomes false on a partial close (a partial is never a clean success). Single-issue receipts are byte-unchanged (every new branch gates on `issue_numbers.length > 1`). Shipped across all four editions (canonical/codex byte-identical closure-contract + claim/sink-merge COMMON_SCRIPTS; gitlab/gitea hand-ported `forge.*` variants); four chains green.

- **router/bundle: the `issue-scout` auto-bundle entry is now reachable (#380).** The scout (shipped by #328 for AC#5/#6 auto-bundle mode) had never fired: `workflow-next` Step 0 unconditionally picked a single issue and never pointed at the scout, the trigger phrasing ("work broadly on a project") was a strict subset of the real intent, the router's "dispatch-free" rule contradicted the scout dispatch, and the env-wiring from the scout's output was undocumented. Per owner-confirmed intent (2026-06-11): when the user does **not** name a specific issue, the router now branches in Step 0 and dispatches the read-only `issue-scout` to pick the next working set, adopting its recommendation — `KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUES` for a high-confidence same-scope bundle **when the resolved path is adaptive**, otherwise `KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUE` from the scout's `primary_issue` (single-issue / medium-low confidence / non-adaptive). The adaptive switch / path intent is resolved **before** the bundle decision so the lane can't recommend a bundle the startup script must refuse (`target_set_not_adaptive`). The scout is explicitly carved out of the "do not invoke phase agents" / "dispatch-free" rules as a pre-claim, read-only survey that claims and writes nothing (#44: agent selects, scripts validate). An explicitly named issue bypasses the scout — single-target behavior byte-unchanged. Prose-only across all four `workflow-next` editions (canonical + gitlab/gitea commands + codex SKILL); the canonical validator pins the branch marker + env-wiring contract so the entry can't go unreachable again. Four chains green.

- **forge/attestation: `--attest-planner-spawn` ported to the gitlab/gitea claim ports (#347).** The #280 planner-attestation back-fill existed only in the canonical claim — the forge sink-merge consumer (#300) shipped while its producer was canonical-only, leaving forge planner attestation structurally dead on two editions. Flag parse + claimProject back-fill ported (the bundle path #370 was already present but dead without the flag parse); the flag token is pinned in all four contract validators (anti-drift) + behavioral tests in both forge walkthroughs. Four chains green.

- **adaptive-node: `close-and-open-next` refuses a no-op ledger splice (#348).** A close whose node-id matched no ledger row, or matched a row not `in_progress`, used to fall through and still append a compliance row — a false compliance row over an unclosed node. Now typed zero-mutation refusals: `close_node_not_in_ledger` (found:false) and `close_transition_disallowed` (row pending/n-a); `n/a` is dropped from `allowFrom` so a skipped node is never flipped to complete. RED→GREEN, mutation-proven tests. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **sink-merge: the FF retry loop now re-rebases on the origin-advance race + resolves the default branch (#350).** The FF-only merge loop RE-REBASES the feature branch onto the updated origin tip before retrying — the only race that makes an FF fail is origin/<defBranch> advancing after the initial rebase, and the prior loop retried the IDENTICAL ff-only merge without re-rebasing, so it could never succeed for its own target race (3 futile retries → exit 2, main root stranded on the feature branch). On final failure the main root is restored to the default branch. The hardcoded `'main'` literal is replaced throughout (rebase, FF pull/merge, push, reset, merge-base skip-check, workflow-only-branch guard) by claim.js's now-exported `defaultBranch()` (origin/HEAD, fallback main) — master-default repos no longer break sink-merge. Canonical walkthrough tests: non-main default merges offline; a mid-flight origin advance recovers via re-rebase. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **scripts: crash-safe atomic durable-state writes (#353, core).** Durable state was mutated with bare `fs.writeFileSync` everywhere except roadmap.js — a crash mid-write truncates the file: a torn workflow-plan.md → plan_hash mismatch → `--resume-check` bricks the run with no recovery; a torn workflow-state.md is silently skipped by readActiveFolders → the project goes invisible. `writeFileAtomicReplace` (tmp + fsync + atomic rename, no-op on unchanged content) added to `adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-anchor + COMMON_SCRIPT, so no new-file registration) and routed through the production durable-state writers: adaptive-node (plan/ledger), parallel-batch (active-batch.json + plan/ledger), claim (workflow-state.md). Grep-able invariant pinned in validate-workflow-contracts; test-claim-hardening covers atomic write + no-op-on-unchanged + no `.tmp` residue. The remainder (single fence-aware slicer, compliance-row dedup, repair-state/sink-pr routing) landed via #354. ×4 editions; four chains green.

- **lifecycle: fail-open hardening — gh timeout, safe branch args, truthful remote_claim, no-commit-on-staging-failure (#356).** `ghExec` (the one uncapped gh round-trip in the lifecycle) now caps every call at `KAOLA_GH_REMOTE_TIMEOUT_MS` (default 30s, ≤600s), mock path included; `removeBranch` refuses a leading-dash/NUL branch via `isSafeBranchArg` (a malformed/hostile state-file branch can no longer reach `git branch -D` as a flag); `postAdvisoryClaim` RETURNS a truthful posted|failed|skipped_offline status surfaced as `remote_claim` in the claim JSON (a claim that landed ZERO remote footprint is visible instead of silently defeating cross-machine claim detection); the cmdFinalize archive-commit's try/commit-as-catch is replaced by an explicit `git diff --cached --quiet` exit-code check so staging failures never cascade into committing whatever happened to be staged (×4 archive blocks). New `scripts/test-claim-hardening.js`. The watch-pr/sink bundle-receipt truthfulness folded into #369 (the issue's named coordination point). Four chains green.

### Removed

- **workflow: retired the advisor gates from every workflow stage + removed the phantom-advisor hook (#372).** The advisor was mandated as a gate at four legacy 6-phase seams (phase2 Step 2, phase3 Step 2 + architect-revision trigger, phase5 CRITICAL consult, finalize closure-decision); the adaptive path already runs advisor-free (planner authorship + post-dominance `code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer`/`adversarial-verifier` gates) and is the default route (ADR 0007). **Zero advisor-consult mandates remain** in any command/skill file across all four editions: phase2/phase3 fold the second opinion into the orchestrator's own judgment (phase3 keeps the architect-revision loop, now triggered by the orchestrator's gap judgment), phase5 keeps the delegated review-fix loop, and finalize routes closure-decision items directly to the USER. Removed the `## Advisor Findings`/`## Advisor Notes` template sections, all four compliance rows, the `advisor-gate` resume-route rows, the contractor-prompt advisor references, and the `workflow-next`/`workflow-init` consult mandates (the init edit landed byte-identically across the 6-file KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE group). **The `kaola-workflow:phantom-advisor` PostToolUse hook is removed end-to-end:** the 4 `.sh` copies deleted, the `PostToolUse` entry dropped from all 6 `hooks.json`, the `validate-script-sync` byte-group removed, install.sh drops it from the 3 `SUPPORT_HOOK_NAMES` blocks **and de-registers a stale managed entry from an existing `~/.claude/settings.json` on upgrade** (the merge loop only visited incoming events — fixed) + deletes the stale installed `.sh`; Codex `.codex/hooks.json` drops 4→3 lifecycle hooks (the existing mergeHooks R3 orphan-sweep de-registers the stale entry). **Validator sweep:** the five gate vocabularies (`Advisor Gate`, `advisor ideation/plan/critical gate`, `closure advisor gate`) are now `assertNotIncludes`-banned (concat-built) across all 4-5 validator forks so the mandates cannot silently return; the forge phantom-advisor hook-present asserts flip to hook-absent; the delegation-policy fixtures relabel off the retired token. **Resume compatibility:** `unresolvedCompliance` maps a legacy `advisor … gate` compliance row forward as satisfied (never pending-blocking) so an in-flight pre-#372 project resumes without bricking. Walkthrough: `testPhantomAdvisorHookGuard` replaced by a fresh-install 3-hook-shape assertion + a resume-compat regression test; the codex/forge mergeHooks orphan fixtures re-keyed off the retired id. Docs (README, api.md, CLAUDE.md) updated to the 3-hook list. ×4 editions; four chains green.

### Changed

- **adaptive: unified fence-aware markdown-section access + folded in the #353-rest writer routing (#354).** The six fence-blind `content.indexOf('\n## Node Ledger')` slicers in `adaptive-node.js` (`spliceLedgerNode`, `readLedgerStatuses`, the `runOrient` in_progress enumerator, `runWriteHalt`, `removeDurableConsentHalt`, and `spliceComplianceRow`), the duplicate compliance-row appender in `parallel-batch.js`, and the validator's `reconcileLedger` now all route through ONE pure, classifier-free fence-aware locator `locateSection(content, heading)` in `adaptive-schema.js` (the ×4 byte-identical anchor). The locator tracks code-fence depth in the heading-finder, so an UPSTREAM FENCED `## Node Ledger` decoy heading (e.g. an illustrative block in `## Nodes`) is skipped and a fenced `## ` line inside the section no longer ends it early — `readDurableConsentHalt` is hardened the same way. The two near-identical compliance-row appenders collapse onto one shared `spliceComplianceSection` (the `## Required Agent Compliance` header/separator/format string lives in exactly one place); the `## Nodes` row-walk parity between `validator.parseNodes` and `classifier.readPlanNodes` is pinned by a fixture parity test. **#353-rest folded in:** the three bare `fs.writeFileSync(stateFile, …)` durable-state writes in `repair-state.js` and the one in `sink-pr.js` (+ the gitlab `sink-mr` / gitea `sink-pr` forge ports) now route through `writeFileAtomicReplace` (tmp + fsync + rename), so a crash mid-write can never leave a torn `workflow-state.md` (which `readActiveFolders` silently skips). New RED→GREEN fence-decoy regression tests (`test-adaptive-node.js` T6/T6b, `test-parallel-batch.js` P1c); the no-bare-write invariant + the shared-slicer symbols are pinned in `validate-workflow-contracts.js`. Behavior-preserving on the non-decoy path (existing `spliceLedgerNode` T1–T5 + walkthrough decoy scenarios stay green). ×4 editions; four chains green.

### Removed

- **adaptive/batch: excised the unreachable write-role isolation machinery from `parallel-batch` (#364).** The write-role batch isolation path — per-member `git worktree add --detach` + seeded snapshots + per-member plan copies + `memberDirty` in-lane porcelain + `mergeRef` capture + `join`'s checkout-from-member-tree (`snapshotMember`/`anchorMergeRef`/the worktree io seams) — was unreachable by default (since #320 `open-batch` serial-degraded any write-role frontier) and non-functional if force-enabled (the harness cannot force a dispatched subagent's CWD, so members wrote to the parent worktree and `seal` then refused `empty_member` after the work was done — a documented isolation leak). `open-batch`/`top-up` now serial-degrade a write-role frontier **unconditionally** (the existing `{degraded:true, reason:'cwd_unenforceable'}` zero-mutation shape is preserved for prose compatibility); read-only fan-out batches are byte-behavior-unchanged and serial write behavior is byte-identical to today's degrade path. `KAOLA_BATCH_CWD_ENFORCED`/`resolveBatchCwdEnforced` are retired from `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (×4); the now-unreachable `joining` manifest state is removed (`opening → open → sealed → joined`); `runJoin` collapses to a manifest-only `sealed → joined` transition. Line delta: `kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` 1454 → 1177 (−277) per edition; `test-parallel-batch.js` 1893 → 1565 (dead E1/E2/E3 e2e-worktree, N6 seal-vacuity, N7 deletion-join, N8 rename-join, N9 malformed write-role evidence, resolver unit, and `cwdEnforced` control tests removed; E1 rewritten to assert the unconditional degrade). Plan-run worktree choreography prose trimmed on all four surfaces. Reintroduction condition recorded in `docs/decisions/0008-excise-write-role-batch-isolation.md` (a real harness cwd/lane-enforcement primitive — #376/#377). ×4 editions; four chains green.

## [5.13.0] — 2026-06-10

Conflict-free 7-lane batch (#344, #345, #346, #349, #361, #363, #370) implemented in parallel with #357 and merged together. Each lane was gated by all four edition chains (`claude`/`codex`/`gitlab`/`gitea`).

### Fixed

- **adaptive/plan-run: `$KAOLA_SCRIPTS` executor handle is now defined before first use (#344).** Every adaptive lifecycle call in the plan-run surface is `node "$KAOLA_SCRIPTS/…"` (14× per edition), but no edition ever defined `$KAOLA_SCRIPTS` — in a consumer plugin install (no local `scripts/` dir) every call depended on the LLM guessing the plugin-cache path. The command editions now define `KAOLA_SCRIPTS` once via the `kaola_script()` resolver (the #245 class) in the Adaptive Worktree block, and the Codex SKILL via the plugin-cache find-fallback. New `scripts/test-plan-run.js` asserts every edition defines the handle before first use and exercises the resolver fallback chain in a consumer-repo-shaped fixture; pinned in all four contract validators.
- **adaptive/finalize: four-gate merge barrier resolves the validator via `kaola_script` (#345).** The four-gate barrier (`--resume-check`/`--gate-verify`/`--barrier-check`/`--verdict-check`) used bare `node scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` paths in the three finalize command editions, so in a consumer repo the gates exited MODULE_NOT_FOUND — a false BLOCK at the only blocking pre-merge enforcement. Now resolved via the resolver (mirroring the codex SKILL), with the resolved invocation + four gate flags pinned (and the bare path banned) in the canonical/gitlab/gitea validators.
- **adaptive/reopen: `reopen-node` purges stale gate verdict evidence (#349).** reopen-node reset a gate to pending and removed its baseline but left its `.cache/<gate-id>.md` verdict evidence, so a later close-without-fresh-dispatch could pass Finalization’s blocking `--verdict-check` on a STALE `verdict: pass` and ship repaired code unreviewed. It now deletes each reset gate’s evidence file (and a fanout adversarial-verifier’s per-instance siblings), surfaced as `evidenceRemoved`; the reopened node’s own evidence is left for re-record. ×4 editions; RED→GREEN unit tests.
- **sink: run all preconditions before the destructive worktree removal (#346).** `sink-merge` called `removeWorktree --force` in Step 0 BEFORE the preconditions, so a sink about to refuse first destroyed the worktree — taking any uncommitted work with it. The preconditions (clean tree, no-live-folder [now branch-scoped], upstream, non-workflow) now all run first, plus a new `assertWorktreeClean` data-loss guard that refuses with zero mutation when the linked worktree is dirty; only then is the worktree removed. ×4 editions; forge tests assert a refused sink leaves the worktree + its uncommitted change intact.
- **adaptive/bundle: `claimBundle` provisioning parity with `claimProject` (#370).** Bundle runs were forced into a degraded in-place posture (worktree hard-suppressed on a false rationale; no dirty-gate; the recorded branch never created so `cmdRelease`’s restore was a no-op and `base_branch` was never written; `--attest-planner-spawn` ignored), and `--target-issues` silently coerced/dropped malformed tokens (`parseInt("4x")`→4). claimBundle now provisions the worktree (run_posture derives), gates a dirty tree, creates the in-place branch + records `base_branch`, honors the attest back-fill, and rolls all of it back atomically; malformed tokens are refused with typed `target_set_invalid_token` (echoing the offender) and the bundle cap is clamped to a hard ceiling. Single-issue claim byte-unchanged. ×4 editions.
- **install: harden install.sh rendering + fail-closed forge verification (#363).** The agent-model manifest is now JSON-encoded via node (a quote/backslash in a model value no longer corrupts `~/.claude/agents/.kaola-agent-models.json`); the hooks.json rewrite is a node transform that FAILS LOUDLY (replacing the python3 heredoc whose errors were swallowed and whose `sed` fallback mangled JSON on `|`/`&` paths); `render_command_file` errors instead of silently emptying a placeholder in a non-`model=` context; and the support-script/hook copy + verify loops fail CLOSED for ALL forges (a missing allowlisted source aborts the install — previously silently skipped for gitlab/gitea, which then verified green: the 5.4.0 incident class). `test-install-model-rendering.js` now runs `--forge=gitlab`/`gitea` end-to-end, asserts a planted typo fails the install, and that a hostile model value round-trips through valid manifest JSON.
- **harness/contractor: execute the bash-in-markdown guards + fix the Step-8a rename parsing (#361).** ~44 fenced bash blocks per edition were executed by no test (only substring-pinned) — the class that shipped #294 and #344/#345. New `scripts/test-bash-block-guards.js` extracts a named fenced block and RUNS it in a prepared fixture: the contractor Step-8a artifact mirror against a real renamed-file + linked-worktree (RED→GREEN), the Finalization four-gate resolver portion, and a cross-edition static guard over the forge finalize blocks. `agents/contractor.md` Step-8a `f="${line:3}"` left the literal `old -> new` string for a rename entry (so `cp` silently skipped the renamed artifact); it now mirrors the new path and strips git path quoting.


## [5.12.0] — 2026-06-10

### Added

- **tests: walkthrough dev-loop — scenario registry, `--only`/`--list`, fail-closed mock, timeouts, isolation (#357).** The canonical walkthrough's hard-coded 213-call `main()` becomes an ordered scenario registry: `--list` prints every scenario name (ordering-coupled head scenarios marked `[shared-tmp group]`, always run as one unit), `--only <name|prefix>` reproduces a single failure in seconds (the full-run sentinel prints only on full runs; a bogus token exits 1 naming it). Hardening: `ghMockEnv` now THROWS on a missing shim file (a typo can no longer fall through to the developer's real `gh`); `runNode` children get a 120s default timeout, a scrubbed inherited-`KAOLA_*` env, and `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null` + `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1`; `main()`'s catch prints `err.stdout`/`err.stderr` tails; a new `testHarnessSelfCheck` (6 assertions) locks the harness behaviors. The four gitlab/gitea edition runner-of-runners print a delimited `CHILD FAILURE` block (byte-identical `tail30` + catch ×4) with the failing child's last-30-line stdout/stderr instead of a bare "Command failed". Registry execution order byte-verified against HEAD; adversarially verified (5 claims, NOT-REFUTED); all four chains green.
- **adaptive: same-scope multi-issue bundle lane (#328).** An additive lane that finishes a coherent set of same-scope issues in one adaptive run, end to end: `issue-set selection → all-or-nothing multi-claim → one worktree/branch → one frozen workflow-plan.md → one plan-run → one finalization closing N issues`. **Single-issue behavior is unchanged** (`--target-issue N` / `KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUE`; AC#1) — bundles are a strictly parallel path. **Claim/startup** (`kaola-workflow-claim.js`, root↔codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea forge ports): a new `--target-issues A,B,C` / `KAOLA_TARGET_ISSUES` startup path with an all-or-nothing `claimBundle` (validate every member open+unclaimed+green/yellow; sorted+deduped `bundle_id` → one `bundle-42-47-53` project + `workflow/bundle-42-47-53` branch; per-member advisory label/comment; reverse-order rollback on partial failure), a `KAOLA_BUNDLE_MAX_ISSUES` cap (default 4), a `target_ambiguity` refusal when both single- and multi-target are set, and the bundle typed-refusal vocabulary (`target_set_empty`/`_too_large`/`_not_adaptive`/`_contains_closed_issue`/`_contains_claimed_issue`/`_conflicts_active_work`/`_has_external_dependency`/`_label_rollback_failed`). **State** (`active-folders.js`, `classifier.js` byte pairs + forge ports): additive `issue_numbers`/`bundle_id`/`closure_policy` parsing (single-issue projects keep `issue_number` alone), and active-folder overlap blocking so a live bundle blocks a duplicate claim of any member (AC#4). **Finalization** (`claim.js`, `roadmap.js`): all-or-nothing closure — close every issue in `issue_numbers`, remove every `.roadmap/issue-N.md`, regenerate `ROADMAP.md` once, archive one bundle folder, and extend the closure receipt (`closed_issues`/`failed_issue_closures`/`roadmap_sources_removed`), warning-first on a single remote-close failure; `cmdRelease`/`cmdWatchPr` clear advisory labels per member (AC#11/12/13). **New read-only `issue-scout` role** (`agents/issue-scout.md` + 3 plugin `.toml`; registered in `install.sh`, `resolve-agent-model.js` ×4, validator `CANONICAL_ROLES` ×4, agent-count 13→14 in the gitlab/gitea contract validators + forge test scripts, and `validate-vendored-agents.js` localAgents): in auto-bundle mode it surveys the backlog and recommends ONE same-scope bundle; the main orchestrator states the set before claim and falls back to single-issue when confidence is low — scripts validate exact targets but never select or substitute (the #44 agent-owns-reasoning rule; AC#5/6). **Resume** (`adaptive-node.js` byte pair + forge ports): `orient` surfaces `bundleId`/`issueNumbers`/`closurePolicy`/`primaryIssue`. **Routing** (commands + skills, all 4 editions): explicit-bundle + auto-bundle entries with the `--target-issue`-unchanged compatibility rule. Docs (`README.md`, `docs/api.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/workflow-state-contract.md`, `docs/conventions.md`) and regression coverage (`test-bundle-state.js`, `test-bundle-claim.js`, `test-bundle-finalize.js`, plus six E2E bundle scenarios in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`); all four edition chains (`claude`/`codex`/`gitlab`/`gitea`) green. Shipped via the adaptive path (dogfooded), with three mid-run plan-repairs (validate-vendored-agents agent-listing, forge `issue-scout.toml` forge-neutrality, forge claim.js finalization-parity).
- **tests: `npm run test:parallel` — four edition chains as parallel jobs, ending #307 `&&`-masking (#358).** A new local-only runner (`scripts/test-parallel.js`, with an embedded `--self-test` mode) spawns the four `test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains concurrently (`spawn` shell:false, `Promise.allSettled` — never short-circuits), buffers output per chain, prints a per-chain PASS/FAIL summary plus each failing chain's last-50-line tail, and exits non-zero iff any chain failed — wall-clock ≈ the slowest single chain, and a red later chain can no longer hide behind a green earlier one. The runner sets `TEST_PARALLEL=1` in every child env; a new `probeTimeoutEnv()` helper (byte-verbatim across the claude walkthrough and the gitlab/gitea test drivers) widens the 13 load-sensitive closure-audit hang-probe margins 300ms→2000ms under that env (default unchanged; outcome assertions unchanged; the 6 parse-behavior literal sites untouched), flake-hardening the known `testClosureAuditExecuteLabelRemovalTimeoutBreaks` CPU-contention race. `package.json` gains exactly one `test:parallel` entry; `npm test` and the four chain entries are byte-unchanged. `docs/conventions.md` now documents `test:parallel` as the recommended way to record the #307 four-chain requirement. All four edition chains green.
- **adaptive: `reopen-node` repairs an upstream-complete node mid-gate (#343).** A post-dominating gate that is still `in_progress` (it just emitted a blocking finding owned by the target node) now folds back to `pending` inside the `reopen-node` transaction instead of being left as a second `in_progress` row with a deleted live baseline — so a mid-gate finding no longer forces advancing the DAG to allDone on a known-broken tree before the repair. A new fail-closed typed refusal `would_orphan_in_progress` fires (pure no-op, before any unlink/write) when any `in_progress` row is not a post-dominating gate of the target; gate `taskTransitions` are now emitted only for rows actually flipped (additive `gatesFolded` field). plan-run docs (all four editions) document the supported mid-gate sequence and the per-node-informational vs finalize-blocking `--verdict-check` distinction. Across all four editions (claude↔codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea forge ports), with anti-drift validator pins.
- **codex: schema-valid + stale-proof agent-profile install (#332).** `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (now a byte-identical ×3 sync group) becomes a validate→copy(temp+rename)→prune→manifest→post-verify pipeline: it refuses to install a schema-invalid source profile, prunes retired Kaola-managed files (manifest-listed or known-retired like the #249 `docs-lookup.toml`) on every reinstall while preserving unknown user TOMLs, writes a `.kaola-managed-profiles.json` ownership manifest (schema_version + per-file sha256), post-verifies, and prints a `status: ok` sentinel. The Codex preflight (`kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js` ×4) becomes a schema-valid gate (not existence-only) with new statuses (`profiles_malformed`/`profiles_stale`/`managed_block_stale`/`profile_schema_version_unsupported` exit 6), an `extra_unmanaged` report field, and a read-only `--doctor`/`--home` mode reporting user/project/plugin-cache freshness. `uninstall.sh` gains manifest-driven managed-profile + managed-`[agents.*]`-block cleanup. Contract validators now assert source-profile schema (codex), edition byte-parity of all 14 role profiles + `config/agents.toml` against the codex tree (gitlab/gitea — each guard mutation-verified to bite), and the README role-catalog (codex). The fix also reconciles the pre-existing #272 `workflow-planner.toml` cross-tree drift (gitlab/gitea converged to the codex wording so all 14 TOMLs are byte-identical ×3) and registers the previously-unregistered `issue-scout` role in all three `config/agents.toml`. RED→GREEN: every guard shown to fire against the pre-fix tree before going green.
- **adaptive/finalize: `finalize --attest-contractor-spawn` (#338).** The contractor's self-attest back-fill (mirror of `--attest-planner-spawn`) records its otherwise-unloggable spawn so the closure receipt reads `finalize_contractor_attested: attested` even on hookless/worktree runs; warn-first, never blocks, and never passed on an inline main-session finalize.
- **adaptive/planner: freeze-time cross-edition write-set completeness checks (#340, refs #328/#306/#309).** The adaptive plan validator now refuses, at freeze, the two #328 plan-repair mechanisms the #306 symbol-grep cannot catch: an `agent-registration gap` (an agent-set delta must carry its full 22-path exact-match registration surface — sibling edition profiles, the three `config/agents.toml` codex-dispatch templates, `validate-vendored-agents.js`, `install.sh`/`uninstall.sh` `REQUIRED_AGENTS`, `resolve-agent-model.js`×4, plan-validator `CANONICAL_ROLES`×4, forge contract-validator + test-script counts), and a `forge-port ordering gap` (a gitlab/gitea edition-named port must be a transitive descendant of every node editing its root source, so the mirror's full-accumulated-root-diff canonical spec exists first). Anchor-gated to the Kaola-Workflow repo, additions-only, never on `--resume-check`. Adds `workflow-planner` profile + plan-run dispatch guidance for both rules and derived (enumeration-free) config↔dir and install↔uninstall parity guards in all four contract validators.
- **adaptive/finalize: keep-open partial-close sink lane (#336).** A run can now finalize as complete while keeping its GitHub/GitLab/Gitea issue **open** as a residual vehicle, with no manual FF-push/worktree/branch cleanup. Set `issue_action: comment_keep_open` in the `## Sink` block (or pass `finalize --keep-open`/`--keep-issue-open` / `sink-merge --keep-issue-open`) to preserve the per-issue `.roadmap/issue-N.md` source (`ROADMAP.md` still lists `#N`), post a keep-open comment instead of closing the issue, and still release the claim and tear down the worktree/branch. The closure receipt records `remote_issue_closed: kept_open` + `roadmap_source_removed: kept`, the new `keep-open-roadmap-preserved` invariant enforces retention (inverting the close-mode `roadmap-source-absent`/`roadmap-mirror-clean` checks), and `closure-audit` no longer reaps the preserved source. Keep-open is **merge-sink-only**: PR/MR sinks — including the exit-3 merge-impossible auto-pivot whose `Closes #N` body would auto-close the issue — are refused with a typed BLOCKED at three layers (prose pre-case, in-arm exit-3, and a script-level guard in `sink-pr`/`sink-mr` before the offline early-return). Extends #333's terminal-state stamping (`last_result: closed_keep_open`). The load-bearing must-not-close behavior is proven by an **online-mock** sink-merge test (`KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=0` + gh/glab/tea shim marker asserts `issue comment`, never `issue close`); all four editions.
- **forge: forge-neutrality guard for agent-profile authoring (#341).** The gitlab/gitea contract validators now run the forbidden-token scan (`assertNoForbidden` over every plugin command/skill/hook/agent/config file) **before** any file-count assertion, so a forge-CLI leak (`gh`/`glab`, a forge brand, a forge request noun) can no longer be masked by a transiently-stale count — the latent #328 defect where a `gh` leak in `issue-scout.toml` stayed invisible until an unrelated count bump. Both edition validators gain a standalone, count-independent `--forbidden-only <file>…` mode (exit 0 + sentinel clean; exit 1 naming the forbidden regex on a leak; exit 2 fail-closed on misuse) so a forge-touching adaptive node can verify just its changed files. Forge-neutral + byte-identical agent-profile authoring is now stated in the planner profile, all three `workflow-planner.toml` mirrors, the four plan-run executor docs, and `docs/conventions.md`/`docs/api.md`, contract-pinned red-first in all four edition validators.
- **adaptive: first-class non-delegable acceptance gates (`main-session-gate`, #334).** A new built-in plan role (no agent profile, never dispatched as a subagent — the main session itself performs the check, e.g. a GPU/visual confirmation or explicit human sign-off, and records `verdict: pass|fail` evidence). It is read-only, shape `sequence` only (never a fan-out member, loop, or select arm), excluded from parallel batches, and carries a freeze-time post-dominance gate (**G3**) plus a runtime `--gate-verify` check with no legal `n/a` skip — so a numerical-green implementation path can never reach the `finalize` sink without crossing the manual decision (the #210 visual-closure gap). `--verdict-check` requires its passing verdict; a plan-repair to implementation re-triggers the gate; pending visual gates now surface consistently at the compact/resume seam across all four editions. Modeled on the `finalize` sink token, so no vendored-agent registration surface applies. RED→GREEN coverage incl. the impl-complete-but-gate-incomplete finalize-blocked regression fixture; all four editions (byte-identical adaptive-schema anchor + plan-validator/adaptive-node/parallel-batch byte pairs + gitlab/gitea ports).
- **forge: bundle-lane behavioral coverage in the edition walkthroughs (#342, #328 CR2).** The gitlab/gitea walkthroughs (`simulate-gitlab/gitea-workflow-walkthrough.js`) each gain six E2E bundle scenarios — claim-creates-one-folder, refusal-leaves-no-folder (all-or-nothing rollback), duplicate-issue overlap blocking, orient surfaces bundle identity, finalize roadmap cleanup (plural `.roadmap/issue-N.md` removal + one-folder archive + receipt fields), and a single-issue regression — driving the **real** edition CLIs (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-claim.js`/`-adaptive-node.js`) via `KAOLA_GLAB_MOCK_SCRIPT`/`KAOLA_TEA_MOCK_SCRIPT` mocks. Closes the #328 CR2 false-confidence gap where green forge chains certified only structure (contract/byte/forbidden checks) and were blind to a HIGH AC-breaking forge defect; the finalize-roadmap-cleanup scenario is mutation-proven to red against the orphaned-roadmap defect class. Forge-neutral (no `gh`/`glab` literals tripping #341). Adds the edition-behavioral-coverage convention to `docs/conventions.md`. Test+docs only — no production-behavior change.

### Fixed

- **hooks: pre-commit guard now catches `git -C` / flag-bearing commit forms (#351).** The multi-project staging guard gated on a `*"git commit"*` substring, so the workflow's own contractor finalize commit (`git -C "$WT" commit …`) and `git -c k=v commit` variants bypassed it entirely. The hook now tokenizes the invoked command (global flags `-C <path>` / `-c <kv>` / `--long[=val]` recognized before the `commit` subcommand, command passed via env export — no interpolation/injection), inspects the `-C` target repo's staging, and preserves the plain/compound fast path and fail-open posture. Byte-identical ×4 editions; new `testHookGitDashCCommitGuard` walkthrough scenario; all four chains green. Known narrow residual (reviewer R2, documented): a `-C` path containing a space mis-tokenizes and fails open.
- **init: hardcoded maintainer machine path removed from `workflow-init` (×3 editions) (#352).** `commands/workflow-init.md` (claude + gitlab/gitea forks) shipped the maintainer's absolute path `/Volumes/.../andrej-karpathy-skills/...` as the preferred Karpathy-skills source. The relative sibling discovery (`../andrej-karpathy-skills/...`) is now the first preference, with a `KARPATHY_SKILLS_PATH` env-var / ask-the-user fallback — no machine-specific absolute path ships in any edition. The rewritten block is byte-identical across the three command editions; the `kaola-workflow-init` SKILL partners and the byte-locked KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE region are untouched. All four edition chains green.
- **codex: agent role profiles now declare a top-level `name` (#332)** — codex ≥0.138 silently ignored every Kaola agent role profile because none of the 42 source TOMLs defined the now-required `name` key; added `name = "<role>"` to all of them, and a reinstall now repairs stale state instead of perpetuating it. Corrects the README Codex role catalog (now validator-pinned to `config/agents.toml`) and the `install.sh`→`kaola-workflow-init` reference at the hooks line. See the Added entry for the durable installer/preflight/uninstall pipeline this ships alongside.
- **Closure `roadmap-mirror-clean` no longer false-positives on issue cross-references (#339)** — `checkClosureInvariants` now row-anchors the mirror check (multiline `^| #N |` on the generated table's issue column) instead of bare `#N` substring matching, so a legitimate cross-reference to a closed issue inside ANOTHER issue's row (e.g. `place_inside (#562 opacity)` in a next_step cell) no longer flips `closure_invariants.ok:false` after a clean closure, while an actual active `| #N | …` row still violates; all four editions (root/codex byte pair + gitlab/gitea ports), covering `cmdFinalize`, `sink-merge`, and `watch-pr`/`watch-mr` cleanups; regression fixtures (cross-ref pass + active-row fail) in `test-bundle-finalize.js` Test (5) and both forge `test-*-workflow-scripts.js`.
- **adaptive/planner: freeze-time decision-record id preflight — frozen plans can no longer carry stale decision-record numbering (#337).** A consumer-project adaptive run (vRPCADCore #210) froze a plan prescribing decision record `D-210-01` even though a prior partial-close cycle had already shipped that id; nothing mechanical would have caught it. `kaola-workflow-adaptive-handoff.js` (all four editions: claude↔codex byte pair + gitlab/gitea forge ports) gains **step 1.5** — a read-only preflight between the validator's in-grammar verdict and `--freeze`: an **unfrozen** plan hardcoding a `D-<issue>-NN` token (write-set paths or `## Plan Notes` prose) that the target repo already records (word-bounded match in `docs/**/*.md` filename/content or `CHANGELOG.md`) refuses with typed `decision_id_conflict:` errors (naming the hit paths and the three remediations) plus an additive machine-readable `conflicts` field, BEFORE the first mutation — feeding the existing bounded planner repair loop. Deliberate escapes keep it freeze-time-once and fail-open: already-frozen plans skip the check, `D-<issue>-NN (existing)` annotates a deliberate reference to a shipped record, the `D-<issue>-NEXT` placeholder never matches (the doc-updater node resolves it at execution time), and pure-core callers without the new `findDecisionIdHits` seam keep exact prior behavior. The workflow-planner profile (`agents/workflow-planner.md` + the three `.toml` mirrors) gains the authoring rule: read the target repo's existing records and continue the series with the **next free** number on follow-up / partial-close cycles. RED→GREEN coverage: unit T9a–T9f in `test-adaptive-handoff.js` and the real-subprocess walkthrough fixture `testAdaptiveHandoffDecisionIdConflict`; `docs/api.md` documents the check.
- **adaptive: archived `workflow-state.md` is now terminal on every lane that archives (#333).** A completed keep-open run could archive a project folder whose state still said `status: active`, `next_command: /kaola-workflow-plan-run …`, and `Pending Gates: workflow-plan` — a durable traceability gap where an audit/resume could trust the wrong file. `cmdFinalize`/`watch-pr`/`watch-mr` (all four editions) now stamp a terminal state before archival via a pure `stampTerminalState` helper: `next_command`/`next_skill` neutralized to `none (archived)`, the Planning Evidence `plan_hash` refreshed from the final frozen plan, `## Last Updated` refreshed, and an idempotent `## Closure` receipt block appended (`issue_disposition`, claim/worktree disposition, invariants). A new `finalize --keep-open` flag records a kept-open partial-close honestly (`last_result: closed_keep_open`, `issue_disposition: kept-open`, remote-close probe skipped), and a manual-archive backstop heals a folder archived by a stray `mv`/`git mv` (`archive_state_stamped: repaired`). watch-pr/watch-mr disposition is probe-derived — a merged PR whose issue is still open is recorded `kept-open`, never an unconditional `closed`. Regression fixtures (`testKeepOpenArchiveStamp`, `testManualArchiveBackstop` + forge twins) and the `#217`/`#296 B1` no-new-commit guards stay green via a commit-last reorder. (The full keep-open *sink* lane — roadmap-source retention, guaranteed no-close, FF-push, cleanup — is #336, which builds on this same `--keep-open` flag.)
- **adaptive/worktree: fresh worktree runs no longer require a manual project-folder copy before `orient` (#335).** The handoff now mechanically mirrors the frozen `kaola-workflow/<project>/` into the provisioned worktree (atomic copy → `plan_hash` re-verification → rename-promote, surfaced as `worktree_mirror` in the ready packet); `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` re-runs the idempotent `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js mirror-project` at entry; and `orient` fails closed with a typed `plan_not_mirrored` refusal (naming the exact mirror command) on an unmirrored worktree. Replaces the brittle prose `cp -R` seam from the #210 run. All four editions (claude↔codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea forge ports); the real-subprocess walkthrough `testAdaptiveWorktreeMirrorNoManualCopy` asserts main↔worktree `plan_hash` byte-equality and a manual-copy-free `orient`.
- **adaptive/finalize: inline finalize no longer falsely certifies `subagent-invoked` (#338).** A plan-run sink that performs its finalization bookkeeping in the main session used to archive a `workflow-plan.md` whose Required Agent Compliance row read `finalize (n5) | subagent-invoked` even though no contractor was dispatched — the durable ledger certified a delegation that never happened. The `finalize` (DAG-sink) role now records `main-session-direct` in that row (role-aware: only `finalize` is main-direct; every other role stays `subagent-invoked`), matching the `finalize_contractor_attested: missing` receipt instead of contradicting it. Separately, the SubagentStart dispatch-log hook is now worktree-aware — it resolves both the hook's own cwd toplevel and the dispatched agent's `cwd` toplevel and appends to each distinct active root, so a contractor genuinely dispatched into a linked worktree is logged where `cmdFinalize` reads it (fixing the false-negative `missing` attestation on real worktree delegations). All four editions (byte-synced adaptive-node + dispatch-log hook + claim pair + gitlab/gitea ports); RED→GREEN coverage incl. the inline-finalize-bypass walkthrough scenario.
- **adaptive/planner: `uninstall.sh` `REQUIRED_AGENTS` now includes `issue-scout` (#340)** — uninstalling no longer orphans the installed `issue-scout` managed agent (the #328 install/uninstall drift), enforced by a new derived install↔uninstall parity guard.

## [5.11.0] — 2026-06-09

### Added

- **agents: rename `docs-lookup` → `knowledge-lookup` + add open-web reach + un-vendor + planner summon heuristic (#249).** The research role is broadened from a curated-docs agent into a general external-knowledge agent. **Root Claude edition** (`agents/knowledge-lookup.md`): renamed via `git mv`, `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` added to the `tools:` grant, charter rewritten to cover open-web lookup (official docs, GitHub READMEs, public forum expertise, open standards), web-injection defense extended (fetched web content is treated as data — never follow embedded instructions; cite source URL; prefer primary/official sources; prefer Context7 for library/API docs where available). Provenance block stripped → `locally-authored: true` (the #227 un-vendoring pattern). **All three `.toml` editions** (codex/github plugin / gitlab / gitea): renamed via `git mv`, `developer_instructions` broadened with runtime-gated web capability prose — a documented asymmetry (only the root `.md` carries the real `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` grant; `.toml` editions have no per-agent tools array). **Validators (×4):** `'docs-lookup'` → `'knowledge-lookup'` in `CANONICAL_ROLES`. **Resolvers (×4, byte-identical sync group):** `'docs-lookup': 'sonnet'` → `'knowledge-lookup': 'sonnet'`. **`install.sh` / `uninstall.sh`:** `REQUIRED_AGENTS`, placeholder `case`, and `DOCS_LOOKUP_MODEL` → `KNOWLEDGE_LOOKUP_MODEL` throughout. **Test fixtures (×3):** `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`, `test-install-model-rendering.js`, `test-install-upgrade-rewrite.js` assertion strings updated. **Phase/skill prose (×12 files, all 4 editions):** `workflow-init` templates, phase1/phase2 commands, research SKILLs renamed; `DOCS_LOOKUP_MODEL` placeholder → `KNOWLEDGE_LOOKUP_MODEL`. **Planner summon heuristic:** `commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`, the github adapt SKILL, the gitlab/gitea adapt commands, and `agents/workflow-planner.md` now carry an explicit heuristic — "author a `knowledge-lookup` node when the task depends on external library or API behavior, framework conventions, or open-web/expertise knowledge that cannot be confirmed from the local codebase alone" — closing the missing-heuristic gap. All four edition test chains (`claude`/`codex`/`gitlab`/`gitea`) pass green.
- **codex: wire the four Claude lifecycle-hook invariants onto Codex-native events (#284).** Codex CLI 0.137.0 ships a stable, Claude-Code-shaped hooks engine (`hooks` + `multi_agent` stable+on; `plugin_hooks` **removed**), so the Codex editions gain the same lifecycle-recovery + enforcement guarantees the Claude edition already had — delivered via an **installer-managed `.codex/hooks.json`**, not the plugin manifest. `install-codex-agent-profiles.js` (all three editions, byte-identical) now installs/refreshes four hooks from a new per-edition `config/hooks.json` template, **idempotently merged by the managed-entry `id` prefix `kaola-workflow:`** (drop-then-re-add; user entries and unrelated events preserved; malformed pre-existing file treated as empty, WARN-first never throws), with the `__KW_PLUGIN_ROOT__` template token resolved to the absolute plugin root at install time and a one-time `/hooks` trust-step line printed. The four hooks: **SessionStart**(`compact`) → the edition compact-resume script (re-inject resume state after compaction; plain stdout, the documented SessionStart injection mechanism); **PreToolUse**(`Bash`) → the pre-commit project-mixing guard; **PostToolUse**(`Write|Edit`) → the phantom-advisor guard; **SubagentStart**(`*`) → the dispatch-log producer that appends each spawn to `.cache/dispatch-log.jsonl`, **making the byte-identical `checkDispatchAttestations` (claim.js / closure-contract.js) LIVE on Codex** — the closure receipt reads `claim_planner_attested: attested` / `finalize_contractor_attested: attested` once the producer populates the log (it was dead-code reading `missing`/`failed` before). The github-Codex tree, which previously shipped only `kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh`, gains byte-identical `kaola-workflow-phantom-advisor.sh` + `kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh` copies registered in `validate-script-sync.js`, and `uninstall.sh` gains symmetric project-local `.codex/hooks.json` managed-entry cleanup. **WARN-first posture preserved end to end** (a missing producer never hard-blocks; with `multi_agent` off the `SubagentStart` hook simply never fires and attestation reads `missing` — non-fatal).
