RED→GREEN coverage across the github-codex walkthrough (AC1 install registration + idempotency, AC2 plain-stdout compact injection, AC3 seeded-log attestation go-live, AC4 dispatch-log producer) and the gitlab/gitea install + codex walkthroughs (the stale "Codex has no dispatch-log hook — M1 deferred to #266" assertion is corrected); all four edition chains green. `README.md` + `docs/architecture.md` + `docs/api.md` document the hooks, the `.codex/hooks.json` managed-entry contract, the `/hooks` trust step, and the `multi_agent`-off precondition. Shipped via the adaptive path (dogfooded): `design → [installer ∥ hookports] → compact → tests → review (G1) → docs → finalize`.

### Fixed

Nine adaptive-runtime / finalize / installer gaps surfaced by the live #284 and #249 adaptive runs (PRs #326, #327, #329, #330, #331). Each fix lives in a testable `.js` seam (no prose-only fixes) with a load-bearing RED→GREEN regression test, applied across all four editions (claude↔codex byte-identical, gitlab/gitea forge ports); all four chains green for each.

- **adaptive: mutation-time task-mirror sync + machine-readable `taskTransitions` (#317).** Ledger-mutating commands (`open-next`, `close-and-open-next`, `write-halt`, `reopen-node`, and the batch `open-batch`/`top-up`/`seal-member`/`seal`/`reconcile`) now regenerate the durable `workflow-tasks.json` mirror and return an explicit `taskTransitions` array (`{id, status, ledger_status, reason, note?}`) the orchestrator applies verbatim — closing the mid-run window where the durable mirror and the session task UI showed nodes `pending` while their subagents were running (the #284 incident). The mirror refresh is **fail-OPEN** (a refresh failure is recorded in `taskMirror` and never rolls back a correct ledger transition); the fused `close-and-open-next` emits both the closed node → `completed` and the opened node → `in_progress`, and `enterBatch` emits only the closed-node transition (open-batch owns the member flips). plan-run docs (all editions) instruct marking every open-batch member `in_progress` *before* dispatch.
- **adaptive: batch evidence can no longer drift into a nested `kaola-workflow/kaola-workflow` cache (#318).** A new `validateProjectName()` guard rejects a reserved/illegal project segment (the literal `kaola-workflow`, plus empty/`.`/`..`/separator) in `record-evidence` and at the CLI entry of every `adaptive-node` subcommand, before any write — so a member's evidence can never create a nested cache path. The guard is segment-based, not a path substring (the repo's own toplevel is `kaola-workflow`), and fails closed (zero mutation on refusal).
- **adaptive: distinguish absent from malformed implementer evidence (#319).** `checkEvidenceShape` gains a `kind` (`absent`|`shape`) discriminator and a `missingTokenClass`; `sealOne`/`runSealMember`/`runSeal`/`close-and-open-next` now return `evidence_absent` vs `evidence_shape_failed` (with the missing token class, e.g. `change-type`) instead of the catch-all `evidence_missing` that conflated the two and forced manual `build-green` patching.
- **adaptive: write-role batch members no longer leak edits to the parent worktree (#320).** Because the harness cannot force a dispatched subagent's CWD, write-role batches now **serial-degrade before dispatch** (`reason:'cwd_unenforceable'`, zero mutation — no member worktree is ever created to be left empty/leaked) instead of relying on the post-hoc `empty_member` guard and manual redistribution. A new fail-closed `KAOLA_BATCH_CWD_ENFORCED` env (default FALSE) gates the dormant worktree-isolation path for a future cwd-forcing harness; read-only batches are unaffected.
- **adaptive: freeze-time and runtime agree on coarse-area disjointness (#321).** A frozen coarse-area-overlapping write antichain (in-grammar/`ask` at freeze) now runs **serially** at runtime instead of hitting a `not_disjoint` refusal — the #320 serial degrade fires before the disjointness re-check, so freeze and runtime both treat the antichain as runnable. The validator is unchanged (same root cause as #320 → same mechanism); the disjointness gate still bites only in the opt-in cwd-enforced mode.
- **adaptive: plan-run never calls `top-up` without an active manifest (#322).** `parallel-batch status --json` now exposes a pure `recommendBatchRoute()` `nextRoute`; the executor branches on it (`top-up` only for an `open`+valid batch; a joined-and-cleared manifest routes to `orient`), eliminating the spurious `no_active_batch` refusal the #249 run hit after a batch joined.
- **adaptive/finalize: sink-merge self-heals a local-only workflow branch (#323).** `assertBranchPushedToUpstream` now auto-runs `git push -u origin <branch>` when the branch has no upstream, then continues — so a worktree-native run reaches the sink without a manual `git push -u` recovery. Fail-CLOSED: a failed push re-throws the original guidance (an un-backed-up branch is never silently merged); the distinct ahead-of-upstream guard is untouched.
- **adaptive/finalize: archived closure artifacts no longer read as pre-sink/stale (#324).** At `status:closed` archive, `archiveProjectDir` normalizes the pre-run blocks `writeState` seeded at claim time (`## Pending Gates` → `- none`; `last_command`/`last_result` → `finalize`/`closed`), neutralizes the pre-sink `finalization-summary.md` sentinels (`READY FOR FINAL GIT GATE`, `Pending final git gate. …`), and rewrites the known false-absolute (`No files changed after those runs`) in `.cache/final-validation.md` to a reuse-boundary caveat — all before the folder is renamed into `archive/`, so a later audit reading only the archive sees terminal, non-contradictory state. A `discard`/`release` archive deliberately keeps mid-run state; the accurate validation-reuse boundary is stated by the agent per the finalize Validation De-Duplication guidance.
- **hardening(codex): `updateHooks()` R1/R2/R3 (#325).** Three LOW follow-ups from the #284 review, in the byte-identical ×3 codex installers: **R1** substitutes `pluginRoot` into the *parsed* command strings (never raw JSON text) inside a WARN-first guard, so a Windows metacharacter path can't break `JSON.parse`; **R2** carries the template `$schema` on a fresh install while an existing user value still wins; **R3** sweeps `kaola-workflow:`-prefixed entries under *every* event before re-adding, so a shrunk managed-event set leaves no orphan. Extracted `buildManagedHooks`/`mergeHooks` pure exports (+ `require.main` guard) make all three unit-testable.

## [5.10.0] — 2026-06-09

### Added

- **adaptive: plan-time cross-edition discipline in the workflow-planner profile (#306, #309).** Two authoring-time heuristics added to step 2 ("Author the plan") of `agents/workflow-planner.md` and its three locally-authored `.toml` mirrors (codex / gitlab / gitea), so a cross-edition change is scoped correctly **before** the plan is frozen instead of breaking at the finalize gate. **#306 (symbol scoping):** for each token/symbol a node adds or removes (a script-name `const`/`require`, a pinned doc phrase, a contract-validator needle, an env var, a renamed forge script), grep it across `scripts/` + every `plugins/*/scripts/` + the edition `commands/`/`skills/` trees and decide include-in-`declared_write_set` vs out-of-scope consciously — a symbol in N editions usually means N write-set members (or N nodes), not one. **#309 (semantic coupling):** file-disjointness is not semantic independence, so a single semantic change split across nodes by `FILE_CEILING` can be rewritten with divergent prose by parallel implementers (the #254 router-rewrite parity defect); keep coupled editions in ONE node when they fit, and when a split is forced give every member a **shared canonical spec** ("mirror edition X verbatim modulo forge nouns") rather than free-form "implement the same logic." Light arm — guidance only (the optional `#306` plan-validator lint and `#309` advisory consistency check are deferred). No pinned planner term removed; the codex↔gitlab `.toml` forge-noun delta is preserved.
- **adaptive: first-class plan-repair of a frozen plan (#308).** A new `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js reopen-node --node-id N` subcommand routes a review-finding or finalize-surfaced repair to an already-`complete` node without hand-editing `workflow-plan.md`: it resets N and its **post-dominating** gate node(s) (`code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer`/`adversarial-verifier`) `complete → pending`, removes their stale `.cache/barrier-base-<id>` baselines, reopens N to `in_progress`, and re-records a fresh baseline at the current merged state. It refuses over a live parallel batch / interrupted top-up (`member.opening:true`) and over a non-complete node. A companion `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js --freeze --repair` mode reconciles the `## Node Ledger` to `## Nodes` — adding a `pending` row for any node missing from the ledger, never dropping a status; the reconcile never moves `plan_hash` (it covers only `## Meta` + `## Nodes`). Across all four editions (claude↔codex byte-identical + gitlab/gitea forge ports). RED→GREEN coverage in `test-adaptive-node.js`, `test-next-action.js`, and the walkthrough; api.md + architecture.md document the new surface.
- **adaptive: plan-validator flags split byte-identity groups at freeze (#301).** The #274 sync-group write-set check now requires a byte-identical group's members to **co-occur in the SAME node** (was: present anywhere in the plan's union), so splitting halves across nodes — which leaves the two copies inconsistent between nodes — is refused at freeze. It also covers the per-forge `workflow-init` CLAUDE.md-template pairs (`commands/workflow-init.md` ↔ the edition's init `SKILL.md`), hardcoded since `validate-script-sync.js` does not export them (region-, not whole-file-identical) — the gap that caused the #286 discard #2. Enforced in every edition; toggle-agnostic; the plan-validator stays byte-identical claude↔codex with matching forge ports.

### Changed

- **adaptive: tighten the session task-list-mirror guidance + wire reopen-node into plan-run repair routing (#248, #308 follow-up).** Prose-only across the adapt + plan-run command/skill files in all four editions. **#248** removes four inferences the orchestrator was trusted to make in the live task mirror: (1) the **fused** `close-and-open-next` returns **two** transitions — the committed node → `completed` AND the newly-opened node → `in_progress` — to be reflected in the SAME update (the half-done inference was marking N done but leaving N+1 unopened); (2) the task tool is **named** at establish-time (`TodoWrite` for the Claude/GitLab/Gitea command editions, the runtime task surface for Codex); (3) a node committed `n/a` maps to `completed` (the task surface has no "skipped" state — the old "`n/a` → skipped" was a no-op); (4) a **halt convention** — on a consent-halt / security / `test_thrash` escalation, leave the blocked node `in_progress` with a `… — HALTED: <reason>` note so the live view shows *why* it stopped. **#308 wiring:** the plan-run repair-routing section now documents *when* to invoke the already-shipped `reopen-node` transaction — a Finalization-surfaced failure or a finding whose `fix_role` work belongs to an already-`complete` node routes to `reopen-node` (reset N + its post-dominating gates, re-record baseline) rather than a hand-edit, with the transitive-readiness guard (#308) withholding the downstream sink until the reopened gate re-passes, and `--freeze --repair` noted for a ledger-row gap. The new prose is byte-consistent across editions (modulo forge-renamed script names + Codex session phrasing).
- **codex/adaptive: wire the four-gate finalize barrier + gate-role machine-verdict emission into the Codex edition (#285).** Two fused gaps closed. **Part 1 (barrier):** the github-Codex finalize `SKILL.md` adaptive branch gated only on a prose "frozen `plan_hash` + ledger complete/n-a" check — the script-enforced `--resume-check`/`--gate-verify`/`--barrier-check`/`--verdict-check` quartet (present + byte-identical in the Codex script tree) was never *invoked*, so G1/H5 reviewer-execution, H1/H3 actual-writes, and the reviewer-verdict gate never ran on a Codex adaptive finalize. The SKILL.md adaptive branch now runs all four with **direct `$?` capture** (never a masking `| tail`) and a typed-refusal stop mirroring the claude finalize command (`commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md`), resolving the validator via the same `$HOME/.codex/plugins/cache` `find`-fallback idiom the skill already uses for `claim_script`. (Codex-SKILL-only by design: the GitLab/Gitea editions expose adaptive via their `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md`, which already carries the block; their `skills/` finalize has no adaptive branch.) **Part 2 (verdict emission):** the three gate-role `.toml` profiles (`code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer`/`adversarial-verifier`, byte-identical across codex/gitlab/gitea) carried the `finding: id=` contract (#290) but never instructed emitting the **column-0 `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: N` block** that `--verdict-check` reads — so once Part 1 makes the gate blocking, a Codex gate node would satisfy it only by accident. Each profile now carries a **Verdict contract** stanza (APPROVE/WARNING/BLOCK → `pass`/`pass`/`fail` for the reviewers; NOT-REFUTED/REFUTED for the skeptic, with its dual cache path — per-instance `adversarial-verifier-<claim-id>.md` under fan-out majority-refute vs. `<node-id>.md` for a single/sequence node). The codex/gitlab/gitea contract validators now **pin `verdict: pass`** in every reviewer body alongside the existing `finding: id=` pin (the #290 precedent), so a re-vendor cannot silently drop it.
- **docs: document `--verdict-check` in api.md + architecture.md at parity with `--gate-verify` (#257).** Follow-up to #251, which added the `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js --verdict-check` verdict gate but deferred the api.md/architecture.md doc work (outside #251's write-set). `docs/api.md` already carried the dedicated subcommand entry/JSON-shape/precedence (added later by #263/#274); the residual gaps are now closed — the api.md **Governance** paragraph names `--verdict-check` alongside `--gate-verify`/`--barrier-check` as a script-enforced authorization boundary, and `docs/architecture.md` documents it in both the commit-node node-END barrier list and the **Enforcement boundary** section (gate-verify proves the reviewer *executed*; verdict-check proves it *approved* — `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`, majority-refute over an `adversarial-verifier` fan-out, fail-closed, blocking at the Finalization merge gate). No stale "only `--gate-verify`" framing remains.
- **docs: mandate all four edition test chains for cross-edition diffs at Finalization (#307).** `npm test` chains the four `test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` runs with `&&`, so it **short-circuits on the first failure** — a red codex/gitlab/gitea chain behind a green claude chain is never reached, and a Finalization gate recording only the claude walkthrough can ship a broken-edition change undetected (the #254 near-miss). `CLAUDE.md` (Running Tests + Validation Policy) and a new `docs/conventions.md` § Testing — Cross-Edition Validation now require, for any diff touching the edition trees (`plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/`, the codex/forge contract validators, or any edition-port / byte-mirror / `COMMON_SCRIPTS` script), all four chains green and **recorded** before Finalization, run **sequentially** (concurrent runs trip the known `testClosureAuditExecuteLabelRemovalTimeoutBreaks` flake). Repo-internal dogfooding guidance only — the shipped end-user finalize command stays edition-generic.
- **workflow: rename "Phase 6" → "Finalization" in the Codex agent `.toml` profiles (#304).** Follow-up to #283, which hard-removed the legacy "Phase 6" runtime surface but left naming prose in the agent `.toml` (deferred because `.toml` is code-producing and could not be repaired after that issue's completed `code-review` gate). The four terminal-routine references — `config/agents.toml` doc-updater `description`, `agents/doc-updater.toml` ("Support … documentation docking"), and `agents/contractor.toml` (the `Finalize bookkeeping (… Step 8a/8b/7/8)` heading + "after all … artifact writes") — now read **Finalization** across all three editions (codex / gitlab / gitea). Pure naming prose: no readers, aliases, or contract assertions. Phase 1–5 names and the package.json/README "6-phase" framing are unchanged.
- **adaptive: durable task mirror auto-generated and reconciled at runtime (#282).** Follow-up to #266, which shipped the `kaola-workflow-task-mirror.js` generator but left it manually-invokable. The adaptive handoff now generates `workflow-tasks.json` once the plan is frozen (so it exists from the first plan-run entry), and `kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js orient` reconciles it on **every** plan-run resume — both by shelling the task-mirror CLI, so the write lands in that subprocess and `orient` stays read-only with respect to the plan/ledger/state. Best-effort (a non-frozen plan degrades silently). Edition-named forge ports call their edition-named task-mirror. api.md + workflow-state-contract.md updated.
- **adaptive: next-action readiness is now transitive (#308).** A node is ready only when its **full transitive ancestor closure** is terminal, not merely its direct deps — so a plan-repair reset of an upstream gate correctly withholds a downstream sink whose own direct deps are still complete (the premature-frontier defect). Identical to the prior direct-deps check during normal forward progress (a node cannot complete before its deps), so only a repair reset is affected.
- **adaptive: default the install switch ON + make adaptive the default path under an ON switch (#254).** A bare `./install.sh` now writes `enable_adaptive:true` (the `ENABLE_ADAPTIVE` default flipped `no`→`yes`); `--enable-adaptive=no` is the opt-out and **actively writes `enable_adaptive:false`** (read-modify-write, `parallel_mode` preserved) so it survives a re-install/upgrade over a stale `:true` — the stale-config trap — with `usage()` and the `--enable-adaptive` help line updated. Under an **ON** switch, the `/workflow-next` Step 0a-1 router now makes **adaptive the default route**: `fast`/`full` are reachable only by an explicit path-naming request (`"fast path"`/`"fast mode"` → fast; `"full path"`/`"full mode"`/`"full review"`/`"all phases"` → full) or an explicit `KAOLA_PATH`, while task-descriptor keywords (`"typo"`, `"trivial"`, `"thorough"`, …) route to the adaptive default (the planner sizes the task — a linear job is just a degenerate DAG). The level-4 "structure question / must earn itself" rubric and the flag-only adaptive keyword list are **retired under ON only**. Applied across all four router editions (`commands/workflow-next.md` + gitlab/gitea command + Claude `kaola-workflow-next` skill) and the four `kaola-workflow-adapt` reachability preconditions (which drop "and the structure question … was affirmatively confirmed").
  - **Switch-OFF behaviour is unchanged, byte-for-byte** (legacy two-way fast/full picker, `full` default, typed refusal on `KAOLA_PATH=adaptive`). The **kill-switch resolution floor stays `env > config > OFF`** — `KAOLA_ENABLE_ADAPTIVE=0` still disables per-session and an absent/cleared config is still OFF; only the install-WRITTEN config now defaults to `:true`. Install-time only: existing installs change on the next `install.sh`.
  - **Contract + tests.** The `'adaptive path selection'` concept drops `flag-only` and adds `default` in **all four contract validators** — the claude `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` + its byte-identical `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/` peer, the codex `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js`, and the gitlab/gitea edition validators (`validate-kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}-contracts.js`). `scripts/test-install-adaptive-config.js` is updated to assert the new default-ON install behavior (bare → `:true`; `--enable-adaptive=no` → `:false`; plus a new stale-config-trap case). Decision recorded in `docs/decisions/0007-adaptive-default-under-switch-on.md` (supersedes the selection / "a custom graph must earn itself" portion of `0003-adaptive-front-end-planner.md` and the #227 structure-question gate).
  - **Verification.** `install.sh` verified for all three config cases (bare → `:true`; `--enable-adaptive=no` fresh → `:false`; `=no` over a stale `:true` → `:false` with `parallel_mode` preserved) by both a scratch-`HOME` manual run and the updated `test-install-adaptive-config.js`. All four edition test chains — claude / codex / gitlab / gitea — pass green. G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; two issues were repaired in-run: a cross-edition Branch-A parity residual, and a finalize-surfaced scope gap (the three edition contract validators + the install test were intrinsic to the router/install change but omitted from the issue's stated scope) closed via an added `harden-editions` plan-repair node. Shipped via the adaptive path (dogfooded): a width-2 disjoint write-role fan-out (`router-plugins` ∥ `core-and-install`) → `harden-editions` → `code-review` gate → `docs` → `finalize`.

### Fixed

- **adaptive: #251 verdict-gate LOW follow-ups — aggregator-layer test + single/sequence cache-path doc (#259).** Two deferred hardening items from the #251 review. **F3:** `test-commit-node.js` gains `test4c` asserting that a present-and-failing whole-plan `verdictCheck` (`{exitCode:1, ok:false}`) drives `overallOk:false` / `result:refuse` through the `combineResults` aggregator — the aggregator already folded `verdictCheck` into `overallOk` (commit-node.js) but no aggregator-layer test referenced it, so this is a coverage lock for existing behavior (GREEN on first run, by design). **F4:** `agents/adversarial-verifier.md` now documents that a **single/sequence (non-fan-out)** adversarial-verifier writes its verdict block to `.cache/{node-id}.md` — the per-node path `verifyVerdictBlock` reads directly for a non-`fanout` skeptic — not the per-instance `adversarial-verifier-*` glob (which applies only under fan-out majority-refute); a block left only at the per-instance path would be missed and counts as a refute. The same dual-path note is carried in the gate-role `.toml` Verdict contract added by #285.
- **adaptive/forge: port the #261 foreign-archive barrier guard to the GitLab/Gitea plan-validators.** The two forge `plan-validator` ports diverged before #261 and never received its `barrierCheck` AC3 carve-out, so a `--barrier-check` on the GitLab/Gitea editions failed **open** on a stray `kaola-workflow/archive/<other-project>/` write (a foreign project's archive band swept onto the branch). Both ports now thread `project: projTag` into `barrierCheck` (the `projTag` they already compute for `barrierRef`) and carry the `foreignArchive` predicate + `foreignArchiveHits` refusal verbatim from base — the `kaola-workflow/archive/` prefix and `.archived-` suffix are edition-constant, so the block ports with no forge-noun rename. RED→GREEN coverage added to `test-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-scripts.js` (foreign refused / own passes / `.archived-` suffix passes / no-project backward-compat passes), mirroring base `test-commit-node.js` Test 6. Folded-in forge-parity follow-up; no separate issue.
- **adaptive: interrupted rolling top-up is correctly reconcilable (#305).** A crash mid `top-up` leaves the batch manifest whole-batch state `open` with one member carrying `opening:true` (appended before its ledger row flipped). The read gates disagreed — `crossCheckStatus` reported `valid_batch` before the flip and `orphan_member_set_mismatch` after, while `runOrient` reported `orphan_multi_in_progress` before and accepted it as a valid batch after — and mutators ran over it. Both gates now short-circuit any manifest with an `opening:true` member to a reconcilable verdict (`batch_topup_incomplete`) consistently before AND after the flip, and every mutating batch command (`open-batch`/`top-up`/`seal`/`seal-member`/`join`) refuses `reconcile_first` while the marker exists. Byte-identical claude↔codex + gitlab/gitea forge ports; shared anti-drift fixture drives both read-gate test sites.
- **test: bind the orphan-legality `in_progress` axis on the orient site (#302, #293 follow-up).** `test-adaptive-node.js` imported `ORPHAN_LEGALITY_IN_PROGRESS_IDS` but never used it; the #293 characterization-lock now derives its ledger row from the shared array, so the import is load-bearing and both `crossCheckStatus`/`runOrient` sites bind the same input by construction.
- **adaptive/finalize: make worktree finalize crash-resumable (#296).** `cmdFinalize` previously archived the project folder via `fs.renameSync` before committing the implementation — a crash between those two steps left the archive dir present but the work uncommitted, with no resume path (the standard `cmdResume` only checks for an active `kaola-workflow/{project}/` folder). Fix: added `detectFinalizeIncomplete(root, project)` (project-scoped `git status --porcelain -- kaola-workflow/archive/{project}` dirty-check) and a detection branch in `cmdResume`. When `cmdResume` is called for a project with no active folder but a dirty archive dir, it now returns `{resumed:true, reason:'finalize_incomplete', next_command:'finalize --keep-worktree'}` instead of dead-ending. Re-running `cmdFinalize --keep-worktree` detects the `source-missing` state and stages the already-archived dir, then completes normally. Project-scoped dirty-check avoids false positives from unrelated uncommitted changes. Fixed across all four claim.js editions (root `scripts/`, github plugin, gitlab, gitea). `commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md` (×3 editions) and `agents/contractor.md` document the `## Crash Recovery` procedure.

## [5.9.0] — 2026-06-08

### Added

- **adaptive: parallel fan-out completed end-to-end (#303).** The adaptive parallel-fanout path is now wired through planning, scheduling, execution, seal, and join. Planner fan-out **width is decoupled from `KAOLA_FANOUT_CAP`** — the cap is a **runtime concurrency limit only** (at most that many members run at once), not a planning-validity bound, so a logical fan-out MAY be wider. The executor performs **rolling bounded dispatch** via a new `parallel-batch top-up` subcommand: it opens up to the cap, queues the rest, and drains the queue as earlier members seal. Scheduling is now **scheduler-aware** — fused advance plus start-frontier batching opens the openable frontier together. **Batch seal** enforces the serial evidence-shape gate plus a **write-role non-empty-in-lane vacuity guard** (a member that leaked no writes into its own lane can no longer pass vacuously). **Write-role join is tree-aware** (handles deletions and renames, not just adds). **`open-batch` gains an active-manifest precondition plus a `--resume-check` integrity gate.** A **crash-safe `opening` transaction marker** (manifest written with the intended member set before the ledger rows flip) plus a **`reconcile` repair subcommand** (roll-forward, or `--abort` roll-back) close the interrupted-open/top-up window; the dead `dispatched` batch state is removed. `decision:ask` is normalized to **audit-metadata-only** in plan-run, and the evidence path is normalized to `.cache/{node-id}.md`.

### Changed

- **workflow: `Finalization` is the route-agnostic terminal routine; the legacy `Phase 6` runtime surface is hard-removed across all three editions (#283).** The shared terminal routine that every route (full, fast, adaptive) ends in is now named **Finalization** — calling it "Phase 6" was only coherent for the old full numbered ladder and actively confusing for the adaptive DAG. No compatibility shim, no dual-artifact reader, a one-way in-flight migration completed inside the issue.
  - **Canonical surfaces.** User command `/kaola-workflow-finalize {project}`; Codex skill `kaola-workflow-finalize`; summary artifact `finalization-summary.md`; route-neutral state `stage: finalization` / `stage_name: Finalization` (runtime no longer generates `phase: 6` / `phase_name: Finalize`); pointers `next_command: /kaola-workflow-finalize` / `next_skill: kaola-workflow-finalize`. Full, fast, and adaptive route text all describe the terminal routine as `Finalization`.
  - **Hard removal (legacy absent).** `commands/kaola-workflow-phase6.md` is renamed to `kaola-workflow-finalize.md` and removed from every installed command surface (github / gitlab / gitea). Runtime no longer accepts `phase6-summary.md` as a finalization input (the `repair-state` completion reader and the `sink-pr`/`sink-mr`/`sink-merge` summary readers now use `finalization-summary.md`), no longer generates `phase: 6`, and no longer routes to `/kaola-workflow-phase6`. The contract validators (`validate-workflow-contracts.js` + its byte-identical `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/` peer, the Codex `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js`, and the gitlab/gitea contract validators) now assert the canonical `finalize.md` route **present** AND the legacy `kaola-workflow-phase6.md` / `phase6-summary.md` route **absent**; `test-install-model-rendering.js` reads the installed `kaola-workflow-finalize.md`.
  - **One-way in-flight migration.** `repair-state` (`migrateActiveLegacyFolder`, mirrored in the gitlab/gitea ports) rewrites any active, non-archived `kaola-workflow/{project}/` folder from the legacy names to the canonical ones (`phase6-summary.md` → `finalization-summary.md`; stale `phase: 6` / `next_command: /kaola-workflow-phase6` state fields → finalization equivalents) the next time it normalizes that folder, with **no persistent legacy reader or alias** remaining afterward.
  - **Scope.** Commands, Codex skill mirrors, runtime scripts + their byte-identical peers, contract validators, the install-model-rendering test, and the user-facing docs (`README.md` headline route-neutralized; ASCII route diagrams, phase/agent/script tables, `docs/{api,architecture,workflow-state-contract,conventions}.md`) across github / gitlab / gitea. Historical `CHANGELOG`/`docs/investigations`/`docs/decisions` entries retain "Phase 6" as accurate records of past releases.
  - **Verification.** `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` exits 0 and `npm test` is green across all four lanes (claude / codex / gitlab / gitea — contract validation + walkthroughs), with real exit codes captured directly. G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0` (one in-scope residual repaired in-run; one docs follow-up resolved by the docs node).
  - **Deferred follow-up (#304).** The Codex agent `.toml` profiles (`config/agents.toml`, `agents/{doc-updater,contractor}.toml`) still carry "Phase 6" naming prose; they were outside #283's scope list (which enumerated `SKILL.md` mirrors, not agent `.toml`) and, being `.toml` (code-producing), could not be repaired after the completed `code-review` gate without disproportionate re-gating. They are naming prose only (not readers/aliases), so the hard-removal acceptance criteria are fully satisfied; the cosmetic cross-surface consistency fix is tracked in #304.

### Fixed

- **adaptive/worktree: reconcile MAIN-repo staged `.roadmap/issue-N.md` orphan at worktree finalize (#297).** On an adaptive worktree run, `kaola-workflow-adaptive-handoff.js` Step 5 creates `kaola-workflow/.roadmap/issue-N.md` in the MAIN repo and `git add`s it without committing (the worktree is forked from a HEAD that lacks the file). At finalize, `archiveProjectDir` running from the worktree found the roadmap source absent in the worktree (`roadmap_source_removed: "absent"`) and left the MAIN repo's staged ADD intact, causing `sink-merge.js`'s `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no` clean check to fail. Fix: added a symmetric main-side reconciliation in `archiveProjectDir`'s existing `mainRoot !== linkedRoot` branch: when the file is staged-ADD-only in MAIN (not on HEAD — guarded by `git cat-file -e HEAD:<relpath>`), run `git -C <mainRoot> rm --cached --force --ignore-unmatch <relpath>` to drop the staged index entry, followed by `fs.unlinkSync` for the working-tree file (ENOENT-guarded). The committed-on-HEAD case (normal worktree-finalize) is explicitly skipped so the feature-branch archive commit handles deletion there, preventing a regression staged `D`. Fix mirrored across all four claim ports (root + byte-identical base-plugin + gitlab-edition + gitea-edition). Two new walkthrough assertions: the staged-ADD-only scenario (reproduces the bug, asserts MAIN clean after finalize) and a MAIN-status clean assertion on the existing committed-on-HEAD test (locks the no-regression). `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` and `npm test` green.

## [5.8.0] — 2026-06-08

### Changed

- **adaptive: enforce planner-first entry before any DAG shaping (#287).** Once the adaptive path is selected and the target issue is known, the FIRST issue-specific action must be dispatching `workflow-planner` — the main session performs only non-design preflight (read rules, confirm the target issue, the authoring-allowed switch check, git freshness, non-design availability checks) and must **not** pre-author the `## Nodes` DAG, choose role sequence/dependencies/shapes/write-sets, or create the node task list before the planner owns the design. A boundary leak observed in the #279 session — the main session pre-shaped the node/task/write-set structure, then dispatched the planner as an `AUTHOR EXACTLY` mechanical worker — is now closed by contract.
  - **New typed refusal `planner_control_boundary_violation`.** The `workflow-planner` refuses to author when its dispatch prompt carries a mandatory/pre-authored `## Nodes` table, `AUTHOR EXACTLY`, or `do not redesign`. Enforced as agent-profile behavioral prose in `agents/workflow-planner.md` (no script ever receives the dispatch prompt — only the planner reads its own), wired into its existing structured-return output contract as a fourth return mode.
  - **Carve-out.** That pre-shaped-prompt shape is allowed **only** when re-dispatching the planner after `handoff_status: plan_invalid` on an UNFROZEN plan, with the validator errors supplied as repair context — nested inside the pre-existing frozen/unfrozen overwrite-guard, so there is no contradiction.
  - **Task-list timing.** The orchestrator task list is created only after `handoff_status: ready_to_run` and after reading the frozen plan.
  - **Contract pins, all editions.** `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` (+ its `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/` byte-twin) pins the `planner_control_boundary_violation` token across all three editions' `kaola-workflow-adapt` command docs (gitlab/gitea via the `routedFixFiles` cross-edition precedent) and `agents/workflow-planner.md`; `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` pins the codex `kaola-workflow-adapt` SKILL.md mirror. Decision recorded in `docs/decisions/0006-planner-first-entry.md`. `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea); G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`.

### Fixed

- **forge-parity: port `checkDispatchAttestations` into GitLab and Gitea `sink-merge.js` closure-receipt paths (#300, #286 R2).** The github `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` ran `checkDispatchAttestations` on its closure-receipt paths (2 call sites); the GitLab and Gitea forks ran it 0 times, so a forge-edition sink-merge close retained the stale `emptyReceipt` `failed` default for `claim_planner_attested` / `finalize_contractor_attested` instead of reaching the real `missing`/`attested` values. Ported the `checkDispatchAttestations([archiveCacheDir, liveCacheDir], receipt)` call into `postMergeCleanup`'s closure-receipt build site in both `kaola-gitlab-workflow-sink-merge.js` and `kaola-gitea-workflow-sink-merge.js`, placed between `buildClosureReceipt` and `checkClosureInvariants` (archive-first candidate ordering), mirroring the github edition exactly. `checkDispatchAttestations` was already exported from both forge `claim.js` modules. RED→GREEN regression assertions in `test-gitlab-sinks.js` (Test 22) and `test-gitea-sinks.js` (Test 22) assert `claim_planner_attested === 'missing'` (not `'failed'`) on a no-dispatch-log sink-merge close; wired into `simulate-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-walkthrough.js` via `run()`. `npm test` green across all four editions. G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; call placement, candidate ordering, and test wiring all verified against the github reference. Recorded as the #286 R2 follow-up (the #286 diff touched `claim.js` watch-pr paths only; `sink-merge.js` was outside its frozen write set). Relates to #286, #280, #277.

- **codex: drop the Claude-only model resolver from the Codex skills + run closure attestation on all receipt paths (#286).** Two small, orthogonal Codex-vs-Claude parity follow-ups to #266.
  - **Fix 1 — Codex skills no longer dangle the `~/.claude/agents` model resolver (AC1).** Codex dispatches a subagent **by role name** — the role's `model_reasoning_effort` tier in its `agents/<role>.toml` profile is the model signal — but several skills instructed resolving a role's model via the `~/.claude/agents`-reading `kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js` and passing `model=`, a Claude-ism a Codex-only install cannot satisfy. The `workflow-init` CLAUDE.md template sentence is rewritten **edition-neutral** ("pass the role's configured model on the spawn call — each agent ships its model in its installed profile") across all three forge byte-identity pairs (`commands/workflow-init.md` ↔ `skills/kaola-workflow-init/SKILL.md`, enforced by `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js`); because the template is shared byte-identically with the Claude command, the generated **Claude CLAUDE.md guidance also changes** — dropping the `~/.claude/agents`/`model=` literals while keeping the pass-the-model intent (the accepted consequence of AC3's "byte-identical claude↔codex"). The two github Codex skills `kaola-workflow-plan-run/SKILL.md` and `kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md` drop their `resolve-agent-model` dispatch clause. `grep -rn "resolve-agent-model|~/.claude/agents" plugins/*/skills/` now returns zero hits.
  - **Fix 2 — closure attestation runs on every receipt path (AC2).** `checkDispatchAttestations` (the #277 M2 dispatch-log attestation) ran only after the `cmdFinalize` closure receipt; the two `watch-pr` `buildClosureReceipt` callers (MERGED + CLOSED branches) never ran it, so a watch-pr close surfaced the stale `emptyReceipt` attestation default (`failed`) instead of a real `missing`/`attested`/`violation`. The call is now wired into both watch-pr paths — archive-cache-first candidate, attestation **before** invariants, mirroring the canonical `cmdFinalize` site — across all **four** `claim.js` editions (the github↔codex byte-identical pair plus the gitlab/gitea forge ports with their `merged`/`closed` + `issue_iid` variants). RED→GREEN regression `testWatchPrMergedClosureReceipt` in `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` asserts the watch-pr MERGED receipt's `claim_planner_attested`/`finalize_contractor_attested` reach a real `missing` (was the stale `failed`).
  - Verified by full `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea), including `validate-script-sync.js` byte-identity for the `claim.js` github pair and `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` CLAUDE.md-template byte-identity for all three init pairs. G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; two LOW non-blocking findings — R1 (gitlab/gitea `commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md` reference `resolve-agent-model`, but those are **Claude** command cards where it is correct; out of scope) and R2 (gitlab/gitea `sink-merge` build a closure receipt without `checkDispatchAttestations`, a **pre-existing** #280 forge-port gap outside this diff) — the latter recorded as a follow-up. Relates to #266, #277.
- **adaptive: close the archive-pollution blind spot in the Phase-6 staging safety nets so a stray cross-issue `archive/<other>/` cannot reach `main` undetected (#261).** Both Phase-6 staging safety nets exempted *every* path under `kaola-workflow/`, so neither caught a stray untracked `kaola-workflow/archive/issue-*/` folder (left by a prior interrupted/staged run) being swept into the final commit — defeating the #231 script-enforced merge-gate guarantee precisely in the archive band. The only thing standing between cross-issue archive pollution and `main` was the orchestrator staging perfectly surgically. Three coordinated, defense-in-depth fixes, each with regression coverage:
  - **`--barrier-check` gains teeth in the archive band (AC3, the headline fix).** `barrierCheck` in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (the byte-identical root ⇄ `plugins/kaola-workflow` #274 mirror pair) scopes the otherwise-blanket `isWorkflowArtifact` exemption: an actual write under `kaola-workflow/archive/<X>/` is exempt **only** when `<X>` is the finalized project (or its `<project>.archived-<ts>` collision-rename); a **foreign-project** archive write is refused with a dedicated typed error and dropped from the production set so it is not double-reported. The finalized project is `opts.project`, threaded from the already-derived `projTag` (the basename of the directory holding `workflow-plan.md`) at the CLI call site — no new flag — and the check is **fail-closed** (absent project ⇒ any archive write is treated as foreign). Fully backward-compatible: evidence/callers with no archive path and no `project` parse exactly as before. New `test-commit-node.js` assertions cover foreign-refuse / own-pass / suffix-pass / no-project-backward-compat.
  - **`cmdFinalize` no longer stages broadly (AC2).** `kaola-workflow-claim.js` (the byte-identical mirror pair) replaces the linked-worktree finalize path's broad `git add -A kaola-workflow/` with explicit, project-scoped staging — `git rm -r --cached --ignore-unmatch` of the live `kaola-workflow/<project>/` (the rename's deletion side) plus an existence-guarded `git add` of the archive destination, `kaola-workflow/.roadmap/`, and `kaola-workflow/ROADMAP.md` — so a stray foreign `kaola-workflow/archive/<other>/` is never swept into the finalize commit. New `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` regression `testFinalizeNarrowStagingExcludesForeignArchive` (RED→GREEN against the linked-worktree fixture, the only path reaching that stage); the existing roadmap-staging guard confirms the narrowing still stages the mirror.
  - **The Phase-6 Staging Guard detects a staged foreign archive (AC1).** The Staging Guard in all three `kaola-workflow-phase6.md` editions (claude/gitlab/gitea) gains a `FOREIGN_ARCHIVE` detector that emits a typed `BLOCKED` block when a staged `kaola-workflow/archive/<other>/` whose `<other>` is not the finalized project is present, *before* the existing multi-project `PROJECT_COUNT` guard. The match tolerates the finalized project's own `.archived-<ts>` collision suffix — symmetric with the barrier-check carve-out — and compares `{project}` by whole-string equality, preventing substring false-matches (e.g. `issue-2610` vs `issue-261`). *(#294 below hardens this comparison from an extended-regex `grep` to a fixed-string `awk` test, so a metacharacter-bearing project name cannot fail the guard open.)*
  - Verified by full `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea), including `validate-script-sync.js` byte-identity for **both** mirror pairs. G1 code-review and G2 security-review both recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; the security review's one non-blocking finding (the Phase-6 guard interpolates a render-time `{project}` into a `grep -E` pattern without `projTag`'s `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` sanitization — a fail-open only under a pathological project name AND an off-path broad `git add`, behind two string-comparison script layers that never use regex) is addressed in-release by #294 (below), which replaces the guard's extended-regex match with a fixed-string comparison. Docs updated in `docs/api.md` + `docs/architecture.md`. Relates to #231 (the merge gate this exemption undercut) and #260 (sibling in-place-sink hardening).
- **adaptive/parallel-batch: three non-blocking hardening fixes from the #281 review, applied across all four editions (#291).** Follow-ups R1/R2/R4 recorded by the #281 G1 `code-review` and `adversarial-verifier`; all three failed closed today, so this is correctness hardening, not a behavior-breaking fix. R3 (write-role-join `gitCheckout` ref-vs-path on the dormant path) remains an explicit out-of-scope honest partial, documented in `docs/investigations/2026-06-07-parallel-ready-set-execution-design.md`.
  - **R1 — `runSealMember` idempotency guard.** `runSealMember` appended a `## Required Agent Compliance` row with no `member.sealed` guard, so a re-run wrote a duplicate row. It now returns early (`result: 'ok'`, `alreadySealed: true`) on an already-sealed member, mirroring `runSeal`'s existing guard. Cosmetic only — no Phase-6 gate parses the compliance section (`--gate-verify` reads ledger+topology, `--verdict-check` reads `.cache/{id}.md`).
  - **R2 — `runOpenBatch` baselines-first ordering.** Batch open recorded the N per-member baselines *after* flipping the N ledger rows to `in_progress` and writing the plan, so a mid-loop baseline failure left flipped rows with no manifest — an orphan needing manual repair. It now records all N baselines *before* any ledger flip or plan write, so a baseline failure makes zero plan/ledger mutation. **Honest scope:** this removes the orphan from a baseline-recording failure but does **not** make open-batch fully atomic — the plan-write → manifest-write gap remains (two files are not atomically writable). Still fails closed.
  - **R4 — partial-seal legality at both AC#5 gate sites.** The "multiple `in_progress` is legal only with a matching active manifest" gate used exact member-set equality at two sites — `crossCheckStatus` (parallel-batch) and the `runOrient` gate (adaptive-node) — which misread a legitimate **partial-seal** (a 3+-member batch where some members have sealed/completed but remain in the manifest while others stay `in_progress`) as `orphan_multi_in_progress`, blocking a legal crash-resume. Both sites now compare `in_progress` against the **unsealed** manifest members (`.filter(m => !m.sealed)`); genuine member-set mismatches still flag orphan (non-regressing against the existing P6c/T20b/T20d fixtures, verified by a shared partial-seal fixture exercising both sites).
  - **Cross-edition parity.** Applied identically to all four editions: the github↔codex byte-identical pair (`scripts/` + `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/`, enforced by `validate-script-sync.js`) and the gitlab/gitea edition-named ports (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-{parallel-batch,adaptive-node}.js`, which are not byte-synced so the parity is a discharged correctness obligation). New unit coverage in `test-parallel-batch.js` (R1/R2/R4a) and `test-adaptive-node.js` (R4b partial-seal fixture); `npm test` green across all four editions.
  - **Known pre-existing follow-up (not introduced by #291).** `crossCheckStatus` and `runOrient` diverge on a single-`in_progress` + all-sealed-manifest input (one flags orphan, the other falls through to the legacy single-node path). Surfaced by the #291 `adversarial-verifier`; out of scope for this pass.
- **adaptive: the Phase-6 Staging Guard's `FOREIGN_ARCHIVE` detector no longer fails open on a metacharacter-bearing project name (#294, #261 follow-up).** The #261 guard rendered the `{project}` token into an extended-regex `grep -v -E -x "{project}(\.archived-.*)?"` without `projTag`'s `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` sanitization (the MEDIUM `S1` finding from the #261 G2 security review). A pathological project name carrying ERE metacharacters (e.g. `.*`) would match-and-exclude *every* candidate, so a genuinely foreign `kaola-workflow/archive/<other>/` would slip past the guard — a defense-in-depth fail-open. (Unreachable in practice: real `issue-N` names carry no metacharacters, and the script-enforced layers that actually keep a foreign archive off the branch — `--barrier-check`'s `foreignArchive` carve-out and `cmdFinalize`'s narrowed staging — compare by string with no regex, so they hold regardless of `{project}`.) The guard now consumes `{project}` as a **fixed string** (`awk -v p="{project}" '$0 != p && index($0, p ".archived-") != 1'`), never as a regex, so no project name can fail it open; whole-string equality still prevents substring false-matches (`issue-2610` vs `issue-261`) and the project's own `.archived-<ts>` collision suffix is still tolerated. Applied identically to all three `kaola-workflow-phase6.md` editions (claude/gitlab/gitea) — no live Codex `phase6.md` exists. Verified directly with a guard-pipeline harness (the regex form fails open on `{project}=.*` + a foreign archive; the fixed-string form blocks it while preserving own-archive / `.archived-<ts>` / foreign-block / substring-anchoring behavior); `npm test` green across all four editions. Relates to #261, #231.
- **adaptive/parallel-batch: complete the write-role `fanout(...)` batch joins — the #281 honest partial (AC#3) — and fix the R3 `gitCheckout` ref-vs-path edge, across all four editions (#292).** #281 shipped read-only sibling batches complete but left write-role joins as a documented honest partial: the join path was dormant (`worktreePath` always `null`) and its `gitCheckout` conflated a ref with a path. This activates the full capability and closes R3, with the test that proves it.
  - **R3 closed.** The `join` io shim conflated a worktree *filesystem path* with a *ref*: it ran `git -C <projectDir> checkout <worktreePath> -- <paths>` — a path in the tree-ish slot, and the wrong cwd. It now runs `git -C <parent-worktree-root> checkout <mergeRef> -- <declared paths>`, where `<mergeRef>` is a gc-anchored commit capturing the member's writes and the declared paths are the repo-root-relative pathspec. A real two-member disjoint write-role batch is exercised end-to-end through the subprocess CLI (not a mock), asserting both members' files land in the **parent worktree with correct content** — proven no-false-green by a mutation test (reintroducing the bug makes the test go RED with `fatal: invalid reference`).
  - **Isolated node worktrees (AC#3 in full).** `open-batch` now provisions one isolated git worktree per write-role member, keyed `(projTag, node-id)` under `.kw/batch/`, each seeded from the parent's current landable state. The plan is copied into each member worktree so the per-node barrier resolves **member-scoped** (`findRepoRoot` → the member worktree), closing a second latent false-green where the previously parent-scoped seal barrier could not see a member writing outside its declared lane. Each member's sealed state is captured as a gc-anchored `mergeRef` (commit-tree + update-ref, surviving gc between seal and join); `join` checks out that ref into the parent — idempotent (re-checkout of identical content is a no-op) and crash-safe across the `joining` manifest state (only unmerged members re-run). `runJoin` now **fails closed** (`missing_merge_ref`) on a member with no captured ref — `joined:true` is reachable only after a real successful checkout.
  - **Serialized fallback, logged.** When the host lacks isolated-worktree capability, `open-batch` returns `{result:'ok', degraded:true, reason:'worktree_unavailable', opened:[]}` with zero ledger/manifest mutation (any partial worktrees rolled back); the orchestrator `log()`s the degradation and falls back to the single-node serial path — correctness preserved, parallelism forgone. Documented in all four `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` doc surfaces (root command + claude SKILL + gitlab/gitea commands). Member worktrees are removed after join; `.kw/` is gitignored, so batch worktrees never enter a sink commit.
  - **Cross-edition parity.** Applied identically to all four `parallel-batch` editions: the github↔codex byte-identical pair (`scripts/` + `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/`, enforced by `validate-script-sync.js`) and the gitlab/gitea edition-named ports (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-parallel-batch.js`, byte-identical bodies modulo the 7 edition-rename lines). New real-git tests (E1 join + E2 member-scoped barrier + E3 degraded fallback) in `test-parallel-batch.js`; all existing read-only batch assertions byte-unchanged. G1 `code-review` and the `adversarial-verifier` both recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; `npm test` green across all four editions. Completes the partial tracked in `docs/investigations/2026-06-07-parallel-ready-set-execution-design.md`; the deletion edge (a member deleting a declared path) is an explicit bounded-scope follow-up.
- **adaptive/parallel-batch: align `crossCheckStatus` with `runOrient` on the single-`in_progress` + non-matching-manifest case, closing the #291 F1 divergence, across all four editions (#293).** The two AC#5 legality checks disagreed on the same input: given a single stale `in_progress` row whose member is already sealed (an active manifest present but not matching), `crossCheckStatus` (`kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js`) reported `orphan_member_set_mismatch` while the mutation-driving `runOrient` gate (`kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`) correctly fell through to the legacy single-node path (`result: ok, batch: null`). The orphan condition is specifically *multiple* `in_progress` rows without a matching active manifest, so the single-row state is legal. This was a pre-existing, diagnostic-only inconsistency (`crossCheckStatus`'s only in-scope caller is the read-only `runStatus`); it failed closed and was surfaced — not introduced — by the #291 `adversarial-verifier` (finding F1, `scope=pre_existing`).
  - **One-predicate fix, `crossCheckStatus` only.** A `ip.length <= 1` short-circuit is hoisted *above* the manifest-comparison branch, so a single `in_progress` row is treated as the legacy single-node path regardless of the manifest — aligning `crossCheckStatus` to `runOrient`'s already-correct `length > 1` orphan guard. `runOrient` production is intentionally **not** modified (it was already right). Genuine multi-`in_progress` mismatches still flag orphan; the R4 partial-seal (T799–T802) and P6a–P6d cases are non-regressing.
  - **Shared anti-drift fixture.** New `scripts/fixtures-orphan-legality.js` exports the single shared input (manifest `[{id:'a',sealed:true}]` + `inProgressIds:['a']` + expectations); it is imported by **both** `test-parallel-batch.js` (the `crossCheckStatus` RED→GREEN assertion on the load-bearing manifest axis) and `test-adaptive-node.js` (the `runOrient` characterization lock), so the two sites cannot silently re-diverge on that axis.
  - **Cross-edition parity.** Applied identically to all four editions: the github↔codex byte-identical pair (`scripts/` + `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/`, enforced by `validate-script-sync.js`) and the gitlab/gitea edition-named ports (`kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-parallel-batch.js`, byte-identical `crossCheckStatus` bodies). G1 `code-review` recorded `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`; `npm test` green across all four editions. One non-blocking follow-up (R1): in `test-adaptive-node.js` the orient assertion shares only the manifest axis with the fixture (it re-derives its `in_progress` ledger row inline) — the `in_progress` axis cannot be mechanically shared by construction because `crossCheckStatus` consumes an array while `runOrient` parses a markdown ledger. Relates to #291 (F1 source), #281.

## [5.7.0] — 2026-06-08

### Changed

- **agents: forked `code-reviewer` + `security-reviewer` from vendored into local agents and moved the #279 findings-emission contract into the reviewer bodies (#279 follow-up).** The #279 machine-readable findings contract was originally documented only in the `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` dispatch prose, on the assumption that the vendored `code-reviewer`/`security-reviewer` agents could not be edited. They can: `validate-vendored-agents.js` only asserts the provenance *lines exist* (it never recomputes the content hash), and the vendored reviewers were already locally extended by design (the `Prompt Defense Baseline` section; the #197 `doc-updater` model override). This relocates the emission contract to its correct home — the reviewer's own persona, where the `verdict:` contract already lived — and makes the project own these agents outright.
  - **Vendored → local.** `code-reviewer` and `security-reviewer` move from `vendoredAgents` to `localAgents` in `scripts/validate-vendored-agents.js` (provenance-exempt, validated as managed local agents). Each agent file's header is trimmed to just the `kaola-workflow-managed-agent: true` marker plus a `locally-forked` note; the per-file MIT provenance block (upstream / blob-sha / sha256 / license / copyright) is removed.
  - **MIT attribution at the project level.** The derived works remain under the upstream MIT License, Copyright (c) 2026 Affaan Mustafa — that notice is now honored centrally in `docs/agents-source.md` (new *Locally Forked (derived from ECC)* section) rather than per-file. The vendored set drops from 9 to 7 files and the Refresh Procedure no longer re-fetches the two reviewers.
  - **Findings contract in every reviewer body, every edition.** A `Machine-Readable Findings` section (flat column-0 `finding:` lines + the closed scope/action/status/fix_role vocabulary) is appended to `code-reviewer` / `security-reviewer` / `adversarial-verifier` across the root `agents/*.md`, the `agents/profiles/higher/*` model variants, and the nine `plugins/{kaola-workflow,kaola-workflow-gitlab,kaola-workflow-gitea}/agents/*.toml`. The reviewer now owns the emission format regardless of which dispatcher invokes it.
  - **Plan-run docs trimmed to a pointer.** The `## Repair routing (#279)` section in the four `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` docs (skill + three commands) drops the duplicated emission format and points at the per-agent contract, keeping only the bounded repair-routing controller. Separation of concerns: the reviewer emits findings, the orchestrator routes them.

  No change to the #279 mechanical gate (`parseNodeFindings` / `unresolvedInScopeFixes` / `--verdict-check`). `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea); `validate-vendored-agents.js` passes with 13 agents (7 vendored + 6 local).

### Fixed

- **adaptive: the #277 closure attestations no longer false-fail on a clean, fully-delegated run (#280).** `claim_planner_attested` and `finalize_contractor_attested` reported `failed`/`missing` even when the `workflow-planner` and `contractor` were genuinely dispatched as subagents — a false negative on the exact "good" path the #277 detector is meant to attest, which defeated its diagnostic value (an operator could not distinguish a real inline bypass from a correctly-delegated run). Two distinct root causes, both fixed while preserving the warn-first contract (attestation still never blocks closure; `closure_invariants.ok` is untouched):
  - **The planner spawn was structurally unloggable.** The `SubagentStart` hook (`hooks/kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh`) appends to `kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/dispatch-log.jsonl`, but the `workflow-planner` is dispatched FIRST and its own claim is what CREATES that project folder — so when the hook fired there was no active project to attribute the spawn to, and the planner was never recorded. There is no harness environment variable that distinguishes a subagent from the main session (verified by a live two-sided probe: a delegated subagent and the main session see byte-identical env), so the planner now **self-attests**: its relocated startup invocation in `agents/workflow-planner.md` passes a new `--attest-planner-spawn` flag, and `kaola-workflow-claim.js` back-fills a `workflow-planner` dispatch-log entry (fail-open `try/catch`) when it creates the project `.cache/`. The back-fill is gated **strictly** on the flag, so a genuine zero-spawn inline bypass (the #276 shape) — which never runs the relocated planner procedure and so never passes the flag — still reports `missing`/`failed`. This matches #277's stated "Honest limits": the detector catches the casual/accidental bypass, not a determined one.
  - **`sink-merge` re-derived the attestation from the wrong (already-archived) path.** `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` built its `closure_receipt` via `buildClosureReceipt(...)` without ever calling `checkDispatchAttestations`, so the `emptyReceipt` `'failed'` default survived into the sink receipt — even though `cmdFinalize`'s own receipt (which already runs the check archive-first) was correct, and even though the contractor IS logged. `sink-merge` now runs the same archive-first `checkDispatchAttestations([archive/.cache, live/.cache], receipt)` (reusing the exported helper) before emitting the receipt, so both the `cmdFinalize` receipt AND the `sink-merge` closure_receipt agree.
  - Applied byte-identically to both copies of `claim.js` and `sink-merge.js` (the `scripts/` ⇄ `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/` #274 mirror pairs); `emptyReceipt`/`closure-contract.js`, the 3-way byte-synced dispatch-log hook, and the Codex `.toml` planner ports are deliberately untouched (Codex attestation parity is tracked separately by #266/#286). Regression coverage in `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`: AC1 (fully-delegated ⇒ both attestations `attested` in BOTH the `cmdFinalize` receipt and the `sink-merge` closure_receipt), AC2 (zero-spawn inline ⇒ both stay `missing`/`failed`), and a non-circular contract guard asserting `agents/workflow-planner.md` actually passes the flag (the behavioral tests set the flag themselves, so the guard proves production passes it too). `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea).

### Added

- **contracts: regression-pin the reviewer findings-emission contract PRESENCE across all four editions (#290, #279/#288 follow-up).** PR #288 relocated the #279 machine-readable findings-emission contract INTO the reviewer agent bodies, but nothing pinned that section's *presence* — a future re-vendor, refactor, or agent rewrite could silently drop it from one or more editions while `npm test` stayed green, quietly reverting the feature for that edition even though the mechanical gate (`parseNodeFindings` / `unresolvedInScopeFixes` / `--verdict-check`) still nominally existed. The gate (the consuming half) was well tested; the emission half (the instruction that makes reviewers actually write `finding:` lines) was unguarded.
  - **Presence pin in every edition contract validator.** Each of the four edition contract validators now asserts the cross-format token `finding: id=` is present in its edition's reviewer agent bodies: `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` (and its byte-identical `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` #274 mirror) pins the five Claude `.md` bodies (`agents/{code-reviewer,security-reviewer,adversarial-verifier}.md` + `agents/profiles/higher/{code-reviewer,security-reviewer}.md`); `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` (Codex) and the GitLab/Gitea `validate-kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}-contracts.js` each pin their three plugin `.toml` reviewer bodies — 14 reviewer bodies pinned in total (5 `.md` + 9 `.toml`).
  - **Token-pin, not heading-pin (AC2).** The pin targets the common emission line `finding: id=`, which is identical across formats, rather than the format-specific heading (`Machine-Readable Findings` in `.md`, `Findings contract` in `.toml`), so the legitimate `.md`-vs-`.toml` phrasing differences are not false-flagged. Reviewer agent bodies are unchanged (the pin guards their *presence*; it does not edit them).
  - **Proven by mutation (AC1).** Removing the findings-emission section from any reviewer body (`.md` or `.toml`, any edition) now fails `npm test`: the per-edition RED proof confirmed each of the four validators exits non-zero on a planted removal and that the full suite is green with all bodies intact. `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea); `validate-script-sync.js` confirms the Claude validator pair stays byte-identical. Mirrors the existing prose-pin class in `validate-workflow-contracts.js` (e.g. the `build-error-resolver` subagent_type pin, the fast-path `code-reviewer` mandatory pin).
- **adaptive: true parallel ready-set (batch) execution for adaptive DAGs — "one frontier unit at a time" (#281).** The adaptive executor opened and dispatched exactly one ready node at a time, so the supported fan-out / adversarial-verification / tournament / composed-DAG patterns were topological/validation features rather than wall-clock parallel execution. #281 adds a script-mediated batch lifecycle so a frontier of independent ready siblings can be opened together, dispatched concurrently, sealed per-member, joined, and used to unblock downstream — without weakening any freeze/decidability/gate invariant. The load-bearing constraint shapes the whole design: **a script cannot spawn agents and a subagent cannot dispatch a subagent, so the only real concurrency is the main session issuing multiple `Agent()` calls in one message.** Responsibilities split accordingly — the script owns batch STATE, the orchestrator owns DISPATCH.
  - **New `kaola-workflow-parallel-batch.js` aggregator (STATE only, never dispatches).** Pure composition over `next-action.js` + `commit-node.js` (+ `plan-validator.js`), mirroring how `adaptive-node.js` shells siblings and imports only pure helpers (`spliceLedgerNode`) — never require-and-mutates them. Subcommands: `open-batch` (flip the N ready-pending ledger rows to `in_progress` + record N per-member baselines via `commit-node --start`; fail-closed `not_disjoint` on overlapping write-role sets; cap at `FANOUT_CAP`), `seal-member`/`seal` (per-member barrier via the unchanged `commit-node --node-id`), `join` (path-scoped, idempotent merge of disjoint write-role member worktrees into the parent; no-op for read-only batches), and read-only `status` (manifest + cross-check). Batch state lives in a non-hashed runtime manifest `kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/active-batch.json` with the five-state lifecycle `open → dispatched → sealed → joining → joined` (the `BATCH_STATES` vocabulary is aggregator-local, deliberately NOT in `adaptive-schema.js`). 75-assertion `scripts/test-parallel-batch.js` over `$TMPDIR` fixtures.
  - **`next-action.js` distinguishes ready-pending from active (AC#1/AC#5), purely additive.** Two pure-subset fields added — `readyPending` (ready nodes whose own status is `pending`, the openable batch frontier) and `active` (every `in_progress` node) — while `readySet`/`nextNode`/`allDone` and the stall/deadlock predicate stay byte-unchanged, so the single-node loop and the existing `test-next-action.js` assertions keep working unchanged (65 assertions: 33 back-compat + 32 new).
  - **`orient` enforces the AC#5 typed refusal (the load-bearing legality gate).** `runOrient` in `adaptive-node.js` now enumerates ALL `in_progress` ledger rows and emits the typed refusal `{result:'refuse', reason:'orphan_multi_in_progress'}` when more than one `in_progress` row exists without a valid active batch manifest whose member set matches them — because in this codebase a "typed refusal" must be a script emission, not orchestrator prose. Strictly additive and read-only: the single-node resume path (`inProgressNode`/`cacheState`) and `consentHalt`/`escalatedToFull`/`allDone` are unchanged; new `inProgressNodes[]`/`batch` fields are added alongside (`test-adaptive-node.js` 104 → 135 assertions).
  - **Executor + planner semantics.** The `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` command + SKILL move from "one role node at a time" to "one **frontier unit** at a time" (single-node legacy path preserved; batch path = `open-batch` → concurrent `Agent()` dispatch in one message → record-evidence per member → `seal` → `join` → advance), and the `workflow-planner` profile gains an "author **EFFICIENT DAGs**" instruction (expose independent work as siblings; serialize only for true deps / shared lanes / selectors / loops / gates).
  - **No new barrier/gate surface; four-edition parity.** A batch is N invocations of the existing per-node `commit-node` barrier, so `--gate-verify`/`--verdict-check`/Phase-6 `--barrier-check` all continue to hold. Ships across all four editions (root + claude byte-identical `parallel-batch.js`/`next-action.js`/`adaptive-node.js`; renamed `kaola-gitlab-*`/`kaola-gitea-*` forks; `adaptive-schema.js` untouched), registered in `COMMON_SCRIPTS`, the three `install.sh` `SUPPORT_SCRIPT_NAMES` blocks, `package.json`, and the contract validators. Design reference: `docs/investigations/2026-06-07-parallel-ready-set-execution-design.md` (resolves the issue's dangling reference). `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` exits 0; `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea). The G1 code-review and a read-only adversarial-verifier both recorded `verdict: pass`; the verifier's first pass refuted an earlier draft (the `orient` AC#5 gap above), which was then repaired and re-verified.
  - **Honest partials / recorded follow-ups (never silently dropped).** Wall-clock parallelism is provided by the main-session dispatch, not the aggregator, so it is not demonstrable by the aggregator's unit tests (which prove state correctness). **Write-role `fanout(...)` batches** require host isolated-worktree support; where it is absent the write-role frontier degrades to serialized execution (correctness preserved, parallelism forgone) — documented in the design note. Reviewer follow-ups recorded for a future hardening pass: a duplicate inline compliance row (cosmetic), `open-batch` ledger-flip-before-baseline atomicity (fails closed today), the write-role-join `gitCheckout` ref-vs-path edge on the dormant path, and an `orient`/`crossCheckStatus` partial-seal exact-equality case (a 3+-member granular-seal batch on crash-resume reads as `orphan_multi_in_progress` and fails closed — a coordinated two-gate subset-predicate fix is the follow-up).
- **adaptive: an unresolved in-scope reviewer/verifier `action:fix` finding now fails the mechanical verdict gate, forcing repair before finalize (#279).** Adaptive review safety previously depended on the frozen DAG already containing a repair lane, or on the reviewer choosing `verdict: fail` / `findings_blocking > 0`. A planner cannot know in advance whether a `code-reviewer`, `security-reviewer`, or `adversarial-verifier` node will surface something that should be fixed, so an actionable in-scope finding could be reported as non-blocking, the plan could continue to `finalize`, and the fix could be deferred as a follow-up because the frozen DAG lacked a fix node (the shape #275 exposed: `tdd-guide → code-reviewer → finalize` with follow-up findings and no mechanical route but finalize). #279 redefines gate completion so `verdict: pass` is **invalid** when the evidence carries an unresolved in-scope actionable defect — without ever mutating the frozen DAG ad hoc.
  - **Machine-readable findings contract.** A gate/skeptic role records, alongside its `verdict:` / `findings_blocking:` lines, zero or more flat, column-0-anchored `finding:` lines in its `.cache/{node-id}.md` evidence — e.g. `finding: id=R1 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=…`. Three closed vocabularies in the 4-tree byte-identical `kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (`FINDING_SCOPE_VOCABULARY` = in_scope/out_of_scope/pre_existing/needs_user_decision; `FINDING_ACTION_VOCABULARY` = fix/follow_up/document/none; `FINDING_STATUS_VOCABULARY` = open/resolved/deferred), parsed by a new pure `parseNodeFindings(cacheText)` (native multiline regex, fence-blind-by-column-0-anchor, the same discipline as `parseNodeVerdict`/`parseNodeSelector`; absent findings ⇒ `[]`).
  - **The gate predicate.** A new pure `unresolvedInScopeFixes(findings)` returns the findings that are `scope: in_scope`, `action: fix`, and not explicitly `resolved`/`deferred`. It is **fail-closed on status** (a present finding with a missing/unknown `status` counts as `open` and blocks, so a reviewer cannot bypass the gate by omitting the field) and **severity-agnostic** (a LOW/MEDIUM in-scope fix still blocks — severity governs urgency/escalation, never whether the gate blocks).
  - **`--verdict-check` hardened.** `verifyVerdictBlock` in `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` (the byte-identical root ↔ `plugins/kaola-workflow` pair plus the renamed GitLab/Gitea ports) now fails a completed gate-role node — in BOTH the sequence gate path and the fanout `adversarial-verifier` majority-refute tally — when `unresolvedInScopeFixes(parseNodeFindings(...))` is non-empty, EVEN on `verdict: pass` / `findings_blocking: 0`. The offending finding ids surface in the result's `unresolvedFixes[]`. The change is fully backward-compatible: evidence with no `finding:` lines parses to `[]` and passes exactly as before (the existing #251 verdict cases stay green). The gate stays informational per-node and **blocking** whole-plan in Phase 6, so the linear `implement → review → finalize` shape can no longer finalize while an in-scope actionable review finding is unresolved.
  - **Bounded repair routing.** A new `## Repair routing (in-scope review findings — #279)` section in all four `/kaola-workflow-plan-run` executor docs (the github skill + the github/gitlab/gitea command copies) documents the findings-emission contract and the bounded repair controller: when `--verdict-check` reports `unresolvedFixes`, the orchestrator does NOT finalize — it routes each finding to its `fix_role` (behavior/test/code → `tdd-guide`; no-natural-test/refactor/config/docs glue → `implementer`; build/type/lint/tooling → `build-error-resolver`; security-sensitive → fix role then re-run `security-reviewer`; adversarial refute → fix role then re-run the verifier/quorum), confined to the repair envelope (original write set ∪ tests ∪ docs/changelog ∪ required byte-identical mirrors, enforced by the per-node `commit-node` barrier — an out-of-envelope write is refused, else halt/replan/ask via `write-halt`), re-reviews, and bounds the loop by the static `LOOP_CAP`, escalating via `write-halt` on exhaustion. Findings marked `out_of_scope`/`pre_existing`/`needs_user_decision` (or `action: follow_up`/`document`) do not block but MUST be recorded as explicit, machine-readable follow-ups, never silently dropped.
  - **Regression coverage.** New `#279` cases in `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` `testAdaptiveVerdictCheck`: `parseNodeFindings` units (flat parse, absent ⇒ `[]`, column-0 anchor), the `unresolvedInScopeFixes` predicate (missing-status fail-closed, resolved/deferred pass, severity-irrelevant), an AC1 per-node pure case (`verdict: pass` + unresolved in-scope fix ⇒ `ok: false`), an AC3 case (`out_of_scope` follow-up non-blocking), and the AC6 **whole-plan** `--verdict-check` CLI regression — a completed `code-reviewer` node whose `.cache` carries `verdict: pass` + an unresolved in-scope fix exits 1, with a `status=resolved` negative control exiting 0. `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea). Builds on #251 (the mechanical verdict gate); closes the #275 gap. Follow-up (recorded out-of-scope by the review node): `parseNodeFindings` lowercases finding keys but not values, so a mis-cased `scope=In_Scope` fails open — a future hardening item to align with `parseNodeVerdict` value-lowercasing.
- **adaptive: `select()` composition + runtime test coverage locks in the #263 Classify-And-Act primitives (#267).** #263 shipped the `select(<group>)` selective-execution grammar (`parseNodeSelector`, the `selector_source` column, G-SEL-1..4, `--selector-check`, the `selectorCheck` commit-node barrier) and flipped the `testAdaptivePatternLibrary` tripwire from `refuse` to `in-grammar`, but that coverage only proved the primitives in isolation — nothing exercised them **composed** with the other grammar shapes or **surviving runtime state transitions**. Purely additive test coverage in `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (no grammar/validator change — the issue's anticipated G5 "unhandled validator case" did **not** materialize): a new `validateSelectFixture` helper and four registered functions. **G1 composition** (`testAdaptiveSelectComposition`): `select()` downstream of a write-role `fanout()` whose read-only synth is the `selector_source` (G1a), an `adversarial-verifier` read-only fan-out upstream of a `select()` group (G1b), a `loop(<cap>)` node co-existing with a `select()` group as non-overlapping subgraphs (G1c), and gate-post-dominance over a `select()` superset — both the valid all-arms case and a **negative control** that refuses with the specific `does not post-dominate` error rather than a bare `refuse` (G1d) — all validated against the live validator. **G2 multi-group**: two independent `select()` groups with distinct names (`fix`/`theme`) and distinct classifiers validate in-grammar (the same-name-different-classifier collision is already covered by the #271 refusal test). **G3 n/a propagation** (`testAdaptiveSelectNaPropagation`): an unfrozen plan whose `## Node Ledger` marks one arm `n/a` is run against the **real** `kaola-workflow-next-action.js` (not mocked) — the `n/a` arm is absent from `readySet` and the pending arm is present (`TERMINAL = {complete, n/a}` locked as a fixture). **G4 resume** (`testAdaptiveSelectResumeCheck`): a `plantFrozenPlan` select plan is freeze-then-ledger-mutated to a partial select state (classifier `complete`, one arm `n/a`); `--resume-check` stays `ok:true` because `computePlanHash` covers `## Meta` + `## Nodes` only and **excludes** the mutable `## Node Ledger`, and `next-action` then puts the remaining arm in `readySet`. **G5** (`testAdaptiveSelectSelectorSourceFanoutMember`): a read-only node that is simultaneously a `fanout()` leg AND a `select()` group's `selector_source` is pinned in-grammar — the grammar permits the dual role. Anti-vacuous-discipline: every new function is registered in the run list and verified to emit its `PASSED` line, and the G3/G4/G5 assertions were flip-then-revert mutation-checked to confirm each bites. `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` exits 0 with all new cases; `npm test` green across all four editions (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea).
- **codex/adaptive: harden the Codex harness contract with native dispatch, a durable task mirror, and a compact/resume hook (#266).** The Codex edition still leaned on Claude-Code-shaped assumptions in several places; #266 moves the invariants onto Codex-native surfaces (scripts + durable files + an on-demand hook) so the harness stops relying on Claude `Agent(...)` choreography it cannot mechanically enforce. Delivered across the Codex/GitHub, GitLab, and Gitea editions (the Claude tree carries only byte-identity artifacts):
  - **AC-A — Codex-native dispatch text.** Removed the Claude-only `subagent_type="workflow-planner"` call-syntax leak from the Codex adapt skill's operational dispatch prose (`plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md`), replacing it with a Codex-native role-dispatch description. The `Agent(...)` reference inside the byte-locked `# Claude Project Instructions` init template was deliberately left intact — it is installed-CLAUDE.md payload (correct for a Claude consumer, byte-locked to its paired command file), not the skill's operational contract.
